products.title: Giant Kuhli Loach (Pangio myersi)
products.body_html:
The Siamese Algae Eater (Crossocheilus siamensis) is widely regarded as one of the most effective freshwater algae-eating fish available in the aquarium hobby. Known for its distinctive black horizontal stripe and active swimming behavior, this species is valued for its ability to consume various forms of nuisance algae, including hair algae and filamentous algae.

This offering consists of Tank-Bred specimens, raised in controlled aquarium environments for improved adaptability and stability in captive systems. Tank-bred Siamese Algae Eaters acclimate well to established community aquariums when provided appropriate space and structure.

Well-suited for planted aquariums and medium to large community setups, Crossocheilus siamensis performs best in stable freshwater systems with ample swimming room and environmental structure.

What We Like About This Fish:

• Excellent Algae Control: One of the few species known to consume filamentous and hair algae.
• Active and Engaging: Constant movement adds energy to larger aquariums.
• Community Compatible: Generally peaceful with non-aggressive species.
• Hardy and Adaptable: Tank-bred specimens adjust well to stable freshwater conditions.

Quick Care Guide

• Care Level: Easy to Moderate
• Temperament: Peaceful
• Diet: Omnivorous (algae-focused)
• Social Behavior: Can be kept singly or in small groups
• Average Adult Size: 5–6 inches
• Average Purchase Size: 1.5–2.5 inches

Recommended Tank Parameters

• Temperature: 75–80°F (24–27°C)
• pH: 6.5–7.5
• KH: Low to Moderate
• Minimum Tank Size: 30 gallons

products.title: Magnificent Killifish (Simpsonichthys magnificus)
products.body_html:
Sexed Pair and individuals – Rare, Brilliantly Colored Annual Killifish

For the safety of our animals, we now ship live fish exclusively via UPS Next Day Air. If your order contains this item, you will only see UPS Next Day Air as a shipping option during checkout.

These fish may exhibit slight fin imperfections from natural nipping during group housing. This is cosmetic only and does not affect long-term health.

A breathtaking showcase of color and pattern, the Magnificent Killifish (Simpsonichthys magnificus) is a standout among South American annual killifish. Each purchase includes a sexed male–female pair, allowing hobbyists to enjoy their natural behaviors and easily explore breeding. Males display intense blues, reds, and metallic spotting across the fins and body, while females offer soft, earthy tones with subtle patterning. This species is admired for its vivid appearance, lively behavior, and fascinating life cycle tied to seasonal floodplain environments.

What We Love About This Fish:

• Sexed Pair Included: Every order provides one male and one female for natural behavior and breeding potential.
• Spectacular Male Coloration: Metallic blues and vibrant reds that shine in soft, tannin-rich water.
• Captivating Behavior: Active, alert, and full of character.
• Unique Annual Lifecycle: A rewarding species for aquarists interested in peat-incubation breeding projects.

Natural Habitat:

Simpsonichthys magnificus is a wild-type species native to seasonal pools and floodplains of Brazil, where temporary rain-filled habitats appear during the wet season. These shallow waters are warm, soft, rich with leaf litter, botanicals, and fine sediment. Adults live their full life cycle in these ephemeral pools, while eggs remain in the substrate during the dry season until rains return.

Aquascaping:

Create a soft, atmospheric environment that mirrors the shaded seasonal pools where Simpsonichthys magnificus thrives. Use fine-leaved plants and floating cover to diffuse the light and give these killifish the sense of security they experience in the wild.

• Plants: Choose plants that create gentle shadows and dense refuge—Java Moss, Subwassertang, Water Sprite, Water Wisteria, and floating plants like Frogbit or Red Root Floater. These help replicate the dim, tea-tinted waters they naturally inhabit.
• Hardscape: Incorporate smooth river stones, dark sand, and twisting pieces of driftwood to create small pockets and tucked-away spaces. Annual killifish do best when the scape breaks up open lines of sight and gives them defined territories.
• Botanicals: Add Aquatic Arts–brand botanicals such as Indian Almond Leaves, Catappa Bark, Magnolia Leaves, and Alder Cones. Buddha Pods and Peal Pods work as great organic shelter for these fish as well These enrich the water with tannins, promote natural behavior, and mimic the soft, leaf-littered substrates of their native pools.

Diet and Feeding:

A carnivorous micro-predator, the Magnificent Killifish thrives on a varied diet. Offer high-quality micro pellets, frozen daphnia, baby brine shrimp, bloodworms, and small live foods when possible. Variety helps maintain excellent color, vitality, and breeding readiness.

Tank Mates and Compatibility:

Sold as a male–female pair, these fish do extremely well in a dedicated species tank. They may also be kept with very peaceful, similarly sized fish that prefer soft, tannin-rich water. Avoid fin-nippers, fast-moving species, or any tank mate that may outcompete them for food.

Shrimp Compatibility

Generally safe with adult dwarf shrimp. However, small shrimp fry may be eaten. Dense moss, botanicals, and shaded cover improve fry survival if keeping shrimp alongside them.

Breeding:

Each order includes a ready pair, making breeding approachable. As an annual killifish, S. magnificus deposits eggs in fine substrate or peat moss. Eggs are typically collected, gently dried, and incubated for several weeks before hatching. This species is perfect for hobbyists wanting to learn the classic peat-incubation method used for South American annual killifish.

Recommended Tank Parameters:

• Temperature: 72° – 78° F (22° – 26° C)
• pH: 5.5 – 7.0
• KH: 0 – 4 dKH
• Minimum Tank Size: 10 gallons

Care Guidelines:

• Diet: Carnivorous; thriving on live, frozen, and micro-prepared foods.
• Social Behavior: Provided as a male–female pair. Best kept as a pair or small breeding group; males may spar lightly.
• Origin: Wild-type species; availability varies seasonally.
• Average Adult Size: 1.5 – 2 inches
• Average Purchase Size: 0.75 – 1.25 inches

Why We Recommend This Fish:

Offering unmatched beauty and a fascinating life cycle, the Magnificent Killifish is ideal for aquarists who want a brilliantly colored centerpiece fish with engaging behavior. Because these are sexed pairs, you can immediately enjoy natural interactions and breeding potential.

Live Arrival Guarantee:

All Aquatic Arts brand plants and animals come with a 100% live arrival guarantee, plus free email support.

 

 

 

Magnificent Killifish Q&A

1. What is a Magnificent Killifish (Simpsonichthys magnificus)?

The Magnificent Killifish is a brightly colored South American annual killifish known for its vivid reds, blues, and metallic markings. It is highly valued for its beauty, natural behaviors, and unique seasonal breeding cycle.

2. Can I buy Magnificent Killifish as singles or pairs?

Yes. Aquatic Arts offers single-gender options (male or female) as well as sexed male–female pairs for customers who want to observe natural interactions or attempt breeding.

3. How large do Magnificent Killifish grow?

Adults reach 1.5 to 2 inches, making them an excellent choice for nano and small planted aquariums.

4. What tank size is recommended for Magnificent Killifish?

A 10-gallon tank is recommended for a single fish or a pair. Larger setups are preferred when keeping small groups or breeding.

5. What water conditions do Magnificent Killifish need?

They thrive in soft, slightly acidic water, with a temperature of 72–78°F, pH of 5.5–7.0, and low KH (0–4 dKH).

6. Are Magnificent Killifish suitable for beginners?

Yes—once the tank is properly set up. They do well in stable, planted aquariums and are manageable for beginners who can maintain consistent water parameters.

7. What should I feed a Magnificent Killifish?

They are carnivorous and thrive on micro pellets, frozen foods, live baby brine shrimp, bloodworms, and daphnia. A varied diet enhances coloration and vitality.

8. Can Magnificent Killifish live with other fish?

They do best in a species-only aquarium or with very peaceful fish of similar size. Avoid fin-nippers or fast-moving species that may cause stress or competition.

9. Are Magnificent Killifish safe to keep with shrimp?

They are generally safe with adult dwarf shrimp, but they may prey on shrimp fry. Dense plants and botanicals can provide additional cover for shrimp.

10. How do you breed Magnificent Killifish?

This species lays eggs in fine substrate or peat moss. The eggs are removed and incubated dry for several weeks before hatching. Purchasing a sexed pair makes breeding more accessible for hobbyists interested in annual killifish techniques.

products.title: Hamburger Swordtail (Xiphophorus helleri hybrid) Tank-Bred!
products.body_html:
* For the safety of our animals, we now ship live fish exclusively via UPS Next Day Air.  If your order contains this item, you will only see UPS Next Day Air as a shipping option during checkout *

 

This uncommon swordtail variety is a deluxe variation on the black swordtail, and it has added yellow-orange fins and added body coloration!

 

The Hamburger Swordtail (Xiphophorus helleri hybrid) is a rather uncommon and deluxe Black Swordtail variation that has yellow-orange fins and body coloration in addition to its overall black body coloration with iridescent blue-green flecks. As is typical with swordtails, males often have a long "sword" extension at the bottom of the tail, although some females can also develop a sword. Reliable sexing can be done by checking the anal fin area for the male gonopodium, a tube-like organ used in fertilization during spawning. The sword tail extension is almost always yellow to yellow-orange with this variety. Like most swordtail varieties, the Hamburger Swordtail is a variant of the wild Green Swordtail (Xiphophorus helleri), but it also has genetics of other Xiphophorus species (such as X. maculatus and X. variatus platy varieties) to achieve new color patterns. 

 

Swordtails typically occupy the top level of the water column, although they can often be seen swimming and feeding in the middle and bottom levels as well. They are also not afraid to jump, so they must be kept in an aquarium with either a lid/hood/canopy or at least a significantly lowered water level. They will not eat or bother plants. They are safe with other peaceful fish as long as the other fish are not known to nip at the long tail of the male swordtails. Adult dwarf shrimp may be safe as well, some swordtails will attain a size large enough to consider such small shrimp prey. Larger, peaceful invertebrates can also make good tankmates. Swordtails are best kept in groups, particularly groups where females outnumber males since mature males will constantly pursue females for breeding.

 

While not a picky eater, the Hamburger Swordtail will thrive and remain very colorful on a varied diet of meaty foods. High-quality flake food, pellets, and frozen or freeze-dried tubifex and bloodworms will all be readily accepted.

 

 

What We Like About This Fish:

  • Uncommon variety with deluxe coloration
  • Peaceful disposition with fish and peaceful invertebrates
  • Hardy and adaptable
  • Excellent for inhabiting the top of the aquarium
  • Very undemanding and perfect for beginners and experts alike   

 

RECOMMENDED TANK PARAMETERS:

  • Temperature:  64° - 82.4° F (16° - 28° C)
  • pH:  7.0 - 8.0
  • KH:  10 - 30 dKH
  • Minimum tank size:  10 gallons

 

CARE GUIDELINES: 

  • Diet:  Omnivorous.  Will easily accept high quality dry and frozen foods.
  • Social behavior:  Peaceful, schooling/shoaling.  
  • Origin:  Tank-bred, but indigenous to Mexico.
  • Average adult size:  2.5 - 3 inches (6.3 - 7.5 cm)
  • Average purchase size:  1 - 1.5 inches (2.5 - 3.8 cm) or larger

products.title: Panda Dwarf Cichlid (Apistogramma nijsseni)
products.body_html:
For the safety of our animals, we now ship live fish exclusively via UPS Next Day Air. If your order contains this item, you will only see UPS Next Day Air as a shipping option during checkout.

The Panda Dwarf Cichlid (Apistogramma nijsseni) is one of the most striking and personality-filled dwarf cichlids available. Known for its jet-black patches set against vibrant golden-yellow and blue highlights, this compact species radiates charm and intelligence. A pair will quickly establish a territory, display fascinating courtship behavior, and interact actively with their surroundings—making them a centerpiece in any small aquarium.

At Aquatic Arts, we offer both Tank-Bred and Locally Bred (Indiana) specimens:

  • Tank-Bred: Raised and conditioned in closed aquatic systems for consistent color, size, and calm temperament. Some are bred seasonally in outdoor ponds, then conditioned indoors for stability before shipping.

  • Locally Bred (Indiana): Produced by experienced small-scale breeders in Indiana who focus on strong genetics, vibrant coloration, and healthy pair bonding.

Both variants exhibit the same beautiful patterns and personable behavior that make A. nijsseni a favorite among cichlid enthusiasts.

What We Love About This Fish:

• Beautiful panda-like black and gold coloration with iridescent blue accents
• Highly intelligent and interactive—full of personality
• Peaceful for a cichlid; excellent choice for planted community tanks
• Males develop vivid coloration and elaborate fin extensions when mature

Natural Habitat:

The wild Apistogramma nijsseni originates from the slow-moving, tannin-rich tributaries of the Ucayali River basin in Peru. These blackwater environments are shaded by dense forest canopy, lined with fallen leaves, and filled with submerged roots and leaf litter. The aquarium environment should reflect these natural conditions, emphasizing soft, acidic water and ample cover.

Aquascaping:

The Panda Dwarf Cichlid flourishes in a plush, nature-inspired aquarium that recreates the quiet, shaded backwaters of the Amazon. Every detail—from the soft substrate to the filtered golden light—should evoke a sense of calm and complexity, allowing these intelligent fish to display their full range of natural behaviors.

• Plants: Dense clusters of Cryptocoryne, Assorted Sword Plants, and Java Fern form a lush mid- to background canopy, while fine-leaved species like Myriophyllum or Water Sprite soften the layout and provide shelter for fry. Floating plants such as Frogbit or Water Lettuce help diffuse light, enhancing the intimate, dappled glow that A. nijsseni prefers.

• Hardscape: Create a sense of depth and comfort with intertwined driftwood roots, river stones, and sand pathways that lead into shaded alcoves. A soft, fine sand substrate allows for their natural sifting and foraging behavior while showcasing their subtle color shifts.

• Botanicals: Use Aquatic Arts Indian Almond Leaves, Catappa Bark, and Alder Cones to release natural tannins, enrich the water with humic compounds, and support beneficial biofilm growth. Layer these botanicals to build texture and visual warmth throughout the scape. For additional structure and spawning sites, incorporate Cholla Wood and Coconut Hut Shelters — perfect for nesting pairs and microfauna development.

When arranged thoughtfully, this environment becomes more than an aquarium — it’s a living, breathing biotope where your Panda Dwarf Cichlids will thrive, display, and even raise young in complete comfort.

Diet and Feeding:

Feeding the Panda Dwarf Cichlid is as rewarding as observing its behavior. These inquisitive fish respond eagerly to a varied diet that mirrors the abundance of their natural Amazon habitat.

A balanced feeding routine should blend both prepared and fresh options. Offer high-quality micro pellets as a daily staple, ensuring even small individuals can feed comfortably. Supplement with live or frozen foods such as brine shrimp, bloodworms, and daphnia to satisfy their instinct for movement and hunting. These protein-rich meals intensify their coloration and bring out their trademark golden glow.

For digestive health and balance, include occasional vegetable-based flakes or spirulina-enriched foods, which provide trace nutrients and support overall vitality. Feeding smaller amounts multiple times a day encourages natural foraging and keeps the cichlids active and engaged with their surroundings.

Tank Mates and Compatibility:

Panda Dwarf Cichlids are generally peaceful but can be territorial during breeding. They coexist well with calm species such as Tetras, Rasboras, Pencilfish, and Corydoras Catfish. Avoid aggressive or fin-nipping fish. Keep a bonded pair or a single male with multiple females in a well-structured tank.

Shrimp Compatibility:

Not suitable for cohabitation with small ornamental shrimp. A. nijsseni may prey on shrimp or fry, especially during breeding. Larger Amano Shrimp can coexist if ample cover and plant density are provided.

Breeding:

A cave-spawning species, the female lays eggs on the roof of a shelter or within a secluded structure such as a coconut hut, or Breeding Cave, . After hatching, the female guards the fry while the male defends the territory. Spawning is encouraged by maintaining slightly acidic, warm water (pH 6.0–6.5; ~80°F/27°C) and offering plenty of hiding spots and botanicals.

Recommended Tank Parameters:

• Temperature: 76° - 82° F (24° - 28° C)
• pH: 6.0 - 7.2
• KH: 1 - 8 dKH
• Minimum Tank Size: 20 gallons (pair)

Care Guidelines:

• Diet: Carnivorous omnivore; prefers protein-rich foods.
• Social Behavior: Peaceful but territorial during breeding.
• Origin: Tank-Bred (pond or closed system) or Locally Bred in Indiana.
• Average Adult Size: 2 – 2.5 inches (5 – 6.3 cm)
• Average Purchase Size: 1 – 1.5 inches (2.5 – 3.8 cm)

Bring home the Panda Dwarf Cichlid and enjoy one of the aquarium world’s most charismatic dwarf species. With their intelligence, color, and captivating behavior, these fish are living proof that the smallest creatures can leave the biggest impression.

Live Arrival Guarantee:

products.title: Gold Dust Sailfin Molly (Poecilia latipinna)
products.body_html:
For the safety of our animals, we now ship live fish exclusively via UPS Next Day Air. If your order contains this item, you will only see UPS Next Day Air as a shipping option during checkout.

The Gold Dust Sailfin Molly is a captivating color variant of the Sailfin Molly, prized for its gleaming gold-to-black gradient and graceful sail-like dorsal fin. With its bold coloration and lively temperament, this variety brings warmth and motion to any aquarium.

At Aquatic Arts, we offer both Tank-Bred and Locally Bred (Indiana) specimens:

  • Tank-Bred: Raised in carefully maintained aquatic systems for consistent color, size, and adaptability.

  • Locally Bred (Indiana): Produced by trusted independent breeders here in Indiana, known for maintaining strong genetics and natural hardiness through selective breeding.

Both options display the same beauty, health, and peaceful disposition that make this molly a favorite among aquarists.

What We Love About This Fish:

• Brilliant two-tone blend of metallic gold and deep black
• Males develop a large, dramatic sailfin with elegant movement
• Peaceful, social, and adaptable to community or lightly brackish tanks
• Excellent beginner fish with a calm, active nature

Natural Habitat:

The wild Poecilia latipinna originates in the southeastern United States, where it inhabits shallow coastal waters, marshes, and estuaries. These areas are rich in vegetation and feature slightly brackish conditions. The Gold Dust Sailfin Molly is a selectively bred variant developed in captivity to emphasize its gold and black contrast while preserving the resilience of its wild ancestors.

Aquascaping:

The Gold Dust Sailfin Molly thrives in bright, open aquariums that mimic its natural coastal environment — warm shallows with plenty of movement, sunlight, and vegetation. Creating a balanced blend of plants, texture, and natural materials will bring out both their color and personality.

• Plants: Flowing greens like Vallisneria and Hornwort create a lively backdrop, while Anubias and Amazon Sword offer contrasting leaf shapes and provide gentle shelter. A mix of rooted and rhizome plants keeps the scape dynamic and natural.

• Hardscape: Combine smooth river stones, aged driftwood, and a soft sand or fine gravel substrate to create an inviting landscape for grazing and exploration. The contrast of pale sand beneath the molly’s gold-and-black coloration makes their hues pop.

• Botanicals: Introduce natural tannins and beneficial surface area with Aquatic Arts Indian Almond Leaves, Catappa Bark, or Alder Cones. These botanicals help stabilize water chemistry, encourage biofilm growth, and create a more natural ecosystem.

For additional enrichment, consider accenting the layout with Cholla Wood or a Coconut Hut Shelter, both excellent for algae and biofilm growth that support microfauna and foraging behavior.

Diet and Feeding:

Omnivorous by nature, mollies thrive on a balanced diet that includes high-quality flakes or micro pellets, vegetable matter (such as blanched spinach or zucchini), and live or frozen foods like brine shrimp and daphnia. Offering a mix of plant-based and protein-rich foods enhances both color and vitality.

Tank Mates and Compatibility:

Gold Dust Sailfin Mollies are peaceful community fish that coexist well with other livebearers such as Guppies, Platies, and Swordtails, as well as small schooling species like Tetras and Rasboras. Avoid fin-nipping fish or aggressive cichlids. Keep a ratio of one male to two or more females to prevent stress and display natural social behavior.

Shrimp Compatibility:

Generally compatible with adult freshwater shrimp. However, shrimp fry may be at risk of predation. For mixed tanks, provide dense vegetation, moss, or hiding areas to help shrimp colonies thrive alongside mollies.

Breeding:

Gold Dust Sailfin Mollies are livebearers that give birth to free-swimming fry. Males display to females using their impressive dorsal fin. Provide floating plants or dense cover to protect fry from adults. Fry readily accept powdered foods, crushed flakes, or baby brine shrimp.

Recommended Tank Parameters:

• Temperature: 74° - 82° F (23° - 28° C)
• pH: 7.0 - 8.3
• KH: 10 - 25 dKH
• Minimum Tank Size: 20 gallons

Care Guidelines:

• Diet: Omnivorous; provide both plant and protein foods daily.
• Social Behavior: Peaceful; thrives in small groups.
• Origin: Tank-Bred or Locally Bred in Indiana.
• Average Adult Size: 3 – 4 inches (7.6 – 10 cm)
• Average Purchase Size: 1.5 – 2.5 inches (3.8 – 6.3 cm)

Add vibrant color and personality to your tank with the Gold Dust Sailfin Molly — a lively, golden beauty that’s as hardy as it is eye-catching.

Tank Mates & Compatibility

products.title: L066 Xingu King Tiger Pleco (Hypancistrus seideli)
products.body_html:
* For the safety of our animals, we now ship live fish exclusively via UPS Next Day Air.  If your order contains this item, you will only see UPS Next Day Air as a shipping option during checkout * 

Overview

The L066 King Tiger Pleco (Hypancistrus seideli) is a striking and highly sought-after species from Brazil’s Rio Xingu, famous for its black-and-white striping and elegant, compact form. Unlike most plecos that graze primarily on algae, this species thrives on a protein-based diet and prefers shaded, fast-flowing habitats.

A perfect fit for aquarists who enjoy natural river-style aquascapes, this peaceful bottom dweller brings authentic South American character to any aquarium setup.

What We Love About This Fish:

• Distinctive Patterning: Beautiful black and white banding that varies from fish to fish.
• Compact Size: Averages 6 – 8 cm at purchase, making it well-suited to most community tanks.
• Peaceful & Reserved: Non-aggressive, ideal for planted aquariums.
• True River Behavior: Nocturnal by nature and enjoys resting in caves or under driftwood.

Natural Habitat:

The L066 King Tiger Pleco originates from the Rio Xingu, a clear and powerful tributary of the Amazon River in northern Brazil. The Xingu’s underwater landscape is a mosaic of rocky outcrops, driftwood branches, and sandy channels, with strong water flow and high oxygen levels.

During the wet season, the river floods the forest edge, carrying leaf litter and wood debris that enrich the water with tannins. In the dry season, the current intensifies, and the plecos shelter among rocks and driftwood, where they feed on meaty particles and biofilm.

In the aquarium, they remain nocturnal or crepuscular, resting during daylight hours. Providing ample hiding spots—such as caves, tunnels, and driftwood—helps them feel secure and encourages natural behavior.

Aquascaping:

• Plants: Hardy species such as Anubias, Java Fern, and Amazon Sword that attach easily to rock or wood.
• Hardscape: Combine smooth river stones, driftwood, and slate caves to create shaded areas and natural boundaries.
• Botanicals: Include Indian Almond Leaves, Casuarina Pods, or Catappa Bark from the Aquatic Arts Botanical Combo Pack to simulate blackwater conditions, add tannins, and promote beneficial biofilm.

Diet and Feeding:

The L066 King Tiger Pleco is a carnivorous species that benefits from a high-protein diet with minimal plant matter.
Feed a combination of:

  • Sinking carnivore pellets or wafers designed for bottom dwellers.

  • Frozen or live foods such as bloodworms, brine shrimp, and blackworms.

  • Occasional blanched vegetables like spinach or peas for dietary variety.

Feed primarily after lights-out to align with their natural nocturnal feeding rhythm. While they may nibble on biofilm, they will not clean your tank—regular aquarium maintenance remains essential.

Tank Mates and Compatibility:

A peaceful species that thrives alongside tetras, rasboras, dwarf cichlids, Corydoras, and shrimp. Avoid large, aggressive, or highly territorial bottom dwellers.
When keeping more than one pleco, ensure the tank includes multiple caves and shaded areas to define territory and prevent stress.

Shrimp Compatibility:

This species is compatible with most freshwater shrimp. Adult shrimp are typically ignored, though tiny fry may be disturbed while the pleco forages.
Providing dense plant cover, moss, or leaf litter ensures shrimp colonies remain safe and active.

Breeding:

Breeding occurs in tight-fitting caves or tubes, where males guard the eggs until hatching.
Maintain stable, warm, oxygen-rich water and offer a high-protein diet to promote spawning behavior.
Fry feed on biofilm and fine foods like crushed pellets or baby brine shrimp after becoming free-swimming.

Recommended Tank Parameters:

• Temperature: 78° - 84° F (25° - 29° C)
• pH: 6.5 - 7.5
• KH: 2 - 8 dKH
• Minimum Tank Size: 30 gallons

Care Guidelines:

• Diet: Carnivorous; prefers protein-rich foods with occasional vegetable matter.
• Social Behavior: Peaceful bottom-dweller; mildly territorial toward similar species.
• Activity Level: Primarily nocturnal; prefers dim lighting and shaded cover.
• Origin: Wild-caught from the Rio Xingu, Brazil.
• Average Adult Size: 10 – 12 cm (4 – 5 inches)
• Average Purchase Size: 6 – 8 cm (2.5 – 3 inches)

products.title: L034 Medusa Pleco (Ancistrus ranunculus) - Brazil
products.body_html:
For the safety of our animals, we now ship live fish exclusively via UPS Next Day Air. If your order contains this item, you will only see UPS Next Day Air as a shipping option during checkout.

The L034 Medusa Pleco is one of the most striking and unique members of the Loricariidae family. Known for its distinctive facial bristles and jet-black coloration, this rare species adds an extraordinary visual element to any aquascape. Native to Brazil’s Xingu River basin, it thrives in highly oxygenated, fast-flowing waters over smooth stones and sandy substrate.

What We Love About This Fish:

• Exotic Appearance: Distinctive “Medusa-like” bristles on the head give this pleco its name.
• Jet-Black Beauty: Deep black coloration with a subtle metallic sheen under aquarium lighting.
• Peaceful Temperament: Calm and compatible with most community fish.
• Functional and Fascinating: Helps maintain surface cleanliness while adding visual intrigue.

Natural Habitat:

The Medusa Pleco originates from the Rio Xingu in Brazil, a clearwater tributary of the Amazon characterized by fast-moving water, high oxygen levels, and rocky substrates. These natural conditions should be simulated in the aquarium with good water movement, stable temperatures, and high-quality filtration.

Aquascaping:

• Plants: Hardy, anchored species like Anubias, Java Fern, and Bucephalandra.
• Hardscape: Smooth river rocks, driftwood, and pleco caves for shelter.
• Botanicals: Indian Almond Leaves, Catappa Bark, or alder cones to promote natural water chemistry and biofilm growth.

Diet and Feeding:

Although often seen grazing surfaces, this pleco is not a strict algae eater. Offer a balanced diet of sinking algae wafers, spirulina pellets, blanched vegetables (zucchini, spinach), and high-protein foods such as frozen bloodworms or Repashy blends. Access to driftwood is beneficial for digestion and rasping behavior.

Tank Mates and Compatibility:

The Medusa Pleco is peaceful and does well in community aquariums with small tetras, rasboras, and other non-aggressive fish. Avoid housing with territorial bottom dwellers or fin-nipping species.

Shrimp Compatibility:

Generally safe with adult shrimp, particularly larger Neocaridina and Caridina species. However, newly hatched shrimp fry may be at risk of accidental predation, so providing ample cover with mosses or dense plants is recommended.

Breeding:

In nature, this species spawns in narrow rock crevices. In aquariums, provide tight-fitting caves, strong water flow, and high oxygen levels. Males guard eggs and young until they become free-swimming.

Recommended Tank Parameters:

• Temperature: 78° – 86° F (26° – 30° C)
• pH: 6.4 – 7.4
• KH: 2 – 8 dKH
• Minimum Tank Size: 30 gallons

Care Guidelines:

• Diet: Omnivorous; requires a mix of plant and protein-based foods.
• Social Behavior: Peaceful; best kept singly or with other calm species.
• Origin: Wild-collected from the Xingu River, Brazil.
• Average Adult Size: 4.5 – 5 inches (11 – 13 cm)
• Average Purchase Size: 2.5 – 3.5 inches (6 – 9 cm)

Bring home the mysterious beauty of the L034 Medusa Pleco — a rare and fascinating species prized for its elegance, calm nature, and signature “Medusa” appearance.

products.title: Golden Vampire Pleco (Leporacanthicus sp.)
products.body_html:
For the safety of our animals, we now ship live fish exclusively via UPS Next Day Air. If your order contains this item, you will only see UPS Next Day Air as a shipping option during checkout.

The Golden Vampire Pleco is an exceptionally rare and visually captivating species within the Leporacanthicus genus, renowned for its metallic golden sheen and distinctive fang-like teeth. Found in rocky, fast-flowing waters of the Amazon basin, this pleco combines striking appearance with an assertive yet manageable personality. Its vibrant golden coloration and graceful movement make it a remarkable centerpiece for experienced aquarists and collectors alike.

What We Love About This Fish:

• Brilliant Metallic Coloration: Deep bronze-to-gold body tones that shimmer under aquarium lighting.
• Signature “Vampire” Teeth: Prominent fang-like teeth used for rasping meaty foods and biofilm.
• Rare and Sought-After: A highly prized species seldom seen in the trade.
• Distinct Personality: Bold and curious without being overly aggressive.

Natural Habitat:

This species is collected from fast-flowing tributaries of the Amazon River in Brazil, where oxygen-rich waters flow over rocky substrates. The Golden Vampire Pleco naturally inhabits shaded crevices and rocky ledges, often resting beneath stones or driftwood between feedings.

Aquascaping:

• Plants: Hardy species such as Anubias, Java Fern, and Bucephalandra that can attach to hardscape.
• Hardscape: Smooth stones, caves, and driftwood to provide secure hiding spots.
• Botanicals: Indian Almond Leaves or alder cones to enhance natural tannins and promote healthy water chemistry. Aquatic Arts Botrnical Combo pack has a great selection of leave litter that plecos love.

Diet and Feeding:

The Golden Vampire Pleco is primarily carnivorous, feeding on invertebrates and meaty foods in the wild. In the aquarium, provide a varied diet of sinking carnivore wafers, frozen bloodworms, blackworms, and high-protein Repashy blends we use and recommend Ultra Fresh Carnivore Sticks. Occasional vegetable offerings such as blanched zucchini can supplement their diet and improve digestion.

Tank Mates and Compatibility:

Best kept with peaceful mid- and upper-level fish such as tetras, rasboras, and rainbowfish. Avoid housing with other bottom dwellers or territorial plecos unless the tank is large and structured with ample hiding areas.

Shrimp Compatibility:

May coexist with adult shrimp in larger aquariums, though due to their carnivorous diet, smaller or newly hatched shrimp may be at risk. For mixed setups, provide dense plant coverage or mosses for refuge.

Breeding:

Captive breeding is uncommon but possible under the right conditions. Males tend to guard their chosen caves aggressively during spawning. Strong current, warm temperatures, and well-oxygenated water are key to encouraging breeding behavior.

Recommended Tank Parameters:

• Temperature: 78° – 86° F (26° – 30° C)
• pH: 6.2 – 7.4
• KH: 2 – 8 dKH
• Minimum Tank Size: 40 gallons

Care Guidelines:

• Diet: Carnivorous; prefers meaty foods and high-protein sinking diets.
• Social Behavior: Semi-territorial; best kept singly or with peaceful tank mates.
• Origin: Wild-collected from the Amazon Basin, Brazil.
• Average Adult Size: 6 – 7 inches (15 – 18 cm)
• Average Purchase Size: 3 – 4 inches (7.5 – 10 cm)

Add a touch of brilliance and mystery to your aquarium with the Golden Vampire Pleco, a rare gem that pairs elegance with strength and an unforgettable golden glow.

products.title: Melon Sword (Echinodorus osiris) - Bare Root
products.body_html:
The Melon Sword (Echinodorus osiris) is a very attractive centerpiece plant for the aquarium.  Its green and red leaves make for a very distinctive appearance and its tall, large leaves are remarkably noticeable in heavily planted settings.  Best of all, it is extremely undemanding when it comes to care requirements.

Care for the Melon Sword is very simple and easy.  While it will thrive in high lighting, it is also very durable and can do very well in low to moderate lighting.  It requires nutrient rich substrate for its extensive root structure.  It produces very large leaves, so it must be planted with care to not cast too much shade on other plants as it grows.  It is certainly only suitable for background placement in smaller aquariums, but it can also be placed in the middle ground of larger aquariums.  The Melon Sword reproduces through rhizome division as well as by the production of side shoots, which can be removed and replanted.  It is very intolerant of copper, so please keep this in mind if you use tap water in your aquarium.

The Melon Sword is a member of the plant group known as Rosettes, which are known for their extremely short stem axes and for the way their leaves grow upward from the base of the plant.  This plant can grow up to 20 inches in height with the proper lighting and water quality.  Approximate shipping size of our Melon Swords ranges from 4 to 8 inches tall.  The plant ships a as bare root clump.  As with all of our plants (besides tissue cultures), our Melon Swords are kept in water in a dedicated tank rather than plastic packaging, which means your plant will arrive much healthier.

Like most plants, Melon Sword will benefit from supplementation such as Seachem Flourish, Flourish Excel, Nitrogen and other plant supplements.

No aquarium plant distributor/nursery/wholesaler/retailer can or will guarantee their plants to be 100% snail free. We can not guarantee our plants will be snail free. 

What We Like About This Plant:

  • Tall and wide growth that is ideal for larger aquariums
  • Very easy to propagate
  • Provides a great deal of cover for animal fry
  • Very hardy and durable in a well-lit, nutrient-rich aquarium

 

Care Guidelines:

  • Temperature: 72° - 83° F (22° - 28° C)
  • pH: 6.5 - 7.5
  • Lighting: Moderate to high
  • Origin: Indigenous to South America, cultivated in US nurseries
  • Aquarium placement: Middle to background
  • Care: Easy

 

All Aquatic Arts brand plants and animals come with a 100% live arrival guarantee, plus free email support!

products.title: Aquatic Arts Mixed Fruit Leaf Botanical Pack (Mango, Guava, Banana & Jackfruit) - Dried Botanicals
products.body_html:
Discover one of our new favorites — the Aquatic Arts Mixed Fruit Leaf Botanical Pack, a vibrant blend of four tropical leaves that each bring unique benefits to your aquarium. This curated mix conditions your water naturally, supports biofilm development, and adds stunning visual variety to your aquascape.

Each pack includes 3 of each leaf type (12 leaves total), giving you the perfect balance of tannin release, texture, and longevity.

We love this blend — and we know you will too!

What’s Inside & Why It Matters:

• Mango Leaves (Mangifera indica) – Slowly release beneficial tannins and trace compounds that encourage beneficial bacterial growth. These sturdy leaves last for weeks while adding natural character to your aquascape.

• Guava Leaves (Psidium guajava) – Naturally antibacterial and antifungal, guava leaves help maintain healthy water conditions and support shrimp vitality. They lightly tint the water and promote a stable bioactive environment.

• Banana Leaves (Musa spp.) – Soft and quick to decompose, banana leaves are a favorite for shrimp and fry tanks. They encourage rich biofilm growth and release gentle tannins that foster healthy microfauna populations.

• Jackfruit Leaves (Artocarpus heterophyllus) – Known for their higher tannin content and thick texture, jackfruit leaves condition the water while replicating the natural blackwater habitats of tropical species.

Features We Love:

• Four Botanical Favorites: A balanced mix of mango, guava, banana, and jackfruit leaves.
• Tannin Variety: Layers different leaf types for staggered tannin release and visual depth.
• Supports Biofilm: Promotes natural biofilm growth essential for shrimp, snails, and fry.
• Natural Water Conditioning: Softens water and releases beneficial humic substances.

If you’re looking for even richer tannin coloration or softer water, use this pack alongside our Aquatic Arts Tannin Tea Bags — the perfect pairing for creating beautiful, debris-free blackwater conditions.

Usage:

Rinse the leaves before adding them to your aquarium. Start with 1–2 of each type per 10 gallons of water and adjust based on your desired tint or aesthetic.

You can leave the leaves in place as they soften and gradually decompose—many aquarists enjoy the natural look and the habitat benefits this provides for shrimp, snails, and microfauna. Remove or replace only if you prefer a tidier appearance.

 

Ideal For:

  • Shrimp, snail, and small community tanks

  • Blackwater and botanical-style aquariums

  • Breeding setups for softwater species

  • Terrariums and paludariums as natural décor and humidity holders

ARTS in Action:

Aquascaping: Layers organic texture and tone for a truly natural look.
Responsibility: Sustainably harvested and naturally air-dried, free of chemicals.
Thriving Life: Promotes microfauna, biofilm, and soft-water balance.
Storytelling: Each leaf tells a story of the rainforest canopy, now thriving in your tank.

Bring tropical balance and beauty to your aquarium with the Aquatic Arts Mixed Fruit Leaf Botanical Pack — a natural blend of mango, guava, banana, and jackfruit leaves designed to create a thriving ecosystem you’ll love.

products.title: Aquatic Arts Buddha / Nut Pods — Dried Botanicals
products.body_html:

products.title: Aquatic Arts Tannin Tea Bags (Terminalia catappa) — Natural Tannins Without Debris
products.body_html:
Experience the benefits of natural tannins without the mess. The Aquatic Arts Tannin Tea Bags offer a clean and convenient way to add the water-conditioning power of Terminalia catappa (Indian Almond Leaves) to your aquarium. Each tea bag slowly releases beneficial tannins and humic substances that help create a healthy, balanced environment for fish, shrimp, and other freshwater inhabitants—without leaving behind loose debris or leaf litter.

Perfect for aquarists who appreciate the beauty and function of blackwater conditions, these tea bags provide a gentle amber tint that soothes fish, enhances coloration, and supports natural behaviors such as breeding and foraging.

Features We Love:

• Natural Tannins, No Mess: Enjoy the conditioning power of Indian Almond Leaves without floating debris or disintegrating leaf material.
• Supports Fish Health: Tannins and humic acids promote slime coat production, reduce stress, and mimic natural habitats.
• Enhances Color & Behavior: Deepens colors in fish and shrimp while encouraging more natural activity.
• Safe & Sustainable: Made with responsibly sourced Terminalia catappa and sealed in biodegradable, aquarium-safe sachets.

Usage:

Simply drop one Aquatic Arts Tannin Tea Bag into your aquarium or filter chamber. Each bag treats up to 20 gallons (75 liters) of water. For stronger coloration or softer water, use additional bags as desired. Remove tea bag from the tank within 24 hours after adding it to avoid excessive breakdown of the materials. Use a new bag every 2–3 weeks or during regular water changes to replenish tannic acid. 

Pro Tip: For smaller tanks or shrimp setups, start with a partial steep (5–10 minutes in a separate container) before adding the conditioned water to your tank for precise control over color and tannin strength.

Ideal For:

  • Freshwater fish that thrive in soft, acidic, or blackwater environments (such as tetras, rasboras, bettas, and angelfish)

  • Freshwater shrimp and snails that benefit from biofilm and humic-rich water

  • Aquarists who love the aesthetic of naturally tinted aquascapes but prefer minimal maintenance

ARTS in Action:

Aquascaping: Enhances natural, biotope-inspired layouts with warm amber tones.
Responsibility: 100% natural, biodegradable, and sustainably sourced botanical material.
Thriving Life: Promotes healthy, stress-free behavior and stronger immune systems.
Storytelling: Captures the beauty and calm of tropical blackwater environments in a clean, modern format.

Create natural beauty and balance with the Aquatic Arts Tannin Tea Bags — the cleanest way to bring the benefits of botanicals into your aquarium.

products.title: Albino Cherry Barb (Puntius titteya “Albino”) – Tank Bred
products.body_html:
For the safety of our animals, we now ship live fish exclusively via UPS Next Day Air. If your order contains this item, you will only see UPS Next Day Air as a shipping option during checkout.

The Albino Cherry Barb is a radiant, tank-bred variant of the beloved Cherry Barb, showcasing a warm peach-gold body with pinkish highlights and striking ruby-red eyes. This peaceful schooling fish brings subtle elegance and activity to planted community aquariums. Its gentle temperament and glowing coloration make it an excellent choice for both beginners and experienced aquarists seeking harmony and color in the same fish.

What We Love About This Fish:

• Soft, Luminous Coloration: Warm gold and coral tones accented by vivid red eyes.
• Peaceful Nature: Perfect for planted community aquariums with calm, compatible species.
• Tank-Bred Strength: Raised entirely in aquariums for exceptional health and adaptability.
• Active Schooling Behavior: Graceful movement and coordinated swimming bring life to the midwater zone.

Natural Habitat:

The wild-type Puntius titteya originates from the shaded, plant-rich forest streams of Sri Lanka, where the water is soft, warm, and slow-moving. The Albino variety is a tank-bred morph, selectively developed to highlight the species’ natural pink and orange pigments. Though this color form does not occur in the wild, it maintains the same peaceful disposition and lively activity that have made Cherry Barbs a staple of freshwater aquariums for generations.

Aquascaping:

• Plants: Excellent with Java Fern, Anubias, Cryptocoryne, and stem plants like Ludwigia or Hygrophila.
• Hardscape: Include fine gravel or sand substrate, driftwood, and smooth stones for structure and shade.
• Botanicals: Add Indian Almond Leaves or Alder Cones to create a soft, natural environment and enrich water with tannins. Aquatic Arts Botanical Combo Pack is a curated selection of beautiful and beneficial leave litter.

Diet and Feeding:

The Albino Cherry Barb is an omnivore that thrives on a balanced, high-quality diet. Offer Ultra Fresh Tropical Excellent Bits – Shrimp Delights as the daily staple. Supplement occasionally with live or frozen foods such as Moina fleas, daphnia, and brine shrimp to encourage vibrant coloration, strong immunity, and breeding readiness.

Tank Mates and Compatibility:

A peaceful community species that should be kept in groups of six or more. Excellent companions include rasboras, small tetras, Corydoras catfish, and dwarf gouramis. Avoid aggressive or fin-nipping species to preserve their long fins and stress-free behavior.

Shrimp Compatibility:

Generally safe with adult Neocaridina and Caridina shrimp. Small shrimp fry may be eaten, so provide dense moss or plant cover for protection.

Breeding:

An easy-to-breed egg scatterer that spawns readily in clean, well-maintained tanks. Provide fine-leaved plants or spawning mops and remove adults after spawning to protect the eggs. Fry can be raised on infusoria or finely crushed food once they become free-swimming.

Recommended Tank Parameters:

• Temperature: 72° – 80°F (22° – 27°C)
• pH: 6.5– 7.5
• KH: 2 – 10 dKH
• Minimum Tank Size: 20 gallons

Care Guidelines:

• Diet: Omnivorous; thrives on Ultra Fresh Tropical Excellent Bits and live or frozen foods.
• Social Behavior: Schooling species; best kept in groups of 6 or more.
• Origin: Tank-bred.
• Average Adult Size: 1.5 – 2 inches (3.8 – 5 cm)
• Average Purchase Size: 1 – 1.25 inches (2.5 – 3 cm)

Add a touch of golden light and peaceful movement to your aquarium with the tank-bred Albino Cherry Barb — a hardy, active, and beautifully balanced addition to any community setup.

products.title: Legend Paraiba Angelfish (Pterophyllum scalare “Legend Paraiba”) – Tank Bred
products.body_html:
For the safety of our animals, we now ship live fish exclusively via UPS Next Day Air. If your order contains this item, you will only see UPS Next Day Air as a shipping option during checkout.

The Legend Paraiba Angelfish is a striking tank-bred variety of Pterophyllum scalare, admired for its metallic blue sheen and bold vertical banding that gleams under aquarium lighting. Selectively bred for brilliance and symmetry, this angelfish displays an almost iridescent turquoise glow that shifts as it moves. Graceful, intelligent, and social, the Legend Paraiba makes an impressive centerpiece in medium to large planted aquariums.

What We Love About This Fish:

• Brilliant Metallic Sheen: Unique blue-green highlights that shimmer across the body and fins.
• Graceful Personality: Calm yet commanding presence, making it a stunning display fish.
• Tank-Bred Hardiness: Raised entirely in aquariums for improved health and adaptability.
• Iconic Elegance: Long, trailing fins and bold markings make each fish look sculptural in motion.

Natural Habitat:

The original Pterophyllum scalare species was first documented in the Amazon Basin, particularly in the calm backwaters and floodplain regions of Brazil, Peru, and Colombia. The Legend Paraiba strain was developed through selective breeding in captivity to enhance its metallic turquoise and blue hues. Although a designer morph, it retains the same elegant body shape and peaceful nature of its wild ancestors, thriving in warm, soft, slightly acidic water.

Aquascaping:

• Plants: Amazon Swords, Vallisneria, and tall stem plants offer vertical structure for their long fins.
• Hardscape: Use driftwood, rock clusters, and subdued lighting to accentuate their reflective sheen.
• Botanicals: Incorporate Indian Almond Leaves or Alder Cones to release tannins and reduce stress, echoing their native blackwater conditions. Aquatic Arts Botanical Combo Pack is a curated selection of beautiful and beneficial leave litter. 

Diet and Feeding:

The Legend Paraiba Angelfish is an omnivore and appreciates a diverse diet. Offer Ultra Fresh Tropical Excellent Bits - Shrimp Delights as a daily staple, supplemented with frozen or live foods such as brine shrimp, bloodworms, and daphnia. A protein-rich diet enhances fin growth, coloration, and breeding readiness.

Tank Mates and Compatibility:

This variety is peaceful when housed with compatible species. Ideal tank mates include tetras (in groups), Corydoras catfish, rainbowfish, and peaceful dwarf cichlids. Avoid overly aggressive or fin-nipping species, and ensure tankmates are large enough not to be mistaken for food.

Shrimp Compatibility:

Adult angelfish may eat small shrimp, so they are not recommended for shrimp tanks. If kept with shrimp, provide extensive hiding areas with moss and dense plants.

Breeding:

Like other P. scalare, this variety is a substrate spawner. Pairs clean a flat surface (often plant leaves or rocks) to lay eggs and guard the nest. Slightly warmer temperatures and increased protein intake can encourage breeding. Fry can be raised on infusoria, then freshly hatched brine shrimp.

Recommended Tank Parameters:

• Temperature: 78° – 84°F (25° – 29°C)
• pH: 6.0 – 7.5
• KH: 1 – 6 dKH
• Minimum Tank Size: 30 gallons for a pair; 55+ gallons for groups

Care Guidelines:

• Diet: Omnivorous; thrives on Ultra Fresh Micro Pellets and frozen/live foods.
• Social Behavior: Semi-peaceful; best kept in pairs or groups of 4–6 to disperse mild territoriality.
• Origin: Tank-bred.
• Average Adult Size: 4 – 6 inches (10 – 15 cm) body; up to 8 inches (20 cm) including fins.
• Average Purchase Size: 1.5 – 2 inches (3.8 – 5 cm)

Graceful, intelligent, and luminous — the tank-bred Legend Paraiba Angelfish embodies the perfect balance of beauty and serenity, bringing timeless elegance to any planted aquarium.

The Scratched Inpa Tetra (Inpaichthys parauapiranga) is a rarely seen and highly attractive relative of the well-known Kerri Tetra. This tank-bred variant showcases a soft peach-to-copper body tone accented by a shimmering blue-violet stripe, giving it a luminous appearance that changes subtly depending on light and background. Peaceful, active, and adaptable, this small schooling fish is perfect for planted community aquariums and biotope-style setups inspired by the clearwater streams of Brazil.

What We Love About This Fish:

• Tank-Bred Health: Adapted to aquarium life, hardy, and easy to care for.
• Unique and Rare: A seldom-seen species that brings understated elegance to the aquarium.

Natural Habitat:

Inpaichthys parauapiranga originates from the clear, slow-moving forest streams of the Parauapebas River Basin in Pará, Brazil. These waters are rich in submerged roots, leaf litter, and aquatic vegetation. The species was originally documented in these Amazonian tributaries, where it inhabits calm, shaded areas with soft, slightly acidic water. The tank-bred line offered here retains the delicate beauty of its wild counterparts while being well adjusted to home aquariums.

Aquascaping:

• Plants: Performs beautifully in densely planted aquariums with species such as Cryptocoryne, Amazon Sword, and Water Sprite.

Diet and Feeding:

The Scratched Inpa Tetra is an omnivore and thrives on a varied, high-quality diet. Offer Ultra Fresh Tropical Micro Pellets – Shrimp Delight Formula as the staple food, supplemented with live or frozen treats such as Moina fleas, daphnia, and brine shrimp. A protein-rich, color-enhancing diet helps maintain their healthy glow and lively schooling behavior.

Tank Mates and Compatibility:

Peaceful and social, this tetra thrives in groups of six or more. Ideal companions include rasboras, small danios, pencilfish, Corydoras, and dwarf cichlids. Avoid aggressive or large fish that may intimidate them.

Shrimp Compatibility:

Generally safe with adult Neocaridina and Caridina shrimp. Tiny shrimp fry may be eaten, so dense plant cover or moss is recommended for safe cohabitation. Bamboo Shrimp make great tank mates.

products.title: Aquatic Arts Mango Leaves (Mangifera indica) — Dried Botanicals
products.body_html:
A natural botanical that promotes balance, health, and breeding in your aquarium.

Mango Leaves are one of nature’s most versatile aquarium botanicals. These dried leaves from the Mangifera indica tree are a beautiful addition to any freshwater tank—slowly releasing beneficial tannins and humic substances that help mimic natural blackwater habitats.

Like Indian Almond Leaves, Mango Leaves are prized for their antibacterial and antifungal properties, helping support fish and shrimp health while slightly tinting the water with a warm, amber hue that reduces stress and enhances natural behavior.

As the leaves soften, they form a layer of biofilm—a favorite grazing surface for shrimp, snails, and fry. Their broad shape also makes them an excellent natural breeding platform for many species.

Why We Love Mango Leaves:

  • Natural Water Conditioner: Slowly releases tannins that promote balanced pH and reduce harmful microorganisms.

  • Health Support: Known for mild antibacterial and antifungal benefits that improve fish and shrimp vitality.

  • Biofilm Builder: Provides a rich surface for beneficial microorganisms that shrimp and fry love to graze on.

  • Aesthetic Appeal: Adds a natural, organic look with a soothing amber tint to the water.

Perfect for Breeding Tanks:

Mango Leaves serve as a natural spawning and nursery area for a wide variety of species. Their surface encourages egg attachment and fry shelter, making them ideal for:

  • Dwarf Shrimp (Neocaridina, Caridina, Sulawesi)

  • Small Catfish (Corydoras, Otocinclus, Ancistrus fry)

  • Tetras and Rasboras that scatter eggs among leaf litter

  • Labyrinth Fish (Gouramis, Bettas) that build bubble nests beneath floating leaves

How to Use:

  1. Preparation: Rinse leaves thoroughly and soak in warm, dechlorinated water for 12–24 hours before use to help them sink.

  2. Placement: Lay a few leaves directly on the substrate or let them float until they naturally settle.

  3. Maintenance: Replace leaves once they fully decompose; most last 2–4 weeks depending on tank size and bioactivity.

Environmental Benefits:

  • Releases natural tannins that replicate tropical stream conditions.

  • Supports microbial diversity, contributing to long-term aquarium balance.

  • Enhances coloration and reduces stress for sensitive species.

Product Details:

  • Available Sizes:
    • 5-Pack — ideal for nano and mid-sized aquariums
    • 10-Pack — great for larger tanks or ongoing botanical enrichment

  • Average Leaf Size: 5–7 inches each

  • Origin: Naturally air-dried and prepared for aquarium use

  • Scientific Name: Mangifera indica

ARTS in Action:

  • A (Aquascaping): Creates a beautiful, naturalistic blackwater look.

  • R (Responsibility): Sustainably collected with no chemical treatment.

  • T (Thriving Life): Promotes biofilm, breeding, and natural immune support.

  • S (Storytelling): Inspired by tropical rivers where leaves fall gently into the water, nurturing life below the surface.

Bring natural health and harmony to your tank with Aquatic Arts Mango Leaves! Available in both 5- and 10-pack options for tanks of every size.

Create a Balanced, Thriving Aquarium

From shrimp tanks to tropical terrariums, Mango Leaves are more than a decorative accent, they’re living architecture. Each leaf supports life, stability, and beauty, helping you build an aquarium that’s not only balanced but truly alive.

products.title: Aquatic Arts Beal Pods (Aegle marmelos) — Dried Botanicals
products.body_html:
A long-lasting botanical that balances beauty, function, and ecology—nurturing fish, shrimp, and microfauna in every setup.

Bring the balance and beauty of nature into your aquarium or terrarium with the Beal Pod, the dried fruit husk of the Bael Tree (Aegle marmelos). Naturally hollow and rich in organic texture, this unique botanical provides structure, shelter, and steady tannin release to help create a Balanced Aquarium and support a Thriving Ecosystem.

From blackwater biotopes to tropical terrariums, Beal Pods add both aesthetic appeal and natural function. Shrimp, Plecos, and other biofilm grazers thrive as these pods slowly develop a layer of nutritious microbial growth, mimicking the ecology of wild tropical streams.

What We Love About Beal Pods

• Natural Shelter & Spawning Site: The hollow design provides refuge and breeding areas for shrimp, small fish, and fry—ideal for Apistogrammas, Betta species, Corydoras, and Plecos.
• Perfect for Biofilm Grazers: Plecos, Otocinclus, shrimp, and snails benefit from the biofilm and fungal growth that naturally forms on the pod’s surface.
• Tannin-Rich Botanical: Gradually releases tannins and humic substances that promote healthy water chemistry, mimicking natural blackwater environments.
• Durable & Long-Lasting: The woody shell holds its shape for months before slowly breaking down and enriching the substrate.
• Multi-Habitat Versatility: Excellent for aquariums, paludariums, vivariums, and bioactive terrariums housing frogs, isopods, or tropical geckos.

Natural Habitat Connection

Native to the forests and floodplains of South and Southeast Asia, the Bael Tree’s woody fruit husks often fall into slow-moving streams where fish, shrimp, and amphibians use them as cover and breeding sites. Over time, they release tannins that soften and darken the water—creating the calm, nutrient-rich environments where life thrives. Adding Beal Pods to your aquarium or terrarium recreates that same natural balance, bridging aesthetics and ecology.

Usage in Different Applications

In Aquariums:
Use Beal Pods to create natural hiding places or spawning caves for shrimp, small catfish, and dwarf cichlids. Plecos and Otocinclus will graze on the biofilm that develops, while shrimp feed on the microfauna the pod supports.

For Breeding Tanks:
Position pods near plants or driftwood to provide secure spaces for egg-laying fish and fry. The interior cavity encourages natural spawning behaviors while offering fry protection and a food source from growing biofilm.

In Terrariums & Vivariums:
Excellent for dart frogs, small tropical geckos, or micro lizards. Beal Pods hold moisture, provide shelter, and blend beautifully with tropical foliage or moss-covered hardscape.

For Isopods and Bioactive Setups:
A favorite for isopods and springtails—the pod’s structure supports fungal growth and helps maintain humidity, making it an ideal addition to bioactive cleanup crews.

As Botanical Décor:
Add texture and realism to paludariums or dry displays. Each pod has a unique shape and grain, making it a visually stunning accent in any natural habitat.

Usage & Preparation

  1. Rinse Thoroughly: Remove dust or debris.

  2. Boil or Soak: Simmer for 15–30 minutes to help the pod sink and release excess tannins.

  3. Anchor if Needed: Fill the interior with gravel or substrate to prevent floating.

  4. Terrarium Use: Simply rinse and place—no boiling required.

  5. Monitor and Replace: Pods will slowly decompose over months, enriching your ecosystem and continuing the natural cycle.

Recommended For

  • Shrimp, Snails, and Plecos

  • Otocinclus & Other Biofilm Grazers

  • Dwarf Cichlids & Breeding Fish

  • Tropical Terrariums (Dart Frogs, Micro Geckos)

  • Isopod & Bioactive Enclosures

  • Blackwater & Natural Aquascapes

Create a Balanced, Thriving Aquarium

From shrimp tanks to tropical terrariums, Beal Pods are more than decoration—they’re living architecture. Each pod supports life, stability, and beauty, helping you build an aquarium that’s not only balanced but truly alive.

products.title: Java Moss Mat (Taxiphyllum barbieri / Vesicularia dubyana)
products.body_html:
This is the famous Java Moss (Taxiphyllum barbieri/Vesicularia dubyana), which probably needs no introduction. It is easily the most popular and easy-to-grow live moss in the aquarium hobby. Java Moss is a vibrant green moss that is particularly excellent for aquariums that house fish, snails, and dwarf shrimp, as it provides biofilm, hiding places, and security for all aquarium inhabitants. If you breed shrimp or fish, the fry also greatly appreciate the plant cover. Java Moss is very low-maintenance; it can be kept in any freshwater aquarium and does not need special or high-intensity lighting.

 

This Java Moss ships as a pre-made cocofiber mat that measures roughly 3 by 4 inches.  Java Moss grows easily along driftwood, rocks, and other surfaces. It can also be grown along the rear glass of your aquarium for an impressive live plant backdrop effect! This moss is also quite practical for the aquarium owner because it removes pollutants from the water, greatly reducing the need for water changes and preventing "smelly tank syndrome."  You may receive some strands of moss that are yellow or brown in color - this is normal and the moss should recover within 2 weeks.

No aquarium plant distributor/nursery/wholesaler/retailer can or will guarantee their plants to be 100% snail free. We cannot guarantee our plants will be snail free.

 

What We Like About This Plant:

  • Provides refuge for fish and shrimp fry
  • Can be attached to almost any aquarium décor or left floating in loose form
  • Low light requirements
  • Little to no supplements needed

 

Care Guidelines:

  • Temperature: 59° - 82° F (15° - 28° C)
  • pH: 5.5 - 7.5
  • Lighting: Low to moderate
  • Origin: Farmed in Asia, indigenous to Southeast Asia
  • Aquarium placement: Any
  • Care: Easy

 

All Aquatic Arts brand plants and animals come with a 100% live arrival guarantee, plus free email support!

products.title: Platinum Redtail Dumbo Guppy, (Poecilia reticulata var. "Platinum Redtail Dumbo") - Tank-Bred!
products.body_html:
For the safety of our animals, we now ship live fish exclusively via UPS Next Day Air. If your order contains this item, you will only see UPS Next Day Air as a shipping option during checkout.

The Platinum Redtail Dumbo Guppy is a dazzling variety of guppy that boasts shimmering silver body tones accented by a bold, ruby-red tail and oversized “dumbo” pectoral fins. This combination of vibrant color and dramatic finnage makes it a standout centerpiece in any community or planted aquarium. Bred in-house for resilience and quality, these guppies are excellent choices for both beginner and seasoned aquarists.

What We Love About This Fish:

• Brilliant Contrast – The shimmering platinum body paired with a vivid red tail is eye-catching and unique.
• Striking Fins – Large dumbo pectorals give them a flowing, graceful presence in the tank.
• Peaceful & Hardy – Easy to care for and suitable for a wide range of water conditions.
• Prolific Breeders – Great for hobbyists interested in expanding their colony.

Natural Habitat:

The original guppy (Poecilia reticulata) was first documented in South America, inhabiting freshwater streams, pools, and rivers rich in vegetation. The Platinum Redtail Dumbo Guppy is a selectively bred variety developed in captivity to enhance its brilliant colors and signature dumbo finnage.

Aquascaping:

Platinum Redtail Dumbo Guppies thrive in a thoughtfully designed aquascape that highlights their shimmering colors while offering comfort and security. A lush planted environment not only enhances their beauty but also encourages natural behaviors like grazing and breeding.

• Plants: Dense greenery such as Java Moss, Anubias, Hornwort, and Amazon Sword provides cover for fry, foraging areas, and resting spots. Aquatic Arts offers a wide range of potted plants and moss assortments, making it easy to create a vibrant underwater garden that complements your guppies.

• Hardscape: Incorporating driftwood, smooth river stones, and slate gives structure to the tank and creates gentle contrasts against the guppies’ platinum and red tones. Hardscape also breaks up sightlines, which helps maintain harmony in community tanks.

• Botanicals: Adding natural botanicals enriches the environment by softening the water and releasing beneficial tannins, while also encouraging biofilm growth that guppies and shrimp love to graze on. Our Aquatic Arts Botanical Combo Pack provides a curated selection of botanicals — such as Indian Almond Leaves and alder cones — that promote both beauty and health in your aquarium.

✨ Shop the Aquatic Arts Botanical Combo Pack to bring the perfect balance of function and aesthetics to your guppy setup.

Diet and Feeding:

This omnivorous guppy thrives on a varied diet. Offer high-quality flake food, micro pellets, and occasional treats of frozen or live foods such as brine shrimp, daphnia, or bloodworms. A balanced diet ensures vibrant coloration and robust health. We use and recommend Ultra Fresh Guppy Mignon and Ultra Fresh Apex Guppy diets.

Tank Mates and Compatibility:

Platinum Redtail Dumbo Guppies are peaceful and thrive in community setups. Ideal tank mates include rasboras, tetras, corydoras, and other livebearers. Avoid aggressive or fin-nipping species such as large barbs or cichlids.

Shrimp Safe:

Generally safe with adult shrimp, though tiny shrimp fry may be consumed. Provide ample plant cover if housing with shrimp colonies.

Breeding:

Like all guppies, this variety is a livebearer and breeds readily in captivity. Females give birth to free-swimming fry. A breeding box or dense plant growth will help protect fry from predation.

Recommended Tank Parameters:

• Temperature: 72° - 82° F (22° - 28° C)
• pH: 7.0 - 7.8
• KH: 8 - 12 dKH
• Minimum Tank Size: 10 gallons

Care Guidelines:

• Diet: Omnivorous; feed a varied mix of dry, frozen, and live foods.
• Social Behavior: Peaceful; best kept in groups for activity and display.
• Origin: Tank-bred.
• Average Adult Size: 1.5 – 2 inches (3.8 – 5 cm)
• Average Purchase Size: 1 – 1.5 inches (2.5 – 3.8 cm)

Why Choose This Fish:

The Platinum Redtail Dumbo Guppy combines shimmering beauty, hardy adaptability, and lively behavior, making it one of the most stunning guppy varieties for the home aquarium.

Live Arrival Guarantee:

products.title: Orange Swordtail (Xiphophorus hellerii) – Locally Bred
products.body_html:
For the safety of our animals, we now ship live fish exclusively via UPS Next Day Air. If your order contains this item, you will only see UPS Next Day Air as a shipping option during checkout.

The Orange Swordtail (Xiphophorus hellerii) is one of the most striking livebearers in the aquarium hobby, prized for its vivid coloration and signature tail extension. With its bold orange hue and lively personality, this fish adds nonstop movement and a splash of fiery color to any community aquarium. Our specimens are locally bred, making them hardy, well-acclimated, and a sustainable alternative to imported fish.

What We Love About This Fish:

• Brilliant Orange Coloration – A fiery tone that stands out in planted aquariums.
• Signature Swordtail – Males display the elegant, elongated “sword” extension of the caudal fin.
• Peaceful and Active – A schooling livebearer that adds energy and personality.
• Locally Bred – Adapted to aquarium conditions, hardy, and sustainably raised.

Natural Habitat:

Wild Swordtails are native to rivers and streams throughout Mexico and Central America, typically inhabiting flowing waters with lush vegetation and rocky or sandy substrates. The Orange variety is a selectively bred color morph, developed for its unique coloration and now available as a locally bred, aquarium-raised fish.

Aquascaping:

Swordtails thrive in aquariums that combine open swimming space with areas of cover.

• Plants: Live plants like Amazon Sword, Hornwort, and Water Sprite provide natural shelter and grazing surfaces. Floating plants such as Duckweed or Frogbit help diffuse light and create a comfortable environment.
• Hardscape: Smooth rocks and driftwood pieces can be arranged to create visual interest while leaving plenty of open water for schooling behavior.
• Botanicals: Additions like Indian Almond Leaves or Casuarina pods can help condition the water, encourage biofilm growth, and enhance a natural look.

Diet and Feeding:

Swordtails are omnivores and accept a wide range of foods. A staple of high-quality flakes or micro-pellets should be supplemented with live or frozen treats like brine shrimp and daphnia, as well as vegetable matter such as spirulina flakes or blanched zucchini to keep them healthy and colorful.

Tank Mates and Compatibility:

Orange Swordtails are excellent community fish. They do well with other livebearers (Platies, Mollies, Guppies) as well as peaceful species like tetras, rasboras, and Corydoras catfish. Avoid aggressive or fin-nipping tank mates.

Shrimp Compatibility:

Safe with adult shrimp, but may prey on very small shrimp fry. Provide ample plants and hiding spots if keeping shrimp together.

Breeding:

Like all livebearers, Orange Swordtails are prolific breeders. Females give birth to free-swimming fry, which benefit from dense plant cover or breeder boxes for survival. A ratio of 1 male to 2–3 females is recommended to reduce stress.

Recommended Tank Parameters:

• Temperature: 72° – 82° F (22° – 28° C)
• pH: 7.0 – 8.0
• KH: 8 – 15 dKH
• Minimum Tank Size: 20 gallons

Care Guidelines:

• Diet: Omnivorous; thrives on flakes, pellets, live, and frozen foods.
• Social Behavior: Peaceful, schooling fish best kept in groups.
• Origin: Locally bred in the USA.
• Average Adult Size: 4 – 5 inches (10 – 12.5 cm), with males displaying a longer sword.
• Average Purchase Size: 1 – 2 inches (2.5 – 5 cm)

Bring home the vibrant Orange Swordtail – Locally Bred for a community fish that combines beauty, activity, and hardiness, all while supporting sustainable, domestic breeding.

products.title: Gold Convict Cichlid (Amatitlania nanolutea "Gold") – Locally Bred
products.body_html:
For the safety of our animals, we now ship live fish exclusively via UPS Next Day Air. If your order contains this item, you will only see UPS Next Day Air as a shipping option during checkout.

The Gold Convict Cichlid (Amatitlania nanolutea) is a rare and stunning variation of the convict group, notable for its compact size and golden yellow coloration. Unlike the more common Convict Cichlid, this species remains smaller and is less aggressive, making it a unique addition for aquarists who enjoy Central American cichlids but want something more manageable. Our specimens are locally bred, ensuring excellent health, adaptability, and sustainability.

What We Love About This Fish:

• Bright Gold Coloration – Striking yellow body with subtle vertical barring.
• Smaller Size – Reaches only about 3–4 inches, perfect for mid-sized aquariums.
• Pair Bonding – Forms strong pairs and shows excellent parental care.
• Locally Bred – Healthier, hardier, and raised in aquarium conditions.

Natural Habitat:

The wild form of this species is native to rivers and streams of Costa Rica and Panama, where it inhabits slow-moving waters with sandy or rocky bottoms. This locally bred variety retains the same vibrant personality and behaviors but is more adaptable to community aquariums.

Aquascaping:

Creating the right environment for Gold Convict Cichlids is about combining function with beauty. These fish love to explore, claim territories, and dig when preparing to spawn, so the aquascape should reflect their natural behavior.

• Plants: Choose sturdy, resilient plants like Anubias, Java Fern, or Vallisneria that can be attached to rocks and driftwood instead of planted directly in the substrate. Since Gold Convicts may uproot softer plants while digging, epiphyte plants or potted arrangements are the most reliable choices. Floating plants like Frogbit can provide shade and help diffuse light, giving the fish a more natural environment.

• Hardscape: Rocky caves, stacked stones, and driftwood are essential. These structures not only provide shelter and breeding sites but also help break up line of sight, reducing aggression between pairs or tank mates. Smooth river rocks and slate are especially effective for creating spawning surfaces and territorial boundaries.

• Botanicals: Natural additions like Indian Almond Leaves, Catappa Bark, or alder cones enrich the water with beneficial tannins and humic substances while offering subtle hiding spots for fry. These botanicals create a more natural look and release compounds that can support overall fish health and well-being.

Diet and Feeding:

The Gold Convict Cichlid is an omnivore. It thrives on a balanced diet of high-quality cichlid pellets, flakes, frozen foods (bloodworms, brine shrimp), and live foods. Vegetables such as blanched spinach or spirulina-based foods will enhance color and digestion.

Tank Mates and Compatibility:

Best housed as a bonded pair or with other similarly sized Central American cichlids in larger tanks. While less aggressive than standard Convicts, they are still territorial, especially when breeding. Avoid housing with delicate or very small species.

Shrimp Compatibility:

Not recommended for shrimp tanks. This species will prey on shrimp, including adults.

Breeding:

Breeds readily in aquariums. Pairs form strong bonds and exhibit classic cichlid parental care, guarding eggs and fry aggressively. Eggs are usually laid on flat rock surfaces and hatch in 3–4 days. Fry are free-swimming within a week and can be fed on baby brine shrimp or powdered food.

Recommended Tank Parameters:

• Temperature: 75° – 82° F (24° – 28° C)
• pH: 7.0 – 8.0
• KH: 8 – 15 dKH
• Minimum Tank Size: 20 gallons for a pair

Care Guidelines:

• Diet: Omnivorous; thrives on a varied diet of pellets, frozen, and live foods.
• Social Behavior: Territorial when breeding; best kept as a pair or with similarly sized cichlids.
• Origin: Locally bred in the USA.
• Average Adult Size: 3 – 4 inches (7.5 – 10 cm)
• Average Purchase Size: 1 – 1.5 inches (2.5 – 3.8 cm)

Add a brilliant splash of golden color and fascinating behavior to your aquarium with the Gold Convict Cichlid – Locally Bred, a sustainable and hardy choice for cichlid enthusiasts.
