products.title: Red Robin Gourami (Trichogaster labiosa) - Tank Bred
products.body_html:
Red Robin Gourami (Trichogaster labiosa) - A Peaceful, Vibrant Centerpiece Fish

For the safety of our animals, we now ship live fish exclusively via UPS Next Day Air. If your order contains this item, you will only see UPS Next Day Air as a shipping option during checkout.

The Red Robin Gourami is a vibrant, selectively bred color variant of the Honey Gourami (Trichogaster labiosa). Known for its rich red to orange coloration and exceptionally peaceful nature, this small labyrinth fish is an ideal choice for planted nano and community aquariums.

Captively bred for enhanced color and consistency, the Red Robin Gourami maintains the gentle temperament and adaptability that make Honey Gouramis so beloved. Its slow, graceful swimming style and calm presence add warmth and balance to any thoughtfully aquascaped tank.

What We Love About This Fish:

• Intense Red Coloration: A bold red-orange body that stands out beautifully against green plants.
• Exceptionally Peaceful: One of the gentlest gourami species, well-suited for community and nano tanks.
• Labyrinth Breather: Able to breathe atmospheric air, allowing flexibility in aquarium setups.
• Small Adult Size: Ideal for aquariums where space and harmony are priorities.

Natural Habitat:

The Honey Gourami (Trichogaster labiosa) was first documented in slow-moving, shallow waters of India and Bangladesh, including ponds, marshes, and densely vegetated streams. These environments are rich in plant life, leaf litter, and botanicals, with minimal water movement and warm, stable conditions.

The Red Robin Gourami is a selectively bred color variant developed in captivity, but it thrives in aquariums that recreate this calm, plant-filled habitat.

Aquascaping:

The Red Robin Gourami thrives in aquascapes that feel calm, layered, and richly planted. This species naturally occupies the mid to upper portions of the aquarium, moving gently through vegetation and often resting just beneath the surface.

A planted layout with dense midground growth and soft surface cover encourages confident behavior and enhances coloration. Gentle lighting filtered through floating plants creates dappled shadows that bring out the fish’s warm red tones while reducing stress.

Plants:

Thicker plant groupings help the Red Robin Gourami feel secure and encourage natural, unhurried movement through the tank. Broad-leaf plants provide resting points just below the surface, which this species frequently uses. Ideal choices include Java Fern, Anubias, Cryptocoryne, Water Sprite, and floating plants such as Frogbit or Salvinia.

Hardscape:

Wood with gentle curves and branching structure creates visual depth without overwhelming the aquascape. Position hardscape to form shaded corridors rather than open arches, reinforcing the calm, sheltered environment this fish prefers. Smooth stones and subtle elevation changes help anchor the layout naturally.

Botanicals:

As botanicals soften and break down, they subtly influence water tone and chemistry in ways that benefit both coloration and behavior. Their presence adds seasonal texture and keeps the aquarium visually dynamic over time. Aquatic Arts–branded Indian Almond Leaves, Catappa Bark, and Alder Cones introduce gentle tannins and help recreate a natural, leaf-littered environment. We recommend our Aquatic Arts Botanical Combo Pack for a variety of leaf litter that gourami love.

Water movement should remain gentle and diffused. Sponge filters or spray bars aimed along the back wall allow plants and hardscape to soften flow, creating quiet zones the gourami will return to repeatedly.

Surface structure is especially important. Floating plants, trailing roots, and overhanging wood provide a sense of enclosure while leaving open access to the surface for air breathing and bubble-nesting behavior.

Diet and Feeding:

Red Robin Gouramis are omnivorous and easy to feed. Offer high-quality flakes or micro pellets as a staple diet, supplemented with frozen or live foods such as brine shrimp, daphnia, and bloodworms. A varied diet supports strong coloration and long-term health.

Tank Mates and Compatibility:

Compatible tank mates include small tetras, rasboras, Corydoras, peaceful loaches, and other calm community fish. Avoid aggressive species or fin nippers. Best kept singly or as a male-female pair in smaller aquariums. This fish can be more aggressive than its relatives. 

Shrimp Compatibility:

Generally compatible with adult shrimp in a well-planted aquarium. While not an active predator, very small shrimp or shrimplets may be eaten opportunistically. Dense plants, mosses, and hardscape greatly improve coexistence.

Breeding:

Bubble nest builder. Breeding is possible in a dedicated setup with warm water, calm conditions, floating plants, and conditioned adults. Males will build and guard the bubble nest after spawning.

Recommended Tank Parameters:

• Temperature: 74°–82°F (23°–28°C)
• pH: 6.0–7.5
• KH: 2–8 dKH
• Minimum Tank Size: 20 gallons

Care Guidelines:

• Diet: Omnivorous
• Social Behavior: Slighly aggressive; best kept singly or as a pair
• Origin: Captive bred, selectively bred color variant
• Average Adult Size: 3-4 inches
• Average Purchase Size: 0.75–2 inches

Live Arrival Guarantee:

products.title: L056 Chubby Pleco L056 (Pseudorinelepis genibarbis)
products.body_html:
For the safety of our animals, we now ship live fish exclusively via UPS Next Day Air. If your order contains this item, you will only see UPS Next Day Air as a shipping option during checkout.

The Chubby Pleco (Pseudorinelepis genibarbis), also known by its L-number designation L056, is a distinctive armored catfish from Brazil. True to its name, this pleco has a robust, “chubby” body with rows of bony plates and a unique sucker mouth adapted for life along the bottom of fast-moving rivers. While less flashy than other plecos, its unusual form, armored appearance, and hardworking scavenger behavior make it an appealing addition for aquarists looking for something different.

What We Love About This Fish:

• Distinctive “chubby” body and heavily armored appearance
• Hardy and adaptable to a range of freshwater aquariums
• Peaceful disposition; works well in community setups
• Active bottom-dweller that helps with cleanup and adds personality

Natural Habitat:

The Chubby Pleco is native to the rivers of Brazil, particularly slow- to moderately flowing tributaries with sandy or silty bottoms. These waters often contain submerged roots, wood, and scattered rockwork that provide shelter and grazing surfaces.

Aquascaping:

• Plants: Hardy rooted plants such as Amazon Swords and Vallisneria are suitable, though décor should emphasize open bottom space.
• Hardscape: Provide driftwood, caves, and smooth stones for shelter.
• Botanicals: Indian Almond Leaves or alder cones can add tannins and encourage natural foraging behavior.

Diet and Feeding:

Chubby Plecos are omnivorous scavengers. In the aquarium, they thrive on sinking algae wafers, vegetable matter (blanched zucchini, spinach, cucumber), and occasional protein-based foods such as shrimp pellets or bloodworms. They also sift through substrate for detritus.

Tank Mates and Compatibility:

Generally peaceful, this pleco coexists well with most community fish including tetras, rasboras, corydoras, and other non-aggressive species. Avoid housing with overly territorial plecos or large predatory fish.

Shrimp Compatibility:

Safe with adult shrimp, but may opportunistically consume small shrimp fry if food is scarce.

Breeding:

Very rare in home aquariums. Limited information is available on captive breeding, as most specimens are wild-caught.

Recommended Tank Parameters:

• Temperature: 74° – 82° F (23° – 28° C)
• pH: 6.0 – 7.5
• KH: 2 – 12
• Minimum Tank Size: 40 gallons

Care Guidelines:

• Diet: Omnivorous; accepts algae wafers, vegetables, and protein foods.
• Social Behavior: Peaceful bottom-dweller.
• Origin: Wild-caught from Brazil.
• Average Adult Size: 6 – 8 inches (15 – 20 cm)
• Average Purchase Size: 2.5 – 3 inches (6.35 - 7.62 cm)

Add the unique Chubby Pleco to your aquarium for its hardy nature, armored look, and steady bottom-dwelling presence.

Live Arrival Guarantee:

products.title: L018 Gold Nugget Pleco (Baryancistrus xanthellus) - Brazil
products.body_html:
For the safety of our animals, we now ship live fish exclusively via UPS Next Day Air. If your order contains this item, you will only see UPS Next Day Air as a shipping option during checkout.

The L018 Gold Nugget Pleco is one of the most recognizable and admired Amazonian plecos in the hobby. Deep velvet-black body coloration is contrasted by vivid golden spotting and a bold yellow dorsal and caudal edge, creating a striking visual presence against wood and stone. Baryancistrus xanthellus is not simply decorative—it is structural, purposeful, and best appreciated in aquariums built with intention.

Collected from Brazil, this species thrives in well-oxygenated systems with strong filtration, stable parameters, and abundant driftwood.

What We Love About This Fish

• High-Contrast Coloration: Gold spotting against jet-black body tones.
• Signature Yellow Edging: Distinctive dorsal and tail fin margins.
• Iconic L-Number Species: Highly sought-after Amazonian pleco.
• Algae-Grazing Behavior: Active grazer when provided proper diet and flow.

Quick Care Guidelines

• Temperature: 78°–84°F (26°–29°C)
• pH: 5.5–7.2

• Diet: Primarily herbivorous with supplemental protein; requires algae wafers, vegetable matter, driftwood, and occasional protein-based foods.
• Social Behavior: Generally peaceful but territorial toward other plecos in confined spaces.

• Community Tank Suitable: With Caution – Best with medium to large peaceful species in spacious aquariums.
• Shrimp Safe: Not Recommended – May consume shrimp, particularly juveniles.

products.title: L015 Candy Stripe Pleco (Peckoltia vittata)
products.body_html:
For the safety of our animals, we now ship live fish exclusively via UPS Next Day Air. If your order contains this item, you will only see UPS Next Day Air as a shipping option during checkout.

The Candy Stripe Pleco (Peckoltia vittata), also known as L015, is a beautifully patterned Loricariid catfish featuring bold cream-to-gold stripes against a deep brown body. Hailing from Brazil’s Amazon Basin, this manageable species stays smaller than many plecos and is both hardy and peaceful, making it an ideal choice for planted community aquariums.

What We Love About This Fish:

• Beautiful striped pattern that stands out against driftwood and plants
• Peaceful and compatible with most community fish
• Helps control algae, though requires supplemental feeding
• Interesting nocturnal behavior with regular activity in well-furnished aquariums

Natural Habitat:

The Candy Stripe Pleco originates in the Brazilian Amazon Basin, where it inhabits soft, acidic, moderately flowing waters rich in submerged wood and rocky cover. In nature, it clings to driftwood and stones while grazing on biofilm, algae, and detritus.

Aquascaping:

• Plants: Broad-leaf plants such as Anubias, Java Fern, and Amazon Swords create shaded areas for security.
• Hardscape: Driftwood is essential, along with caves, rocks, and roots for shelter and territory.
• Botanicals: Indian Almond Leaves, Catappa Bark, and alder cones provide natural tannins and promote biofilm growth.

Diet and Feeding:

This omnivore grazes on algae and biofilm but must be supplemented with sinking wafers, algae pellets, blanched vegetables (zucchini, cucumber, spinach), and occasional protein foods such as bloodworms or brine shrimp. Driftwood also assists in digestion.

Tank Mates and Compatibility:

Candy Stripe Plecos are peaceful with most mid- and top-dwelling community fish, such as tetras, rasboras, and corydoras. They may show mild territorial behavior toward other bottom dwellers if the tank is undersized or lacks adequate hiding areas.

Shrimp Compatibility:

Safe with adult shrimp, though they may opportunistically consume small shrimp fry if food is scarce. Breeding shrimp colonies should have plenty of cover.

Breeding:

A cave-spawning pleco; males guard eggs and fry until they are free-swimming. Breeding in captivity is possible with the right caves, pristine water, and patience.

Recommended Tank Parameters:

• Temperature: 72° – 82° F (22° – 28° C)
• pH: 5.5 – 7.5
• KH: 2 – 12
• Minimum Tank Size: 30 gallons

Care Guidelines:

• Diet: Omnivorous; requires algae, veggies, and occasional protein.
• Social Behavior: Peaceful; semi-territorial with other plecos.
• Origin: Wild-caught from Brazil.
• Average Adult Size: 4.5 – 5.5 inches (11 – 14 cm)
• Average Purchase Size: 2 – 3.5 inches (5 – 9 cm)

Add the Candy Stripe Pleco to your aquarium for a hardy, peaceful, and eye-catching bottom dweller with classic Brazilian charm.

products.title: Assorted Giant Multicolor Plakat Betta (Betta splendens) - Tank-Bred!
products.body_html:
PLEASE NOTE: The photos on this listing are representative of this variety, not photos of the exact fish that you will receive. Though they are part of a common color variety, each specimen is one-of-a-kind in coloration.

* For the safety of our animals, we now ship live fish exclusively via UPS Next Day Air.  If your order contains this item, you will only see UPS Next Day Air as a shipping option during checkout * 

The Assorted Giant Multicolor Plakat Betta is a striking, tank-bred variety of Betta splendens known for its bold size and vivid coloration. Unlike long-finned bettas, plakats feature shorter, sturdier fins that allow for agility and strength. Each fish displays unique multicolor patterns, making every specimen one-of-a-kind.

Aquatic Arts does not endorse the long-term keeping of bettas in confined conditions or aquariums without filtration or heating.

What We Love About This Fish:

• Unique “giant” plakat form with larger body size
• Brilliant multicolor patterns, every fish is different
• Strong, short fins built for health and agility
• Hardy and adaptable in well-maintained aquariums

Natural Habitat:

Wild Betta splendens originate from Southeast Asia, living in shallow waters such as rice paddies, canals, and slow-moving streams. The Giant Multicolor Plakat is a selectively bred variety developed in captivity for size, color, and strength.

Aquascaping:

• Plants: Broad-leaved plants such as Anubias and Java Fern provide natural resting places where bettas can perch close to the surface. Floating plants like Frogbit or Water Lettuce diffuse lighting and help replicate their shaded natural habitats, reducing stress while giving them a sense of security.
• Hardscape: Use smooth driftwood, river stones, or betta-specific caves to create territories and shelter. Avoid sharp or rough decorations that could damage their fins.
• Botanicals: Indian Almond Leaves, alder cones, and catappa bark enrich the water with tannins, lower stress, and support overall health while mimicking their natural Southeast Asian waters.

Diet and Feeding:

Primarily carnivorous, these bettas thrive on high-protein foods. Offer a staple of quality betta pellets, with regular feedings of live or frozen foods such as brine shrimp, bloodworms, and daphnia to maintain color and vitality.

Tank Mates and Compatibility:

Male bettas should be housed singly. Female sororities are possible with careful monitoring. In larger setups, peaceful bottom dwellers such as Corydoras or snails may coexist, but avoid aggressive or fin-nipping fish.

Shrimp Compatibility:

Not shrimp compatible. Bettas may prey on both adult shrimp and shrimp fry.

Breeding:

Tank-bred bettas will often display bubble-nesting behavior. Males care for the eggs until hatching, but breeding requires dedicated setups to ensure safety for both parents.

Recommended Tank Parameters:

• Temperature: 76° – 82° F (24° – 28° C)
• pH: 6.5 – 7.5
• KH: 3 – 6 dKH
• Minimum Tank Size: 15 gallons (larger recommended for long-term health)

Care Guidelines:

• Diet: Carnivorous; thrives on high-protein foods.
• Social Behavior: Males are territorial and should be kept singly.
• Origin: Tank-bred.
• Average Adult Size: 3 – 3.5 inches (7.6 – 9 cm)
• Average Purchase Size: 2 – 2.5 inches (5 – 6.3 cm)

Bring Home a Giant Multicolor Plakat Betta:
A bold, vibrant variety of Betta splendens that makes a remarkable centerpiece for aquarists prepared to provide the proper environment and care.

The Blue Coral Guppy is a dazzling variety that features deep iridescent blues across its body and fins. Both males and females are available, with males displaying the most intense coloration while females add a subtler, elegant contrast. Proudly bred in-house at Aquatic Arts, these guppies are hardy, adaptable, and consistently healthy — perfect for community aquariums.

What We Love About This Fish:

• Brilliant blue coloration that enhances any aquascape
• Hardy and beginner-friendly, yet stunning enough for advanced aquarists
• Peaceful temperament, ideal for planted community tanks
• Active swimmers that bring lively movement to the aquarium

Natural Habitat:

The original guppy (Poecilia reticulata) was first documented in South America, thriving in freshwater streams, ponds, and pools. The Blue Coral variety was selectively bred in captivity to emphasize its striking coloration and shimmering appearance.

Aquascaping:

• Plants: Java Moss, Hornwort, Amazon Sword, Guppy Grass, and floating plants such as Frogbit or Duckweed
• Hardscape: Smooth river stones, driftwood, or slate to provide structure without overshadowing their colors
• Botanicals: Indian Almond Leaves, alder cones, or catappa bark to enrich the aquarium and promote natural foraging

Diet and Feeding:

Blue Coral Guppies thrive on a varied omnivorous diet. Provide a staple of high-quality flake food or micro pellets, and supplement with live or frozen foods like brine shrimp, daphnia, White Worms, Grindal Worms, and bloodworms to boost color and vitality. 

Tank Mates and Compatibility:

This guppy is peaceful and does well with rasboras, small tetras, Corydoras catfish, and other community species. Avoid housing them with aggressive or fin-nipping fish.

Shrimp Compatibility:

Compatible with adult and larger dwarf shrimp, but may prey on small shrimp fry. Provide mosses and dense plant cover to protect young shrimp.

Breeding:

Guppies are livebearers that breed readily in aquariums. Males court females with vivid displays, and females give birth to free-swimming fry. Dense vegetation or breeding boxes will increase fry survival.

Recommended Tank Parameters:

• Temperature: 72° – 82° F (22° – 28° C)
• pH: 6.8 – 7.8
• KH: 8 – 12 dKH
• Minimum Tank Size: 10 gallons

Care Guidelines:

• Diet: Omnivorous; accepts dry, frozen, and live foods.
• Social Behavior: Peaceful and best kept in groups.
• Origin: Bred by Aquatic Arts.
• Average Adult Size: 1.5 – 2 inches (3.8 – 5 cm)
• Average Purchase Size: 1 – 1.5 inches (2.5 – 3.8 cm)

Bring Home the Blue Coral Guppy:
A vibrant, hardy, and peaceful community fish that adds unmatched motion and color to planted aquariums. An excellent choice for aquarists of all levels.

products.title: Black Chin Livebearer (Poeciliopsis prolifica) – Bred by Aquatic Arts
products.body_html:

The Black Chin Livebearer is a rare and fascinating species of livebearer fish, prized for its peaceful temperament and unique appearance. Its name comes from the distinctive dark patch under the jaw, which sets it apart from other livebearers. Hardy and active, these fish are perfect for planted aquariums and community tanks. We are proud to offer tank-bred specimens raised in-house at Aquatic Arts for stronger health and sustainability.

What We Love About This Fish:

• Distinctive dark chin marking for easy identification
• Peaceful, active schooling fish with great community compatibility
• Hardy and adaptable, excellent for beginner to advanced aquarists
• Livebearing species with fascinating breeding behavior

Natural Habitat:

The original Black Chin Livebearer (Poeciliopsis prolifica) was first documented in Central America, inhabiting clear streams and rivers with moderate flow and dense vegetation. This variety is tank-bred in captivity, maintaining the species’ unique traits while offering a sustainable alternative to wild collection.

Aquascaping:

• Plants: Vallisneria, Hornwort, Amazon Sword, Java Moss
• Hardscape: River rocks, driftwood, and sand or fine gravel substrate
• Botanicals: Indian Almond Leaves, alder cones, and Catappa bark for tannins and natural cover. Our Botanical Combo Pack has a great selection of curated Botanicals that these fish love!

Diet and Feeding:

Black Chin Livebearers are omnivores. They thrive on a high-quality staple diet of flake food or micro pellets, supplemented with live or frozen foods such as brine shrimp, daphnia, and bloodworms. Offering vegetable matter like spirulina flakes or blanched spinach helps maintain their health and coloration.

Tank Mates and Compatibility:

These livebearers are excellent community fish. They do well with peaceful species such as tetras, rasboras, corydoras, and dwarf shrimp (though very small shrimp fry may be consumed). Avoid housing them with aggressive or fin-nipping fish.

Shrimp Friendly (With Caution):

Black Chin Livebearers are generally peaceful and can coexist with adult dwarf shrimp in a well-planted tank. However, very small shrimp or shrimp fry may be eaten if they can fit in the fish’s mouth. Providing plenty of moss, plants, and hiding places will help shrimp colonies thrive alongside them.

Breeding:

As livebearers, Black Chin Livebearers give birth to free-swimming fry rather than laying eggs. They breed readily in aquariums, and females can store sperm to produce multiple broods from a single mating. A heavily planted tank provides safe hiding places for fry.

Recommended Tank Parameters:

• Temperature: 72° - 82° F (22° - 28° C)
• pH: 7.0 - 8.0
• KH: 8 - 15 dKH
• Minimum Tank Size: 15 gallons

Care Guidelines:

• Diet: Omnivorous. Flake, pellet, frozen, and live foods.
• Social Behavior: Peaceful schooling fish, best kept in groups of 6 or more.
• Origin: Tank-bred by Aquatic Arts.
• Average Adult Size: 1.5 - 2 inches (3.8 - 5 cm)
• Average Purchase Size: 0.75 - 1.25 inches (2 - 3 cm)

Bring home the Black Chin Livebearer for a rare, hardy, and lively addition to your community aquarium. Their unique markings and peaceful nature make them a standout species that thrives in planted tanks.

products.title: "Tiger Striped" Amazon Frogbit, Locally Grown!
products.body_html:
This stunning floating plant is a rare variant of the Amazon Frogbit, featuring tiger striped markings around the edges!

  PLEASE NOTE: We cannot ship this plant to California or Washington

The Tiger Striped Amazon Frogbit (Limnobium laevigatum) is a Central and South American floating plant that is commonly used in ponds, but it is also very useful in the aquarium. It can grow to be much larger than many other floating aquarium plants, so it is an excellent option for providing shade and cover for more reclusive species. Its leaves are very lily-like and will float at the water's surface, which can also be very useful for small semi-aquatic animals in terrariums and paludariums. Unlike the traditional Amazon Frogbit that has solid green leaves, this particular variant has tiger stripe lines throughout most if not all of its leaves and grows to the perfect size of 3-4 inches (7.5-10 cm) per pod.

Care is extremely easy for the Tiger Striped Frogbit. Since it is a floating plant, it does not require planting in the substrate. Its leaves can grow to a massive size, so it often needs to be thinned out to allow light and oxygen to reach the rest of the aquarium. It can withstand wide temperature range and has very few specialized requirements other than that the top of the plant should not be submerged or else it will rot. It does not require CO2 supplementation. We recommend high lighting for this particular variant to maintain the stunning markings on the plant. 

No aquarium plant distributor/nursery/wholesaler/retailer can or will guarantee their plants to be 100% snail free. We can not guarantee our aquarium plants will be snail free.

 

PLEASE NOTE: We cannot ship this plant to California

 

What We Like About This Aquatic Plant:

  • Locally Grown! 
  • Large floating plant that is ideal for providing shade and refuge
  • Very easy to propagate
  • Provides a great deal of cover for animal fry
  • Very hardy and durable

Floating Plant Care Guidelines:

  • Temperature: 64° - 86° F (18° - 30° C)
  • pH: 6.0 - 7.5
  • Lighting: Moderate to High
  • Origin: Locally Grown, but indigenous to Central and South America
  • Aquarium placement: Floating
  • Care: Easy

products.body_html:

The Apistogramma baenschi, also known as the Inca Dwarf Cichlid Fish or “Inca 50,” is one of the most colorful and sought-after Apistogramma species. With its brilliant yellow body, bold black markings, and striking fin extensions, this stunning dwarf cichlid is a true centerpiece in any well-planted aquarium. We are proud to offer locally bred specimens at Aquatic Arts, ensuring healthier fish and sustainable availability.

What We Love About This Fish:

• Vivid yellow coloration with dramatic black patterning.
• Dwarf cichlid with fascinating behavior and strong personality.
• Peaceful when kept in proper harems, perfect for planted aquariums.
• Locally bred for excellent health, adaptability, and sustainability.

Natural Habitat:

Wild-type Apistogramma baenschi are native to slow-moving, blackwater tributaries in Peru. They thrive in shaded streams with sandy substrates, leaf litter, and dense cover. The locally bred Inca Dwarf Cichlids we offer have been raised in aquariums, making them well-adapted to community tanks while retaining their natural beauty.

Aquascaping:

• Plants: Thrive in planted setups with Java Fern, Amazon Sword, and Cryptocoryne. Floating plants like Frogbit provide shade and mimic natural conditions.
• Hardscape: Sandy substrate with driftwood, roots, and leaf litter for cover and breeding sites.
• Botanicals: Indian Almond Leaves, oak leaves, and alder cones help simulate their blackwater environment.

Diet and Feeding:

The Inca Dwarf Cichlid Fish is a micro-predator. Feed a varied diet of high-quality flakes and pellets supplemented with frozen or live foods such as brine shrimp, daphnia, bloodworms, and Grindal worms. This variety enhances coloration and encourages breeding.

Tank Mates and Compatibility:

Best kept in species-only tanks or carefully chosen communities. Suitable tank mates include small, peaceful tetras, pencilfish, corydoras, and dwarf rasboras. Avoid housing with aggressive or fin-nipping fish.

Shrimp Compatibility:

Apistogramma may prey on shrimp fry and even smaller adult shrimp. They are not recommended for dedicated shrimp colonies, though larger ornamental shrimp may coexist if the aquarium has dense cover.

Breeding:

Apistogramma baenschi are cave spawners. Provide coconut shells, clay pots, or caves for females to guard their eggs. Males may display and defend territory, while females exhibit strong maternal care of eggs and fry. Locally bred stock is more likely to breed successfully in home aquariums.

Recommended Tank Parameters:

• Temperature: 76°–82° F (24°–28° C)
• pH: 6.0–7.2
• KH: 2–8 dKH
• Minimum Tank Size: 20 gallons (larger recommended for harems)

Care Guidelines:

• Diet: Carnivorous micro-predator; thrives on frozen/live foods.
• Social Behavior: Keep as a harem (1 male with 2–3 females) or pairs.
• Origin: Locally bred by Aquatic Arts.
• Average Adult Size: 2.5–3 inches (6–7.5 cm)
• Average Purchase Size: .75–1 inches

The Green Lantern Platy is a striking, tank-bred variety of the Southern Platyfish. With its vivid green iridescence contrasted against dark black fins and body markings, this hardy livebearer is a showpiece for any freshwater aquarium. We are proud to offer specimens bred in-house at Aquatic Arts, ensuring vibrant health and sustainable practices.

What We Love About This Fish:

• Brilliant green sheen and bold black fins that catch the eye.
• Peaceful and active schooling fish that thrives in community aquariums.
• Hardy and adaptable—perfect for beginners and seasoned aquarists alike.
• Livebearer species that can reproduce readily in the home aquarium.

Natural Habitat:

The original Southern Platyfish (Xiphophorus maculatus) is native to slow-moving rivers, streams, and warm ditches in Central America, particularly Mexico, Belize, and Guatemala. This Green Lantern variety was developed through selective breeding in captivity to highlight its striking coloration.

Aquascaping:

• Plants: Works beautifully in planted tanks with Java Fern, Anubias, Hornwort, and floating plants such as Frogbit or Duckweed.
• Hardscape: Complements natural décor like river rocks, slate, and driftwood.
• Botanicals: Indian Almond Leaves or alder cones can provide tannins and natural enrichment.

Diet and Feeding:

Green Lantern Platies are omnivores that will accept a wide variety of foods. Offer a high-quality flake or pellet base, supplemented with live or frozen foods such as daphnia, brine shrimp, and bloodworms. They will also graze on algae and biofilm.

Tank Mates and Compatibility:

This platy is peaceful and well-suited to community tanks. Ideal companions include guppies, mollies, swordtails, tetras, rasboras, corydoras, and peaceful dwarf cichlids. Avoid housing with large or aggressive fish.

Shrimp Compatibility:

Generally peaceful with shrimp. Adult shrimp are usually left alone, but very small shrimp fry may be consumed. Providing hiding spots with moss and plants will help protect young shrimp.

Breeding:

Like other platies, the Green Lantern is a prolific livebearer. Males and females can be easily distinguished by the male’s gonopodium (modified anal fin). Females may give birth to 20–40 fry every 4–6 weeks. Fry grow quickly when provided with hiding spots and finely powdered foods.

products.title: Grindal Worm Starter Culture (2 oz Cup) - Bred by Aquatic Arts
products.body_html:
Live Feeder Worms for Fish & Invertebrates

For the safety of our animals, we now ship live cultures exclusively via UPS 2nd Day Air. If your order contains this item, you will only see UPS 2nd Day Air as a shipping option during checkout.

Grindal Worms (Enchytraeus buchholzi) are a nutritious, easy-to-culture live food that have become a favorite among freshwater hobbyists. Smaller than white worms but larger than microworms, Grindal Worms are the perfect live treat for community fish, dwarf cichlids, bettas, gouramis, killifish, and shrimp colonies. At Aquatic Arts, we proudly offer starter cultures bred in-house to ensure strength and vitality.

What We Love About This Live Food:

• High in protein and lipids—ideal for growth, breeding, and conditioning fish.
• Perfect size for small to medium fish and shrimp.
• Easy to culture in small containers with minimal equipment.
• Sustainable, long-term live food option for any home fishroom.

Starter Culture Details:

Each starter culture comes in a 2 oz cup with a thriving base population of Grindal Worms and the medium they’ve been growing in. This is enough to establish your own continuous culture at home.

Breeder Kits Available:

For those who want a quick, ready-to-go setup, we also offer complete Breeder Kits that include:
• One established 2 oz starter culture
• Culture container with lid
• Bedding medium
• Starter food supply

Culture & Feeding Instructions:

Grindal Worms are simple to maintain and will reproduce continuously when provided with the right conditions:

  • Feeding: Sprinkle a small amount of food (such as oatmeal paste, finely powdered fish food, or specialized worm food) on the surface of the bedding. Cover lightly with a piece of damp plastic or glass to keep moisture in and provide a feeding surface.

  • Frequency: Feed once daily or every other day, adjusting the amount so the food is mostly consumed within 24 hours. Overfeeding can lead to mold growth, so keep portions modest.

  • Breeding: As long as the culture remains moist and fed regularly, the worms will multiply quickly. Within 1–2 weeks, you should have a dense, harvestable population.

  • Harvesting: Collect worms by scraping them from the surface of the food or from the underside of the cover plate. Rinse briefly before feeding to your fish.

Care & Culture Guidelines:

• Temperature: 65°–75° F (18°–24° C)
• Feeding Medium: Oatmeal, powdered fish food, or specialized worm food
• Harvest Frequency: Once established, cultures can be harvested several times per week
• Longevity: With proper care, a single starter can supply live food indefinitely

products.title: Aquatic Arts Shrimp Tank Starter Pack
products.body_html:
The Ultimate Feeding & Foraging Combo

Feed. Forage. Thrive.

Start your shrimp colony off right with the Aquatic Arts Shrimp Tank Starter Pack—a carefully curated bundle designed to provide complete nutrition, natural enrichment, and essential tank conditioning. This all-in-one value pack includes everything you need to keep your freshwater shrimp colony healthy, active, and thriving.

This combo features both of our most popular shrimp essentials:

  • The Aquatic Arts Invert Variety Pack

  • The Aquatic Arts Botanical Combo Pack

Whether you're just starting out or looking to upgrade your shrimp care routine, this set offers a powerful combination of proven products for long-term success.

Why This Combo is a Must-Have:

  • Complete Diet Support – Delivers daily nutrition, growth, color enhancement, and molting aid.

  • Natural Foraging Environment – Botanicals promote biofilm, offer cover, and mimic natural shrimp habitats.

  • Tank Conditioning Benefits – Tannins from botanicals improve water quality and reduce stress.

  • Exceptional Value – Save money by purchasing these trusted products as a discounted bundle.

What’s Inside:

Aquatic Arts Invert Variety Pack

A full spectrum of nutrient-rich, high-quality foods:

  • Spirulina Flakes – Rich in plant proteins and essential vitamins for daily feeding.

  • Snowflake (Soybean Hulls) – Long-lasting and ideal for encouraging natural grazing.

  • Bee Pollen – A nutrient-dense superfood that supports immune health and breeding.

  • Algae Wafers – Great for groups and bottom dwellers; packed with algae and plant fiber.

  • Sinking Pellets – Protein-heavy support for growth, coloration, and molting.

Feeding Instructions:
Feed once per day in small amounts. Snowflake and bee pollen can remain in the tank longer. Alternate between food types throughout the week to offer variety and ensure balanced nutrition.

Aquatic Arts Botanical Combo Pack

A hand-picked mix of natural items to enhance shrimp well-being and tank aesthetics. Includes:

  • Indian Almond Leaf (Catappa Leaf) – Releases tannins and supports water conditioning.

  • Alder Cone Shake Portion – Promotes biofilm growth, anti fungal properties, and adds visual texture.

  • Mulberry Leaf – Provides cover and grazing area with biofilm for shrimp and shrimplets.

  • Coconut Petal Stem – Encourages microfauna and adds rustic appeal.

  • Cholla Wood – Unique structure great for biofilm and hiding spots.

Botanical Use Instructions:
Soak or boil botanicals before adding to your aquarium to help them sink and release tannins gently. Start with 1–2 items at a time and rotate new pieces in every couple of weeks. These botanicals provide long-term benefits and should be replaced gradually as they break down.

Perfect For:

  • Neocaridina Shrimp (e.g., Cherry, Blue Dream, Sunkist)

  • Caridina Shrimp (e.g., Crystal Red, Tiger, Babaulti)

  • Sulawesi Shrimp

  • Freshwater Snails and Nano Fish also benefit from this setup!

Take the guesswork out of shrimp care. Give your shrimp the best start possible—with the ultimate starter pack trusted by hobbyists and breeders alike.

 Feed. Forage. Thrive.

Feeding Suggestions:

Perfect For:

products.title: Fissidens Nobilis Moss (Fissidens nobilis) 3 x 3 inch Mat
products.body_html:
Fissidens Nobilis Moss (Fissidens nobilis), is a very rare Southeast Asian aquatic moss that is occasionally available for the freshwater aquarium.  This plant is a very fluffy, lush green plant with palm-like stems that will attach to most surfaces and can eventually grow into a large mat of impressive size.  Like many similar plants, it provides refuge for shrimp and fish fry and makes for an attractive and useful grazing area for shrimp.  It can be easily attached to driftwood and rockwork.  It is very slow-growing and requires low to moderate lighting.  

We offer this moss in a solid stainless steel mat form that makes it easy to attach to and begin propagating on rocks, driftwood, or aquarium walls and substrate.  The moss is grown outdoors in Taiwan, and may contain a few pest snails or terrestrial bugs (the bugs will not survive or reproduce underwater).

Fissidens Nobilis Moss is a very easy plant to keep without much extra care, but like all plants, it can benefit from CO2 supplementation (although this is not required).  Like many mosses, it does not typically respond positively to most chemical plant supplements.  Many aquarists report success with this moss purely through keeping a well-maintained aquarium with no supplementation.

 No aquarium plant distributor/nursery/wholesaler/retailer can or will guarantee their plants to be 100% snail free. We can not guarantee our plants will be snail free.

What We Like About This Plant:

  • Excellent (and potentially more attractive) alternative to Java Moss
  • Can be attached to almost any aquarium or terrarium décor
  • Low to moderate light requirements
  • Little to no supplements needed

Care Guidelines:

  • Temperature: 65° - 77° F (18.3° - 25° C)
  • pH: 5.0 - 7.5
  • Lighting: Low to moderate
  • Origin: Farmed in and indigenous to Southeast Asia
  • Aquarium placement: Any
  • Care: Easy to moderate

All Aquatic Arts brand plants and animals come with a 100% live arrival guarantee, plus free email support!

products.title: Lemon Vampire Crab (Geosesarma sp. “Lemon”)
products.body_html:
Bright Yellow Semi-Aquatic Crab

For the safety of our animals, we now ship live crabs exclusively via UPS Next Day Air. If your order contains this item, you will only see UPS Next Day Air as a shipping option during checkout.

⚠️ Important Note: This species is semi-terrestrial, NOT fully aquatic. Its habitat must include at least 50–70% land area with access to fresh water. It is best suited for a paludarium-style enclosure. These crabs should not be mixed with other crab species due to territorial aggression.

Description

The Lemon Vampire Crab (Geosesarma sp. “Lemon”) is a stunning semi-terrestrial crab with vibrant yellow claws and striking body coloration. Native to forested regions of Southeast Asia, this small species thrives in humid paludarium-style setups where land and freshwater are both available. Communal within their own species, Lemon Vampire Crabs bring constant activity, color, and personality to naturalistic enclosures.

What We Love About This Crab:

  • Brilliant Yellow Claws: Unique lemon coloration that pops in planted tanks.

  • Semi-Aquatic Lifestyle: Perfect centerpiece for paludariums.

  • Natural Scavenger: Helps clean leaf litter and leftover food.

  • Fascinating Colony Behavior: Peaceful within groups of its own species.

  • Escape Artist Fun: Active climbers — secure lids are essential!

  • Nocturnal Explorers: Most active at night, when they scavenge and explore.

Natural Habitat

Lemon Vampire Crabs inhabit forest floors near streams, ponds, and riverbanks in Southeast Asia. They live among leaf litter, roots, and vegetation, spending most of their time on land but regularly venturing into freshwater for hydration and molting.

Tank Setup & Aquascaping

Lemon Vampire Crabs thrive in paludariums with a land-heavy balance (50–70% land). Use moist soil, sand, or coco fiber for substrate, and decorate with driftwood, rocks, and climbing branches to mimic their forest habitat. Provide shallow freshwater pools for drinking and molting.

Live plants such as Creeping Jenny, Java Moss (on wood or coconut huts), Java Fern, Adiantum Philippense with Vesicularia Dubyana (Java Moss) on Wood

 work beautifully alongside botanicals like Catappa, Jackfruit, Banana, Guava, and Mango leaves or our Aquatic Arts Botanical Combo Pack. Thick leaf litter and coconut huts provide cover, while springtails and isopods can be added to create a bioactive setup that breaks down waste and reduces mold.

Diet and Feeding

Lemon Vampire Crabs are omnivorous scavengers. In captivity, they thrive on a varied diet:

  • Dry & Prepared Foods: High-quality sinking flakes and pellets.

  • Freeze-Dried Foods (from Aquatic Arts): Daphnia and Mealworms.

  • Fresh Produce: Cucumber skins, zucchini, and fruits like grapes.

  • Protein: Insects, worms, and frozen or live foods.

  • Calcium: Provide cuttlebone or mineral sources to aid exoskeleton growth.

Tank Mates and Compatibility

  • With Own Species: Best kept in small colonies; males may spar, but aggression is limited if space and cover are provided.

  • With Other Species: Not recommended. These crabs may prey on shrimp, snails, or small fish.

  • Best Setup: Species-only paludarium colonies.

In Nature vs. In Your Tank

In Nature In Your Tank
Lives in leaf litter and forest edges near streams Thrives in paludariums with leaf litter and botanicals
Climbs roots, branches, and rocks Needs secure lid and climbing décor
Scavenges detritus, algae, and small invertebrates Eats pellets, freeze-dried daphnia/mealworms, veggies, and fruits
Active at night in communal colonies Nocturnal scavenger in species-only setups

Breeding

Breeding can occur in captivity. Females carry eggs under their abdomen until they hatch into fully formed miniature crabs, rather than going through a larval stage.

Recommended Tank Parameters:

  • Temperature: 75° - 82° F (24° - 28° C)

  • pH: 7.5 - 8.0

  • KH: 4 - 10 dKH

  • Humidity: 75%+

  • Minimum Tank Size: 5 gallons for 1 male + 2 females; larger colonies require more space

Care Guidelines:

  • Diet: Omnivorous scavenger, accepts dry foods, freeze-dried invertebrates, and produce.

  • Social Behavior: Peaceful with own species; aggressive toward other crabs.

  • Origin: Southeast Asia (wild-type ancestry, selectively collected).

  • Average Adult Size: ~2 inches (5 cm)

  • Average Purchase Size: 1 – 2 inches (2.5 – 5 cm)

Paludarium Partners (Recommended Add-Ons)

  • Aquatic Arts Botanical Combo Pack

  • Creeping Jenny, Java Moss, and Coconut Huts

  • Climbing Wood & Rockwork

  • Bioactive Cleaners: Springtails and Isopods

B-Grade Option

We also offer a B-Grade variant for these crabs. B-Grade crabs may be missing one or more legs or claws at the time of purchase. This is not a health issue — crabs will fully regenerate lost appendages within 1–2 molts. All B-Grade crabs are otherwise healthy, active, and display excellent coloration.

Live Arrival Guarantee

All Aquatic Arts brand animals come with a 100% live arrival guarantee, plus free email support from real aquarists to ensure your success.

The Aquatic Arts Difference

At Aquatic Arts, we don’t just ship animals — we help you build thriving ecosystems. Every crab is carefully handled, shipped overnight for health and safety, and backed by expert guidance so you can enjoy your hobby with confidence.

products.title: Hamburger Platy (Xiphophorus maculatus) – Locally Bred
products.body_html:
For the safety of our animals, we now ship live fish exclusively via UPS Next Day Air. If your order contains this item, you will only see UPS Next Day Air as a shipping option during checkout.

The Hamburger Platy is a striking, locally bred livebearer named for its distinctive horizontal black and red bands that resemble the layers of a sizzling burger. Hardy, peaceful, and full of energy, this platy is perfect for aquarists of all levels. Its playful swimming style and bold colors make it a standout in any freshwater community tank.

What We Love About This Fish:
• Locally Bred Quality – Healthy, hardy stock raised right here in the USA.
• Unique Patterning – Iconic black and red “burger” bands unlike any other platy.
• Hardy and Adaptable – Thrives in a wide range of water conditions.
• Peaceful Community Member – Safe with most similarly sized fish and invertebrates.

Natural Habitat:
While domesticated for the aquarium trade, platys originate from slow-moving rivers, canals, and warm freshwater habitats in Central America, often among aquatic plants and gentle current.

Aquascaping:
• Plants: Java Fern, Anubias, Hornwort, and floating plants like Frogbit for shade.
• Hardscape: Smooth stones, driftwood, and open swimming space.
• Botanicals: Indian Almond Leaves or alder cones to encourage natural behavior and biofilm growth.

Diet and Feeding:
Omnivorous and eager feeders, Hamburger Platys accept high-quality flake foods, micro pellets, and frozen or live treats like brine shrimp, daphnia, and bloodworms. A varied diet enhances their vibrant colors.

Tank Mates and Compatibility:
An ideal community fish, compatible with tetras, rasboras, danios, corydoras, snails, and shrimp. Avoid aggressive or fin-nipping species.

Shrimp Safe:
Yes, adult platys are shrimp-safe, though very small shrimp fry may be eaten. Provide hiding spaces for breeding shrimp colonies.

Breeding:
As livebearers, platys give birth to free-swimming fry after a gestation period of around 28 days. Breeding is straightforward in well-maintained aquariums, and fry can be raised on crushed flakes or specialized fry food.

Recommended Tank Parameters:
• Temperature: 70° – 78° F (21° – 26° C)
• pH: 7.0 – 8.2
• KH: 8 – 12 dKH
• Minimum Tank Size: 10 gallons

Care Guidelines:
• Diet: Omnivorous; thrives on varied dry, frozen, and live foods.
• Social Behavior: Peaceful; best in small groups.
• Origin: Locally bred in the USA.
• Average Adult Size: 2 – 2.5 inches (5 – 6.3 cm)
• Average Purchase Size: 1 – 1.5 inches (2.5 – 3.8 cm)

Why We Recommend This Fish:
The Hamburger Platy offers a perfect mix of bold coloration, hardy nature, and peaceful temperament—an ideal choice for community aquariums. Being locally bred means you’re getting fish raised in conditions similar to your own tank, for better adaptability and long-term health.

products.title: King Rabbit Snail (Cerithidea sp.)
products.body_html:
Cerithidea King Rabbit Snail (Cerithidea sp.) – Semi-Aquatic Brackish Snail

 

⚠️ Important Legal Note: All aquatic invertebrates are prohibited to possess in any form or sell in the state of Maine. Please check your local regulations before purchasing. We are unable to ship livestock from this listing to Maine. If you reside in Maine and try to checkout with this in your cart you will be unable to checkout and receive an error message until it is removed from the cart.

 

For the safety of our animals, this species will only ship with UPS Next Day Air. If your order contains this item, you will only see UPS Next Day Air as a shipping option during checkout.

Description

The Cerithidea King Rabbit Snail is a fascinating semi-aquatic invertebrate with striking, elongated spiral shells and curious behavior that sets it apart from fully aquatic snails. Unlike traditional freshwater rabbit snails (Tylomelania), these rare brackish snails naturally inhabit mangrove and tidal mudflat environments, where they spend part of their time submerged and part of their time above water. Their earthy shell tones—ranging from tan to chocolate brown with a smooth to slightly ridged texture—give them a rugged, prehistoric charm.

Highly sought-after by advanced aquarists and brackish enthusiasts, Cerithidea King Rabbit Snails are excellent for unique paludarium, vivarium, or shoreline-style aquarium setups.

They are natural climbers and will scale glass, décor, and even out of the tank entirely, so a secure, tight-fitting lid is required to keep them safely contained.

What We Love About This Snail:

  • Unique Habitat Needs: Semi-aquatic lifestyle adds a new dimension to brackish aquariums and paludariums.

  • Hardy and Long-Lived: Adaptable to a range of brackish salinities when properly acclimated.

  • Natural Behavior: Burrows into mud or sand, climbs surfaces, and forages along shorelines.

  • Rare in the Hobby: An uncommon invertebrate sure to draw attention from other aquarists.

Natural Habitat:

Cerithidea snails are native to mangrove forests, estuaries, and tidal flats in Southeast Asia and the Indo-Pacific. These environments have slow-moving, brackish water with both submerged and exposed surfaces, offering them a safe place to graze and burrow.

Aquascaping Recommendations:

  • Substrate: Fine sand or mud for burrowing.

  • Hardscape: Driftwood, mangrove roots, and smooth stones that create both submerged and exposed resting areas.

  • Plants: Mangroves, salt-tolerant grasses, or emersed plants that thrive in brackish conditions.

  • Botanicals: Indian Almond Leaves, mangrove leaves, and alder cones to enrich habitat and encourage biofilm growth.

Diet and Feeding:

These snails are detritivores and algae grazers, feeding on biofilm, decaying plant matter, and organic debris. Supplement with sinking algae wafers, blanched vegetables (such as zucchini or spinach), and brackish-safe prepared foods.

Tank Mates and Compatibility:

  • Peaceful with other non-predatory brackish tank mates, including mollies, bumblebee gobies, and some small puffers (with caution).

  • Avoid housing with aggressive crustaceans, large fish, or snail-eating species.

Breeding:

Breeding in captivity is challenging due to their tidal breeding cycle and specific larval development needs. Eggs are often laid above the waterline and hatch into planktonic larvae that require full marine conditions to mature.

Recommended Tank Parameters:

  • Temperature: 75° – 82° F (24° – 28° C)

  • pH: 7.5 – 8.5

  • Salinity: SG 1.005 – 1.015 (low- to mid-brackish)

  • Minimum Tank Size: 10 gallons with land/water interface

  • Lid: Secure, tight-fitting lid required to prevent escape

Care Guidelines:

  • Diet: Omnivorous grazer; supplement with algae wafers, blanched vegetables, and biofilm.

  • Social Behavior: Peaceful, best in small groups or as singles in community setups.

  • Origin: Wild-collected.

  • Average Adult Size: 2 – 3 inches (5 – 7.5 cm)

  • Average Purchase Size: 1.5 – 2 inches (3.8 – 5 cm)

Live Arrival Guarantee:

products.title: White Worm Starter Culture – Live Food
products.body_html:
Live Food for Fish Fry & Small Inverts

White Worms (Enchytraeus albidus) are a classic and highly nutritious live food that’s perfect for conditioning adult fish and feeding fry or smaller predatory species. Small Frogs, Newts and Salamanders also love these live worms. Our White Worm Starter Culture comes ready to grow, making it easy to establish your own colony at home. These soft-bodied worms are packed with protein and healthy fats, making them an excellent supplement to dry foods.

Cultures are started fresh at the time of shipment and include live worms, active bedding, and enough starter food to get you going.

Why We Recommend This:

  • Excellent Protein Source – Ideal for fry growth, fish conditioning, and boosting breeding readiness.

  • Easy to Cultivate – Maintain your own colony with minimal effort and consistent harvests.

  • Great for Picky Eaters – Many nano fish and inverts eagerly accept white worms.

  • Cost-Effective – Grow your own live food at home and reduce reliance on commercial feeds.

How to Use:

  1. Remove lid and place culture in a cool, dark space (ideally 50–65°F / 10–18°C).

  2. Feed small amounts of oatmeal, bread soaked in milk, or specialized worm food 2–3 times a week.

  3. Harvest worms as needed using a piece of glass or plastic on top of the substrate.

For best results, avoid keeping the culture in warm areas or direct sunlight, and don’t overfeed to prevent mold.

Ideal For:

  • Dwarf cichlids

  • Bettas and gouramis

  • Killifish and livebearers

  • Freshwater nano predators and fry

  • Frogs
  • Newts and Salamanders

Storage & Care Tips:

  • Keep the culture slightly moist but not soggy.

  • Stir the bedding gently every few days to maintain airflow.

  • Replace substrate every 1–2 months to keep the colony healthy.

Live Arrival Guarantee:

All Aquatic Arts live food cultures come with a 100% live arrival guarantee. If your culture arrives in poor condition, contact us within 2 hours of delivery with clear photos, and we’ll make it right.

Looking to expand your live food options? Be sure to check out our Microworm Starter Culture—another easy-to-grow, protein-rich food perfect for fry and nano fish!

products.title: Gold Angelfish (Pterophyllum scalare) – U.S.A. Bred
products.body_html:
Classic Beauty

For the safety of our animals, we now ship live fish exclusively via UPS Next Day Air. If your order contains this item, you will only see UPS Next Day Air as a shipping option during checkout.

The Gold Angelfish is a stunning, selectively bred variant of the ever-popular freshwater angelfish. With its soft metallic gold coloration, graceful fins, and tall body shape, this elegant cichlid makes a peaceful yet commanding presence in any planted or community aquarium. This variety is especially striking under bright aquarium lighting, where its subtle shimmer truly stands out.

These specimens are responsibly bred in the U.S.A., not imported, making them exceptionally hardy and well-adapted to aquarium conditions.

What We Love About This Fish:

• Golden Hue: Beautiful, pale gold body with reflective tones that intensify with age and good care
• Graceful Movement: Long flowing fins and slow, deliberate swimming style
• U.S.A. Bred & Adaptable: Raised domestically for excellent health and compatibility with tank life
• Social but Peaceful: Can be kept in groups or pairs, and generally peaceful with most tankmates

Natural Habitat:

Descended from wild angelfish native to the Amazon Basin. In the wild, these fish inhabit calm, slow-moving waters with dense vegetation and submerged driftwood or roots.

Aquascaping:

Gold Angelfish thrive in tall, vertically oriented tanks with natural decor. We recommend:

  • Plants: Tall background plants like Amazon Swords, Vallisneria, and floating plants like Frogbit for shade and comfort

  • Hardscape: Driftwood, vertical rocks, and branchy wood structures for swimming lanes

  • Botanicals: Add our Aquatic Arts Botanical Combo Pack or the AquaZen Botanical Zen Pack to naturally enrich the environment, promote biofilm, and help condition the water with beneficial tannins

Diet and Feeding:

These angelfish are eager eaters and benefit from a varied omnivorous diet. Offer:

  • Ultra Fresh Tropical Micro Pellets – Shrimp Delight Formula for daily nutrition and optimal coloration

  • Occasional live or frozen foods like brine shrimp or bloodworms

  • Spirulina flakes or veggie-based options to round out their diet

Tank Mates and Compatibility:

Gold Angelfish do well in peaceful community tanks and make excellent centerpiece fish. Good tank mates include:

  • Corydoras

  • Larger tetras (like Rummy Nose, Black Skirt, and Lemon Tetras)

  • Dwarf Gouramis

  • Peaceful barbs and non-aggressive dwarf cichlids

Avoid aggressive or fin-nipping fish.

Shrimp Safe:

Gold Angelfish may eat small or baby shrimp. Larger adult shrimp can sometimes coexist in heavily planted tanks with plenty of cover.

Breeding:

Mature pairs will lay eggs on vertical surfaces such as broad leaves or slate. They may guard the eggs and fry, but a separate breeding setup is recommended for successful rearing.

Recommended Tank Parameters:

• Temperature: 76° - 84° F (24.4° - 28.9° C)
• pH: 6.5 - 7.4
• KH: 3 - 8 dKH
• Minimum Tank Size: 20 gallons for a pair; 30+ gallons for groups

Care Guidelines:

• Diet: Omnivorous; provide a varied diet for color and health
• Social Behavior: Best kept in groups of 4–6 or as bonded pairs
• Origin: Bred in the U.S.A.
• Average Adult Size: 5 – 6 inches (up to 8 inches with fins)
• Average Purchase Size: 1.5 – 2.5 inches

Bring home the elegance of a U.S.A. Bred Gold Angelfish—a peaceful showstopper with a golden glow that elevates any aquarium.

products.title: Elegant Pencilfish (Nannostomus limatus)
products.body_html:
For the safety of our animals, we now ship live fish exclusively via UPS Next Day Air. If your order contains this item, you will only see UPS Next Day Air as a shipping option during checkout.

The Elegant Pencilfish (Nannostomus limatus) is a rare and graceful species, known for its slender body, metallic sheen, and peaceful demeanor. Native to the blackwater streams of Brazil, this species is only sporadically available in the aquarium trade and is prized by advanced hobbyists and biotope keepers. These wild-collected specimens display the natural beauty and subtle coloration that make this fish a true connoisseur’s choice.

What We Love About This Fish:

• Graceful and slender build with understated metallic coloring
• Rarely seen in the hobby
• Peaceful, shoaling nature ideal for planted nano and community tanks
• Low bioload and calm demeanor, perfect for blackwater setups

Natural Habitat:

Nannostomus limatus comes from acidic, tannin-rich waters in the Amazon basin of Brazil. These streams are shaded by overhanging vegetation and littered with leaf debris, creating dim, tranquil conditions with minimal water flow.

Aquascaping:

• Plants: Floating plants like Frogbit or Salvinia, plus fine-leaved species like Cabomba, or Java Moss
• Hardscape: Malaysian or Spiderwood driftwood and smooth river stones to mimic streambeds
• Botanicals: Indian Almond Leaves, Casuarina pods, and bark to simulate natural blackwater conditions. Our Aquatic Arts Botanical Combo Packs are a great way to try all different types of botanicals. Also the AquaZen Botanical Zen Pack has all three items to make black water easy.

Diet and Feeding:

These fish are micropredators, feeding on tiny invertebrates in the wild. In captivity, they accept live baby brine shrimp, microworms, daphnia, and powdered or micro-sized dry foods like Ultra Fresh New Born Fish foods. 

Tank Mates and Compatibility:

Elegant Pencilfish are peaceful and best kept in groups of 6 or more. Ideal tank mates include other small, calm species like Ember Tetras, pygmy Corydoras, and peaceful shrimp. Avoid larger or nippy fish.

Not all Shrimp Safe:
Generally safe with adult dwarf shrimp. Provide dense plant cover to protect any shrimp fry as they are known to be on the menu.

Breeding:

Breeding is rarely documented but likely follows typical Pencilfish behavior: egg-scattering among fine plants or moss. Use soft, acidic water and remove adults after spawning.

Recommended Tank Parameters:

• Temperature: 74° - 80° F (23° - 27° C)
• pH: 5.0 - 6.8
• KH: 0 - 4 dKH
• Minimum Tank Size: 10 gallons

Care Guidelines:

• Diet: Micropredator; feed a variety of live, frozen, and micro dry foods
• Social Behavior: Peaceful; keep in groups of 6 or more
• Origin: Brazil
• Average Adult Size: 1.2 inches (3 cm)
• Average Purchase Size: 0.75 – 1 inch (1.9 – 2.5 cm)

A Subtle Treasure for the True Aquascaper:
The Elegant Pencilfish is a refined and peaceful shoaler that brings movement and subtle brilliance to any softwater aquarium. Their rarity and grace make them a favorite among experienced hobbyists and blackwater aquascaping fans.

products.body_html:
For the safety of our animals, we now ship live fish exclusively via UPS Next Day Air. If your order contains this item, you will only see UPS Next Day Air as a shipping option during checkout.

The Blueberry Tetra (Hyphessobrycon wadai) is a relatively new and stunning addition to the freshwater aquarium hobby. Known for its vivid violet-blue sheen and peaceful nature, this small tetra lights up any planted aquarium. These tank-bred specimens are hardy, easy to care for, and best enjoyed in schools of six or more where their colors intensify and their graceful schooling behavior shines.

What We Love About This Fish:

• Iridescent Blue-Purple Hue: Glistens like a ripe blueberry under aquarium lighting.
• Rare and Eye-Catching: Still uncommon in the hobby but easy to keep.
• Peaceful Schooling Fish: Great for nano tanks and planted community setups.
• Tank Bred and Hardy: Adapted to aquarium life and water parameters.

Natural Habitat:
Originally discovered in the Rio Aripuanã region of Brazil, Blueberry Tetras inhabit slow-moving, tannin-rich forest streams and tributaries with dense vegetation and soft, acidic water.

Aquascaping:
• Plants: Pair with fine-leaved plants like Water Sprite, Frogbit, and Ludwigia for contrast.
• Hardscape: Use driftwood and leaf litter to recreate their natural biotope.
• Botanicals: Add Catappa leaves, alder cones, or softwood bark to release tannins and support natural behavior. for a variety of beneficial botanicals try our Aquatic Arts Botanical Combo Pack or a AquaZen Botanical Zen Pack.

Diet and Feeding:
An omnivore that readily accepts micro pellets, flake foods, and frozen or live fare like baby brine shrimp or daphnia. A varied diet rich in color-enhancing ingredients helps maintain their vibrant appearance.

Tank Mates and Compatibility:
Peaceful and social, Blueberry Tetras do best with other gentle fish such as Pencilfish, Corydoras, smaller Rasboras, and peaceful Dwarf Cichlids. Avoid aggressive or large fish that may outcompete or intimidate them.

Shrimp Safe:
Generally safe with adult dwarf shrimp, though small shrimp fry may be at risk. A well-planted tank with hiding places helps shrimp colonies coexist peacefully.

Breeding:
An egg-scattering species. Provide soft water, fine-leaved plants or spawning mops, and remove adults post-spawning to avoid egg predation. Fry require tiny food like infusoria or powdered fry food.

Recommended Tank Parameters:
• Temperature: 72° - 80° F (22° - 27° C)
• pH: 6.0 – 7.5
• KH: 2 – 10 dKH
• Minimum Tank Size: 15 gallons

Care Guidelines:
• Diet: Omnivorous. Feed small, varied foods.
• Social Behavior: Peaceful. Keep in groups of 6 or more.
• Origin: Tank-Bred
• Average Adult Size: 1.25 – 1.5 inches (3.2 – 3.8 cm)
• Average Purchase Size: 0.75 – 1 inch (1.9 – 2.5 cm)

Looking for an uncommon gem that thrives in planted aquariums? The Blueberry Tetra is a true standout that adds natural shimmer, activity, and peaceful energy to any well-kept freshwater setup.

products.title: Rosy Barb (Puntius conchonius) – Tank Bred
products.body_html:

The Rosy Barb (Puntius conchonius) is a vibrant and active freshwater fish known for its shimmering rose-gold coloration and playful schooling behavior. These tank-bred specimens are hardy, adaptable, and perfect for both beginner and experienced aquarists. Males typically display a more intense red coloration, especially during breeding, while females are a softer gold-silver tone. Their energetic swimming and group dynamics make them a favorite in community tanks.

What We Love About This Fish:

• Brilliant Coloration: Males glow with rosy-red hues, especially in clean, well-fed tanks.
• Hardy and Adaptable: Great for beginners and tolerant of a wide range of water conditions.
• Active Schooling Behavior: Fun to watch in groups of 5 or more.
• Peaceful, Yet Lively: Compatible with many other community fish species.

Natural Habitat:
Native to South Asia, Rosy Barbs are typically found in clear, slow-moving rivers, streams, and ponds. They thrive in well-oxygenated waters with vegetation and open swimming areas.

Aquascaping:
• Plants: Vallisneria, Java Fern, Hornwort, and Water Wisteria are ideal.
• Hardscape: Use smooth stones, driftwood, and open swimming space for active schooling.
• Botanicals: Indian Almond Leaves or alder cones can be added to promote natural behavior and enrich water conditions. Aquatic Arts Botanical Combo Pack along with AquaZen Botanical packs are a great way to get a variety of leave litter to explore in your aquarium.

Diet and Feeding:
Rosy Barbs are omnivores and will readily accept high-quality flake food, micro pellets, and frozen or live foods such as brine shrimp, daphnia, or bloodworms. A varied diet enhances their coloration and vitality.

Tank Mates and Compatibility:
These barbs are peaceful but energetic. Best kept with similarly sized and active fish like Danios, Rainbowfish, Rasboras, and larger Tetras. Avoid long-finned or slow-moving tank mates like Bettas, which may be stressed by their high activity.

Shrimp Safe:
Not recommended for tanks with dwarf shrimp, as they may nip or eat smaller shrimp. They can coexist with larger Amano shrimp and adult snails in a well-decorated tank.

Breeding:
Rosy Barbs are egg scatterers and will breed readily in a separate spawning tank. Provide fine-leaved plants or spawning mops. Adults should be removed after spawning to prevent them from eating the eggs.

Recommended Tank Parameters:
• Temperature: 64° - 75° F (17.7° - 23.8° C)
• pH: 6.5 – 7.5
• KH: 4 – 10 dKH
• Minimum Tank Size: 20 gallons

Care Guidelines:
• Diet: Omnivorous—feed a varied diet for best color and health.
• Social Behavior: Active and social; best in schools of 5 or more.
• Origin: Tank-Bred
• Average Adult Size: 2.5 – 3 inches (6.4 – 7.6 cm)
• Average Purchase Size: 1 – 1.5 inches (2.5 – 3.8 cm)

A lively and colorful addition to any community tank, the Rosy Barb is an excellent choice for aquarists looking for an energetic and resilient fish that thrives in groups.

products.title: Halloween Vampire Crab (Geosesarma bicolor)
products.body_html:
PLEASE READ: This species is a semi-terrestrial crab. NOT FULLY AQUATIC. Its habitat must be at least half (and preferably more) land with freshwater. It is an ideal paludarium animal. Our Vampire Crabs of different colors are not the same species and should not be kept together. 

 

* For the safety of our animals, we now ship Vampire Crabs exclusively via UPS Next Day Air. If your order contains this item, you will only see UPS Next Day Air as a shipping option during checkout *

 

⚠️ Important Legal Note: All aquatic invertebrates are prohibited to possess in any form or sell in the state of Maine. Please check your local regulations before purchasing. We are unable to ship livestock from this listing to Maine. If you reside in Maine and try to checkout with this in your cart you will be unable to checkout and receive an error message until it is removed from the cart.

The Halloween Vampire Crab is a fascinating semi-terrestrial species that is beautifully marked. It is communal with its own species and is an excellent choice for the paludarium, even nano-sized enclosures!



The Halloween Vampire Crab (Geosesarma bicolor) is a small, very colorful, semi-terrestrial species that is native to Java, Indonesia. It inhabits forest areas near streams, rivers, and ponds. It spends most of its time on land, but also often submerges in freshwater, including when it molts. It is communal with its own species, and males typically are larger than females, and also have much larger claws. 

 

In nature, the Halloween Vampire Crab lives in leaf litter and vegetation. In the paludarium, it requires ample land area. It typically will not eat healthy plants, but it will often help keep them clean and it may consume dead leaves. Like most crabs, it needs plenty of cover, especially when molting, so lots of decor is necessary. While it is communal with its own species, it is often aggressive with other types of crabs, including other vampire crab species with which it competes in the wild. It is a skilled climber, so extra care must be taken to ensure that it does not escape its enclosure.

 

Like most crabs, the Halloween Vampire Crab is an omnivorous scavenger and an opportunistic hunter on occasion. It feeds on filamentous algae and detritus in the paludarium and will also readily accept high-quality dry foods that are rich in plant matter. Fresh and dried vegetables are also excellent food sources, as are insects, worms, and other invertebrates. Due to its opportunistic nature, the Halloween Vampire Crab might eat other ornamental invertebrates and fish, so its tankmates must be chosen with great care.

   

 

*IMPORTANT* Please "Choose a Variant" above before adding this crab to your cart. We offer a "B-Grade" variant for these crabs. Our “B-Grade” crabs are simply specimens that are missing legs or claws unless otherwise stated. Loss of appendages is not permanent and happens commonly with crustaceans.  They are completely healthy with excellent coloration and will completely regenerate lost appendages within 1 to 2 molts.


 

What We Like About This Crab:
  • Generally peaceful with its own species
  • Fascinating behavior and beautiful coloration
  • Ideal for paludariums
  • Easy to feed
  • Excellent scavenger
 
RECOMMENDED TANK PARAMETERS:
  • Temperature: 75° - 82° F (24° - 28° C)
  • pH: 7.5 - 8.0
  • KH: 4 - 10 dKH
  • Humidity: 75+%
  • Minimum tank size: 5 gallons for a male and 2 females. Larger colonies require larger tanks.
 
CARE GUIDELINES:
  • Diet: Omnivorous. High-quality sinking flakes and pellets, plants/algae, and freeze-dried or frozen meaty foods will be readily accepted. Cuttlebone or a similar material should be added to the aquarium to supplement calcium, which will aid in exoskeleton production
  • Social behavior: Peaceful with its own species. May fight with or prey on other species.
  • Origin: Java, Indonesia
  • Average adult size: 2 inches (5 cm) or less
  • Average purchase size: 1 - 2 inches (2.5 - 5 cm)

products.title: Angel Licorice Gourami ( Parospromenus anjunganensis )
products.body_html:
For the safety of our animals, we now ship live fish exclusively via UPS Next Day Air. If your order contains this item, you will only see UPS Next Day Air as a shipping option during checkout.

The Angel Licorice Gourami is a stunning and rare species of dwarf labyrinth fish known for its iridescent shimmer and intricate fin patterns. This species is one of the more vividly marked Parosphromenus and remains relatively unknown in the aquarium trade. Its shimmering turquoise and deep indigo hues, combined with contrasting black stripes and delicate fins, make it a hidden gem for experienced aquarists and nano tank enthusiasts.

What We Love About This Fish:

• Unique, Rare Species: Extremely limited availability in the hobby.
• Breathtaking Coloration: Deep metallic blues, blacks, and iridescent green highlights.
• Peaceful and Fascinating: Ideal for blackwater nano biotopes and species-only tanks.
• Bubble-Nest Breeder: Engaging courtship and breeding behavior in the right environment.

Natural Habitat:

The Angel Licorice Gourami hails from peat swamp forests and slow-moving blackwater streams in West Kalimantan, Borneo. These habitats are shaded, rich in leaf litter and tannins, and extremely low in minerals, creating very soft, acidic water conditions.

Aquascaping:

• Plants: Floaters like Salvinia and Frogbit for dim lighting; Java Fern and Cryptocoryne for cover.
• Hardscape: Malaysian driftwood, catappa bark, and smooth river stones for natural decor.
• Botanicals: Ideal candidates include Indian Almond Leaves, Casuarina pods, and alder cones to help maintain acidity and promote biofilm.

Diet and Feeding:

Angel Licorice Gouramis are micro-predators. In the aquarium, they thrive on live and frozen foods such as baby brine shrimp, daphnia, microworms, and blackworms. Some individuals may accept high-quality micro pellets over time, but live food is strongly recommended to maintain coloration and health.

Tank Mates and Compatibility:

This species is best kept in a peaceful, species-only tank or alongside other ultra-peaceful, softwater-dwelling microfish. Avoid aggressive or boisterous tank mates.

Shrimp Safe:

May be safe with adult dwarf shrimp, but not recommended for shrimp colonies or breeding setups. Shrimp fry will likely be eaten.

Breeding:

Parosphromenus anjunganensis is a cave-spawning bubble-nest builder. Males court females with dramatic fin displays. Once spawning occurs, the male tends to the eggs in a small crevice or leaf shelter. Extremely soft, acidic water and leaf litter are required to encourage breeding.

Recommended Tank Parameters:

• Temperature: 75° - 80° F (24° - 27° C)
• pH: 4.0 - 6.0
• KH: 0 - 2 dKH
• Minimum Tank Size: 5 gallons (ideal in pairs or trios)

Care Guidelines:

• Diet: Carnivorous micro-predator. Prefers live and frozen food.
• Social Behavior: Peaceful, best kept in pairs or trios.
• Origin: Wild-collected or specialty tank-bred (depending on availability).
• Average Adult Size: 0.8 - 1.2 inches (2 - 3 cm)
• Average Purchase Size: 0.6 - 1 inch (1.5 - 2.5 cm)

This rare and delicate beauty is a must-have for blackwater enthusiasts and nano tank keepers looking for something truly unique. Add a pair to your specialized setup and enjoy the glow of one of nature’s hidden jewels!

products.title: Crystal Red Shrimp (Caridina cf. cantonensis) – Bred by Aquatic Arts
products.body_html:
For the safety of our animals, we now ship live shrimp exclusively via UPS Next Day Air. If your order contains this item, you will only see UPS Next Day Air as a shipping option during checkout.

Our Bred by Aquatic Arts Crystal Red Shrimp are striking ornamental shrimp raised in-house for premium color, pattern clarity, and stability. These selectively bred Caridina shrimp display vivid white and red banding that adds elegant contrast to any freshwater aquascape.

As peaceful scavengers, they thrive in planted tanks and love to graze on biofilm, algae, and botanicals. Our domestic breeding program ensures high-quality genetics and shrimp that are well-adjusted to aquarium life.

What We Love About These Shrimp:

  • Bred in-house at Aquatic Arts for quality, coloration, and hardiness

  • Beautiful contrast of red and white banded patterns

  • Excellent biofilm grazers and algae controllers

  • Peaceful and social—ideal for shrimp-only or mixed Caridina tanks

  • Perfect for natural-style and botanical aquascapes

Aquascaping:

Crystal Red Shrimp thrive in stable, planted aquariums that mimic their natural softwater habitat.

  • Plants: Mosses (Java Moss, Christmas Moss), Anubias Nana Petite, Bucephalandra, Subwassertang

  • Hardscape: Cholla Wood, Driftwood, Lava Rock, and Shrimp Hides for cover and surface area

  • Botanicals: Add the AquaZen Leaf Starter Pack or Aquatic Arts Botanical Breeder Combo Pack to introduce tannins, soften the water, and boost natural biofilm growth—ideal for foraging and molting support

These shrimp are especially sensitive to water quality, so we recommend using buffered substrate and RO water with appropriate remineralization for long-term success.

Diet and Feeding:

Crystal Red Shrimp require a plant-based, mineral-rich diet to support healthy molting, growth, and color intensity.

Recommended Foods:
🔸 Aquatic Arts Snowflake Premium Shrimp Food – Long-lasting soybean hulls that promote biofilm growth and don't foul water
🔸 Aquatic Arts Pollen Granules Premium Shrimp Food – Packed with vitamins, minerals, and immune-boosting ingredients
🔸 Aquatic Arts Sinking Pellets – A daily staple high in plant protein and perfect for group feeding

We now offer a sample pack of all of our shrimp diets. 

Supplement with blanched spinach, Indian Almond Leaves, and occasional botanicals for enrichment.

Tank Mates and Compatibility:

Crystal Red Shrimp are best kept in species-only tanks or with other peaceful invertebrates.
✔️ Compatible with: Other softwater Caridina, Nerite Snails, and peaceful nano fish (with caution)
❌ Avoid: Larger or predatory fish, or even some dwarf cichlids that may prey on shrimp

Recommended Tank Parameters:

  • Temperature: 68° – 76° F (20° – 24.5° C)

  • pH: 6.2 – 6.8

  • dGH: 4 – 6

  • dKH: 0 – 2

  • TDS: 100 – 200 ppm

  • Minimum Tank Size: 5 gallons

Buffered substrate and remineralized RO/DI water are recommended for long-term success.

Breeding:

These shrimp breed readily in stable, softwater setups. Females carry eggs beneath their tail for around 28 days. Baby shrimp hatch as miniature versions of the adults and feed on biofilm, algae, and powdered shrimp foods. A mature, plant-heavy tank supports higher survival rates.

Care Guidelines:

  • Diet: Algae, biofilm, and Aquatic Arts premium shrimp foods

  • Behavior: Peaceful, highly social, best kept in groups

  • Origin: Bred by Aquatic Arts (USA)

  • Average Adult Size: 1 – 1.2 inches (2.5 – 3 cm)

  • Average Purchase Size: 0.25 – 0.75 inch (0.63 – 1.9 cm)

Each fish features the classic tall, wing-like fins and elegant body shape that make angelfish so beloved—paired with a peaceful temperament and striking visual appeal.

Whether you’re setting up a planted Amazon-style tank or adding elegance to a community setup, these angelfish make an excellent choice. Our domestic breeding program focuses on quality, not quantity, producing fish that are not only beautiful but also well-adapted to aquarium life.

What We Love About These Fish:

  • Bred and raised at Aquatic Arts—no imports, no unknowns

  • Includes Gold, Marble, White, and Black variants

  • Elegant centerpiece fish with flowing fins and calm personalities

  • Safe for planted tanks and easy to care for

Natural Habitat:

Pterophyllum scalare originate from the soft, tannin-rich backwaters of the Amazon River basin. These slow-moving habitats are filled with submerged roots, leaf litter, and aquatic plants—conditions that can be recreated in the home aquarium with gentle flow and botanical elements.

Aquascaping:

  • Plants: Amazon Sword, Vallisneria, Java Fern, Anubias, Cryptocoryne,

  • Hardscape: Vertical driftwood branches, river stones, floating plants

Diet and Feeding:

These omnivores do best on a varied diet of high-quality dry, frozen, and live foods.

🔹 Ultra Fresh Excellent Bits – Tropical Fish Formula
A premium slow-sinking micro pellet made with whole shrimp, spirulina, and garlic. Supports vibrant coloration, smooth fin development, and overall vitality—perfect for daily feeding.

Tank Mates and Compatibility:

Best kept with peaceful community fish that won’t nip fins. Avoid small nano fish that may be seen as prey or aggressive species like Tiger Barbs. Good companions include Corydoras, larger Tetras, Rasboras, peaceful Gouramis, and Swordtails.

NOT Shrimp Safe:

⚠️ Caution – May eat very small or baby shrimp. Adult Neocaridina or Amano Shrimp may coexist in large, densely planted tanks.

Breeding:

Angelfish are monogamous, substrate-spawning fish that lay eggs on flat surfaces or broad leaves. Once a bonded pair forms, they can breed readily in a quiet, stable environment. Raising fry is possible in a separate tank with proper care.

Recommended Tank Parameters:

  • Temperature: 76° – 82° F (24° – 28° C)

  • pH: 6.5 – 7.5

  • KH: 3 – 8 dKH

  • Minimum Tank Size: 20 gallons (tall tanks preferred)

Care Guidelines:
