products.title: Marsilea angustifolia (Narrowleaf Four-Leaf Clover) – Tissue Culture Cup
products.body_html:
The Marsilea angustifolia, commonly known as Narrowleaf Four-Leaf Clover, is a unique carpeting plant that brings a delicate, clover-like appearance to the foreground of freshwater aquariums. With its fine stems and narrow four-lobed leaves, it resembles miniature shamrocks dancing in the current. Its soft, grass-like texture creates a natural meadow-like effect that’s perfect for aquascapes focused on subtle detail and flow.

Offered in a tissue culture cup, this plant is grown in sterile conditions, free of pests, algae, and snails—making it perfect for shrimp tanks and pristine planted setups. It establishes slowly but steadily and benefits from CO₂ injection and good lighting for dense, horizontal growth.

Features We Love:

  • Unique Appearance: Clover-like leaves with a fine, narrow shape

  • Foreground Charm: Ideal for natural-style or Iwagumi layouts

  • Tissue Culture Clean: Pest-free and safe for invertebrates

  • Soft Texture: Adds contrast to more rigid or bold-leaved plants

Care Guidelines:

  • Light: Medium to high

  • Temperature: 68° – 82° F (20° – 28° C)

  • pH Range: 6.0 – 7.5

  • Growth Rate: Slow to moderate

  • Ideal Placement: Foreground

Pro Tips:

  • Trim Horizontally: Encourage horizontal runners by trimming upright growth

  • CO₂ Recommended: Grows more compact and lush with CO₂ injection

  • Use Root Tabs: Supplement with AquaZen Root Tabs to support early establishment

  • Rinse and Divide: Gently rinse the tissue culture gel and divide into small clumps for even coverage

  • Pair Thoughtfully: Looks great alongside Eleocharis, small crypts, or mosses for a layered look

Bring a soft, clover-like carpet to your scape with Marsilea angustifolia (Narrowleaf Four-Leaf Clover)—a clean, tissue-cultured plant perfect for aquascapers who love quiet detail and natural flow.

products.title: Myriophyllum mattogrossense (Green Watermilfoil) – Tissue Culture Cup

Grown in sterile lab conditions, this tissue-cultured version is 100% free of pests, algae, and snails—making it an excellent choice for shrimp tanks and sensitive setups. With proper lighting and nutrients, M. mattogrossense grows quickly and responds well to trimming, allowing aquascapers to shape it into dense bushes or use it for background layering.

Features We Love:

  • Soft, Flowing Texture: Feathery foliage sways beautifully in water current

  • Color Versatility: Green to reddish tones depending on conditions

  • Tissue Culture Clean: Grown in sterile conditions—no pests, snails, or algae

  • CO₂ Optional: Grows well without CO₂, but faster and more vibrant with it

Create a natural, flowing look in your aquascape with the clean and vibrant Green Watermilfoil (Myriophyllum mattogrossense) tissue culture—ideal for adding texture and motion to planted tanks.

products.title: Staurogyne sp. ‘Porto Velho’ Plant – Potted
products.body_html:
The Staurogyne sp. ‘Porto Velho’ is a rare and visually captivating stem plant that brings rich texture and earthy tones to the foreground or midground of a planted aquarium. Originally collected near Porto Velho in Brazil, this variant has not yet been formally described in scientific literature, but it is prized by aquascapers for its slender leaves and dark, moody coloration.

Unlike its more common cousin Staurogyne repens, this variety grows narrower, elongated leaves with hues of deep green, bronze, and sometimes reddish-purple under high light and rich nutrients. Its low, bushy growth form makes it a fantastic choice for natural aquascapes with a mature, grounded feel.

Pre-potted for convenience, the Staurogyne sp. ‘Porto Velho’ is easy to plant and relocate without disturbing your substrate.

Features We Love:

  • Rare Variety: A lesser-seen Staurogyne with narrow, bronze-to-purple leaves

  • Natural Texture: Adds earthy tone and depth to the midground or foreground

  • Compact Form: Ideal for shaping or layering in aquascaped layouts

  • Root-Ready: Arrives in a pot—easy to place and establish

Care Guidelines:

  • Light: Medium to high

  • Temperature: 72° – 82° F (22° – 28° C)

  • pH Range: 6.0 – 7.5

  • Growth Rate: Moderate

  • Ideal Placement: Foreground to midground

Pro Tips:

  • Trim Often: Regular trimming encourages bushy horizontal growth

  • Enhance Coloration: Higher lighting and fertilization bring out reddish and bronze tones

  • Feed at the Roots: Use AquaZen Root Tabs to boost leaf size and plant health

  • Contrast Builder: Pairs beautifully with lighter greens or mosses in natural-style scapes

  • Transition Note: May experience some melting after planting—new submerged growth follows soon after

Bring rare texture and contrast to your layout with Staurogyne sp. ‘Porto Velho’, a perfect plant for hobbyists looking to expand beyond the ordinary.

products.title: Assorted Sword Plants (Echinodorus sp.) – Bunched or Potted, Emerged or Submerged Growth
products.body_html:
Add bold, vibrant greenery to your aquarium with our Assorted Sword Plants (Echinodorus sp.), available in two growth forms:

  • Emergent-Grown Swords: Grown above water in a greenhouse. These plants develop thick, sturdy leaves and strong roots, making them highly resilient during shipping and handling. Emergent plants often establish quickly once planted, sending out underwater leaves as they settle into the tank. They are kept in water, but the leaves are not converted yet.

  • Submerged-Grown Swords:
    Grown fully underwater in a pond or aquatic grow-out system. These plants are already converted to underwater growth, allowing hobbyists to skip the typical melt or transition period. Their existing leaves are aquarium-ready from the start.

These assortments are hand-selected by our Curator to ensure a mix of beautiful forms, colors, and leaf shapes. Sword plants are hardy, beginner-friendly rooted plants that provide shelter, grazing surfaces, and a natural centerpiece for freshwater aquariums.

Each order contains a true Echinodorus species and may include favorites such as Amazon Sword, Red Rubin Sword, Melon Sword, Kleiner Sword, Altlandsberg Sword, or other striking varieties depending on availability.

Why You Might Choose Each Option

Emergent-Grown Benefits

  • Thicker, more durable leaves during transport and planting

  • Strong root systems for fast anchoring in substrate

  • Produces fresh underwater leaves naturally as it settles in

  • Great value for hobbyists who enjoy watching plants transition and grow

Submerged-Grown Benefits

  • Already in true underwater form

  • Little to no leaf melt during adjustment

  • Ideal for aquariums that need an immediate “full” planted look

  • Perfect for shrimp and nano tanks that benefit from ready-to-graze foliage

Both are healthy, aquarium-ready plants — just two different approaches to how they were grown.

Features We Love

  • Two growth choices to match your aquarium setup and planting style

  • Curated assortment—no mystery plants or fillers

  • Hardy and beginner-friendly

  • Excellent midground and background centerpiece

  • Heavy root feeders that pull nutrients directly from substrate

Care Guidelines

  • Light: Moderate to high

  • Temperature: 72°–82° F (22°–28° C)

  • pH: 6.5–7.5

  • Growth Rate: Moderate to fast

  • Placement: Midground or background

Pro Tips

  • Use root tabs for best results with sword plants

  • Stronger lighting deepens color in red and patterned varieties

  • Remove older leaves to encourage new growth

  • Emergent plants may shed a few older leaves while new underwater foliage grows

products.title: Leopard Molly (Poecilia sphenops) – Tank Bred
products.body_html:

The Leopard Molly is a striking livebearer with a golden or white base and bold black leopard-like spots across its body and fins. Hardy and full of personality, this variety of Poecilia sphenops brings energy and visual excitement to freshwater aquariums. Whether kept as a centerpiece in a nano setup or as part of a larger peaceful community, the Leopard Molly adds flash and flair with every flick of its tail.

What We Love About This Fish:

• Bold Spotted Pattern: Distinct leopard-like black markings over a gold or white body.
• Hardy and Easy to Care For: Excellent for beginners and seasoned aquarists alike.
• Peaceful Community Fish: Compatible with a wide range of peaceful tank mates.
• Active and Social: Constantly moving, often interacting with tank mates and decor.

Natural Habitat:

Mollies are native to freshwater and brackish streams, ponds, and coastal areas of Central America. The Leopard Molly is a selectively bred variant and thrives in well-maintained, slightly alkaline freshwater tanks.

Aquascaping:

• Plants: Enjoys tanks with Java Fern, Anubias, Vallisneria, and Hornwort.
• Hardscape: Rocks, driftwood, and open swimming areas are ideal.
• Botanicals: Use Indian Almond Leaves or Catappa Bark for added tannins and a natural look.

Diet and Feeding:

Mollies are omnivores and graze frequently on algae and plant matter. They thrive on a varied, nutrient-rich diet.

We recommend:
• Ultra Royal Guppy Mignon Pellets – Shrimp Delight Formula

• Blanched spinach, algae wafers, and spirulina flakes
• Live/frozen treats like daphnia or brine shrimp for extra protein

Tank Mates and Compatibility:

Leopard Mollies are peaceful and get along well with guppies, platies, Corydoras, snails, and other similarly sized peaceful fish. Avoid housing with fin-nipping or aggressive species.

Shrimp Safe:

Generally safe with adult dwarf shrimp, but juveniles may be at risk. Provide dense plant cover for cohabitation.

Breeding:

Mollies are prolific livebearers. A ratio of 1 male to 2-3 females helps reduce stress and improves breeding success. Babies are free-swimming and should be separated or provided hiding spaces to avoid predation.

Recommended Tank Parameters:

• Temperature: 74° - 82° F (23° - 28° C)
• pH: 7.2 – 8.4
• KH: 8 – 20 dKH
• Minimum Tank Size: 10 gallons (larger recommended for groups)

Care Guidelines:

• Diet: Omnivorous; offer a varied diet with vegetable matter.
• Social Behavior: Peaceful; best kept in groups.
• Origin: Tank-bred.
• Average Adult Size: 2.5 – 4 inches (6.3 – 10 cm)
• Average Purchase Size: 1.5 – 2.5 inches (3.8 – 6.3 cm)

Bring Color and Character to Your Aquarium:
The Leopard Molly is the perfect blend of vivid color and playful behavior. Whether you're a new hobbyist or building a vibrant community tank, this hardy livebearer won’t disappoint.

products.title: Opaline Gourami (Trichopodus trichopterus) Tank Bred

The Opaline Gourami is a striking and durable variant of the classic Three-Spot Gourami, prized for its marbled blue and silvery patterning that shimmers as it moves. With long, trailing pelvic fins and intelligent, curious behavior, this labyrinth fish makes a peaceful yet commanding centerpiece for community and planted tanks. Tank-bred and very adaptable, the Opaline Gourami is a favorite among both beginner and experienced aquarists.

What We Love About This Fish:

• Bold Patterning: Unique opalescent blue-and-silver marbling.
• Graceful Movement: Long pelvic fins sway like whiskers as they explore.
• Labyrinth Breather: Can breathe air at the surface, allowing for lower-oxygen environments.
• Hardy and Community-Friendly: A great choice for most peaceful aquariums.

Natural Habitat:

Originally found in Southeast Asia, Three-Spot Gouramis inhabit slow-moving, warm waters with dense plant growth—such as rice paddies, swamps, and still streams. Their natural environment is rich in vegetation and surface cover.

Aquascaping:

• Plants: Use Amazon Swords, Java Fern, Water Sprite, or Floating Plants like Duckweed or Frogbit.
• Hardscape: Include driftwood and rockwork to provide shelter and reduce stress.
• Botanicals: Indian Almond Leaves or softwood bark can release tannins and create a more natural environment.

Diet and Feeding:

Omnivorous and undemanding, Opaline Gouramis will readily accept flakes, pellets, and frozen foods. Include a mix of protein and plant-based options for optimal health.
We recommend Ultra Royal Guppy Mignon Pellets – Shrimp Delight Formula, a small, soft pellet made with real shrimp and plant ingredients that promotes vibrant color, energy, and growth.

Tank Mates and Compatibility:

Best suited to peaceful community tanks. Good tank mates include rasboras, Corydoras, livebearers, small barbs, and non-aggressive tetras. Avoid long-finned or aggressive fish, and consider only one male per tank unless space is abundant.

Shrimp Safe:

May be safe with adult shrimp in a heavily planted aquarium. Caution advised with shrimp fry or smaller ornamental shrimp.

Breeding:

A bubble-nesting species. Males will construct nests at the water's surface and protect the eggs after spawning. Provide floating plants and calm water conditions for successful breeding.

Recommended Tank Parameters:

• Temperature: 74° - 82° F (23° - 28° C)
• pH: 6.5 – 7.5
• KH: 4 – 12
• Minimum Tank Size: 20 gallons

Care Guidelines:

• Diet: Omnivorous; enjoys a mix of flakes, pellets, and frozen/live foods.
• Social Behavior: Peaceful but males may show territorial behavior.
• Origin: Tank bred.
• Average Adult Size: 4 – 5 inches (10 – 12.7 cm)
• Average Purchase Size: 1.5 – 2.5 inches (3.8 – 6.4 cm)

A Hardy Beauty with a Shimmering Finish

The Opaline Gourami adds a touch of brilliance and personality to your tank. With its bold pattern, calm temperament, and adaptability, it’s an ideal centerpiece for aquarists looking to elevate their community setup with something both classic and captivating.

Diet and Feeding:

Lavender Gouramis are omnivorous and enjoy a varied diet. Offer high-quality flake or pellet food, along with frozen or live options such as bloodworms, brine shrimp, and daphnia.
We recommend Ultra Fresh Royal Mignon Pellets – Shrimp Delight Formula, a small, soft pellet made with real shrimp that enhances color, appetite, and health—perfectly sized for gouramis.

Tank Mates and Compatibility:

This species does well with other peaceful fish such as rasboras, small barbs, tetras, Corydoras, and livebearers. Avoid fin-nipping species and other aggressive gouramis. Males may be territorial with one another in smaller setups.

Shrimp Safe:

May coexist with adult shrimp in a heavily planted tank. Some risk to shrimp fry. Provide plenty of cover if keeping with ornamental shrimp species.

Breeding:

Bubble-nester species. Males build nests at the surface and guard the eggs. Breeding should be done in a dedicated tank, with calm surface water and floating plants to support nest building.

Recommended Tank Parameters:

• Temperature: 75° - 82° F (24° - 28° C)
• pH: 6.5 – 7.5
• KH: 4 – 12
• Minimum Tank Size: 20 gallons

Care Guidelines:

• Diet: Omnivorous; accepts dry, frozen, and live food.
• Social Behavior: Generally peaceful; males may be territorial.
• Origin: Tank bred.
• Average Adult Size: 4 – 5 inches (10 – 12.7 cm)
• Average Purchase Size: 1.5 – 2.5 inches (3.8 – 6.4 cm)

A Peaceful Presence with a Pastel Glow

The Lavender Gourami is more than just a beautiful fish—it’s a calm and intelligent companion that brings grace to any peaceful community tank. With proper care, this elegant species will thrive and display its full range of soft, iridescent colors.

products.title: Malibu Hi-Fin Sunset Platy (Xiphophorus maculatus) – Locally Bred
products.body_html:
For the safety of our animals, we now ship live fish exclusively via UPS Next Day Air. If your order contains this item, you will only see UPS Next Day Air as a shipping option during checkout.

With its dramatic sail-like dorsal fin and radiant sunset coloration, the Malibu Hi-Fin Sunset Platy is a breathtaking addition to the peaceful community aquarium. This variety features bold shades of orange and yellow with subtle gradient transitions, mimicking a golden sunset on the ocean horizon. Bred locally by experienced aquarists, these livebearers are a hardy, beginner-friendly species that thrive in a planted tank environment and bring effortless beauty and motion to your aquascape.

What We Love About This Fish:


• Unique Finage: Extra-long dorsal fin with graceful, flowing movement.
• Striking Sunset Colors: Vibrant orange and yellow hues with natural gradient patterns.
• Great for Communities: Peaceful, social, and compatible with other peaceful species.
• Locally Bred: Raised with care by expert hobbyists in the United States.

Natural Habitat:

Originally native to Central America, wild platies inhabit slow-moving rivers, warm springs, and densely vegetated streams. They prefer shallow waters with plenty of plant life for cover and grazing.

Aquascaping:

• Plants: Ideal tankmates include Water Sprite, Hornwort, Java Fern, and Floating Plants like Frogbit or Duckweed.
• Hardscape: Use smooth river stones and driftwood for a natural look.
• Botanicals: Consider Indian Almond Leaves or Alder Cones to encourage natural grazing and create tannin-rich microhabitats.

We recommend our Botanical Combo Pack, which has a great assortment of popular botanicals.

Diet and Feeding:

Platies are omnivorous and thrive on a varied diet. Offer high-quality flake or micro pellet food, blanched vegetables like zucchini or spinach, and occasional live or frozen treats such as daphnia or baby brine shrimp.
We recommend Ultra Royal Guppy Mignon Pellets – Shrimp Delight Formula, a protein-rich, color-enhancing food made with real shrimp and natural ingredients. This small pellet is ideal for platies and other livebearers, promoting vibrant color, strong growth, and long-term vitality.

Tank Mates and Compatibility:

This species is peaceful and thrives in groups. Great tankmates include guppies, mollies, swordtails, Corydoras catfish, small tetras, and dwarf shrimp. Avoid fin-nipping or overly aggressive fish.

Shrimp Safe:

Safe with adult shrimp. Fry may be at risk, so provide dense plant cover if keeping shrimp colonies.

Breeding:

Livebearer species. A 1:2 male-to-female ratio is ideal. Provide hiding spots for fry among plants or spawning mops.

Recommended Tank Parameters:

• Temperature: 70° - 80° F (21° - 27° C)
• pH: 7.0 – 8.2
• KH: 8 – 20
• Minimum Tank Size: 10 gallons

Care Guidelines:

• Diet: Omnivorous; feed a variety of high-quality flakes, frozen, and plant matter.
• Social Behavior: Peaceful; best kept in groups.
• Origin: Locally bred in the United States.
• Average Adult Size: 1.5 – 2.5 inches (3.8 – 6.4 cm)
• Average Purchase Size: 1 – 1.5 inches (2.5 – 3.8 cm)

Add a Splash of Coastal Sunset to Your Aquarium!

The Malibu Hi-Fin Sunset Platy brings both visual drama and easy care to any freshwater aquarium. Whether you're a new hobbyist or a seasoned aquarist, this unique, sail-finned beauty is a showpiece worth adding.

products.title: Albino Sailfin Pleco (Pterygoplichthys gibbiceps), Tank-Bred
products.body_html:
* For the safety of our animals, we now ship live fish exclusively via UPS Next Day Air.  If your order contains this item, you will only see UPS Next Day Air as a shipping option during checkout *

The L083/L165 Albino Sailfin Pleco is an excellent fish for the larger aquarium and is one of the large pleco species that grows to a still manageable size! 
 
The L083/L165 Albino Sailfin Pleco (Pterygoplichthys gibbiceps), also known as the Leopard or Gibby Pleco, is a member of the Loricariidae family of South American suckermouth catfishes. It can grow to a rather impressive size, but not so large that it will outgrow a reasonably large aquarium. Its overall pink and yellow coloration is very attractive and distinctive. This species is a generally peaceful fish that is extremely hardy and capable of cohabitating with most other tropical freshwater species.
 
The Albino Sailfin Pleco is an excellent and resilient aquarium fish, but its eventual adult size necessitates a large aquarium. It is primarily nocturnal, but will often become active during daytime hours once established in an aquarium with plenty of driftwood, rockwork, and other hiding places. It typically will not bother plants intentionally, but larger specimens may inadvertantly uproot plants as they move around the bottom of the aquarium. Durable plants such as Java Fern and Anubias are good choices for the aquascape of this species. Male plecos may be somewhat territorial with other males of their own or similar species, but disputes can usually be diffused by providing ample territory and hiding places for each fish. All other tankmates will be generally ignored, although it is possible that small shrimp might fall prey to the Albino Sailfin Pleco. This fish produces substantial waste, so regular water changes and powerful filtration are an absolute necessity for its aquarium. The Albino Sailfin Pleco is compatible in many aquarium setups and is often an effective algae eater, but it must be kept in an aquarium of appropriate size and must never be released into US waterways. Aquarists who keep this species must plan accordingly regarding the housing of this long-lived fish.
 
The Albino Sailfin will eat plenty of algae in the aquarium, especially as a juvenile. It should also be fed a diet heavy in vegetable matter, including fresh and blanched vegetables (cucumber, zucchini, peas, spinach, etc.), high-quality vegetable flakes, sinking algae wafers, and pellets. Occasional meaty foods also beneficial when fed sparingly. This fish eats constantly, so too much meat/animal protein in its diet can be unhealthy for the fish as well as the level of waste it produces in the aquarium.
 
 

What We Like About This Fish:

  • The Albino Sailfin Pleco is a generally peaceful and hardy addition to the large aquarium.  
  • This is one of the more manageable "large" plecos.
  • This fish will not bother most plants, but it will likely help with algae control, especially as a juvenile. It will also scavenge and consume most uneaten fish food.
  • The Albino Sailfin Pleco is an aquarium fish with a unique appearance as well as plenty of personality.
  • It is compatible with a wide variety of tankmates, both large and small. 
RECOMMENDED TANK PARAMETERS: 
  • Temperature:  74° - 84° F (23° - 28° C)
  • pH:  6.5 - 7.8 (flexible as long as sudden changes are avoided)
  • KH:  6 - 10 dKH
  • Minimum tank size:  100+ gallons for an adult
 
CARE GUIDELINES:
  • Diet:  Omnivorous, but meatier foods should only be fed sparingly.
  • Social behavior:  Generally solitary, but will socialize somewhat, especially during breeding. Mature males may fight.
  • Origin:  Captive-bred, but indigenous to Rio Negro, Brazil.
  • Average adult size:  15 - 18 inches (38 - 46 cm)
  • Average purchase size:  Juvenile:  2.4 - 2.8 inches (6 - 7 cm)

products.title: Thai Onion Plant (Crinum thaianum), Plant with Bulb
products.body_html:
This rare plant will grow to an impressive size and flower beautifully under optimal conditions!

The Thai Onion Plant (Crinum thaianum) is a unique bulb plant that can be grown emersed or submersed. It can grow quite tall and will produce a large white flower under good conditions. This plant grows in a rosette formation and is generally resilient as long as it is not moved often.

Care for the Thai Onion Plant is not difficult as long as a few key needs are met. Its bulb base should not be completely buried in the substrate or it can rot. It requires moderate to high light. It does not require CO2 supplementation, though it will certainly benefit from such supplementation and is more likely to flower under such conditions. The Thai Onion Plant develops a strong root structure, so it will benefit from nutrient-rich substrate. It is best planted in the middle or background of the aquarium.

In addition to its attractive appearance, Thai Onion Plant provides a valuable refuge for aquarium animals, especially dwarf shrimp, nano fish, and fry. Thai Onion Plant can easily flourish and grow to become a major centerpiece of the planted aquarium!

Like most plants, Thai Onion Plant will benefit from supplementation such as Seachem Flourish, Flourish Excel, Nitrogen and other plant supplements. However, supplementation is not absolutely required for this plant to grow and stay healthy in a nutrient-rich aquarium.

No aquarium plant distributor/nursery/wholesaler/retailer can or will guarantee their plants to be 100% snail free. We can not guarantee our plants will be snail free.

 

What We Like About This Plant:

  • Grows to an impressive size
  • Beatiful long, green leaves
  • Truly aquatic and undemanding
  • Fairly uncommon in the aquarium hobby 

Care Guidelines:

  • Temperature: 68° - 82.4° F (20° - 28° C)
  • pH: 6.0 – 9.0
  • Lighting: Moderate to High
  • Origin: Indigenous to Thailand, cultivated in US nurseries
  • Aquarium placement: Middle and Background, but bulb should not be fully buried in substrate
  • Care: Easy to Moderate

 

All Aquatic Arts brand plants and animals come with a 100% live arrival guarantee, plus free email support!

products.title: Trident Java Fern (Microsorum pteropus var. “Trident”), Bunch
products.body_html:
This variant of the ever-popular Java Fern has very distinctive “trident” formations on its leaves!

 

Trident Java Fern (Microsorum pteropus var. “Trident”) is a fascinating variant of the classic Java Fern.  This variant is distinctive for its narrow leaves that form trident-like formations instead of the straighter, broader leaves of the standard Java Fern.  

Care for this variant is identical to the classic Java Fern, so it should be attached to aquarium décor.  It requires only low to moderate lighting and can thrive in a variety of water parameters, including lightly brackish conditions.  With quality care, the Trident Java Fern will grow at a moderate rate.

In addition to its attractive appearance, Trident Java Fern provides a valuable refuge for aquarium animals, especially dwarf shrimp, nano fish, and fry.  Trident Java Fern can easily flourish and grow to become a major centerpiece of the planted aquarium!

Like most plants, Trident Java Fern will benefit from supplementation such as Seachem Flourish, Flourish Excel, Nitrogen and other plant supplements.  However, supplementation is not required for this plant to grow and stay healthy.

Approximate shipping size is 3-5 stems at 10-15 inches tall.

 No aquarium plant distributor/nursery/wholesaler/retailer can or will guarantee their plants to be 100% snail free. We can not guarantee our plants will be snail free.


What We Like About This Plant:

  • Grows steadily when kept in nutrient-rich water
  • Excellent beginner plant
  • Can be attached to almost any aquarium décor
  • Unique variant of a classic species

 

Care Guidelines:

  • Temperature: 68° - 82.4° F (20° - 28° C)
  • pH: 5.0 - 8.0
  • Lighting: Low to moderate
  • Origin: Indigenous to Southeast Asia, cultivated in US nurseries
  • Aquarium placement: Middle and Background, but rhizome should not be completely buried
  • Care: Easy

products.title: White Spotted Towuti Giant Sulawesi Rabbit Snail (Tylomelania towutensis)
products.body_html:
⚠️ Important Legal Note: All aquatic invertebrates are prohibited to possess in any form or sell in the state of Maine. Please check your local regulations before purchasing. We are unable to ship livestock from this listing to Maine. If you reside in Maine and try to checkout with this in your cart you will be unable to checkout and receive an error message until it is removed from the cart.


* For the safety of our animals, we now ship Sulawesi Snails exclusively via UPS Next Day Air.  If your order contains this item, you will only see UPS Next Day Air as a shipping option during checkout *



This exceptional snail is one of the celebrated Rabbit Snail species, and it is a wonderful aquarium inhabitant with proper care!

Sulawesi Rabbit Snails (Tylomelania sp.) are some of the most fascinating snails in the aquarium hobby, and the White Spotted Towuti Snail is no exception.  Also known as Elephant Snails, their natural habitat is in Indonesia and they are only rarely seen in the United States.  The "Rabbit" name likely comes from their rabbit-like faces and drooping antennae that resemble a rabbit's ears.  However, the resemblance to an elephant is also clear with their wrinkled, rubbery-looking skin and their long "snouts" (which is functionally more like the snail's foot).  Needless to say, these snails are certainly unique in appearance in several aspects!

Our White Spotted Rabbit Sulawesi Snails possess black bodies adorned with white spots.  Their shells are long and spiral-shaped and taper toward a point at the end, which makes the shell resemble unicorn's horn in shape.  Like Gold/Yellow Rabbit Snails, these shells are usually brown with white coloration at the tip of the shell. These snails seem curious by nature, and each snail uses its elongated, muscular "foot" to pull and “hop” itself around on the glass and aquarium decor.  This is a very striking snail and is sure to impress viewers, almost all of which will have never seen one before.

Giant Sulawesi Snails are some of the largest freshwater snails in the world, growing as large as 4 inches as adults.  Since they can grow so large, they need room to move, so it's a good idea to avoid dense vegetation or overcrowding in your aquarium.  They are decent algae-eaters, but they also feed on leftover food and any kind of fish food.  While most aquatic plants are safe from these snails, they have been known to munch on more delicate or thin-leaved plants.

There are reports of successful breeding of these snails in the aquarium, but they generally reproduce much too slowly for overpopulation to be a concern. Wild-caught adult specimens may have some shell damage, but they will readily spawn in the home aquarium. Their fry will have a pristine shell and will provide years of viewing with good care!

What We Like About These Snails:

  • Black with white-spotted body coloration
  • Interesting movement and curious nature
  • Completely peaceful
  • Grow to a large and impressive size

RECOMMENDED TANK PARAMETERS: 

  • Temperature:  76° - 84° F (24.4° - 29° C)
  • pH: 7.5 - 8.5
  • KH:  2 - 15 dKH
  • Minimum tank size:  10 gallons per snail

CARE GUIDELINES:

  • Diet:  They eat algae and decaying plant matter, and they have also been known to munch on more delicate plants.  They can mostly subsist on leftover fish/invertebrate flakes or pellets, but their diets should be occasionally supplemented with algae wafers and/or blanched vegetables such as zucchini, kale, spinach, or cucumber.
  • Warning! Avoid any food product, medication, or plant fertilizer that contains Copper of any form. Copper is toxic to invertebrates. Many commercial fish foods, medications, and plant fertilizers contain copper or more commonly copper sulfate. Always look at the ingredient list for any product going into the aquarium to ensure they are copper free.
  • Social behavior:  Peaceful and solitary; will not engage with tankmates.
  • Origin:  Sulawesi region of Indonesia
  • Average adult size:  3 inches (7.6 cm)
  • Average purchase size:  1+ inch (2.5 cm) 
All Aquatic Arts brand plants and animals come with a 100% live arrival guarantee, plus free email support!

products.title: Gold Ring Danio (Brachydanio tinwini)
products.body_html:
* For the safety of our animals, we now ship live fish exclusively via UPS Next Day Air.  If your order contains this item, you will only see UPS Next Day Air as a shipping option during checkout *

 

The Gold Ring Danio is a somewhat newly-discovered species that is an extremely active and nice looking fish for the nano, planted, and community aquarium!

 

The Gold Ring Danio (Brachydanio tinwini) is a very active, beautifully-marked species that is native to Myanmar. At an adult size of 1 - 1.2 inches (2.5 - 3 cm), this peaceful schooling fish is an excellent addition to the well-decorated community aquarium. It is tolerant of a wide variety of water parameters, including temperature, so it is compatible with virtually all peaceful fish and invertebrates!

 

Like many typical danios, the Gold Ring Danio is very active and should be kept in schools of at least 6-10 specimens, although even larger schools are more optimal. This fish is very hardy and undemanding in a well-maintained aquarium, but it needs a fair amount of space due to its active and somewhat nervous nature. Stress and nervousness are minimal when this fish is kept in a larger school. The Gold Ring Danio is completely peaceful and rather social with many other fish species as well. It is compatible with almost any smaller, active, peaceful fish and will sometimes appear to school and interact with other species regularly. It will thrive in a planted aquarium with a good amount of decor such as rockwork and driftwood and its color will be most vibrant over dark substrate. It will not bother ornamental plants. It is compatible with any peaceful invertebrates that are too large to be considered prey, but it may prey on dwarf shrimp fry. This fish is a prodigious swimmer and jumper, so a tight-fitting lid or significantly lowered waterline is necessary to prevent startled or excited specimens from jumping out of the aquarium.

 

The Gold Ring Danio is a micropredator in nature, feeding on tiny invertebrates, particularly insects. In the aquarium, it is very unfussy and will accept most high-quality dry foods. However, it should also be fed a varied diet that includes live or frozen meaty foods of appropriate size. 

 

 
 
What We Like About This Fish:
  • Beautiful coloration
  • Very hardy and adaptable
  • Form schools/shoals with each other and with other species
  • Safe with plants
  • Completely peaceful
 
RECOMMENDED TANK PARAMETERS: 
  • Temperature:  64.4° - 79° F (18° - 26° C)
  • pH:  6.5 - 7.5
  • KH:  1 - 5 dKH
  • Minimum tank size:  10-15+ gallons for a school of 6-12 
 
CARE GUIDELINES:
  • Diet:  Micropredator. Will readily accept most dry foods but should be fed a varied diet including frozen and/or live meaty foods of appropriate size.
  • Social behavior:  Peaceful, schooling/shoaling.

products.title: Botanical Breeder Combo Pack (Sulawesi Shrimp)
products.body_html:
Litter your tank with our Sulawesi Shrimp Botanical Breeder Pack!

The botanicals in this pack are perfect for Sulawesi Shrimp. This curated collection of popular aquatic botanicals will meet the hopes and dreams of your Shrimp! 

 

This Botanical Pack Includes:

  • 3 Catappa Leaves
  • 2 Banana Leaves
  • 2 Coconut Flowers
  • 1 Lotus Pod
  • 1 Alder Cone Shake


Each botanical offers unique attributes that will support your breeding colony of Sulawesi Shrimp as well as other shrimp colonies!

Catappa and Banana Leaves are an excellent naturalistic decoration, food source, and herbal disease preventative for the freshwater aquarium! Catappa and Banana Leaves serve as an ideal growth medium for biofilm and diatoms, both of which are key food sources to countless aquatic animal species, including shrimp and other invertebrates as well as fish. 

Both leaf types also have buffering qualities. As they decompose, they release tannins (tercatin, punicalin, punicalagin) that lower the pH of the aquarium, but at a gradual rate. This is particularly beneficial for aquatic animals that require acidic, low-pH water. However, for Sulawesi shrimp both leaves can also be used sparingly in high-pH setups as well, especially when their pH-lowering quality is counteracted with pH-raising decor such as lace rock and dried coral. This way the shrimp can still have all the bennifits of the leaves without the pH drop. 

Both leaf types have extremely valuable theraputic qualities and can often serve as a harmless preventative medicine in the aquarium. They are highly anti-bacterial and anti-fungal and support or increase slime coat, scale, and shell production in fish and invertebrates. This is particularly crucial during the introduction and acclimation of animals to the aquarium and after water changes.

Alder Cones release tannins that help prevent fungal infections and fight bacteria in aquariums.  The cones add a natural beauty to any aquarium in which they are placed, and dwarf shrimp particularly love to graze for biofilm on the surfaces of the cones. Coconut Petals are an interesting item that offer the same benefits as Almond leaves and Alder Cones. 

Lotus pods are a great part of any shrimp setup, but very helpful for Sulawesi shrimp! These pods help provide lots of hiding spaces for the shrimp to retreat into the natural holes on the pods. Because these shrimp can be very reclusive having lots of hideaways is very helpful to these shrimp so they feel more comfortable in their tank. The more comfortable they are the more they will come out and forage away from cover. 

 

For Individual Purchases Please Refer to our Botanical Page!

products.title: Blushing Blue Ghost Angelfish (Pterophyllum scalare) – Tank Bred
products.body_html:
For the safety of our animals, we now ship live fish exclusively via UPS Next Day Air. If your order contains this item, you will only see UPS Next Day Air as a shipping option during checkout.

With its shimmering pale blue body and dramatic black-tipped fins, the Blushing Blue Ghost Angelfish ( a New Variant) is a showstopper in any aquarium. This unique tank-bred variety combines the soft, pearlescent look of a ghost angelfish with subtle pink “blushing” around the gill plates—and striking seal-point-style black top and bottom fins, reminiscent of a Siamese cat. Their graceful movement and vertical presence make them ideal centerpiece fish for planted or peaceful community tanks.

What We Love About This Fish:

• Stunning pale blue body with a luminous ghost-like appearance
• Soft pink blush on the gills under the right lighting
• Distinct black top and bottom fins—a rare, seal-point look
• Tank-bred for hardiness and adaptability to aquarium life

Natural Habitat:

A domestic variety of Pterophyllum scalare, which originates from slow-flowing tributaries in the Amazon Basin. Though tank-bred, they thrive in environments that mimic their wild roots: soft, slightly acidic water with vertical space, cover, and gentle flow.

Aquascaping Suggestions:

• Plants: Amazon Sword, Vallisneria, Java Fern, Hornwort
• Hardscape: Upright driftwood, river stones, and leaf litter for natural ambiance
• Botanicals: Indian Almond Leaves or Catappa Bark to enrich water and promote healthy behavior. Or try one of our Botanical Combo Packs.

Diet and Feeding:

Blushing Blue Ghost Angelfish are omnivores and do best on a varied, high-quality diet. We recommend Ultra Fresh Excellent Bits – Tropical Fish, a soft, slow-sinking pellet made with over 60% fresh meat and seaweed—perfect for mid-water feeders like angelfish. These bits help support digestion, color enhancement, and overall vitality. For added nutrition and enrichment, offer live daphnia, Iive Scuds, brine shrimp, or bloodworms several times per week. A varied diet helps encourage natural behaviors and supports breeding health.

Tank Mates and Compatibility:

Best kept with peaceful fish that won't nip at long fins. Great companions include tetras, rasboras, corydoras, and peaceful dwarf cichlids. Avoid boisterous or aggressive species.

Not Shrimp Safe:

Generally safe with adult shrimp in heavily planted tanks, but may snack on tiny shrimp or shrimplets in less-covered environments. But not adviced.

Breeding:

Pairs will form naturally and lay eggs on vertical surfaces like broad leaves or flat rocks. In community setups, it's best to move the pair or eggs to a separate tank if you want to raise fry successfully.

Recommended Tank Parameters:

• Temperature: 75° - 82° F (24° - 28° C)
• pH: 6.5 - 7.5
• KH: 3 - 8 dKH
• Minimum Tank Size: 30 gallons

Care Guidelines:

• Diet: Omnivore—accepts pellets, flakes, frozen/live food, and live daphnia
• Social Behavior: Peaceful; best kept singly, in pairs, or small peaceful groups
• Origin: Tank-bred in the USA
• Average Adult Size: 5 - 6 inches (12.7 - 15.2 cm)
• Average Purchase Size: 1.5 - 2.5 inches (3.8 - 6.4 cm)

Bring serene beauty and graceful movement to your aquarium with the Blushing Blue Ghost Angelfish—complete with its rare seal-point flair.

products.title: Tequila Goodeid (Zoogoneticus tequila), BREDBY: Aquatic Arts
products.body_html:
For the safety of our animals, we now ship live fish exclusively via UPS Next Day Air. If your order contains this item, you will only see UPS Next Day Air as a shipping option during checkout.

The Tequila Goodeid (Zoogoneticus tequila) is a critically endangered livebearer native to a single watershed in western Mexico. Once considered extinct in the wild, this species has been preserved thanks to passionate conservation efforts and responsible breeding by aquarists. These specimens are USA bred from long-term captive domestic stock—not wild-caught—ensuring ethical sourcing and continued support of this species’ survival.

Named for the Teuchitlán River near the town of Tequila in Jalisco, Mexico, this species is admired for its bold body shape and distinct coloration. Males display an orange tail base with shimmering scales, while females are larger and exhibit a more subtle silver pattern. These fish thrive in cooler, well-oxygenated aquariums with plenty of plant cover and should always be kept in social groups.

What We Love About the Tequila Goodeid:

• Critically endangered species with a compelling conservation story
• USA bred from domestically raised, non-wild stock
• Peaceful temperament and ideal for planted community tanks
• Helps preserve a species once nearly lost to extinction

Natural Habitat:

This species is native to the Río Teuchitlán in west-central Mexico. Its natural habitat includes spring-fed streams and pools with aquatic vegetation, moderate flow, and clear, cool water. It prefers mineral-rich, alkaline conditions.

Aquascaping:

• Plants: Ideal tankmates include Hornwort, Java Moss, Guppy Grass, and Vallisneria
• Hardscape: Smooth river stones, branches, and areas for cover
• Botanicals: Indian almond leaves or alder cones may help promote biofilm and offer refuge for fry

Diet and Feeding:

The Tequila Goodeid is an omnivore and benefits from a varied diet. Offer high-quality flake food, micro pellets, spirulina, blanched veggies (such as zucchini), and live or frozen foods like daphnia, brine shrimp, or grindal worms. They graze on biofilm and algae in planted tanks.

Tank Mates and Compatibility:

Very peaceful. Best kept with other gentle species that prefer cooler temperatures. Avoid aggressive or fin-nipping tankmates. Keep in groups of 6 or more with a ratio of 2–3 females per male.

Shrimp Safe:

✔ Yes – Safe with adult shrimp. May eat newly hatched shrimp.

Breeding:

A livebearing goodeid. Unlike guppies or mollies, fry are larger and born less frequently, but in excellent health. Provide dense cover for fry survival. These are USA bred from captive stock that has been domestically maintained for generations—no wild specimens or recent imports.

Recommended Tank Parameters:

• Temperature: 68° - 74° F (20° - 23° C)
• pH: 7.0 – 8.0
• KH: 6 – 12 dKH
• Minimum Tank Size: 20 gallons

Care Guidelines:

• Diet: Omnivorous – needs variety including high-protein and veggie-based foods
• Social Behavior: Peaceful and shoaling; best in groups
• Origin: USA bred from domestically raised, non-wild stock
• Average Adult Size: 2.5 inches (6.3 cm)
• Average Purchase Size: 1 – 1.5 inches (juveniles, unsexed)

Support Conservation with Every Tank:
The Tequila Goodeid is not just a beautiful addition to your aquarium—it’s a symbol of how aquarists can protect endangered species through ethical, mindful care. Available now from Aquatic Arts—bred in the USA, with purpose and pride.

products.title: Stigmatochromis melanchros Cichlid – Locally Bred
products.body_html:
Locally Bred in Indiana


For the safety of our animals, we now ship live fish exclusively via UPS Next Day Air. If your order contains this item, you will only see UPS Next Day Air as a shipping option during checkout.

Stigmatochromis melanchros is one of the rarest and most visually striking predator haps from Lake Malawi. These juveniles were bred locally in Indiana by an expert cichlid breeder, and we’re proud to offer this exceptionally uncommon species exclusively through Aquatic Arts.

Adult males develop an intense dark coloration with subtle iridescence and electric blue accents in the fins—an appearance that gives this fish its “melanchros” (meaning "black-colored") name. Photos of the breeding adults are included in this listing to show what these juveniles will grow into with proper care. Currently available fish are still young and uncolored.

What We Love About This Fish:

• One of the rarest Stigmatochromis species in the hobby
• Striking adult coloration: jet black body with glowing blue highlights
• Powerful, torpedo-shaped build perfect for open swimming
• Locally bred in Indiana from a stunning breeding pair

Natural Habitat:

Native to the deep rocky zones of Lake Malawi, Stigmatochromis melanchros is a piscivorous predator that cruises through open waters near boulder piles, hunting smaller fish. Its natural environment features clean, mineral-rich water and high oxygenation.

Aquascaping:

• Plants: Not recommended—these large cichlids may ignore or disturb them
• Hardscape: Create a layout with large rocks, caves, and long open swimming lanes
• Botanicals: Not necessary but can be used sparingly for natural aesthetics without altering pH

Diet and Feeding:

This species is a carnivore that thrives on a diet of protein-rich foods. Feed frozen or live foods like krill, mysis shrimp, and chopped fish, along with high-quality carnivore pellets. For a daily staple, we recommend Ultra Fresh Royal Cichlid Premium Pellet – Shrimp Delight, which promotes vibrant coloration and supports growth and immune health.

Tank Mates and Compatibility:

Suitable tank mates include other large, semi-aggressive haps or peacocks. Avoid overly aggressive species and small, delicate fish. Provide plenty of space and hiding spots to diffuse territorial behavior, especially when housing multiple males.

Shrimp Safe:

❌ No – This species is a predatory hunter and will eat shrimp or smaller fish.

Breeding:

A maternal mouthbrooder. Males become territorial and display to females during courtship, clearing out a spawning site. Females incubate the eggs in their mouths for several weeks. The juveniles offered here are offspring from a stunning locally bred pair.

Recommended Tank Parameters:

• Temperature: 76° - 82° F (24° - 28° C)
• pH: 7.8 – 8.6
• KH: 8 – 14 dKH
• Minimum Tank Size: 90 gallons

Care Guidelines:

• Diet: Carnivorous – thrives on high-protein frozen and pellet foods
• Social Behavior: Semi-aggressive; males will defend territory during breeding
• Origin: Locally bred in Indiana
• Average Adult Size: 10 – 12 inches (25 – 30 cm)
• Average Purchase Size: 2 – 3 inches (juveniles not yet showing adult coloration)

Add One of Lake Malawi’s Rarest Predators to Your Collection:
Stigmatochromis melanchros is a hidden gem in the cichlid world. With jet-black coloration and an elegant, torpedo-shaped body, this locally bred predator is a powerful and captivating choice for experienced aquarists looking to grow out a showstopper—available now from Aquatic Arts.

Ptychochromis grandidieri is a rare and fascinating species native to the rivers and freshwater habitats of eastern Madagascar. We are proud to offer juveniles bred right here in Indiana by a dedicated local breeder. This is a true collector’s cichlid—an uncommon find that brings bold personality and impressive size to larger aquariums.

Juveniles currently available are still developing their mature coloration, which includes subtle iridescence, strong barring, and a commanding, robust body shape. As adults, these fish display complex behaviors and become striking centerpiece fish in semi-aggressive setups.

What We Love About This Fish:

• Locally bred in Indiana by a knowledgeable cichlid specialist
• Rare Malagasy species, seldom available in the aquarium trade
• Grows into a large, commanding fish with rich character
• Thrives in large tanks with structured aquascapes

Natural Habitat:

In the wild, Ptychochromis grandidieri inhabits lowland rivers and freshwater streams in eastern Madagascar. These environments are typically warm, slow to moderately flowing, and filled with branches, roots, and smooth stones. The species prefers neutral to slightly alkaline water with moderate hardness.

Aquascaping:

• Plants: Use with caution—large cichlids may uproot delicate plants. Hardy epiphytes like Anubias or Java Fern on driftwood may be used.
• Hardscape: Provide caves, large driftwood pieces, and open swimming space.
• Botanicals: Add Indian almond leaves, pods, or alder cones to create a more natural look and enrich water conditions.

Diet and Feeding:

This species is omnivorous with a preference for protein-rich foods. Offer a mix of high-quality pellets, frozen foods (such as krill, bloodworms, or mysis), and occasional vegetable matter. An excellent staple is Ultra Fresh Royal Cichlid Premium Pellet – Shrimp Delight, which provides shrimp protein, natural color enhancers, and essential nutrients.

Tank Mates and Compatibility:

Best housed with similarly sized, semi-aggressive fish in a large tank. While they are generally less aggressive than African rift lake cichlids, they can be territorial—especially when breeding. Avoid small, delicate, or passive tankmates.

Shrimp Safe:

❌ No – Not recommended with shrimp or other small invertebrates.

Breeding:

A biparental substrate-spawning species. Males and females form pairs and defend a territory where eggs are laid and guarded. Breeding may occur in large aquariums with proper conditions and established pairs. These juveniles were bred from a local, proven pair in Indiana.

Recommended Tank Parameters:

• Temperature: 75° - 80° F (24° - 27° C)
• pH: 7.0 – 8.0
• KH: 6 – 12 dKH
• Minimum Tank Size: 75 gallons for a pair; 90+ gallons for community setups

Care Guidelines:

• Diet: Omnivorous, with a focus on protein-rich prepared and frozen foods
• Social Behavior: Semi-aggressive; pairs can be territorial
• Origin: Locally bred in Indiana
• Average Adult Size: 9 – 12 inches (23 – 30 cm)
• Average Purchase Size: 2 – 3 inches (juvenile)

A Rare Gem from Madagascar:
Ptychochromis grandidieri is a unique and rewarding fish for serious aquarists. Its rarity, intelligence, and impressive size make it a standout addition to any large aquarium. Thanks to our trusted local breeder, you now have the opportunity to raise this exceptional species—available only from Aquatic Arts.

This unique blend of real fruit, leafy greens, protein, and bioavailable calcium is designed to meet the dietary needs of aquatic invertebrates and reptiles. With craveable natural ingredients like banana, mango, black soldier fly larvae, and collard greens, this formula supports shell growth, molting, and long-term health—while actually encouraging picky eaters to feed.

Unlike powdered or synthetic calcium supplements, this ready-to-feed formula includes 30% bioavailable calcium and whole-food ingredients that snails, shrimp, and bottom dwellers recognize and enjoy. Blue spirulina and garlic add essential micronutrients and immune support, and it holds its shape in water without fouling your tank.

This is the best of both worlds—a formula that boosts structure-building calcium and offers a clean, tank-safe feeding experience. Whether you're raising Mystery Snails, breeding shrimp, or caring for turtles, this is a must-have in your invert care routine.

What We Love About This Product:

• Whole Food Nutrition – No synthetic isolates, just black soldier fly larvae, egg, fruits, and greens

Ideal for:

Support better molting, shell growth, and feeding response with a nutrition-forward blend your pets will actually enjoy.

products.title: Live Tubifex Worms (Tubifex tubifex) – Aquatic Arts
products.body_html:
Live Tubifex Worms (Tubifex tubifex) – 1 oz Portion

 

Also known as sludgeworms, Tubifex Worms are a nutrient-dense live food that’s a favorite among breeders and serious aquarists alike. Packed with protein and movement, these thin, red worms stimulate natural feeding behavior and are an excellent choice for conditioning and spawning preparation.

Why We Like Them:

  • Excellent Breeding Conditioner: Ideal for preparing egg-layers and livebearers to spawn.

  • Great for Finicky Eaters: Even picky species can’t resist the wriggling motion.

  • Natural Hunter Enrichment: Encourages active foraging in bottom-dwellers and micro-predators.

  • High Protein Content: Perfect for growing out fry or bulking up adult fish.

Who Loves Tubifex Worms:

  • Dwarf Cichlids (Apistogramma, Ram Cichlids)

  • Bettas (Betta splendens and wild types)

  • Livebearers (Guppies, Endlers, Platies, Swordtails)

  • Killifish (Nothobranchius, Aphyosemion)

  • Loaches (Kuhli, Clown, Dwarf Chain Loaches)

  • Tetras (Ember, Neon, Green Fire, Rummynose)

  • Gouramis, Rasboras, Gobies, and more!

Care and Storage Tips:

  • Water Depth: Keep in a shallow container with just 1/4" of dechlorinated water.

  • Temperature: Ideal range is 55–77°F (13–25°C). Avoid refrigeration—they do not tolerate cold storage well.

  • Rinse Daily: Use cold, dechlorinated water to rinse at least once a day to prevent fouling.

  • Avoid Overcrowding: Spread out the worms to reduce die-off and maintain cleanliness.

  • Natural Enhancements: Add dried botanicals like Indian Almond Leaves, Mulberry Leaves, or Catappa Bark to the water. These can help reduce microbial buildup, provide tannins, and even subtly enrich the worm environment.

Important Note on Smell:

Tubifex Worms are affectionately called sludgeworms for a reason—they carry a distinct earthy or muddy odor. This is completely natural and doesn’t impact quality. Proper care and rinsing will keep them fresh and usable.

Whether you’re preparing a breeding pair, feeding fry, or just offering your fish something exciting, Live Tubifex Worms deliver a high-value, high-impact feeding experience.

Sold in 1 oz Portions
Each portion is packed fresh and shipped with care.

Live Arrival Guarantee:

products.title: Snakeskin AKA Rhombo AKA Orange Buffalo Barb (Desmopuntius rhomboocellatus)
products.body_html:
* For the safety of our animals, we now ship live fish exclusively via UPS Next Day Air.  If your order contains this item, you will only see UPS Next Day Air as a shipping option during checkout * 

Dramatic, unique coloration, peaceful disposition, and small size makes this barb an ideal fish for the nano and planted community aquarium! This species of many names is also known as the Orange Buffalo Barb.

The Snakeskin Barb (Desmopuntius rhomboocellatus), also known as the Rhombo or Orange Buffalo Barb, is a very peaceful schooling species that is an excellent fish for the community nano or planted aquarium. It is known for its python-like markings and small size. It is rather exceptional in appearance, but not as often available as many more widespread barb species. It is native to Indonesian Borneo. When kept in larger groups, males will display their best coloration while competing to attract females.

The Snakeskin Barb typically occupies the top and middle level of the water column, although it can often be seen swimming and feeding in the middle and bottom levels as well. It is safe with other peaceful, small fish. It may prey on smaller dwarf shrimp and their fry. Larger, peaceful invertebrates can make good tankmates. The Snakeskin Barb must be kept in groups of 6 or more, although larger groups are more ideal so that subdominant specimens are not bullied by dominant individuals during breeding behavior. Its natural habitat is tropical rainforest areas that typically have clear water. It requires clean water to thrive and it will display optimal coloration and health in a planted aquarium with dark substrate. This fish is native to somewhat dimly-lit streams, so low-light plants such as Java Fern, Anubias species, and Bucephalandra (Buce) species are excellent plants for its aquarium.

While not a picky eater, the Snakeskin Barb will thrive and remain very colorful on a varied diet of omnivorous foods. High-quality flake food, pellets, and live, frozen, or freeze-dried Artemia, microworms, Tubifex, and finely chopped bloodworms will all be readily accepted. 

What We Like About This Fish:

  • Exquisite python-like coloration
  • Peaceful disposition with fish and peaceful invertebrates too large to be considered prey
  • Very active and noticeable fish in any aquarium
  • Ideal for planted aquariums

RECOMMENDED TANK PARAMETERS:

  • Temperature: 68 - 82° F (20°-28° C)
  • pH:4.0 - 7.0
  • KH: 3 - 12 dKH
  • Minimum Tank Size: 10 gallons for a group of 6 - 8

CARE INFORMATION:

  • Diet: Micropredator. Will easily accept high quality dry and frozen foods of appropriate size. Requires a varied diet for optimal health and coloration.
  • Social Behavior: Peaceful, schooling/shoaling.
  • Origin: Indonesian Borneo
  • Average Adult Size: 2  inches (5 cm)
  • Average Purchase Size: 1 inch (2.5 cm)

products.title: AquaZen Botanical Zen Pack
products.body_html:
AquaZen Botanical Zen Pack

Tannins, textures, and tranquility—AquaZen style.

 

Introducing the first official botanical release from our AquaZen line—crafted to bring serene beauty and natural balance to your aquarium, paludarium, or terrarium. The AquaZen Botanical Zen Pack is a thoughtfully curated trio of all-natural botanicals that provide more than just visual appeal: each piece enriches your ecosystem with beneficial tannins, biofilm-promoting surfaces, and natural shelter that encourages thriving aquatic life.

🌿 What’s Inside:

  • 5 Medium Catappa Leaves – Slowly release tannins and humic substances that support immune health, reduce minor fungal concerns, and encourage natural behaviors. Great for biofilm development—a key food source for shrimp and fry.

  • 2 Catappa Bark Pieces – Dense and long-lasting, these bark segments leach beneficial compounds into the water and offer natural hiding spaces.

  • 8 Casuarina Pine Pods (Casuarina equisetifolia) – A pinecone-style botanical that adds visual texture and durable structure. Releases mild antibacterial and antifungal tannins, creates biofilm-rich surfaces, and offers long-term visual interest. These pods release fewer tannins than alder cones, making them perfect for small aquariums or shrimp tanks.

🧭 How to Use in a 10-Gallon Shrimp Aquarium:

To avoid over-conditioning the water in a small system, we recommend starting with half the pack:

  • 2–3 Catappa Leaves

  • 1 Catappa Bark Piece

  • 3–4 Casuarina Pine Pods

This offers natural grazing grounds, mild pH buffering, and gentle tannin release—ideal for shrimp, snails, nano fish, and breeding tanks.

Maintenance Tips:

  • Replace leaves every 2–3 weeks as they break down

  • Bark and pods can remain for several months

  • Always rinse and briefly boil botanicals before use

  • Monitor pH and clarity when adding new botanicals

🌊 Ideal For:

  • Freshwater Aquariums (Blackwater or Softwater)

  • Shrimp & Snail Tanks

  • Paludariums & Semi-Aquatic Setups

  • Naturalistic Terrarium Displays

💡 Pro Tips:

  • Layer botanicals beneath driftwood or among plants for a natural look

  • Use sparingly in small tanks; botanicals are powerful in even low amounts

  • Encourage microfauna and biofilm to grow as part of a healthy food web

 

Natural beauty. Balanced ecosystems. One Zen Pack.
The first of many in our AquaZen botanical line—designed to support your aquascape and its inhabitants, naturally.

products.title: Kats Aquatics DIY "Snello" Gel Food 4oz
products.body_html:

One Bag Makes Over 400 Heart-Shaped Servings!
Crafted by the biologist behind Kats Aquatics Calcium, Snello Gel Food is redefining what gel food should be. This whole food, super-premium formula was created for everyday feeding—and every bite supports growth, shell strength, and long-term vitality.

 

Whether you're feeding snails, shrimp, plecos, corydoras, or even aquatic turtles, Snello delivers the balanced nutrition your animals need to thrive.

🐌 Not Just for Snails

Despite the name, Snello is a favorite among a wide range of aquatic pets:
• Bottom feeders like plecos and Corydoras

• Fish love grazing on it for a natural veggie boost

🌿 Why Hobbyists Love Snello

• Biologist-formulated – from the same creator as Kats Aquatics Calcium

Feed real food. Watch real growth.
Snello Gel Food is the gold standard in aquatic nutrition—backed by science, loved by aquarists.

products.title: Casuarina Pine Pods (Casuarina equisetifolia)
products.body_html:
Casuarina Pine Pods (Casuarina equisetifolia) – Long-Lasting Botanical for Aquariums, Terrariums, and Paludariums (6-Pack or 12-Pack)

An attractive, low-tannin alternative to alder cones—ideal for shrimp, snails, and blackwater-style setups.

Bring structure, subtle texture, and biofilm support to your tank or terrarium with Casuarina Pine Pods, also known as Casuarina Seed Cones. These naturally dense, pinecone-like botanicals come from the Casuarina equisetifolia tree and are a durable, low-tannin addition to any freshwater or high-humidity environment.

Each pod is thoroughly cleaned and ready for use in aquariums, paludariums, or terrariums—providing both visual appeal and long-term function.

Why We Love Them:

• Visually Striking – Their natural, pinecone-like texture enhances any nature-themed layout
• Durable & Slow-Breaking – Lasts significantly longer than alder cones in water
• Low-Tannin Release – Won’t overly tint water or drastically lower pH
• Versatile Use – Excellent in aquariums, terrariums, or paludariums for structure and shelter

How They Differ from Alder Cones:

While similar in appearance, Casuarina Pine Pods are more durable and release far fewer tannins than traditional Alder Cones (Alnus incana). If you want the visual appeal and surface area for biofilm without significantly darkening your water or lowering pH, Casuarina is the better choice. They’re perfect for long-term setups where stability and structure matter more than water conditioning.

Great for These Species:

• Aquarium Fish – Betta fish, rasboras, gouramis, tetras, and dwarf cichlids benefit from shelter and natural decor
• Aquatic Inverts – Shrimp, snails, and plecos love to graze on the biofilm that grows on these pods
• Terrarium & Paludarium Dwellers – Vampire crabs, isopods, dart frogs, and springtails use them for cover, moisture retention, and climbing surfaces

Preparation Instructions:

Rinse well and boil for 15–30 minutes before use to help them sink and minimize any initial tannin release. Pods may take time to become fully waterlogged but will eventually sink and remain intact for months.

Size & Quantity Options:

• 6-Pack – Perfect for nano tanks, betta habitats, or small terrariums
• 12-Pack – Ideal for community tanks or large display enclosures

Each pod measures approximately 1.5–2.5 inches. As these are natural items, size and shape may vary slightly.

products.title: Dwarf Red Coral Platy (Xiphophorus maculatus) - Tank-Bred!
products.body_html:
* For the safety of our animals, we now ship live fish exclusively via UPS Next Day Air.  If your order contains this item, you will only see UPS Next Day Air as a shipping option during checkout *

 

The Dwarf Red Coral Platy is a vibrant, compact livebearer that packs stunning color into a small size. Unlike standard platies, this tank-bred variety has been selectively bred for a smaller body and intense red-orange hue, making it ideal for nano tanks or peaceful community setups. Hardy, active, and beginner-friendly, Dwarf Red Coral Platies are easy to care for and add constant motion and brilliance to any aquarium.

What We Love About This Fish:

• Brilliant Coral Red Color: Their eye-catching red hue stands out beautifully in planted tanks.
• Smaller Size: More compact than standard platies—perfect for nano tanks.
• Peaceful and Social: Gets along well with other peaceful fish and invertebrates.
• Easy to Breed: A great choice for hobbyists interested in livebearer reproduction.

Natural Habitat:

While originally derived from populations in Central America, the Dwarf Red Coral Platy is a selectively bred aquarium strain. In nature, platies are found in slow-moving rivers, canals, and warm streams with dense aquatic vegetation and soft to moderate flow.

Aquascaping:

• Plants: Floating and stem plants like Hornwort, Water Sprite, and Amazon Swords provide cover and breeding spaces.
• Hardscape: Smooth stones, driftwood, and open swimming areas keep them active and comfortable.
• Botanicals: Though not essential, botanicals like Indian Almond Leaves and alder cones can enhance the tank’s natural feel and support water quality. We recommend the Aquatic Arts Botanical Combo Pack to round out a peaceful, shrimp-safe aquascape.

Diet and Feeding:

These omnivores aren’t picky and do best with a variety of high-quality prepared foods.

✅ Recommended Food:
• Ultra Royal Guppy Mignon Pellets – Shrimp Delight Formula – Soft, slow-sinking pellets made with real shrimp and ideal for small livebearers like platies. Supports color and immune health.
• High-Quality Flakes and Veggie-Based Pellets – Spirulina and algae-based foods help maintain vibrancy.

✅ Supplemental Treats:
Blanched vegetables, freeze-dried daphnia, or baby brine shrimp.

Tank Mates and Compatibility:

Very peaceful and perfect for community aquariums. Compatible with tetras, rasboras, small gouramis, cory catfish, snails, and dwarf shrimp. Males can occasionally chase females, so maintaining a group with a 1:2 male-to-female ratio is ideal.

Shrimp Safe:

Yes – adult platies generally coexist peacefully with shrimp. Baby shrimp may be at risk if not provided with enough cover.

Breeding:

Dwarf Red Coral Platies are livebearers and will breed readily in the aquarium. Females give birth to free-swimming fry about once a month. To raise fry successfully, provide fine-leaved plants or a breeding box for shelter.

Recommended Tank Parameters:

• Temperature: 70° – 78° F (21° – 26° C)
• pH: 6.8 – 8.0
• KH: 6 – 12 dKH
• Minimum Tank Size: 10 gallons

Care Guidelines:

• Diet: Omnivorous. Enjoys a varied diet of pellets, flakes, and veggie-based foods.
• Social Behavior: Peaceful and active. Keep in small groups.
• Origin: Tank Bred
• Average Adult Size: 1.25 – 1.5 inches (3.2 – 3.8 cm)
• Average Purchase Size: 0.75 – 1.25 inches (2 – 3.2 cm)

Tiny, Colorful, and Full of Life
The Dwarf Red Coral Platy is the perfect choice for aquarists who want all the charm and activity of a livebearer in a smaller, more manageable size. Whether you're starting a nano tank or adding to a peaceful community setup, these platies shine.

All Aquatic Arts brand plants and animals come with a 100% live arrival guarantee, plus free email support!

products.title: Rosy Bitterling (Rhodeus ocellatus ocellatus)
products.body_html:
* For the safety of our animals, we now ship live fish exclusively via UPS Next Day Air.  If your order contains this item, you will only see UPS Next Day Air as a shipping option during checkout *

This lovely fish is very active and is an excellent tankmate for many other subtropical fish and invertebrates and can even be kept in ponds in warmer areas of the US!

The Rosy Bitterling is a peaceful, uniquely fascinating freshwater species known for its subtle pink sheen, streamlined body, and remarkable spawning behavior. These small, hardy fish make an excellent addition to a community or species-only aquarium, especially for aquarists interested in breeding behaviors or naturalistic setups. Unlike many popular schooling fish, Bitterlings exhibit intriguing social interactions and breed using live freshwater mussels as part of their reproductive cycle.

This offering is for tank-bred specimens, ensuring hardiness and ease of acclimation in home aquariums.

What We Love About This Fish:

• Soft Pink and Silver Coloration: Reflective body with iridescent hues and a hint of blush.
• Unique Breeding Behavior: Uses live mussels to deposit eggs, making spawning a truly one-of-a-kind event to observe.
• Community Friendly: Peaceful nature, best kept in small schools.
• Tank Bred: Ethically sourced and acclimated to aquarium conditions.

Natural Habitat:

Native to East Asia, particularly slow-flowing streams and shallow ponds across China, Japan, and Korea. These habitats typically have sandy or silty substrates, dense vegetation, and freshwater mussel populations, where Bitterlings naturally spawn.

Aquascaping:

• Plants: Fine-leaved plants like Hornwort, Water Sprite, and Ludwigia help replicate their natural environment and provide refuge.
• Hardscape: Smooth pebbles and sand or silt substrate. Provide shaded areas with driftwood and gentle flow.
• Botanicals: Enhance their environment with Indian Almond Leaves, Guava Leaves, and Alder Cones to support biofilm development and water quality. We recommend the Aquatic Arts Botanical Combo Pack to round out a natural and enriching setup.

Diet and Feeding:

Rosy Bitterlings are omnivores and accept a wide range of prepared foods.

✅ Primary Foods:
• Ultra Fresh Tropical Micro Pellets – Shrimp Delight Formula – Perfectly sized for their small mouths, these micro pellets are made with real shrimp and natural color enhancers to support growth and vitality.
• High-Quality Flakes or Crushed Pellets – Supplemented with spirulina or insect protein.

✅ Occasional Treats:
Frozen or live daphnia, baby brine shrimp, or bloodworms.

Tank Mates and Compatibility:

These fish are best kept in groups of 5 or more. Peaceful by nature, they do well with other small, non-aggressive species such as rasboras, small barbs, white clouds, Corydoras, and snails. Avoid large or boisterous tank mates that may outcompete them or cause stress.

Not Shrimp Safe:

Fry may be at risk if food is limited, but Bitterlings are not aggressive hunters. Provide plenty of cover to protect shrimp colonies.

Breeding:

One of the most interesting fish to breed! Rosy Bitterlings deposit their eggs inside the gills of live freshwater mussels (such as Live Freshwater Mussels, when available). Fry hatch and remain inside the mussel for several weeks before swimming out. For breeding attempts, maintaining suitable mussels and clean, oxygen-rich water is critical.

Recommended Tank Parameters:

• Temperature: 64° – 75° F (18° – 24° C)
• pH: 6.8 – 7.6
• KH: 3 – 12 dKH
• Minimum Tank Size: 10 gallons (20+ gallons recommended for groups)

Care Guidelines:

• Diet: Omnivorous. Eats small prepared foods and live/frozen fare.
• Social Behavior: Schooling. Keep in groups of 5 or more.
• Origin: Tank Bred
• Average Adult Size: 2 – 2.5 inches (5 – 6.3 cm)
• Average Purchase Size: 1 – 1.5 inches (2.5 – 3.8 cm)

Peaceful, Curious, and Unique
With their reflective coloration and incredible breeding habits, Rosy Bitterlings bring subtle beauty and rare behaviors to your freshwater aquarium. A perfect choice for aquarists looking to try something out of the ordinary.
