products.title: Honeycomb Bristlenose Pleco (Ancistrus sp. “Wabenmuster”) – Tank Bred
products.body_html:
For the safety of our animals, we now ship live fish exclusively via UPS Next Day Air. If your order contains this item, you will only see UPS Next Day Air as a shipping option during checkout.

The Wabenmuster Bushynose Pleco, also known as the Honeycomb Pleco, is a highly sought-after tank-bred Ancistrus variant known for its bold, geometric white or yellow pattern against a dark black body—resembling a honeycomb or mesh net. Peaceful, hardy, and highly efficient at grazing algae, this fish is a top-tier choice for planted and community aquariums.

Unlike many larger plecos, this Ancistrus stays a manageable size, making it ideal for aquarists who want both beauty and functionality in their tank.

What We Love About This Fish:

• Unique Honeycomb Pattern: A stunning and rare design not seen in most pleco species.
• Natural Algae Control: Helps keep aquarium surfaces clean.
• Peaceful Personality: A great community tank member.
• Tank Bred: Sustainably raised in captivity for excellent health and adaptability.

Natural Habitat:

Ancistrus species are native to shaded, slow-flowing rivers and tributaries in South America. These areas are rich in driftwood, submerged roots, and botanical debris, all of which contribute to biofilm and algae growth that these plecos naturally consume.

Aquascaping:

• Plants: Hardy species like Java Fern, Anubias, Bolbitis, and Amazon Swords provide cover without being uprooted.
• Hardscape: Include driftwood (especially Malaysian and Mopani), stone caves, and cholla wood to encourage natural grazing behavior and offer secure resting areas.
• Botanicals: This species thrives in aquariums enriched with natural botanicals that support biofilm growth and mimic their native environment. Indian Almond Leaves, Guava Leaves, Jackfruit Leaves, Cholla Wood, and Alder Cones are especially well-suited for Ancistrus. We recommend adding the Aquatic Arts Botanical Combo Pack to your setup. It’s a hand-selected variety that enhances water quality, encourages foraging, and completes the natural feel of your aquarium.

Diet and Feeding:

These plecos do consume algae, but a balanced, varied diet is essential for their long-term health.

✅ Primary Foods:
• Aquatic Arts Algae Wafers – A premium blend of spirulina, sinking plant matter, and protein-rich ingredients designed to mimic a natural diet.

✅ Supplemental Foods:
Blanched zucchini, spinach, green beans, and occasional treats like Repashy gel food or frozen bloodworms are also welcome.

Tank Mates and Compatibility:

Compatible with most peaceful community fish, including tetras, rasboras, peaceful cichlids, livebearers, shrimp, and snails. Avoid keeping multiple male Ancistrus in small tanks, as they may show mild territorial behavior around caves.

Shrimp Safe:

Safe with adult shrimp and typically does not bother shrimp fry. Ensure enough cover and feeding stations to avoid competition.

Breeding:

Relatively easy to breed. Provide a dedicated spawning cave and maintain stable water quality. Males will guard and fan the eggs until hatching.

Recommended Tank Parameters:

• Temperature: 74° – 82° F (23° – 28° C)
• pH: 6.5 – 7.5
• KH: 2 – 12 dKH
• Minimum Tank Size: 20 gallons

Care Guidelines:

• Diet: Omnivorous bottom grazer. Provide algae wafers, vegetables, and occasional protein-rich treats.
• Social Behavior: Peaceful, semi-territorial with conspecific males.
• Origin: Tank Bred
• Average Adult Size: 4 – 5 inches (10 – 12.5 cm)
• Average Purchase Size: 1.5 – 2.25 inches (3.8 – 5.7 cm)

Bring Home a Living Work of Art
With its captivating honeycomb pattern and beneficial algae-eating behavior, the Wabenmuster Pleco is both beautiful and functional. Pair it with natural botanicals and a premium diet for a truly thriving aquarium experience.

Live Arrival Guarantee:

All Aquatic Arts brand plants and animals come with a 100% live arrival guarantee, plus free email support from our expert team!

products.title: Liberty Molly (Poecilia salvatoris) – Locally Bred
products.body_html:
For the safety of our animals, we now ship live fish exclusively via UPS 1 Day Service. If your order contains this item, you will only see UPS 1 Day as a shipping option during checkout.

A classic livebearer with a bold twist, the Liberty Molly is a rare and strikingly patterned molly species featuring red, blue, and black hues across its fins and body. Unlike other mollies, Liberty Mollies are known for their assertive personalities and vivid coloration—especially in males. These locally bred specimens are hardier, better acclimated to aquarium life, and perfect for hobbyists looking to keep something with character and color. Choose from individual males, females, or breeding pairs for your aquarium.

What We Love About This Fish:

• Uncommon Beauty: Tri-colored fins with red, black, and blue make this molly stand out in any setup.
• Locally Bred: Acclimated to aquarium life, making them hardier and more sustainable.
• Active and Bold: Adds movement and attitude to the tank.
• Livebearing Species: A great candidate for home breeding projects.

Natural Habitat:

Native to coastal freshwater streams and brackish estuaries in Central America, Liberty Mollies thrive in warm, mineral-rich waters with moderate flow and dense vegetation.

Aquascaping:

• Plants: Consider hardy species like Hornwort, Vallisneria, and Java Fern. Floating plants like Frogbit also work well for cover.
• Hardscape: Use rounded stones, driftwood, and crushed coral or aragonite to help maintain a higher pH and hardness.
• Botanicals: Our Aquatic Arts Botanical Combo Pack is a great starting point. For Liberty Mollies, we also recommend cinnamon bark, loquat leaves, and terminalia arjuna leaves, which provide tannins, stimulate natural grazing behavior, and promote a healthy slime coat. These botanicals also help enrich the water with minerals beneficial to livebearers.

Diet and Feeding:

Liberty Mollies are omnivores with a strong appetite for both plant matter and protein. Feed them a varied diet rich in fiber and nutrients. We recommend Ultra Fresh Vegetables Shrimp Pie as an excellent staple—it’s packed with seaweed, vegetables, and shrimp, perfect for mollies. Supplement with live or frozen daphnia, spirulina, or blanched vegetables like zucchini.

Tank Mates and Compatibility:

Liberty Mollies are semi-aggressive compared to most mollies and may nip at slower or long-finned tank mates. They do best with other robust species such as Swordtails, Platies, larger Tetras, and fast-moving Rasboras. Avoid keeping them with timid or slow-moving fish.

Not Shrimp Safe:

Caution advised. Liberty Mollies may harass or eat small or baby shrimp. Adult Neocaridina shrimp may be safe in large, well-planted tanks with hiding places.

Breeding:

These fish are livebearers and will reproduce regularly in the home aquarium. For best results, keep one male with two or more females and provide ample plant cover or botanicals where fry can hide. Separation tanks or breeder boxes can help increase fry survival rates.

Recommended Tank Parameters:

• Temperature: 72° - 80° F (22° - 27° C)
• pH: 7.5 - 8.5
• KH: 10 - 20 dKH
• Minimum Tank Size: 20 gallons (larger if keeping in groups)

Care Guidelines:

• Diet: Omnivorous – feed a balanced mix of veggie-rich and protein foods.
• Social Behavior: Semi-aggressive; active swimmers.
• Origin: Locally bred (USA)
• Average Adult Size: 2.5 – 3 inches (6.3 – 7.6 cm)
• Average Purchase Size: 1.5 – 2 inches (3.8 – 5 cm)

Add bold color and personality to your tank with Liberty Mollies – Locally Bred. These energetic livebearers offer a vibrant alternative to standard mollies and thrive in a properly maintained setup with just a touch of extra attitude.

Live Arrival Guarantee:
All Aquatic Arts brand animals and plants come with a 100% live arrival guarantee, plus free support from our customer service team.

products.title: Green Hornet Endler Guppy (Poecilia wingei) – Locally Bred
products.body_html:
For the safety of our animals, we now ship live fish exclusively via UPS 1 Day Service. If your order contains this item, you will only see UPS 1 Day as a shipping option during checkout.

Vibrant, hardy, and endlessly active, the Green Hornet Endler Guppy is a spectacular addition to nano and community aquariums alike. Known for its shimmering emerald body and high-contrast black and yellow markings, this variety brings electric color and nonstop motion to any aquascape. Available as individual males, females, or breeding pairs, these tank-bred gems are perfect for beginners and seasoned hobbyists alike. Born and raised in the USA!

What We Love About This Fish:

• Stunning Coloration: Males feature metallic green bodies with deep black and yellow patterning—truly unique among Endlers.
• Tank-Bred Hardiness: These guppies are bred for adaptability and thrive in a range of freshwater conditions.
• Perfect for Breeding Projects: Pairs or groups readily reproduce with proper care.
• Peaceful and Active: Great for planted and community tanks; always on the move.

Natural Habitat:

The wild ancestors of Endler Guppies originate from small warm ponds and slow-moving streams in Venezuela. They thrive in shallow, plant-filled waters rich in leaf litter and detritus.

Aquascaping:

• Plants: Use Java Moss, Hornwort, Water Sprite, and floating plants like Salvinia or Frogbit for fry cover and natural aesthetics.
• Hardscape: Driftwood branches and rounded river stones mimic the shallow streambeds Endlers love.
• Botanicals: We recommend our Aquatic Arts Botanical Combo Pack, which includes catappa leaves, alder cones, loquat leaves, and other enriching botanicals. You can also supplement with items like cinnamon bark, guava leaves, jackfruit pods, or moringa leaves. These natural materials release tannins, promote biofilm growth, offer shelter for fry, and help mimic a more natural and enriching environment.

Diet and Feeding:

Endler Guppies are omnivorous and should be fed a varied diet to promote vibrant coloration and breeding activity. A great daily staple is Ultra Fresh Royal Guppy Mignon Pellets – Shrimp Delight Formula, designed for small tropical fish. Supplement with live or frozen daphnia, baby brine shrimp, or finely crushed flake for variety.

Tank Mates and Compatibility:

These guppies are peaceful and do well with other nano fish like Ember Tetras, Pygmy Corydoras, Celestial Pearl Danios, and peaceful invertebrates. Avoid housing them with fin-nippers or large, aggressive species.

Shrimp Safe:

Safe with adult Neocaridina and Caridina shrimp. Fry may be at risk, so provide moss and hiding spaces for baby shrimp.

Breeding:

Green Hornet Endlers breed readily in well-maintained aquariums. Females give birth to live fry every 3–4 weeks. For best results, maintain a ratio of 1 male to 2–3 females and provide ample plant cover and botanicals for fry survival.

Recommended Tank Parameters:

• Temperature: 72° - 82° F (22° - 28° C)
• pH: 6.8 - 7.8
• KH: 8 - 12 dKH
• Minimum Tank Size: 5 gallons (larger recommended for breeding groups)

Care Guidelines:

• Diet: Omnivore – enjoys micro pellets, flake, and live/frozen foods.
• Social Behavior: Very active and peaceful; best kept in groups.
• Origin: Tank-bred (domestically sourced)
• Average Adult Size: 0.8 – 1.2 inches (2 – 3 cm)
• Average Purchase Size: 0.5 – 1 inch (1.3 – 2.5 cm)

Bring your tank to life with the dazzling motion and vibrant hues of the Green Hornet Endler Guppy—a perfect blend of beauty, activity, and personality in a compact, hardy fish.

products.title: Red Wag Platy (Xiphophorus maculatus) - Locally Bred!
products.body_html:
* For the safety of our animals, we now ship live fish exclusively via UPS Next Day Air.  If your order contains this item, you will only see UPS Next Day Air as a shipping option during checkout *

The Reg Wag Platy is an all-time classic that is colorful, peaceful, and very hardy!


The Red Wag Platy (Xiphophorus maculatus) is a classic platy variety that displays red-orange and black coloration. There are nearly endless color (and other) variants of this fish, and the Red Wag Platy is a standout. This is a very peaceful, undemanding fish that will thrive in most aquariums, especially the planted aquarium.  
 
The Red Wag Platy is compatible with most other peaceful aquarium species.  It is an omnivore, so it will potentially prey on smaller dwarf shrimp and their fry, but it is otherwise safe to keep with peaceful invertebrates as well. This fish is not a picky eater and it will accept a variety of high-quality dry foods, but these should be supplemented with a variety of frozen foods as well. It will mostly occupy the middle and upper regions of the aquarium, and it does have the ability to jump out of the water, especially while in the early stages of acclimating to a new aquarium. As a result, a secure lid is highly recommended to ensure that this fish does not jump out of the tank.
 
Plants are not absolutely necessary for the Red Wag Platy, but they will certainly be appreciated. All stem plants, Marimo Balls, and mosses are safe to keep with this fish. The Red Wag Platy is an excellent fish for beginners as well as experts!

A Platy with a Fancy Tail (Variant)

The Red Wag Pin Tail Platy is a unique variant that combines the bold black tail and fins of the classic Red Wag with an elegant, elongated pin tail shape. This locally bred beauty adds an extra touch of flair to community aquariums, with its active personality and striking contrast of red and black. Like all platies, it's hardy, peaceful, and perfect for hobbyists of all levels.


 
What We Like About This Fish:

  • Hardy and adaptable
  • Beautiful orange and black coloration
  • Small size, perfect for planted aquariums
  • Peaceful disposition
  • Perfect for the planted aquarium

 
RECOMMENDED TANK PARAMETERS:
  • Temperature:  64° - 77° F (17.7° - 25° C)
  • pH:  7.0 - 8.2, though a maximum of 7.5 is ideal
  • KH:  10 - 25
  • Minimum tank size:  10 gallons

 
CARE GUIDELINES:
  • Diet:  High quality flake foods and frozen foods such as bloodworms
  • Social behavior:  Shoaling, peaceful with most species.  
  • Origin:  Tank-bred, but indigenous to Central and South America
  • Average adult size:  2 - 2.8 inches (5 - 7.1 cm).  Females are larger than males.
  • Average purchase size:  .5+ inches (1.27+ cm)

products.title: Platinum Green Tiger Barb (Puntigrus tetrazona) – Tank Bred
products.body_html:
For the safety of our animals, we now ship live fish exclusively via UPS Next Day Air. If your order contains this item, you will only see UPS Next Day Air as a shipping option during checkout.

The Platinum Green Tiger Barb is a stunning variation of the classic Tiger Barb, displaying a shimmering green metallic sheen layered over bold vertical stripes. These tank-bred fish are eye-catching, energetic, and thrive in active community aquariums. Their schooling behavior and vibrant coloration make them a dynamic centerpiece in any freshwater setup.

What We Love About This Fish:

• Unique Metallic Green Coloration: Iridescent sheen adds brilliant contrast to planted tanks.
• Lively Schooling Behavior: Highly active swimmers that shine in groups of 6 or more.
• Great for Community Setups: Best kept with other fast-moving or semi-aggressive species.
• Hardy and Adaptable: Perfect for intermediate aquarists seeking movement and color.

Natural Habitat:

Originally found in slow-moving streams and rivers of Southeast Asia, Tiger Barbs prefer shallow, warm waters with dense vegetation and natural debris. In the wild, they inhabit environments with soft, slightly acidic water and scattered leaf litter.

Aquascaping:

• Plants: Ideal with Java Fern, Cryptocoryne, Vallisneria, and other sturdy plants.
• Hardscape: Use driftwood, river stones, and smooth rocks to create cover and boundaries.
• Botanicals: Add our Aquatic Arts Botanical Combo Pack to introduce natural tannins, create foraging areas, and promote beneficial microfauna. This curated mix supports fish health and mimics their native environment.

Diet and Feeding:

Platinum Green Tiger Barbs are omnivores and require a varied diet for optimal color and health. Offer a staple of high-quality flakes or micro pellets, along with frozen or live foods like bloodworms or daphnia. For maximum nutrition and color enhancement, feed Ultra Fresh Tropical Micro Pellets – Shrimp Delight Formula, specially designed for active tropical fish like barbs.

Tank Mates and Compatibility:

Compatible with other semi-aggressive fish of similar size and temperament. Avoid slow-moving or long-finned species. Best results are seen when kept in schools of 6 or more to reduce nipping and establish natural social behavior.

Not Shrimp Safe:

Not recommended with small or baby shrimp, as Tiger Barbs may nip at them. Larger adult shrimp in heavily planted tanks with ample hiding spots may coexist with caution.

Breeding:

Breeding is possible in a separate tank with fine-leaved plants or spawning mops. Remove adults after spawning to protect eggs, as they may eat them.

Recommended Tank Parameters:

• Temperature: 72° - 79° F (22° - 26° C)
• pH: 6.8 - 7.5
• KH: 3 - 10 dKH
• Minimum Tank Size: 20 gallons

Care Guidelines:

• Diet: Omnivorous; variety of dry, frozen, and live foods recommended
• Social Behavior: Active, schooling fish; keep in groups of 6 or more
• Origin: Tank-bred
• Average Adult Size: 2 – 2.5 inches (5 – 6.3 cm)
• Average Purchase Size: 1 – 1.5 inches (2.5 – 3.8 cm)

Want to liven up your tank with movement and shine?
The Platinum Green Tiger Barb delivers striking color and energy to the midwater level of your aquarium. Add a group today and watch the shimmer and schooling action take over!

products.title: Tiger Swordtail (Xiphophorus hellerii) – Tank Bred
products.body_html:
For the safety of our animals, we now ship live fish exclusively via UPS Next Day Air. If your order contains this item, you will only see UPS Next Day Air as a shipping option during checkout.

The Tiger Swordtail is a bold, eye-catching variant of the classic Swordtail. Its striking vertical striping resembles that of a tiger, set against a backdrop of orange, gold, or cream tones. Swordtails are named for the elongated lower lobe of the male’s tail fin, giving them a unique and elegant silhouette. This tank-bred strain is both hardy and active, making it ideal for beginner and experienced aquarists alike.

What We Love About This Fish:

• Unique tiger-striped pattern with vibrant contrasting colors
• Peaceful and social, great for community tanks
• Very hardy and adaptable to a wide range of conditions
• Active swimmers that thrive in planted aquariums

Natural Habitat:

Native to Central America, wild Swordtails are found in streams, rivers, and pools with clear to slightly turbid water. They inhabit areas rich in aquatic vegetation, with moderate current and abundant cover from predators.

Aquascaping:

• Plants: Vallisneria, Bacopa, Hornwort, Amazon Sword, and Java Fern all provide excellent cover and help replicate the Swordtail’s natural habitat.
• Hardscape: Incorporate river rocks, smooth stones, and driftwood to create hiding spots and soft boundaries.
• Botanicals: Add leaf litter or Indian almond leaves for tannin release and environmental enrichment. We recommend our Aquatic Arts Botanical Combo Pack, which includes a curated mix of leaves, seed pods, and driftwood pieces perfect for Swordtails and other livebearers.

Diet and Feeding:

Tiger Swordtails are omnivores that benefit from a varied diet rich in both protein and plant matter. A staple food should be supplemented with occasional treats.

Recommended food:
Ultra Fresh Royal Guppy Mignon Pellets – Shrimp Delight Formula – an excellent choice for Swordtails, providing a balanced mix of seafood proteins and plant-based nutrients to enhance coloration and health. These micro pellets are perfectly sized and easy to digest, making them ideal for daily feeding.

Tank Mates and Compatibility:

Swordtails are peaceful but active, making them suitable for community tanks with other non-aggressive fish such as Tetras, Corydoras, Mollies, Platies, and Rasboras. Avoid housing with fin-nippers or overly aggressive species.

Maybe Shrimp Safe:

Generally safe with adult shrimp. Fry may be at risk, so provide adequate plant cover if cohabiting with breeding shrimp colonies.

Breeding:

Livebearers by nature, Swordtails breed readily in home aquariums. Females give birth to free-swimming fry after a gestation of about 28 days. Provide floating plants or dense cover to protect fry from adult fish.

Recommended Tank Parameters:

• Temperature: 72° - 82° F (22° - 28° C)
• pH: 7.0 - 8.3
• KH: 8 - 20 dKH
• Minimum Tank Size: 20 gallons

Care Guidelines:

• Diet: Omnivorous. Accepts flake food, micro pellets, blanched vegetables, and live/frozen treats.
• Social Behavior: Peaceful and social. Best kept in groups or a ratio of 1 male to 2+ females.
• Origin: Tank-Bred
• Average Adult Size: 3 – 4 inches (7.6 – 10.2 cm)
• Average Purchase Size: 1.25 – 2 inches (3.2 – 5 cm)

Add a Splash of Bold Personality to Your Aquarium!
With their striking stripes, peaceful nature, and hardy disposition, Tiger Swordtails are a fantastic addition to any well-maintained freshwater setup.

products.body_html:

Our Premium Assorted Mollies are lively, colorful, and perfect for any peaceful freshwater setup. Mollies are popular for their ease of care, bold personality, and wide range of patterns and colors. This assorted pack includes healthy, tank-bred individuals from a rotating mix of varieties such as black, dalmatian, gold dust, marble, and more — offering a beautiful contrast of shapes and shades in every group.

What We Love About These Fish:

• Incredible Variety: Each group features a vibrant mix of color and fin types.
• Easy to Care For: Hardy and adaptable, making them ideal for beginners and experienced hobbyists alike.
• Peaceful and Active: Great additions to planted and community tanks.
• Livebearers: Fascinating to observe as they may give birth to fry in the aquarium.

Natural Habitat:

Mollies are native to coastal streams and brackish estuaries in Central and North America. In aquariums, they do well in freshwater and can tolerate low levels of salinity.

Aquascaping:

• Plants: Enjoys heavily planted tanks with floating and midground cover (Hornwort, Vallisneria, Anubias).
• Hardscape: Use smooth stones or driftwood to create gentle structure and swimming corridors.
• Botanicals: Indian almond leaves can support coloration and overall fish health.

Diet and Feeding:

Mollies are omnivorous with a strong appetite for both plant and protein-based foods. Offer high-quality flakes, spirulina-based pellets, and occasional treats like frozen daphnia or bloodworms. Blanched veggies like zucchini or spinach are also appreciated.

Tank Mates and Compatibility:

Mollies are peaceful community fish and do well with tetras, corydoras, swordtails, platies, guppies, and rasboras. Males may sometimes show mild aggression; keeping a ratio of 2-3 females per male helps reduce this behavior.

Shrimp Safe?

Generally safe with adult shrimp. May nip at tiny or newly molted shrimp if underfed or overcrowded. Providing moss or hiding spaces helps protect shrimp fry.

Breeding:

Mollies are livebearers and breed readily in home aquariums. Females give birth to live fry approximately every 4-6 weeks. Fry can be raised in the main tank if dense plant cover or moss is available for shelter.

Recommended Tank Parameters:

• Temperature: 74° – 82° F (23° – 28° C)
• pH: 7.5 – 8.5
• KH: 10 – 25 dKH
• Minimum Tank Size: 20 gallons

Care Guidelines:

• Diet: Omnivorous; enjoys algae and supplemental vegetable matter
• Social Behavior: Peaceful and active; best kept in groups
• Origin: Tank-bred
• Average Adult Size: 3 – 4.5 inches (7.6 – 11.4 cm)
• Average Purchase Size: 1.5 – 2.5 inches (3.8 – 6.3 cm)

Add Color, Personality, and Life to Your Tank

Whether you’re new to the hobby or adding to a thriving community tank, Premium Assorted Mollies are a reliable and rewarding choice. With their bold colors and energetic personalities, they bring constant movement and charm to any aquarium.

products.body_html:
For the safety of our animals, we now ship live fish exclusively via UPS Next Day Air. If your order contains this item, you will only see UPS Next Day Air as a shipping option during checkout.

The Two-Striped Dwarf Cichlid is one of the most striking members of the Apistogramma genus, known for its iridescent body coloration and bold double-stripe pattern. Native to the soft, tannin-rich waters of the Amazon basin, this stunning dwarf cichlid displays radiant hues of blue, yellow, and red depending on its locality. Our specimens are tank-bred and adapted to aquarium life, making them more hardy and beginner-friendly than their wild-caught counterparts.

What We Love About This Fish:

• Dazzling Coloration: Males in particular show brilliant electric blues, shimmering golds, and bold black stripes.
• Peaceful Personality (for a cichlid): Can be kept in peaceful community aquariums with proper tankmates.
• Small Size, Big Presence: Their confident behavior and vivid appearance make them ideal showpiece fish for smaller tanks.
• Natural Breeding Behavior: Fascinating to watch during spawning—especially the female’s parenting display.

Natural Habitat:

Found in slow-moving streams and floodplain pools in Peru and Brazil, the Two-Striped Dwarf Cichlid thrives in warm, soft, acidic water rich in leaf litter and submerged branches. Their environment is often shaded and tinted brown with tannins from decaying plant matter.

Aquascaping:

• Plants: Floating plants like Frogbit or Water Lettuce help diffuse light. Rooted plants like Amazon Sword, Cryptocoryne, and Java Fern make ideal cover.
• Hardscape: Include driftwood, coconut hides, and caves for territory and breeding.
• Botanicals: Add Indian Almond Leaves, Catappa Bark, and alder cones for tannins and natural shelter.

Diet and Feeding:

These omnivores do best with a high-protein diet. Offer quality sinking pellets, frozen foods (bloodworms, brine shrimp, daphnia), and live treats like blackworms for optimal health and coloration.

Tank Mates and Compatibility:

Compatible with peaceful mid- to upper-level fish like pencilfish, small tetras, hatchetfish, and Corydoras. Avoid boisterous or fin-nipping species, and ensure plenty of space if housing multiple Apistogrammas to avoid territorial disputes.

Shrimp Safe:

Generally safe with adult shrimp, though smaller shrimp or fry may be preyed upon. Provide ample cover if keeping with dwarf shrimp.

Breeding:

A cave-spawner and attentive parent, the female typically guards the eggs and fry while the male patrols the territory. Slightly lower pH and warm temperatures encourage breeding. Conditioning with live food can help trigger spawning.

Recommended Tank Parameters:

• Temperature: 76° – 84° F (24° – 29° C)
• pH: 5.5 – 7.0
• KH: 0 – 4 dKH
• Minimum Tank Size: 20 gallons (for a pair or harem)

Care Guidelines:

• Diet: Omnivorous—requires a variety of high-protein foods.
• Social Behavior: Semi-aggressive when breeding; best kept as a pair or a male with 2 females in larger tanks.
• Origin: Tank-bred.
• Average Adult Size: 2.5 – 3 inches (6.3 – 7.6 cm)
• Average Purchase Size: 1.25 – 1.75 inches (3.2 – 4.4 cm)

Looking for a centerpiece fish that dazzles with both beauty and personality? The Two-Striped Dwarf Cichlid brings Amazonian flair and rewarding behavior to any well-planned setup.

products.title: Anubias Nangi (Anubias barteri "Nana" x Anubias gilletii)
products.body_html:
This interesting Anubias hybrid is not as widely known as some of its relatives, but it is a superb aquarium plant!

Anubias Nangi (Anubias barteri “Nana” x Anubias gilletii) is an Anubias hybrid that was developed decades ago but is not extremely well-known in the aquarium hobby. It has relatively long and pointed leavese, which set it apart from many more common Anubias plants. Like virtually all Anubias plants, it is extremely hardy, undemanding, and can thrive in a variety of water parameters.

Care for Anubias Nangi is identical to other Anubias plants. It is best placed in the midground and will look its best under low to moderate lighting. Excessive lighting can result in unattractive and pesky algae growth on its leaves. However, this algae growth can easily be kept under control in an aquarium with a healthy population of shrimp (especially Amano or Short Nose Shrimp), snails, and/or algae-eating fish that will constantly graze on the algae without harming the Anubias plant itself. Most herbivorous animals will not harm Anubias Nangi whatsoever, although some bio-fim/”wood-eating” Panaque genus plecos may “chew” holes in its leaves. Like many Anubias species, this plant is slow and steady in growth and does not seem to respond significantly to CO2 supplementation. It thrives best when attached to driftwood or rockwork. It can be planted in the substrate but its rhizome (roots) must not be buried in the substrate or it will rot and recede. It can also be planted emersed in terrariums, paludariums, and viquariums.  

No aquarium plant distributor/nursery/wholesaler/retailer can or will guarantee their plants to be 100% snail free. We can not guarantee our plants will be snail free.

What We Like About This Plant:

  • Unique Anubias hybrid that is not as known as its relatives
  • Excellent for the low-light aquarium midground
  • Extremely versatile and durable
  • Thrives when tied/attached to aquarium driftwood and rockwork
  • Requires low to moderate lighting and little to no supplementation in nutrient-rich water

 

Care Guidelines:

  • Temperature: 72° - 80° F (22° - 27° C)
  • pH: 5.5 - 9.0
  • Lighting: Low to moderate
  • Origin: Lab-grown tissue culture, but indigenous to Africa
  • Aquarium placement: Midground
  • Care: Easy

products.title: Crossband Chocolate Gourami (Sphaerichthys selatanensis) – Tank Bred
products.body_html:
For the safety of our animals, we now ship live fish exclusively via UPS Next Day Air. If your order contains this item, you will only see UPS Next Day Air as a shipping option during checkout.

The Crossband Chocolate Gourami is a rare and captivating species native to the soft, acidic peat swamps of southern Borneo. With its deep chocolate coloration accented by subtle vertical crossbands and delicate iridescence, this shy anabantoid is a true gem for the experienced aquarist. Unlike the more well-known Chocolate Gourami (Sphaerichthys osphromenoides), this species exhibits a more robust body and distinctive crossband patterning.

Live Foods are great enrichment and what your fish crave! Try our Live Daphnia to give your fish a natural food source today!

What We Love About This Fish:

• Unique Appearance: Rich chocolate brown coloration with elegant vertical crossbands and subtle shimmer.
• Peaceful Temperament: Ideal for calm, low-stress community tanks with similarly gentle species.
• Labyrinth Organ: Like all gouramis, they can breathe atmospheric air and sometimes “sip” from the surface.
• Mouthbrooder Behavior: Males incubate fertilized eggs in their mouths—fascinating to observe during breeding.

Natural Habitat:

This species inhabits slow-moving, tannin-stained waters of blackwater streams in Indonesia. The environment is typically acidic, densely vegetated, and shaded by forest canopy with abundant leaf litter.

Aquascaping:

• Plants: Floating plants (like Frogbit or Salvinia) and soft, dense species such as Cryptocoryne, Java Fern, and Anubias.
• Hardscape: Driftwood branches, root tangles, and coconut huts provide essential cover.
• Botanicals: Indian Almond Leaves, guava leaves, or catappa bark help recreate a blackwater environment and provide tannins for fish health.

Diet and Feeding:

Carnivorous by nature. Offer a varied diet of frozen foods (daphnia, cyclops, bloodworms), micro-pellets, and live food. May be picky eaters initially—acclimate with high-quality live or frozen fare.

Tank Mates and Compatibility:

Should be housed only with extremely peaceful species such as small rasboras, chili or green neon tetras, pygmy Corydoras, or other gentle Southeast Asian fish. Avoid fast-moving, aggressive, or competitive feeders.

Shrimp Safe:

Generally safe with adult dwarf shrimp, though smaller shrimp and fry may be at risk. Dense plants and botanicals help provide safe zones.

Breeding:

A paternal mouthbrooder. Males carry fertilized eggs for 10–20 days until fry are released. Soft, acidic water with low lighting and minimal disturbance can encourage breeding. Pair bonding and careful conditioning are essential.

Recommended Tank Parameters:

• Temperature: 75° – 82° F (24° – 28° C)
• pH: 4.0 – 6.5
• KH: 0 – 2 dKH
• Minimum Tank Size: 10 gallons for a pair (20+ gallons for a group)

Care Guidelines:

• Diet: Carnivorous. Prefers live or frozen foods; supplement with high-protein micro pellets.
• Social Behavior: Peaceful and shy. Best kept in small groups with plenty of cover.
• Origin: Tank-bred or wild-collected (specify based on your stock).
• Average Adult Size: 1.5 – 2 inches (3.8 – 5 cm)
• Average Purchase Size: 1 – 1.25 inches (2.5 – 3.2 cm)

Looking to add a true rarity to your blackwater or Southeast Asian biotope tank? The Crossband Chocolate Gourami brings elegance, calm, and a glimpse into unique anabantoid behaviors.

products.title: Madame Wilfron Gonnere Hardy Lily (Nymphaea sp.) plant with bulb
products.body_html:
Madame Wilfron Gonnere Hardy Lily is a very lovely and compact-growing plant that is excellent for small to medium ponds!

 

Madame Wilfron Gonnere Hardy Lily (Nymphaea sp.) is a small to medium hardy lily that is excellent for small to medium ponds. It can also be used in larger ponds as long as they are not too deep, but it will not grow to a larger size with more space. This lily is also known for its remarkable pink blooms.

(No aquarium plant distributor/nursery/wholesaler/retailer, can or will guarantee their plants to be 100% snail free, we can not guarantee our plants will be snail free)

 

***PLEASE NOTE - This plant can sometimes arrive as a bulb with no leaves attached. This is normal, and in most cases, new leaves will sprout within a few weeks. If your lily bulb loses its leaves upon arrival, please plant it as usual if the bulb is still firm and healthy. If it does not begin to re-sprout, please contact us and we will honor our live arrival guarantee and replace it or credit you!

 

What We Like About This Plant:

  • Incredible pink blooms
  • Hardy and adaptable in most pond settings
  • Grows to impressive size
  • Colorful foliage
  • Undemanding in care

 

Care Guidelines:

  • Planting Depth: Initially submerge in 6 - 18 inches of water. As the plant grows, depth can be increased to as much as 30" (except for dwarf varieties, which must be kept at 10 - 12 inches depth).
  • Exposure: Full sun
  • Average Petals Per Bloom: 38 - 40
  • Bloom Diameter: 5 - 6 inches
  • Mature Plant Spread: 3 - 4 feet
  • USDA Hardiness Zones: 4 - 11
  • Planting Instructions: Lilies prefer to be planted near the top of the potting soil. Fill an 8-inch or larger pot or planting bag roughly two-thirds full with a sand/heavy soil mix. Make a mound in the center of the pot with a handful of soil. Place the root or rhizome on the mound and spread the roots out over the mound. Use soil to cover the plant roots, but do not cover the crown of the bulb. Only top off the pot with lava rock or gravel if necessary to prevent fish from uprooting the lily.

• Ideal for Breeding Projects: Perfect for guppy enthusiasts looking to expand their collection.

Aquascaping:

Black Metal Snakeskin Guppies thrive in well-planted aquariums that replicate their natural habitat. Suggested plants include:


• Java Moss: Provides cover and a safe space for fry.
• Hornwort: Perfect for background planting and adding vertical interest.
• Anubias and Amazon Swords: Low-maintenance plants that enhance the aquarium’s aesthetics.
• Floating Plants (Duckweed, Frogbit): Offer shade and additional cover, creating a natural look.

Not Shrimp Safe:

These guppies are generally peaceful and compatible with larger shrimp species. However, smaller shrimp or fry may be seen as food. Adding hiding spots with dense plants or decorations can promote harmony.

 

Diet:

Black Metal Snakeskin Guppies are omnivores and flourish with a varied diet:

  • Staple Diet: High-quality flake food or micro pellets.
  • Treats: Live or frozen foods such as brine shrimp, daphnia, and bloodworms.
  • Vegetables: Occasional blanched vegetables like zucchini or spinach.

A varied diet helps maintain vibrant coloration and optimal health.

Botanicals:

Incorporating botanicals such as Indian Almond Leaves, Catappa Bark, and Alder Cones enhances the tank environment for Black Metal Snakeskin Guppies. These botanicals release tannins and humic substances, supporting natural health and vibrant coloration while mimicking their native habitat.

Recommended Tank Parameters:

• Temperature: 72° - 82°F (22° - 28°C)
• pH: 6.8 - 7.8
• KH: 8 - 12 dKH
• Minimum Tank Size: 10 gallons

Care Guidelines:

• Diet: Omnivorous. Will easily accept high-quality dry and frozen foods.
• Social Behavior: Peaceful and best kept in groups.
• Origin: Bred by Aquatic Arts.
• Average Adult Size: 1.5 - 2 inches (3.8 - 5 cm)
• Average Purchase Size: 1 - 1.5 inches (2.5 - 3.8 cm)

products.title: Lloydiella AKA Creeping Jenny (Lysimachia nummularia), bunch
products.body_html:
Lloydiella is a very hardy stem plant that has bright green leaves and is perfect for low-tech setups!

 

Creeping Jenny AKA Lloydiella (Lysimachia nummularia ) is a very hardy, attractive stem plant with bright green leaves. It can thrive in low-tech setups as long as it has moderate to (preferably) high lighting. As it matures, it can grow to an impressive height that is very suitable for the background of the aquarium. While Creeping Jenny naturally grows emersed, it is very adaptable to long-term submersed conditions. In addition to being an excellent aquarium plant, it is also ideal for paludariums and terrariums.

 Lloydiella is an easy plant to maintain. It does not require CO2 supplementation, though it will certainly benefit from it. It will look its best under moderate to high lighting and will be sturdiest under high lighting. Due to the thin texture of its leaves, Lloydiella is not suitable for aquariums with destructive animals such as crayfish, large cichlids, or goldfish.

 

Like most plants, Lloydiella will benefit from supplementation such as Seachem Flourish, Flourish Excel, nitrogen, and other plant supplements. It actually requires a relatively high level of nutrients and will begin to yellow if it is undernourished. However, it is a rather resilient plant and its high nutrient consumption will contribute greatly to limiting algae growth. 

What We Like About This Plant: 

  • Bright green coloration
  • Prolific growth that is ideal for the aquarium background or use in paludarium/terrarium 
  • Very easy to propagate
  • Provides a great deal of cover for animal fry
  • Very hardy and durable in a well-lit, nutrient-rich aquarium

 

Care Guidelines:

  • Temperature: 59° - 79° F (16° - 26° C)
  • pH: 6.0 - 8.0
  • Lighting: Moderate to High
  • Origin: Indigenous to temperate Asia and Europe, cultivated in US nurseries
  • Aquarium placement: Midground, Background
  • Care: Easy

 

All Aquatic Arts brand plants and animals come with a 100% live arrival guarantee, plus free email support!

products.title: Tropical Water Lily - Nymphaea Emerald (Blue) – Tuber
products.body_html:
Bring vibrant color and natural elegance to your outdoor pond with the Nymphaea Emerald (Blue) Tropical Water Lily. Offered as a dormant tuber, this lily is ready to be planted in soil and nurtured into one of the most stunning blue-blooming tropical lilies available. With proper care, this plant produces large, fragrant blooms that open in the day and close at night, floating beautifully among its lush green leaves.

Tropical water lilies thrive in full sun and warm water and are perfect for adding striking color and surface coverage to garden ponds, water features, and outdoor container setups.

Features We Love:

  • Brilliant Blue Blooms: Produces vibrant, sky-blue flowers with a tropical flair.

  • Fragrant & Day-Blooming: Opens during daylight hours and emits a soft floral scent.

  • Ideal for Outdoor Ponds: Thrives in warm water and sunlit environments.

  • Easy to Grow: Sold as a tuber, simply plant in soil under shallow water and let it develop naturally.

Care Guidelines:

  • Light: Full sun (6+ hours per day)

  • Temperature: 70°F and up (21°C+); best when water temperatures are consistently warm

  • Planting Instructions: Plant the tuber in a wide container filled with aquatic soil. Submerge the pot so the soil is just below the water surface, about 6–12 inches deep.

  • Growth Rate: Moderate to fast once established

  • Placement: Floating-leaf plant best suited for medium to large ponds or water gardens

🌿 Pro Tip: Pond Placement Tips

  • Use a large, shallow planting container filled with aquatic planting media (not standard garden soil).

  • Do not bury the entire tuber—leave the growing tip exposed just above the soil line.

  • Place the container in the warmest, sunniest spot of your pond to promote vigorous growth and faster blooming.

  • Avoid disturbing the water surface too much in the area where blooms are expected for optimal flower display.

Enhance your water garden with the unmatched beauty of Tropical Water Lily - Nymphaea Emerald (Blue) and enjoy the serene elegance it brings all season long.

100% Live Arrival Guarantee

All Aquatic Arts brand plants come with a 100% live arrival guarantee, plus free email support! If your lily tuber doesn't arrive healthy and viable, we'll make it right.

products.title: Golden Melon Sword Plant (echinodorous Osiris)
products.body_html:
The Golden Melon Sword Plant, Echinodorus osiris, is a vibrant and bold centerpiece for any freshwater aquarium. Known for its striking golden to light green leaves with warm orange-red undertones, this South American native is a favorite for both beginners and advanced aquascapers. With the right conditions, its long, broad leaves can add dramatic vertical structure and stunning contrast to your aquascape.

Choose from:

  • Bareroot: A loose plant weighted with roots ready to be placed into substrate. 

  • Potted: Grown in a sturdy pot for easy placement and minimal disturbance to your substrate.

Features We Love:

  • Warm Color Palette: Ranges from bright lime green to bronze-gold tones, especially under high light.

  • Hardy and Adaptable: Thrives in a variety of tank setups and lighting conditions.

  • Excellent Centerpiece: Ideal for midground or background placement in medium to large tanks.

  • Great for Shrimp and Fish: Broad leaves offer shelter and biofilm-grazing surfaces.

Care Guidelines:

  • Light: Moderate to high lighting brings out best coloration.

  • Temperature: 72° – 82°F (22° – 28°C)

  • pH Range: 6.5 – 7.5

  • Growth Rate: Moderate to fast with adequate lighting and nutrients

  • Ideal Placement: Midground to background

Pro Tips:

  • Prune regularly to encourage new growth and prevent overcrowding.

  • Supplement with root tabs or nutrient-rich substrate to support its extensive root system.

  • Co2 is optional but will dramatically enhance coloration and growth speed.

  • Avoid shading from taller plants to preserve leaf color vibrancy.

  • Perfect for natural-style aquascapes with driftwood and swords or stem plant companions.

Bring a golden glow to your aquascape with the Golden Melon Sword Plant, available in your choice of bunched or potted form—an easy, elegant way to elevate any planted aquarium.

products.title: Macculloch's Dwarf Rainbowfish (Melanotaenia maccullochi) - Tank-Bred!
products.body_html:
* For the safety of our animals, we now ship live fish exclusively via UPS Next Day Air.  If your order contains this item, you will only see UPS Next Day Air as a shipping option during checkout * 

﻿This uncommon dwarf rainbowfish species adds a splash of brilliant color to any aquarium!

Macculloch's Dwarf Rainbowfish (Melanotaenia maccullochi) is a more uncommon, but very colorful dwarf rainbowfish species.  It lives in very large schools in areas with dense vegetation, so it is an ideal candidate for the planted aquarium and appreciates floating plants in its habitat.  As an adult, this rainbowfish's body features iridescent scales, accented with bright red fins.  As with most rainbowfish, mature males display the brightest coloration, particularly while competing/posturing with other males.  Females also possess the same attractive coloration, but to a lesser extent. 

Macculloch's Dwarf Rainbowfish is generally peaceful with other fish species of similar size, but smaller shrimp fry may fall prey.  Its mouth and throat are relatively small for its body size, so it might be possible to keep it with adult dwarf shrimp.  All other invertebrates (including larger Amano, Bamboo, and Vampire Shrimp) should be compatible with no problems.  

The most intense color will be displayed when this fish kept in schools of 6 or more (including multiple males) in a heavily planted, spacious aquarium to ensure that subdominant males are not overly harassed by dominant males.  As with many rainbowfish, darker substrate will also often result in better coloration. Macculloch's Dwarf Rainbowfish is very easy to feed and will accept most high quality dry foods, but also requires various frozen and live foods in its diet for optimal health and coloration.

 

What We Like About This Fish:
  • Active, schooling community fish that thrives in planted aquariums
  • Compatible with many invertebrates
  • Adults display brilliant red and blue coloration
  • Active and easy to feed
 
RECOMMENDED TANK PARAMETERS:
  • Temperature:  68° - 86° F (20° - 30° C), although 72° - 82° F is optimal
  • pH:  5.5 - 80
  • KH:  8 - 15 dKH
  • Minimum tank size:  20 gallons
 
CARE GUIDELINES: 
  • Diet:  Omnivorous.  High quality dry foods will be accepted, but should be supplemented often with small, meaty frozen and live foods.
  • Social behavior:  Schooling/shoaling, should be kept in as large of groups as possible. Dominant males may attack or kill subdominant males in small aquariums. Peaceful with most other comparably-sized fish.
  • Origin:  Tank-bred, but indigenous to New Guinea, Indonesia.
  • Average adult size:  2.8 inches (7.1cm)
  • Average purchase size:  .5 - 1 inch (1.2 - 2.5 cm)

products.title: Red Mosaic Guppy (Poecilia reticulata) – Tank Bred
products.body_html:
For the safety of our animals, we now ship live fish exclusively via UPS Next Day Air. If your order contains this item, you will only see UPS Next Day Air as a shipping option during checkout.

The Red Mosaic Guppy is a dazzling tank-bred variety known for its fiery red coloration and intricately patterned tail. Named for its striking “mosaic” pattern of interwoven red and transparent segments, this guppy is a showstopper in planted or community aquariums. These active livebearers are hardy, peaceful, and easy to breed, making them ideal for both beginners and experienced aquarists.

What We Love About This Fish:

  • Eye-Catching Coloration: Intense red body with a flowing, mosaic-patterned caudal fin.

  • Peaceful & Social: Great in community tanks and thrives in groups.

  • Hardy and Adaptable: Excellent for beginners and forgiving of a wide range of water parameters.

  • Easy to Breed: Livebearer species that reproduces readily under the right conditions.

Natural Habitat:

Originally native to slow-moving streams in South America, modern Red Mosaic Guppies are tank-bred and adapted to a wide range of conditions. They thrive in well-filtered aquariums with gentle flow and stable temperatures.

Aquascaping Recommendations:

  • Plants: Java Moss, Anubias, Hornwort, and floating plants like Frogbit help replicate natural cover.

  • Hardscape: Use river stones, driftwood, or ceramic shelters to offer hiding spots.

  • Botanicals: Indian Almond Leaves or Alder Cones can support overall health and water quality.

Diet and Feeding:

Red Mosaic Guppies are omnivorous and should be fed a variety of foods including high-quality flake or micro pellet food, as well as frozen or live treats such as baby brine shrimp, daphnia, or bloodworms to enhance color and vitality.

Tank Mates and Compatibility:

Compatible with most peaceful community fish such as rasboras, small tetras, corydoras, and snails. Avoid fin-nipping species or aggressive tankmates.

Shrimp Safe:

Generally safe with adult shrimp. Small shrimp fry may be at risk unless sufficient cover is provided.

Breeding Information:

These guppies are prolific livebearers. For best results, keep a ratio of 1 male to 2-3 females to reduce stress and encourage breeding. Fry will survive best in a densely planted or mossy tank with fine-leaved plants for cover.

Recommended Tank Parameters:

  • Temperature: 72° – 82° F (22° – 28° C)

  • pH: 6.8 – 7.8

  • KH: 8 – 12 dKH

  • Minimum Tank Size: 5 gallons (10+ gallons recommended for groups)

Care Guidelines:

  • Diet: Omnivorous. Feed a mix of dry, frozen, and live foods.

  • Social Behavior: Peaceful and active; thrives in groups.

  • Origin: Tank Bred

  • Average Adult Size: 1.5 – 2 inches (3.8 – 5 cm)

  • Average Purchase Size: 1 – 1.5 inches (2.5 – 3.8 cm)

Add a burst of bold red energy to your aquarium with the Red Mosaic Guppy—a vibrant, hardy, and endlessly fascinating livebearer!

Live Arrival Guarantee:

products.title: Gold Barb (Puntius semifasciolatus) – Tank Bred
products.body_html:
For the safety of our animals, we now ship live fish exclusively via UPS Next Day Air. If your order contains this item, you will only see UPS Next Day Air as a shipping option during checkout.

The Gold Barb is a peaceful, hardy schooling fish with gleaming metallic golden coloration and a subtly patterned body. Tank-bred specimens like these are ideal for beginner and experienced aquarists alike, offering easy care and vibrant color that pops in any community setup. With their active nature and peaceful demeanor, Gold Barbs are a fantastic addition to planted tanks and social aquariums.

What We Love About This Fish:

  • Brilliant Metallic Coloration: Glowing gold body with green or reddish undertones.

  • Peaceful Nature: Friendly with other peaceful species, perfect for community aquariums.

  • Active Swimmers: Schooling fish that stay active and bring energy to the tank.

  • Hardy and Adaptable: Great for new aquarists or low-maintenance setups.

Natural Habitat:

The wild form of Puntius semifasciolatus originates from freshwater rivers and streams in Southeast Asia. While the wild type is greenish-silver, this beautiful golden morph has been selectively bred in captivity. These tank-bred specimens prefer tanks with moderate current, open swimming space, and vegetation.

Aquascaping Recommendations:

  • Plants: Vallisneria, Java Fern, Water Wisteria, and Anubias are excellent choices for cover and grazing.

  • Hardscape: Smooth stones, driftwood, and caves offer shelter and mimic their natural surroundings.

  • Botanicals: Indian Almond Leaves or Alder Cones are optional but can enrich the environment.

Diet and Feeding:

Gold Barbs are omnivorous and not picky eaters. Offer high-quality flakes or pellets daily, supplemented with frozen foods like bloodworms, brine shrimp, and daphnia. They also enjoy fresh vegetables like blanched spinach or zucchini.

Tank Mates and Compatibility:

Gold Barbs do well in peaceful community tanks. Keep in groups of 5 or more to prevent nipping and encourage natural schooling behavior. Suitable tank mates include other barbs, danios, rasboras, corydoras, rainbowfish, and most livebearers.

Shrimp Safe:

Generally safe with adult shrimp, though they may eat small shrimp fry. Dense planting and mosses can help protect smaller inverts.

Breeding Information:

Egg-scatterers that will readily spawn in the right conditions. Provide fine-leaved plants or spawning mops, and remove adults after spawning to prevent them from eating the eggs.

Recommended Tank Parameters:

  • Temperature: 68° – 77° F (20° – 25° C)

  • pH: 6.5 – 7.5

  • KH: 4 – 10 dKH

  • Minimum Tank Size: 20 gallons

Care Guidelines:

  • Diet: Omnivorous. Balanced diet with high-quality dry and frozen foods.

  • Social Behavior: Schooling; best in groups of 5 or more.

  • Origin: Tank Bred

  • Average Adult Size: 2.5 – 3 inches (6.3 – 7.6 cm)

  • Average Purchase Size: 1 – 1.5 inches (2.5 – 3.8 cm)

Bright, active, and beginner-friendly—the Gold Barb is a golden gem for any peaceful freshwater aquarium.

Looking for a fish that glides through your aquarium like a silver arrow with attitude? The Blackline Tail Tetra (Moenkhausia costae) is a sleek, fast-moving schooling fish with a bold, jet-black stripe running from mid-body through the tail—like a racing stripe on a sports car. This underrated South American native is tank-bred and full of energy, giving your community tank that extra spark of contrast and movement.

Perfect for aquascapes that need motion without chaos, these tetras bring a calm, controlled energy to midwater zones and look especially stunning under soft lighting.

Natural Habitat:

Native to the shaded tributaries and forest streams of South America, where they cruise through leaf-littered waters with plenty of submerged roots and driftwood.

Aquascaping Ideas:

• Plants: Use bright green plants like Vallisneria, Dwarf Sagittaria, or Limnophila to make their silver sheen pop

Sharp stripes. Smooth swimmers.
The Blackline Tail Tetra brings style, flow, and balance to any aquascape. Add this underrated standout to your tank today and watch your setup come alive.

This rare, tank-bred hybrid combines the iridescent, galaxy-like spotting of the Celestial Pearl with the graceful striping and shimmer of the Emerald Dwarf. The result is a small, peaceful schooling fish that glows with personality and vibrant, shimmering detail unlike anything else in your tank. This cross breed has been know to do this naturally in the wild.

products.title: Ceramic Breeder Brick
products.body_html:
Bring naturalistic function and visual appeal to your aquarium with our Ceramic Breeder Bricks. Proudly made in the USA, these handmade ceramic structures are crafted with precision and purpose—ideal for breeding freshwater shrimp, plecos, and other aquatic life that seek shelter and security for reproduction.

Measuring approximately 2 inches wide by 4 inches tall, each brick features a decorative brown finish that blends effortlessly with planted and hardscape aquascapes. Whether you're encouraging hiding behavior, promoting breeding, or simply elevating the look of your tank, these bricks are a functional favorite among aquarists of all levels.

Why We Love These Bricks:

products.title: Golden Dojo Loach (Misgurnus anguillicaudatus) –  Tank-Bred
products.body_html:

The Golden Dojo Loach, a color variant of the Oriental Weatherfish, is a peaceful and highly active bottom-dweller that brings both personality and practical value to your freshwater aquarium. Its golden-yellow coloration and eel-like body make it a standout, while its quirky behavior—including reacting to changes in barometric pressure—adds constant entertainment. These fish are tank-bred and currently 3+ inches in size.

⚠️ Legal Notice: The Oriental Weatherfish aka Dojo Loach (Misgurnus anguillicaudatus) is classified as an invasive species in many U.S. states. We cannot ship this species to the following states due to state regulations:
California, Illinois, Michigan, Minnesota, New York, Wisconsin, Oregon, New Jersey, and Alabama.
Please check your local laws before purchasing. If you reside in one of the above states and try to checkout with this fish in your cart you will be unable to checkout and receive an error message until the fish is removed from the cart.

What We Love About This Fish:

  • Striking Coloration: Bright golden body adds bold contrast to planted tanks.

  • Entertaining Behavior: Known as a “weather loach” for its storm-predicting antics.

  • Peaceful Disposition: A calm bottom-dweller suitable for community tanks.

  • Hardy & Adaptable: Tolerates a wide temperature range and is very beginner-friendly.

Natural Habitat:

Native to East Asia, this species is typically found in slow-moving, heavily vegetated waters with soft, muddy bottoms. In aquariums, they thrive with soft substrate, gentle current, and plenty of hiding spots.

Aquascaping:

• Plants: Java Fern, Anubias, Vallisneria, Hornwort
• Hardscape: Smooth stones, driftwood, fine sand or soft gravel
• Botanicals: Indian Almond Leaves and alder cones help mimic their natural environment

Diet and Feeding:

Golden Dojo Loaches are omnivores and active foragers. They readily accept sinking pellets, Repashy gel food, frozen foods (like bloodworms and brine shrimp), and fresh vegetables such as blanched spinach or zucchini.

Tank Mates and Compatibility:

Peaceful and social, they do best in groups of 3 or more and can be housed with similarly gentle species like danios, rasboras, white clouds, and some goldfish varieties. Avoid aggressive or territorial fish.

Not Shrimp Safe:

May eat shrimp fry or small dwarf shrimp. If cohabiting with shrimp, provide dense plant cover and multiple hiding spots.

Breeding:

Rare in home aquariums. Breeding may require temperature cycling to mimic seasonal changes. They scatter eggs in fine plants or moss.

Recommended Tank Parameters:

• Temperature: 50° – 77°F (10° – 25°C)
• pH: 6.5 – 8.0
• KH: 4 – 12 dKH
• Minimum Tank Size: 30 gallons (larger recommended for groups)

Care Guidelines:

• Diet: Omnivorous – appreciates a varied diet of prepared, frozen, and fresh foods
• Social Behavior: Very social, best kept in groups
• Origin: Tank-Bred
• Average Adult Size: 6 – 8 inches (can exceed 10 inches)
• Average Purchase Size: 2.5+ inches

A splash of gold and a bundle of energy—bring home the Golden Dojo Loach for a bottom-dweller that keeps things moving and fun.

The Dojo Loach, also known as the Weather Loach, is a long-bodied, peaceful bottom-dweller known for its quirky behavior and unique ability to detect changes in barometric pressure. These fascinating fish are extremely hardy and add both movement and character to any cool or temperate freshwater aquarium. These specimens are tank-raised and currently 3+ inches in size.

⚠️ Important Legal Note: The Oriental Weatherfish aka Dojo Loach (Misgurnus anguillicaudatus) is considered an invasive species in several U.S. states and is illegal to possess or sell in: California, Illinois, Michigan, Minnesota, New York, Wisconsin, Oregon, New Jersey, and Alabama. Please check your local regulations before purchasing. We are unable to ship this species to these states. If you reside in one of the above states and try to checkout with this fish in your cart you will be unable to checkout and receive an error message until the fish is removed from the cart.

What We Love About This Fish:

  • Unique Behavior: Responds to changes in atmospheric pressure—earning it the nickname “Weather Loach.”

  • Peaceful Nature: Excellent for community aquariums with other non-aggressive fish.

  • Bottom Cleaner: Constant scavenger that helps manage leftover food and detritus.

  • Cold-Water Hardy: Thrives in a wide range of temperatures, perfect for unheated indoor tanks.

Natural Habitat:

Native to East Asia, the Dojo Loach is found in slow-moving rivers, marshes, and rice paddies with soft, muddy bottoms. In the home aquarium, they prefer gentle flow, soft substrate, and areas of dense vegetation or shaded hiding spots.

Aquascaping:

  • Plants: Java Fern, Anubias, Vallisneria, Hornwort

  • Hardscape: Smooth river stones, driftwood, sand or fine gravel

  • Botanicals: Indian Almond Leaves and alder cones help mimic natural environments and enrich the water with tannins

Diet and Feeding:

Dojo Loaches are omnivores and eager scavengers. Feed them a variety of sinking pellets, Repashy gel foods, frozen bloodworms or brine shrimp, and fresh vegetables like zucchini or blanched spinach.

Tank Mates and Compatibility:

Dojo Loaches are extremely peaceful and do well with other calm community fish such as rasboras, danios, white cloud minnows, and goldfish. Avoid aggressive or overly territorial tankmates.

Not Shrimp Safe:

May coexist with adult shrimp, but they can eat smaller shrimp or fry, especially in less heavily planted tanks. Provide dense plant coverage and hiding spots if mixing with inverts.

Breeding:

Rare in captivity. Breeding typically requires simulated seasonal cues like temperature drops and rises. Eggs are usually scattered among fine-leaved plants or moss.

Recommended Tank Parameters:

  • Temperature: 50° – 77°F (10° – 25°C)

  • pH: 6.5 – 8.0

  • KH: 4 – 12 dKH

  • Minimum Tank Size: 30 gallons (larger recommended for groups)

Care Guidelines:

  • Diet: Omnivorous. Feed a varied diet including frozen, live, and prepared foods.

  • Social Behavior: Very social—best kept in groups of 3 or more.

  • Origin: Tank-Raised

  • Average Adult Size: 6 – 8 inches (can exceed 10 inches in spacious environments)

products.title: Dragon Super Delta Tail Betta (Betta splendens) - Tank Bred
products.body_html:
For the safety of our animals, we now ship live fish exclusively via UPS Next Day Air. If your order contains this item, you will only see UPS Next Day Air as a shipping option during checkout.

With bold, metallic scale patterns and broad, dramatic fins, the Dragon Super Delta Tail Betta is a true showpiece in any nano or desktop aquarium. Available in Male and Female variants, this stunning variety features a signature "dragon" look—thick, armored-looking iridescent scales and elegant tails that fan to nearly 180 degrees when fully flared.

Important Note: Bettas are highly territorial. Male Dragon Super Delta Tails should never be housed with other male bettas, or with female bettas, as this can lead to aggressive fighting. Females may be kept in a sorority (female-only group), but only with proper care, space, and observation.

What We Love About This Fish:

  • Metallic Armor-Like Scales: The dragon trait gives them a striking, reptilian shimmer.

  • Super Delta Tail: A graceful fan-like tail that spreads impressively when flaring.

  • Personality Plus: Bettas are known for being curious, interactive, and intelligent.

  • Perfect for Nano Setups: A single betta can thrive in a well-maintained small tank.

Natural Habitat:

Originally derived from rice paddies and slow-moving streams in Southeast Asia, bettas thrive in calm waters with plenty of hiding spots, plant cover, and open space to explore.

Aquascaping:

  • Plants: Floating plants like Frogbit or Water Lettuce, plus rooted or low-light plants like Anubias, Java Fern, and Cryptocoryne.

  • Hardscape: Driftwood and smooth stones offer shelter and reduce stress.

  • Botanicals: Indian Almond Leaves help mimic natural tannin-rich waters, promoting vibrant color and health. Other botanicals can also offer the same benefits like Alder Cones, Catappa Bark, Jack Fruit Leaves, and Cinnamon leaves. 

Diet and Feeding:

Bettas are carnivorous and enjoy a varied diet. Feed high-quality betta pellets daily, and supplement with frozen or live treats like brine shrimp, daphnia, or bloodworms for enrichment and color enhancement.

Tank Mates and Compatibility:

  • DO NOT House With Other Bettas: Male bettas will fight with other males and often with females.

  • Compatible With: Peaceful nano species like snails and shrimp (with caution), or fish that do not resemble bettas or nip fins.

  • Avoid: Fin nippers, aggressive tankmates, or other bettas.

Not Shrimp Safe:

Can be kept with adult shrimp in well-planted tanks, but smaller shrimp or fry may be at risk.

Recommended Tank Parameters:

  • Temperature: 76° - 82° F (24.4° - 27.7° C)

  • pH: 6.5 – 7.5

  • KH: 2 - 8

  • Minimum Tank Size: 5 gallons

Care Guidelines:

  • Diet: Carnivorous; varied high-protein diet.

  • Social Behavior: Solitary; very aggressive toward other bettas.

  • Origin: Tank-bred.

  • Average Adult Size: 2.5 – 3 inches (6.3 – 7.6 cm)

  • Average Purchase Size: 1.5 – 2.5 inches (3.8 – 6.3 cm)

Treat Yourself to a Living Jewel

Whether you’re looking to add bold movement and color to your home or office, or searching for a pet with personality, the Dragon Super Delta Tail Betta is a breathtaking choice.

products.title: Platinum Veiltail Angelfish (Pterophyllum scalare) – Tank-Bred
products.body_html:
For the safety of our animals, we now ship live fish exclusively via UPS Next Day Air. If your order contains this item, you will only see UPS Next Day Air as a shipping option during checkout.

A true showstopper in any freshwater aquarium, the Platinum Veiltail Angelfish features a gleaming white body with long, flowing fins that ripple gracefully with every movement. This selectively bred variety of Pterophyllum scalare is known for its elegant appearance and peaceful, inquisitive nature. Tank-bred for hardiness and adaptability, this angelfish thrives in community setups with gentle tank mates and a well-planted environment.

What We Love About This Fish:

• Brilliant Platinum Coloration: Striking metallic white body shines under aquarium lighting.
• Elegant Fins: Extra-long veil fins add motion and beauty to any aquascape.
• Peaceful Personality: Generally calm and social—great for community tanks with compatible fish.
• Hardy and Adaptable: Tank-bred specimens do well in a variety of water parameters.

Natural Habitat:

While the wild ancestors of angelfish come from the slow-moving, heavily vegetated tributaries of the Amazon Basin, this platinum variant has been selectively bred in captivity. It still appreciates warm, calm waters with plenty of vertical swimming space and tall plants for cover.

Aquascaping:

• Plants: Amazon Sword, Vallisneria, Java Fern, and other tall or broad-leaved plants.
• Hardscape: Use smooth river stones, vertical driftwood, and tall structures.
• Botanicals: Indian Almond Leaves and Catappa Bark can provide tannins and a more natural feel.

Diet and Feeding:

An omnivore by nature, the Platinum Veiltail Angelfish will readily accept high-quality flakes, pellets, and frozen or live foods such as bloodworms, brine shrimp, and daphnia. A varied diet helps maintain its health, coloration, and fin development.

Tank Mates and Compatibility:

Best kept with peaceful community fish of similar size. Avoid aggressive or fin-nipping species such as barbs. Good companions include peaceful tetras, corydoras, peaceful gouramis, and larger rasboras.

Not Shrimp Safe:

May be safe with adult shrimp but may eat small shrimp or shrimp fry. Provide dense plant cover and hiding spots if housed together.

Breeding:

Angelfish are egg layers and form monogamous pairs. They will typically lay eggs on vertical surfaces such as broad plant leaves or aquarium glass. In a well-maintained tank, breeding is possible, especially if a bonded pair is formed.

Recommended Tank Parameters:

• Temperature: 75° - 82° F (23.8° - 27.7° C)
• pH: 6.5 – 7.5
• KH: 3 – 8 dKH
• Minimum Tank Size: 20 gallons for a pair, 30+ gallons for a small group

Care Guidelines:

• Diet: Omnivorous; feed high-quality flakes and supplement with frozen or live foods.
• Social Behavior: Generally peaceful; best in groups or bonded pairs.
• Origin: Tank-bred
• Average Adult Size: 5 – 6 inches (12.7 – 15.2 cm) body length, fins can extend longer
• Average Purchase Size: 1.5 – 2.5 inches (3.8 – 6.3 cm)

Looking for a graceful centerpiece with shimmering beauty and long, flowing fins? The Platinum Veiltail Angelfish is the perfect addition to a peaceful planted aquarium.

products.title: Red Dwarf Rasbora (Microrasbora sp. cf. rubescens)
products.body_html:
For the safety of our animals, we now ship live fish exclusively via UPS Next Day Air. If your order contains this item, you will only see UPS Next Day Air as a shipping option during checkout.

The Red Dwarf Rasbora is a stunning and diminutive freshwater fish species that adds vibrant color and lively energy to nano and planted aquariums. With its shimmering red hues and delicate schooling behavior, this species makes a striking addition to peaceful community tanks or species-specific setups. Tank-bred specimens offer a sustainable and healthy option for aquarists seeking this rare beauty.

What We Love About This Fish:

  • Vibrant Red Coloration: A deep ruby-red body with iridescent highlights that shimmer under aquarium lighting.
  • Perfect for Nano Tanks: Small size and peaceful nature make it ideal for smaller aquariums.
  • Hardy and Adaptable: A great choice for beginners and experienced aquarists alike.
  • Active Schooling Behavior: Adds dynamic movement to the tank when kept in groups of 8 or more.

Natural Habitat:

The Red Dwarf Rasbora is native to slow-moving, densely vegetated waters of Myanmar. These environments often have soft, slightly acidic water with abundant leaf litter and submerged plants, creating shaded areas and low light conditions.

Aquascaping:

  • Plants: Ideal tank mates for heavily planted tanks with Java Moss, Cryptocorynes, Dwarf Hairgrass, and Anubias. Floating plants such as Frogbit or Red Root Floaters provide shade, which mimics their natural habitat.
  • Hardscape: Include fine-grain substrate, driftwood, and leaf litter for a natural look and to create hiding spots.
  • Botanicals: Indian Almond Leaves and alder cones release tannins, enhancing water quality and encouraging natural behaviors.

Diet and Feeding:

Red Dwarf Rasboras are micro-predators and omnivores. Feed them a varied diet that includes:

  • High-quality micro pellets
  • Fine flake foods
  • Live or frozen foods like brine shrimp, daphnia, and cyclops

Regular feedings of live or frozen food will help maintain their vibrant coloration and promote health.

Tank Mates and Compatibility:

The Red Dwarf Rasbora is a peaceful and social species, best kept in groups of 8 or more. Suitable tank mates include:

  • Other small, peaceful fish like Celestial Pearl Danios and Ember Tetras
  • Small shrimp species like Neocaridina or Caridina (though shrimp fry may be at risk)
  • Bottom dwellers like Corydoras or Kuhli Loaches

Avoid aggressive or overly active species that may outcompete them for food.

Shrimp Safe:

Adult shrimp are generally safe with these rasboras, but shrimp fry may be considered prey. Dense plant cover and hiding spots are recommended.

Breeding:

Red Dwarf Rasboras are egg scatterers. To encourage breeding, provide a densely planted tank with fine-leaved plants or spawning mops. Slightly cooler water changes and increased live food feedings can trigger spawning. Remove adults after spawning to protect the eggs.

Recommended Tank Parameters:

  • Temperature: 72° - 78° F (22° - 26° C)
  • pH: 6.0 - 7.0
  • KH: 1 - 5 dKH
  • Minimum Tank Size: 5 gallons (10 gallons or larger recommended for schools)

Care Guidelines:

  • Diet: Omnivorous. A mix of micro pellets, flakes, and live/frozen foods is ideal.
  • Social Behavior: Peaceful, thrives in schools of 8 or more.
  • Origin: Tank-bred.
  • Average Adult Size: 0.75 - 1 inch (1.9 - 2.5 cm)
  • Average Purchase Size: 0.5 - 0.75 inch (1.3 - 1.9 cm)

Bring vibrant beauty and natural elegance to your aquarium with the Red Dwarf Rasbora—a perfect choice for nano aquariums and planted tank enthusiasts.

products.title: Taiwanese Dragon Micro Goby (Schismatogobius ampluvinculus)
products.body_html:
* For the safety of our animals, we now ship live fish exclusively via UPS Next Day Air.  If your order contains this item, you will only see UPS Next Day Air as a shipping option during checkout *

Live Foods are great enrichment and what your fish crave! Try our Live Daphnia to give your fish a natural food source today!

 

We are very excited to offer the Taiwanese Dragon Micro Goby (Schismatogobius ampluvinculus).  It is somewhat surprising that this fish is not more well-known because it has the ability to camouflage and dramatically change color to match its surroundings.  It can achieve nearly any shade of brown, black, gray, and near white coloration.  This is a true freshwater goby that is compatible with snails and small nano fish, making it a perfect nano aquarium specimen. There have been some instances where they may go after dwarf shrimp but they are safe to keep with larger species of shrimp.
 
The Taiwanese Dragon Micro Goby is a temperate species that requires sub-tropical water conditions.  It thrives in planted or heavily decorated aquariums with moderate to high water flow, and it requires soft sand or smooth gravel substrate in which it can bury itself on occasion.  This fish should initially be fed the smallest of meaty frozen and live foods, but can often be weaned onto high quality dry foods as well.  While it is not a schooling fish per se, it can easily be kept in groups whose interaction is very entertaining to watch.
 
It is important to note that this goby is a truly scaleless fish, so please be cautious when using medications in its aquarium.  Some medications require a lower dosage when used with scaleless fish.
 


 
What We Like About This Fish:
  • New to the aquarium hobby
  • Extremely rare, but very interesting
  • Completely peaceful disposition 
  • Highly variable coloration within individual specimens
  • Generally safe to keep with adult dwarf shrimp
 
RECOMMENDED TANK PARAMETERS:
  • Temperature:  64° - 75° F (18° - 24° C)
  • pH:  7.0 - 7.8
  • KH:  8 - 20 dKH
  • Minimum tank size:  5 gallons for one and 10-15 gallons for three or more
 
CARE GUIDELINES: 
  • Diet:  Carnivorous.  Should initially be fed small, meaty frozen foods.  May eventually accept dry foods.
  • Social behavior:  Peaceful.  Can be kept in groups. 
  • Origin:  Taiwan and Japan.
  • Average adult size:  1 inch (2.5 cm), sometimes up to 2 inches (5 cm).
  • Average purchase size:  1/2 - 1+ inch (1.3 - 2.5+ cm)

products.title: Platinum Longfin Short body Koi (Cyprinus carpio) – Tank-Bred
products.body_html:
For the safety of our animals, we now ship live fish exclusively via UPS Next Day Air. If your order contains this item, you will only see UPS Next Day Air as a shipping option during checkout.

The Platinum Longfin Short body Koi is a dazzling, all-white koi variety known for its shimmering metallic sheen and uniquely compact body shape. A type of koi that reaches a smaller adult size compared to standard koi, these fish typically grow to just 6–8 inches long. While they share the same graceful fins and body structure as standard butterfly koi, their defining feature is a shorter, more compact body, giving them a charming and distinct look. These tank-raised specimens are currently 3+ inches in size and perfect for growing out in a pond or large aquarium.

What We Love About This Fish:

• Brilliant Platinum White Body: Glows under natural or aquarium lighting
• Elegant Long Fins: Flowing fins add beauty and motion to any setup
• Compact Size: Short body koi are ideal for hobbyists with smaller ponds or indoor tanks
• Cold-Water Hardy: Suitable for year-round pond life in many climates

Natural Habitat:

Though koi are fully domesticated, they originate from wild carp in Eurasian rivers and lakes. Short body koi, like this variety, are selectively bred for their compact form and manageable size, making them perfect for ornamental setups without the need for massive pond space.

Aquascaping:

• Plants: Water Lettuce, Hornwort, Anacharis (ensure durability against nibbling)
• Hardscape: Rounded river rocks, submerged driftwood, shaded hiding zones
• Botanicals: Optional—these koi prefer open space for swimming over heavy botanicals

Diet and Feeding:

These koi are enthusiastic omnivores. Provide a high-quality koi pellet as the staple diet, supplemented with fresh greens, fruits, and occasional frozen foods like bloodworms. Feed more frequently in warmer months when activity increases.

Tank Mates and Compatibility:

Best kept with other koi or large, peaceful pond fish. Their peaceful nature and similar care requirements make them perfect companions for standard or longfin koi.

Not Shrimp Safe:

Not recommended for tanks with shrimp or small inverts. Koi are opportunistic feeders and may eat small tankmates.

Outdoor Pond & Water Feature Compatibility:

Perfect for outdoor water gardens and backyard ponds. Shortbody koi are ideal for medium-sized ponds where full-grown standard koi may outgrow the space. Their hardy nature and eye-catching fins shine in well-maintained environments with calm water and seasonal stability.

Breeding:

Breeds in spring under the right conditions. Females scatter adhesive eggs among aquatic plants or spawning mops. Remove adults post-spawn if fry are to be raised.

Recommended Tank Parameters:

• Temperature: 50° - 77°F (10° - 25°C)
• pH: 7.0 - 8.0
• KH: 4 - 12 dKH
• Minimum Tank Size: 75 gallons for juveniles; at least 300–500 gallons for adults (less than standard koi due to smaller adult size)

Care Guidelines:

• Diet: Omnivorous; requires a balanced pellet-based diet with fresh treats
• Social Behavior: Peaceful, best in groups
• Origin: Tank-Raised
• Average Adult Size: 6 – 8 inches
• Average Purchase Size: 3+ inches
• Body Shape Note: They have the same body shape and fins as standard koi, but they are shorter and more compact overall.

A Smaller Koi with Big Presence!
The Platinum Longfin Shortbody Koi offers all the grace and beauty of traditional koi—just in a more compact size. Perfect for hobbyists who want showpiece koi without the need for enormous pond space.
