products.title: Panda Butterfly Koi (Cyprinus carpio) – Tank-Raised
products.body_html:
For the safety of our animals, we now ship live fish exclusively via UPS Next Day Air. If your order contains this item, you will only see UPS Next Day Air as a shipping option during checkout.

The Panda Butterfly Koi is a breathtaking long-finned variety of koi carp, featuring bold black and white coloration reminiscent of its namesake. With graceful, trailing fins and high-contrast patterns, this koi is a standout in ponds and large aquariums. These tank-raised specimens are currently 3+ inches in size and will grow more impressive with age.

What We Love About This Fish:

• Striking Black and White Patterning: Elegant, high-contrast body makes it easy to spot
• Flowing Butterfly Fins: Long, delicate fins add beauty and motion to your setup
• Hardy and Peaceful: Ideal for community ponds and large aquariums
• Highly Interactive: Can become friendly and recognize their keepers over time

Natural Habitat:

Derived from the Common Carp, koi have been bred in Japan for centuries. Panda Butterfly Koi prefer calm, well-oxygenated waters with stable temperatures and plenty of swimming room. Though originally from rivers and lakes, today’s koi are entirely domesticated.

Aquascaping:

• Plants: Hardy pond plants like Water Lettuce, Water Hyacinth, and Anacharis
• Hardscape: Smooth river rocks, submerged driftwood, shallow ledges or caves
• Botanicals: Not typically used in koi ponds, but leaves and alder cones can be added in indoor tanks for enrichment

Diet and Feeding:

Omnivorous and easy to feed, Panda Butterfly Koi thrive on high-quality pellet food, supplemented with leafy greens, frozen treats (like bloodworms or brine shrimp), and occasional fruits or vegetables. Feed 2–3 times daily when temperatures are above 50°F (10°C).

Tank Mates and Compatibility:

Peaceful and social, Butterfly Koi are best kept with other koi or large, non-aggressive fish. Avoid small or delicate species that may be outcompeted for food or mistaken for food.

Not Shrimp Safe:

Not recommended for tanks with shrimp. Koi may eat or disturb invertebrates, especially in smaller settings.

Outdoor Pond & Water Feature Compatibility:

Perfect for outdoor ponds and water gardens, Panda Butterfly Koi are cold-tolerant and can overwinter in ponds with proper aeration and depth. Their elegant fins are best showcased in calm water with minimal flow. Provide shade and protection from predators. In warmer months, they become especially active and interactive with their environment.

Breeding:

Breeding typically occurs in spring in outdoor ponds when temperatures rise. Females scatter eggs among plants, which are then fertilized by males. If breeding is desired, provide ample plant coverage or spawning mops.

Recommended Tank Parameters:

• Temperature: 50° - 77°F (10° - 25°C)
• pH: 7.0 - 8.0
• KH: 4 - 12 dKH
• Minimum Tank Size: 75 gallons indoors (ideal for juveniles); 1000+ gallons for adult pond groups

Care Guidelines:

• Diet: Omnivorous, loves pellets, veggies, and occasional live/frozen food
• Social Behavior: Schooling and peaceful, best in groups
• Origin: Tank-Raised
• Average Adult Size: 18 – 24 inches (with proper space and care)
• Average Purchase Size: 3+ inches

Add Elegance and Motion to Your Pond with Panda Butterfly Koi!
These long-finned beauties are more than just decorative—they’re living works of art that grow more impressive with time.

products.title: Black Diamond Neon Tetra (Paracheirodon innesi var. "Black Diamond") - Tank-Bred!
products.body_html:
* For the safety of our animals, we now ship live fish exclusively via UPS Next Day Air. If your order contains this item, you will only see UPS Next Day Air as a shipping option during checkout *

The Black Diamond Neon Tetra is a very rare, black variant of the classic Neon Tetra, a very colorful and active nano fish!

The Black Diamond Neon Tetra (Paracheirodon innesi var. "Black Diamond") is an excellent choice for the community aquarium, especially planted aquariums. This variant of the classic Neon Tetra is fairly new to the hobby and is not commonly available. This is a very peaceful fish that is suitable for beginners and experts alike! ﻿Please note that this is a black variant of the true Neon Tetra (﻿Paracheirodon innesi﻿), NOT the more common Black Neon Tetra (﻿Hyphessobrycon herbertaxelrodi﻿), which is an entirely different species.

The Black Diamond Neon Tetra typically occupies the top and middle level of the water column, although it can often be seen swimming and feeding in the middle and bottom levels as well. It is safe with other peaceful, small fish. Adult dwarf shrimp are generally safe as well, but adult the Black Diamond Neon Tetra may prey on dwarf shrimp fry. Larger, peaceful invertebrates can also make good tankmates. The Black Diamond Neon Tetra must be kept in schools of 6 or more. Since it is tank-raised, this fish is very adaptable, but its natural habitat contains soft, acidic water rich in tannins and leaf litter.  

While not a picky eater, the Black Diamond Neon Tetra will thrive and remain very colorful on a varied diet of meaty foods. High-quality flake food, pellets, and frozen or freeze-dried meaty foods will all be readily accepted.  

 

What We Like About This Fish:
  • Incredible overall black coloration
  • Peaceful disposition with fish and peaceful invertebrates
  • Very active and noticeable fish in any aquarium
  • Excellent for inhabiting the top of the aquarium
 
RECOMMENDED TANK PARAMETERS:
  • Temperature:  68° - 82° F (20° - 27.7° C)

CARE GUIDELINES:

products.title: Purple Waffle Plant (Hemigraphis alternata)
products.body_html:
The Purple Waffle Plant is a unique and eye-catching addition to any freshwater setup, prized for its crinkled, textured leaves and deep purple undersides. While it’s often used as an accent in aquariums, it’s best suited for terrariums, paludariums, or as an emergent feature in semi-aquatic environments. Available in either a weighted bunch or planted pot, it brings bold color and texture to any display.

Features We Love:

  • Striking Coloration: Deep green tops with rich purple undersides.

  • Textured Foliage: Waffle-like leaves create a dramatic, decorative look.

  • Versatile Use: Ideal for terrariums, paludariums, or emersed aquascaping.

  • Two Planting Options: Choose a bunched stem group or rooted potted form.

Care Guidelines:

  • Light: Low to moderate (does best with indirect light or low aquarium lighting).

  • Temperature: 65° - 80° F (18° - 27° C)

  • pH Range: 6.0 – 7.5

  • Growth Rate: Moderate

  • Ideal Placement: Emersed use in terrarium setups or as a decorative emersed feature in aquariums

Pro Tips:

  • Not Fully Aquatic: Will deteriorate if fully submerged long term—best for semi-aquatic setups.

  • Use in Open-Top Tanks: Can thrive with roots in water and leaves above the surface.

  • Humidity Helps: Loves humid conditions—ideal for vivarium's and high-moisture environments.

  • Pair with Hardscape: Looks stunning against rocks and driftwood in paludariums.

Bring a bold splash of purple and texture to your aquascape or terrarium with the unforgettable Purple Waffle Plant—a standout choice for unique, semi-aquatic displays.

 

100% Live Arrival Guarantee:
All Aquatic Arts plants come with a 100% live arrival guarantee. We ensure your Purple Waffle Plant arrives healthy and ready to thrive, with free email support always available.

products.title: Lemon Bacopa (Bacopa caroliniana)
products.body_html:
Lemon Bacopa is a bright, aromatic stem plant known for its vibrant green leaves and fresh citrus scent when handled. Whether in a weighted bunch or nestled in a convenient pot, this hardy stem plant adds a lush, refreshing touch to any freshwater aquarium. Ideal for background or midground placement, it thrives under moderate to high lighting and is perfect for both beginners and experienced aquascapers.

Features We Love:

  • Fresh Citrus Aroma: Leaves release a subtle lemony scent when trimmed or crushed.

  • Hardy and Adaptable: Grows steadily in a wide range of freshwater conditions.

  • Great for Aquascaping: Perfect for backgrounds or trimming into bushy shapes.

  • Available in Two Forms: Choose between a traditional bunched stem group or a rooted potted version for easy planting.

Care Guidelines:

  • Light: Moderate to high.

  • Temperature: 68° - 82° F (20° - 28° C)

  • pH Range: 6.5 – 7.5

Pro Tips:

  • Great for Shrimp Tanks: Offers cover and biofilm-grazing surfaces.

products.title: Kleiner Prinz Sword (Echinodorus sp. hybrid)
products.body_html:
The Kleiner Prinz Sword is a rare hybrid that matures to incredible red coloration and only grows to 12 inches or less!

 

Kleiner Prinz Sword (Echinodorus sp.) is a rare and beautiful sword plant hybrid. Its bright green and red leaves are large and vibrant, and the plant only grows to a maximum of 12 inches, making it ideal for aquariums that are too small for most other sword plants. It can even thrive in low-tech setups wiith moderate to high lighting and nutrient-rich substrate!

Care for Kleiner Prinz Sword is very simple. This plant will benefit from CO2 supplementation, but it does not require it. While it will thrive in high lighting, it is also very durable and can do very well under moderate lighting. It requires nutrient-rich substrate for its extensive root structure. It produces very large leaves, so it must be planted with care to not cast too much shade on other plants as it grows. It is certainly only suitable for background placement in smaller aquariums, but it can also be placed in the middle ground of larger aquariums. Kleiner Prinz Sword reproduces through rhizome division as well as by the production of side shoots, which can be removed and replanted.  It is very intolerant of copper, so please keep this in mind if you use tap water in your aquarium.

 

Kleiner Prinz Sword is a member of the plant group known as Rosettes, which are known for their extremely short stem axis and for the way their leaves grow upward from the base of the plant. This plant can grow up to 12 inches in height with the proper lighting and water quality, but it often grows to only 8-10 inches. Approximate shipping size of our Kleiner Prinz Swords is 5-6 inches tall.  As with all of our plants (besides tissue cultures), our Rose Sparkle Swords are kept in water in a dedicated tank rather than plastic packaging, which means your plant will arrive much healthier.

 

Like most plants, Kleiner Prinz Sword will benefit from supplementation such as Seachem Flourish, Flourish Excel, Nitrogen, Iron, and other plant supplements.

No aquarium plant distributor/nursery/wholesaler/retailer can or will guarantee their plants to be 100% snail free. We can not guarantee our plants will be snail free. 

What We Like About This Plant:

  • Distinctive green and red leaves
  • Smaller maximum size than many other sword plants
  • Very easy to propagate
  • Provides a great deal of cover for animal fry
  • Very hardy and durable in a well-lit, nutrient-rich aquarium

 

Care Guidelines:

  • Temperature: 72° - 84° F (22° - 29° C)
  • pH: 6.5 - 7.5
  • Lighting: Moderate to high
  • Origin: Parent species indigenous to South America, hybrid cultivated in US nurseries
  • Aquarium placement: Middle to Background
  • Care: Easy

 

All Aquatic Arts brand plants and animals come with a 100% live arrival guarantee, plus free email support!

products.title: Assorted Chocolate Shrimp (Neocaridina davidi) – BREDBY: Aquatic Arts
products.body_html:
Our Assorted Chocolate Shrimp are a rich and earthy in-house variant of Neocaridina davidi, featuring deep chocolate brown tones mixed with occasional cherry red and blue hues. No two shrimp are exactly alike—some display solid dark brown, while others show iridescent undertones or streaks of warm or cool color. Every Shrimp is unique and has beautiful coloration. This natural variety makes them an excellent choice for aquarists who appreciate subtle color gradients and a more organic look in planted or natural-style aquascapes.

Bred by our team here at Aquatic Arts, these shrimp are raised in optimal conditions to ensure health, hardiness, and color stability. They are active grazers that help manage algae and biofilm, all while adding beauty and charm to your aquarium.

What We Love About These Shrimp:

• Beautiful Natural Variation: Deep chocolate tones with possible hints of cherry or blue.
• BRED BY: Aquatic Arts: Healthy, well-established genetics and vibrant color.
• Excellent Algae Grazers: Constantly clean plant leaves, hardscape, and substrate.
• Hardy and Easy to Keep: Perfect for beginners or colony builders.

Natural Habitat:

While the Neocaridina species originates from freshwater streams in Taiwan and southern Asia, this Chocolate variant is a selectively bred line created in captivity. These shrimp are adapted to life in planted aquariums and thrive in mature systems with biofilm and stable water parameters.

Aquascaping:

• Plants: Mosses like Java Moss or Christmas Moss are ideal for grazing. Add Anubias, Bucephalandra, and Cryptocoryne for cover.
• Hardscape: Shrimp love textured surfaces like lava rock, driftwood, and cholla wood.
• Botanicals: Indian Almond Leaves and alder cones provide tannins and biofilm-friendly surfaces while helping maintain water quality.

Diet and Feeding:

These shrimp are omnivorous scavengers that thrive on a varied diet. Feed high-quality shrimp pellets, Snowflake food (soybean hulls), and pollen granules. Supplement with blanched vegetables (like zucchini or spinach) and occasional protein treats such as bloodworms or baby brine shrimp.

Tank Mates and Compatibility:

Chocolate Shrimp are peaceful and do best with other dwarf shrimp or similarly sized, non-aggressive tank mates. Compatible species include snails, rasboras, small tetras, and Corydoras. Avoid larger or predatory fish such as cichlids or gouramis.

Shrimp Safe:

Yes – perfectly shrimp safe when kept with proper tank mates. Also safe in mixed Neocaridina colonies, though color mixing may occur over time.

Breeding:

Very easy to breed in a stable tank with good water quality. Females carry eggs beneath their tails until hatching. Young shrimp will remain in the tank if plenty of moss or cover is available. Breeding may produce a mix of chocolate and related colors over time.

Recommended Tank Parameters:

• Temperature: 64° – 78° F (17.8° – 25.5° C)
• pH: 6.8 – 7.5
• KH: 0 – 10
• GH: 6 – 15
• TDS: 180 – 400 ppm
• Minimum Tank Size: 2 gallons (10+ gallons recommended for breeding colonies)

Care Guidelines:

• Diet: Omnivorous. Feed a mix of algae-based and protein-rich foods.
• Social Behavior: Peaceful. Best kept in groups of 5 or more.
• Origin: Bred by Aquatic Arts.
• Average Adult Size: 0.75 – 1.25 inches (2 – 3.2 cm)
• Average Purchase Size: 0.5 – 0.75 inches (1.2 – 2 cm)

Add Depth and Warmth to Your Shrimp Tank
The Assorted Chocolate Shrimp line brings rich, earthy tones to your tank with the occasional pop of cherry or blue shimmer. Whether you're building a new colony or adding character to a mixed tank, these shrimp offer natural elegance with a hardworking attitude.

products.title: Black Darter Tetra (Poecilocharax weitzmani)
products.body_html:
For the safety of our animals, we now ship live fish exclusively via UPS Next Day Air. If your order contains this item, you will only see UPS Next Day Air as a shipping option during checkout.

The Black Darter Tetra (Poecilocharax weitzmani) is a striking and highly sought-after micro predator native to the blackwater streams of the Amazon Basin. Known for its elegant, dart-like swimming style and rich black coloration with contrasting gold or bronze highlights, this rare tetra is a real standout in any well-maintained aquarium.

While small in size, the Black Darter Tetra has a bold presence and is best appreciated in a species-only or carefully curated community tank with other peaceful fish of similar size. Due to its rarity and specific care needs, this species is most suitable for experienced aquarists looking to create a quiet, dimly lit setup with soft, acidic water—similar to the fish’s natural habitat.

What We Love About This Fish:

• Sleek and Sophisticated: Velvety black coloration with metallic gold accents.
• Rare and Wild-Collected: Not commonly found in the hobby.
• Dynamic Behavior: Graceful, darting motion adds movement and intrigue.
• Best in Biotope Setups: Ideal for soft, blackwater aquascapes.

Natural Habitat:

Native to the upper Rio Negro and Orinoco basins, these fish inhabit slow-moving forest streams rich in tannins, leaf litter, and submerged roots. Water is typically soft, acidic, and tea-colored due to decaying plant matter.

Aquascaping:

• Plants: Use floating plants (like Salvinia or Frogbit) to create shaded areas, along with low-light plants such as Java Fern, Anubias, or Cryptocoryne.
• Hardscape: Add driftwood, smooth stones, and leaf litter to mimic a blackwater forest stream.
• Botanicals: Indian Almond Leaves, Catappa Bark, and alder cones enhance water quality and support natural behavior.

Diet and Feeding:

These micro predators feed on small invertebrates in the wild. In the aquarium, they may be picky at first but usually accept live and frozen foods like baby brine shrimp, daphnia, and micro worms. With time, some may adapt to high-quality micro pellets.

Tank Mates and Compatibility:

Best kept in a quiet tank with peaceful, similarly sized species. Ideal tank mates include small rasboras, green neon tetras, pygmy corydoras, and dwarf pencilfish. Avoid aggressive or fast-moving fish.

Shrimp Safe?:

May be safe with adult shrimp, but small shrimp and shrimp fry could be considered prey.

Breeding:

Rarely bred in captivity. Males display vivid coloration and may guard territories. Breeding may occur in soft, acidic water with fine-leaved plants or botanicals for cover, though successful spawns are infrequent.

Recommended Tank Parameters:

• Temperature: 72° – 79° F (22° – 26° C)
• pH: 5.0 – 6.8
• KH: 1 – 6 dKH
• Minimum Tank Size: 10 gallons

Care Guidelines:

• Diet: Carnivorous. Prefers live and frozen foods, with occasional micro pellets.
• Social Behavior: Semi-social; best kept in small groups (5–6) with ample space.
• Origin: Wild collected
• Average Adult Size: 1.25 inches (3.2 cm)
• Average Purchase Size: 0.75 – 1 inch (2 – 2.5 cm)

Add Grace and Mystery to Your Blackwater Tank
The Black Darter Tetra is a jewel for aquarists seeking something truly different—graceful, rare, and beautifully adapted to a naturalistic setup. Whether you’re building a South American biotope or just want a captivating oddball, this species will reward your attention.

products.title: Indian Butterfly Catfish (Erethistes hara)
products.body_html:
For the safety of our animals, we now ship live fish exclusively via UPS Next Day Air. If your order contains this item, you will only see UPS Next Day Air as a shipping option during checkout.

The Indian Butterfly Catfish, also known as the Moth Catfish, is a rare and fascinating bottom-dweller collected from the cool, slow-moving forest streams of India and Bangladesh. Its wide, wing-like pectoral fins and cryptic mottled coloration give it the appearance of a tiny underwater moth or bat when viewed at rest.

This unique catfish is peaceful, extremely shy, and thrives in heavily planted aquariums with soft, slightly acidic water, subdued lighting, and gentle flow. Its slow movements and camouflage make it a perfect fit for quiet, naturalistic tanks, especially nano or biotope-style setups. Though rarely seen in the hobby, this wild-collected gem is a must-have for aquarists who love oddball species with character.

What We Love About This Fish:

• Incredible Camouflage: Looks like a tiny leaf or stone, blending beautifully with natural décor.
• Winged Appearance: Broad, extended pectoral fins give a “moth-like” look.
• Great Nano Catfish: Small and slow-moving, ideal for calm planted tanks.
• Rare and Wild-Collected: Seldom available, not commonly bred in captivity.

Natural Habitat:

Erethistes hara is native to the slow-moving, shaded hill streams and forest creeks of northeastern India and Bangladesh. These waters are typically cool, with low pH, soft substrate, and abundant leaf litter and woody debris.

Aquascaping:

• Plants: Provide dense vegetation like Java Moss, Cryptocoryne, and Anubias.
• Hardscape: Use smooth river stones, driftwood, and plenty of hiding places.
• Botanicals: Add Indian Almond Leaves and alder cones to replicate their natural forest stream environment.

Diet and Feeding:

This catfish is a micro-predator that feeds on tiny invertebrates in the wild. In captivity, they accept live or frozen foods such as baby brine shrimp, daphnia, bloodworms, and blackworms. They may also take high-quality sinking pellets or wafers when acclimated.

Tank Mates and Compatibility:

Extremely peaceful and best kept with other gentle, slow-moving species like small rasboras, ember tetras, and sparkling gouramis. Avoid fast, boisterous fish or anything that may outcompete them for food.

Shrimp Safe?:

Yes – generally safe with adult dwarf shrimp. May consume baby shrimp if not well fed.

Breeding:

Breeding in captivity is very rare and not well documented. Spawning likely occurs under rocks or in secluded areas, triggered by cool, soft water changes and seasonal cues.

Recommended Tank Parameters:

• Temperature: 68° – 76° F (20° – 24.4° C)
• pH: 6.0 – 7.2
• KH: 2 – 8 dKH
• Minimum Tank Size: 5 gallons

Care Guidelines:

• Diet: Carnivorous. Prefers live/frozen foods and protein-rich sinking options.
• Social Behavior: Peaceful and reclusive. Best kept alone or in groups of 3+.
• Origin: Wild collected
• Average Adult Size: 1.5 – 2 inches (3.8 – 5 cm)
• Average Purchase Size: 1 – 1.25 inches (2.5 – 3.2 cm)

A Hidden Gem for the Patient Aquarist
The Indian Butterfly Catfish is a true oddball species—quiet, cryptic, and captivating once established. Whether you’re building a forest stream biotope or just looking to add a calm bottom-dweller with charm, this wild-caught beauty is a rewarding choice.

The Kurorame Medaka is a striking and refined ricefish variant, line bred for its deep grey tones and metallic, reflective scales. Unlike the more common silvery or white-bodied medaka, this variety brings a dramatic contrast and elegance to any aquarium or pond. When paired with lighter-colored medaka like Platinum or Yurishisu varieties, the visual impact is incredible—like a yin-yang dance of shimmering light and shadow.

As with all medaka ricefish, the Kurorame is peaceful, hardy, and easy to care for, making it perfect for both new hobbyists and collectors of rare strains. It shines brightest when viewed from above, making it an excellent candidate for rimless shallow aquariums or patio ponds.

What We Love About This Fish:

• Refined Metallic Coloration: Displays bold grey tones and shimmering, scale-level sparkle.
• Perfect Contrast: A standout when housed with white or light-colored medaka.
• Line Bred Elegance: Carefully developed to ensure consistent, stunning appearance.
• Hardy and Peaceful: A low-maintenance addition to planted tanks or outdoor ponds.

Natural Habitat:

Like all medaka, this variety descends from wild populations native to Japan. These adaptable fish live in rice paddies, streams, and shallow pools, thriving in calm waters with vegetation and natural light. While this variant is not found in the wild, it thrives under similar conditions.

Aquascaping:

• Plants: Ideal with floating plants (Frogbit, Duckweed) and lush stems like Rotala or Bacopa.
• Hardscape: Slate, river pebbles, or driftwood offer great contrast to their body color.
• Botanicals: Indian Almond Leaves and alder cones help promote natural behavior and enhance breeding success.

Diet and Feeding:

Kurorame Medaka are omnivores that accept a wide variety of foods. Feed them high-quality flake or micro pellet foods supplemented with frozen or live items like baby brine shrimp, daphnia, or micro worms to support growth and coloration.

Tank Mates and Compatibility:

They are peaceful and social, best kept in groups. Compatible with other medaka and peaceful species like rasboras, endlers, small snails, and shrimp.

Shrimp Safe:

Yes. Safe with adult shrimp. Fry may be at risk without proper hiding spaces.

Breeding:

Medaka are egg-layers that breed easily. Provide floating plants or a spawning mop. Eggs are carried by the female before being deposited. Hatching occurs in 1–2 weeks depending on temperature.

Recommended Tank Parameters:

• Temperature: 64° – 78° F (17.8° – 25.5° C)
• pH: 6.8 – 7.5
• KH: 3 – 10 dKH
• Minimum Tank Size: 5 gallons

Care Guidelines:

• Diet: Omnivorous; thrives on a varied diet.
• Social Behavior: Peaceful and best in groups of 6+.
• Origin: Tank bred
• Average Adult Size: 1.25 inches (3.2 cm)
• Average Purchase Size: 0.75 – 1 inch (2 – 2.5 cm)

Balance Your Medaka Collection with This Shadowy Sparkle!
Whether you’re building a top-view pond or a detailed aquascape, the Kurorame Medaka adds depth, contrast, and a metallic glimmer that complements other ricefish beautifully. It’s a true collector’s variant that brings poise and personality to any setup.

The Yurishisu Medaka is a stunning and rare variant of the beloved Japanese Ricefish, selectively bred for its striking appearance. With glowing silvery-white bodies and vibrant orange markings concentrated on the head and face, these fish resemble miniature koi when viewed from above—complete with shimmering, metallic “sparkles” that flash in the light. This top-view beauty makes them an exceptional choice for shallow planted aquariums, rimless tanks, or outdoor ponds.

Still relatively new to the aquarium hobby outside of Japan, the Yurishisu variant is prized for both its rarity and elegance. Like other medaka, they are extremely hardy, peaceful, and easy to care for—making them a show-stopping species that's suitable for beginners and experienced keepers alike.

What We Love About This Fish:

• Beautiful Sparkling Coloration: Orange head pattern with silvery, shimmering bodies.
• Top-View Appeal: Koi-like appearance when viewed from above—ideal for shallow tanks or ponds.
• Hardy and Peaceful: Very adaptable and easy to care for in a wide range of setups.
• Rare Variant: A newer and uncommon addition to the ricefish world.

Natural Habitat:

Medaka ricefish are native to Japan and Eastern Asia, where they are found in slow-moving streams, rice paddies, and marshes. They are incredibly resilient and tolerate a range of temperatures and conditions, which makes them an ideal choice for both indoor aquariums and outdoor mini-ponds.

Aquascaping:

• Plants: Floating plants like Frogbit or Water Lettuce enhance their top-view appeal. Submerged plants like Hornwort, Anacharis, and Rotala offer shelter and breeding surfaces.
• Hardscape: Smooth pebbles, moss-covered rocks, and gentle slopes for shallow areas.
• Botanicals: Indian Almond Leaves or alder cones can promote natural behavior and improve water quality.

Diet and Feeding:

Yurishisu Medaka are omnivores that thrive on a varied diet. Offer high-quality flakes, micro pellets, and occasional live or frozen treats like daphnia, baby brine shrimp, or moina for optimal health and coloration.

Tank Mates and Compatibility:

Peaceful and social, they do well in groups and with other gentle species like small rasboras, endlers, and dwarf shrimp. Avoid fin-nippers or larger aggressive species.

Shrimp Safe?:

Yes, safe with adult dwarf shrimp. Shrimp fry may be at risk unless plenty of hiding places are provided.

Breeding:

Like other medaka, Yurishisu Ricefish breed readily in the right conditions. Provide floating plants or spawning mops, and you'll often see females carrying fertilized eggs near their anal fins before depositing them. Hatchlings can be raised on infusoria or powdered fry food.

Recommended Tank Parameters:

• Temperature: 64° – 78° F (17.8° – 25.5° C)
• pH: 6.8 – 7.5
• KH: 3 – 10 dKH
• Minimum Tank Size: 5 gallons

Care Guidelines:
• Diet: Omnivorous. Accepts flakes, pellets, live, and frozen foods.
• Social Behavior: Peaceful and active. Best kept in groups of 6 or more.
• Origin: Tank bred
• Average Adult Size: 1.25 inches (3.2 cm)

products.title: Blue Dragon Guppy (Poecilia reticulata) – Tank Bred
products.body_html:
For the safety of our animals, we now ship live fish exclusively via UPS Next Day Air. If your order contains this item, you will only see UPS Next Day Air as a shipping option during checkout.

The Blue Dragon Guppy is a breathtaking variety of guppy that features a metallic sky-blue body, deep sapphire tail, and intricate dragon-scale patterning along its flanks. These guppies are tank-bred and display incredible coloration and finage that make them stand out in any aquarium. Their vibrant colors, active behavior, and ease of care make them an excellent addition to planted or community tanks.

What We Love About This Fish:

• Radiant Blue Coloration: Males boast a shimmering blue body with dragon-scale pattern and long, flowing fins.
• Peaceful and Hardy: Suitable for both beginners and experienced aquarists.
• Great for Breeding Projects: A prolific livebearer that adds motion and beauty to any tank.
• Active and Social: Constantly swimming and engaging with their surroundings.

Natural Habitat:

While guppies like the Blue Dragon are bred in aquariums, their wild ancestors originate from freshwater streams and pools in South America. These waters are typically slow-moving, warm, and densely vegetated. 

Aquascaping:

• Plants: Ideal choices include Java Moss, Hornwort, Water Sprite, and Amazon Sword. Floating plants like Frogbit or Duckweed help reduce light intensity and offer cover.
• Hardscape: Smooth river stones, lava rock, and Malaysian driftwood create an ideal setup without sharp edges.
• Botanicals: Indian Almond Leaves and Alder Cones can help support overall fish health by releasing beneficial tannins.

Diet and Feeding:

These guppies are omnivores and thrive on a varied diet. Feed high-quality flakes or micro pellets as a staple, with supplements of baby brine shrimp, daphnia, and bloodworms to enhance color and vitality.

Tank Mates and Compatibility:

Blue Dragon Guppies are peaceful and compatible with most other non-aggressive fish. Ideal tank mates include small tetras, rasboras, corydoras, snails, and other livebearers. Avoid housing with fin-nippers or aggressive species.

Shrimp Safe?:

Generally safe with adult freshwater shrimp, though guppies may eat very small shrimp or shrimplets. Provide dense cover or moss to protect baby shrimp.

Breeding:

Livebearers that breed readily in captivity. Males display courtship behavior and females give birth to live young after a 4-week gestation. A well-planted tank improves fry survival rates.

Recommended Tank Parameters:

• Temperature: 72° – 82° F (22° – 28° C)
• pH: 6.8 – 7.8
• KH: 8 – 12 dKH
• Minimum Tank Size: 10 gallons

Care Guidelines:

• Diet: Omnivorous; feed a variety of dry, frozen, and live foods.
• Social Behavior: Peaceful and active; best kept in groups.
• Origin: Tank-bred, not wild-caught.
• Average Adult Size: 1.5 – 2 inches (3.8 – 5 cm)
• Average Purchase Size: 1 – 1.5 inches (2.5 – 3.8 cm)

products.title: Croaking Gourami (Trichopsis vittata)
products.body_html:
* For the safety of our animals, we now ship live fish exclusively via UPS Next Day Air.  If your order contains this item, you will only see UPS Next Day Air as a shipping option during checkout *

 

This uncommon gourami is a small, colorful species that thrives in the planted aquarium!

 

Live Foods are great enrichment and what your fish crave! Try our Live Daphnia to give your fish a natural food source today!

 

The Croaking Dwarf Gourami (Trichopsis vittata) is a colorful, very small labyrinth fish. It inhabits many slow-moving waterways in Southeast Asia. Like all labyrinth fish, it possesses an air-breathing organ that supplements its gills and allows it to live in aquatic areas that are poor in oxygen. Its iridescent green, blue, and red coloration make it a striking addition to the nano and planted aquarium.

 

The Croaking Gourami requires an aquarium with dense vegetation and plenty of cover. A darker substrate will result in the most vivid coloration for this fish. Slow to moderate water movement is preferred, so turbulent conditions should be avoided. This fish is typically timid and peaceful with other fish, but males can be territorial with their own species. This is much less of an issue in larger aquariums. While it is possible to keep this fish with most peaceful invertebrates, it is also possible that it will go after dwarf shrimp, so vigilance and caution are necessary.

 

A balanced diet for the Croaking Gourami should consist of a live, frozen, and dry meaty foods. It will typically require live and frozen foods initially, but will usually accept dry foods in time. 

 




What We Like About This Fish:
  • Bright, multi-colored iridescent coloration 
  • Peaceful disposition 
  • An excellent, graceful addition to nano and planted aquariums
  • Accepts a variety of dry and frozen foods
  • Small size


RECOMMENDED TANK PARAMETERS:
  • Temperature:  72° - 82° F (22° - 28° C)
  • pH:  5.0 - 7.0
  • KH:  1 - 15 dKH
  • Minimum tank size:  10-15 gallons for a small group


CARE GUIDELINES: 
  • Diet:  Micropredator. Requires a varied diet of live, frozen, and freeze-dried foods.
  • Social behavior:  Peaceful and timid.  Can be kept in groups, but males can be territorial with one another. 
  • Origin:  Southeast Asia.
  • Average adult size:  2.4 - 2.8 inches (6 - 7 cm)
  • Average purchase size:  1+ inch (2.5+ cm)
 
All Aquatic Arts brand plants and animals come with a 100% live arrival guarantee, plus free email support!

products.title: Lake Kurumoi Sunset Dwarf Rainbowfish (Melanotaenia parva), Tank-Bred!
products.body_html:
* For the safety of our animals, we now ship live fish exclusively via UPS Next Day Air.  If your order contains this item, you will only see UPS Next Day Air as a shipping option during checkout *

 

The Lake Kurumoi Sunset Dwarf Rainbowfish is a beautiful and extremely colorful species that is sure to be the highlight of any aquarium!

 

The Lake Kurumoi Sunset Dwarf Rainbowfish (Melanotaenia parva) is an exceptionally colorful species that is native to New Guinea. It is a very active fish that lives in schools and looks great in a planted aquarium. This fish is one of the best examples of its name, displaying an incredible variety of colors, especially as it matures to adulthood. As with most larger rainbowfish, both males and females are very colorful, but mature males are deeper-bodied with the most intense coloration. 

 

The Lake Kurumoi Sunset Dwarf Rainbowfish is peaceful with most other fish species, although its size and activitiy can be intimidating to particularly shy fish. It is likely to prey on dwarf shrimp, but it is generally safe to keep with peaceful invertebrates that are too large to be considered prey. The most intense coloration will be displayed when this fish is kept in schools of 6 or more, including multiple males, in a planted, spacious aquarium to ensure that subdominant males are not overly harassed by dominant males. As with many rainbowfish, darker substrate will also often result in better coloration. 

 

While this fish will easily accept high quality dry foods, it should also be fed plenty of small frozen or live foods for optimal health and coloration.

 

What We Like About This Fish:
  • Outstanding coloration that is nearly unrivaled by other species
  • Active, schooling community fish that thrives in planted aquariums
  • Compatible with most fish and many invertebrates
  • Possible to breed in the aquarium
  • Adaptable to a wide variety of water conditions
 
RECOMMENDED TANK PARAMETERS:
  • Temperature:  70° - 80° F (21° - 27° C)
  • pH:  7.0 - 8.0
  • KH:  8 - 20 dKH
  • Minimum tank size:  55 gallons for a school of 6-8 adults
 
CARE GUIDELINES: 
  • Diet:  Omnivorous.  High quality dry foods will be accepted, but should be supplemented often with frozen and live foods.
  • Social behavior:  Schooling/shoaling, and should be kept in as large of groups as possible. Peaceful with most other fish and invertebrates too large to be considered prey.
  • Origin:  Tank-Bred, but indigenous to Lake Kurumoi, New Guinea
  • Average adult size:  4 inches (10 cm)
  • Average purchase size:  Medium: 1.75 - 2 inches (4.5 - 5 inches), Large: 2.75 - 3 inches (7 - 7.6 cm)

products.title: Dawn AKA Panda Tetra (Aphyocharax nattereri), Tank-Bred
products.body_html:
* For the safety of our animals, we now ship live fish exclusively via UPS Next Day Air.  If your order contains this item, you will only see UPS Next Day Air as a shipping option during checkout *

This active tetra is a superb addition to the planted aquarium, and it is an excellent choice for almost any Neotropical setup!

The Dawn Tetra (Aphyocharax nattereri) is a bold, active fish for the community and planted aquarium. This outgoing species is generally peaceful and is a wonderful addition to Amazon/South American-themed setups. It will add a great deal of motion to the tank and is very undemanding regarding care

The Dawn Tetra typically occupies the top and middle level of the water column, although it can often be seen swimming and feeding in the middle and bottom levels as well.   It is safe to keep with most other peaceful, small fish as well as peaceful dwarf and Geophagus cichlids. It may sometimes nip at the fins of slow-moving, long-finned fish, but it is otherwise regarded as very peaceful.  Adult dwarf shrimp are possibly safe tankmates as well, but adult Dawn Tetras may eat small dwarf shrimp and their fry.  Larger, peaceful invertebrates can also make good tankmates.  Since it is tank-bred, the Dawn Tetra is fairly adaptable, but its natural habitat contains soft, acidic water rich in tannins and leaf litter. It does not like overly bright lighting and it requires plenty of decor in the aquarium.

While not a picky eater, the Dawn Tetra will thrive and remain very colorful on a varied diet of meaty foods.  High-quality dry, frozen, and live meaty foods will all be readily accepted.  

     
    What We Like About This Fish:
    • Simple, yet elegant coloration
    • Peaceful disposition with fish and peaceful invertebrates
    • Very active and noticeable fish in any aquarium
    • Hardy and adaptable
    • Thrives in a heavily planted aquarium
     
    RECOMMENDED TANK PARAMETERS: 
    • Temperature:  71.6° - 80.6° F (22° - 27° C)
    • pH:  5.5 - 7.5 (more acidic water is preferred)
    • KH:  1 - 18 dKH
    • Minimum tank size:  15-20 gallons for a group of 8-12 adults
     
    CARE GUIDELINES:
    • Diet:  Mostly carnivorous.  Will  easily accept high quality dry, frozen, and live foods
    • Social behavior:  Peaceful, schooling/shoaling.
    • Origin:  Tank-bred, but indigenous to Brazil, Paraguay, and Bolivia
    • Average adult size:  1.2 - 1.4 inches (3 - 3.5 cm)
    • Average purchase size:  .5 - 1 inch (1.25 - 2.5 cm)

products.title: Gold Gourami (Trichopodus trichopterus 'Gold') - Tank Bred
products.body_html:
For the safety of our animals, we now ship live fish exclusively via UPS Next Day Air. If your order contains this item, you will only see UPS Next Day Air as a shipping option during checkout.

The Gold Gourami is a striking, selectively bred variant of the popular Three-Spot Gourami. With its shimmering golden-yellow body, subtle pearlescent sheen, and elegant fins, this peaceful fish adds both beauty and personality to the freshwater aquarium. These tank-bred specimens are hardy, adaptable, and an excellent choice for beginner and experienced aquarists alike.

What We Love About This Fish:

• Vibrant Coloration: Bright gold body with a soft iridescent glow.
• Peaceful and Hardy: Tolerant of a wide range of water conditions.
• Labyrinth Organ: Can breathe atmospheric air—great for low-oxygen environments.
• Distinct Personality: Intelligent, interactive, and often curious about their owners.

Natural Habitat:

The wild form of Trichopodus trichopterus originates from calm, slow-moving waters in Southeast Asia, such as rice paddies, marshes, and slow streams. These environments are typically dense with vegetation, driftwood, and leaf litter, providing cover and foraging opportunities.

Aquascaping:

• Plants: Floating plants like Amazon Frogbit or Duckweed for shade; rooted plants like Java Fern, Anubias, or Cryptocoryne.
• Hardscape: Use driftwood, smooth river rocks, and low caves to provide hiding areas.
• Botanicals: Indian Almond Leaves and Alder Cones help mimic natural tannin-rich environments and promote health.

Diet and Feeding:

Gold Gouramis are omnivorous and will readily accept high-quality flake foods, floating pellets, and freeze-dried or frozen treats like bloodworms, daphnia, and brine shrimp. Occasional live food will help enhance color and vitality.

Tank Mates and Compatibility:

Gold Gouramis are generally peaceful but may show mild territorial behavior, especially males. Avoid fin-nipping tank mates or other gouramis in smaller tanks. Suitable tank mates include peaceful tetras, rasboras, Corydoras, and larger shrimp species.

Shrimp Safe?:

Caution advised. While they may coexist peacefully with adult Amano shrimp, smaller ornamental shrimp and their fry may be at risk of being eaten.

Breeding:

Gold Gouramis are bubble nest builders. Males create floating nests and court females. A separate breeding tank with gentle filtration and floating plants is recommended for best success. Remove adults once eggs are laid to protect the fry.

Recommended Tank Parameters:

• Temperature: 72° - 82° F (22° - 28° C)
• pH: 6.5 - 7.5
• KH: 3 - 8 dKH
• Minimum Tank Size: 20 gallons

Care Guidelines:

• Diet: Omnivore – provide a mix of dry, frozen, and live foods.
• Social Behavior: Peaceful with caution—best kept singly or in a well-planned community.
• Origin: Tank-bred
• Average Adult Size: 4 – 6 inches (10 – 15 cm)
• Average Purchase Size: 1.5 – 2.5 inches (3.8 – 6.3 cm)

Add a splash of sunshine to your aquarium with the graceful and colorful Gold Gourami—a fantastic centerpiece fish with personality and flair.

Live Arrival Guarantee:

products.title: Long Finned Green Dragon Bristlenose Pleco (Ancistrus sp.) USA Bred
products.body_html:
Flowing Fins and Algae-Eating Power


For the safety of our animals, we now ship live fish exclusively via UPS Next Day Air. If your order contains this item, you will only see UPS Next Day Air as a shipping option during checkout.

The Long Finned Green Dragon Bristlenose Pleco is a dazzling tank-bred variety of Ancistrus, featuring vibrant green coloration and elegant, flowing fins that add graceful movement to any aquarium. These USA-bred plecos are more than just beautiful—they’re hardworking algae grazers and peaceful community members that thrive in a wide range of freshwater setups.

What We Love About This Fish:

• Elegant Long Finnage: Dramatic tail and pectoral fins create beautiful motion.
• Stunning Green Coloration: Deep green body with a metallic sheen.
• Algae-Eating Machine: Excellent at keeping tank surfaces clean.
• Peaceful Nature: Safe with most non-aggressive tankmates.
• Hardy and Adaptable: Tank-bred in the USA for resilience and ease of care.

Natural Habitat:

While this variant is selectively bred in the USA, its wild ancestors are native to South American rivers and streams. These slow-moving waters are rich in driftwood, leaf litter, and rocky areas—perfect for foraging and shelter.

Aquascaping:

• Plants: Ideal with Java Fern, Anubias, Bolbitis, and other low-light species.
• Hardscape: Include smooth river rocks, slate caves, and Malaysian driftwood to aid digestion.
• Botanicals: Indian Almond Leaves and Alder Cones help release tannins and create a more natural feel.

Behavior and Social Traits:

Long Finned Green Dragon Bristlenose Plecos are generally calm and curious, often venturing out even during the day.
• Peaceful community fish that won’t bother tankmates.
• Territorial males: Best to keep only one male per tank unless it's large with plenty of cover.
• Active foragers: Constantly grazing on driftwood, décor, and glass.

Diet and Feeding:

These plecos are omnivorous with a strong preference for plant matter. A well-rounded diet should include:
• High-quality algae wafers or spirulina-based pellets
• Blanched vegetables like zucchini, cucumber, and spinach
• Occasional protein treats such as shrimp pellets or frozen bloodworms

Tank Mates and Compatibility:

This peaceful species fits well in most community aquariums. Suitable tankmates include:
• Tetras, Rasboras, Guppies, Mollies
• Corydoras, Kuhli Loaches, and peaceful gouramis
• Avoid aggressive or fin-nipping species that may damage their long fins.

Shrimp Safe:

Generally safe with adult Neocaridina and Caridina shrimp. Provide moss, caves, and leaf litter to protect shrimp fry.

Breeding:

Males will claim caves and guard eggs laid by females. Provide multiple hiding places, stable parameters, and plenty of driftwood to support successful breeding.

Recommended Tank Parameters:

• Temperature: 72° - 82° F (22° - 28° C)
• pH: 6.5 - 7.5
• KH: 3 - 10 dKH
• Minimum Tank Size: 20 gallons (30+ gallons recommended for multiple plecos)

Care Guidelines:

• Diet: Omnivorous—focus on algae wafers, vegetables, and some protein.
• Social Behavior: Peaceful; males may become territorial with one another.
• Origin: Tank-bred in the USA.
• Average Adult Size: 4 - 5 inches (10 - 12 cm), excluding fin extensions
• Average Purchase Size: 1.5 - 2.5 inches (3.8 - 6.3 cm)

Why Choose the Long Finned Green Dragon Bristlenose Pleco?

This pleco offers the perfect blend of beauty and function. Its long, flowing fins and deep green color make it a showstopper, while its algae-eating ability and peaceful demeanor make it a valuable part of any freshwater cleanup crew.

The Assorted Dwarf Rasbora (Boraras sp.) pack offers a stunning collection of some of the smallest and most colorful freshwater fish in the hobby. This selection may include various species such as the Chili Rasbora (Boraras brigittae), Strawberry Rasbora (Boraras naevus), Phoenix Rasbora (Boraras merah), Least Rasbora (Boraras urophthalmoides), and Pygmy Rasbora (Boraras maculatus)—each known for its bright coloration, peaceful nature, and shoaling behavior.

NOTE: This pack may contain a mix of different species or all of the same species depending on availability at the time of shipping. A mixed assortment is not guaranteed.

Despite possible species variation, all Boraras sp. can shoal together peacefully and thrive in the same water parameters, making this pack a perfect addition to any planted nano aquarium.

What We Love About This Fish:

• Vibrant Variety: May include a colorful mix of Boraras species
• Nano-Sized: Ideal for small tanks and aquascapes
• Peaceful Nature: Great for community tanks with other small species
• Shoaling Behavior: Best kept in groups for natural movement and color

Natural Habitat:

Each Boraras species originates from slow-moving, heavily shaded forest streams in Southeast Asia. These environments are rich in leaf litter and plant debris with soft, acidic water.

Aquascaping:

• Plants: Fine-leaved and floating plants like Java Moss, Frogbit, and Cryptocoryne
• Hardscape: Driftwood, leaf litter, and soft substrate for a natural feel
• Botanicals: Indian Almond Leaves and Alder Cones help recreate their native habitat

Diet and Feeding:

These fish are micro-predators and do best with a varied diet. Feed high-quality micro pellets, crushed flakes, and live or frozen foods like baby brine shrimp, daphnia, and cyclops for optimal health and color.

Tank Mates and Compatibility:

Perfect for peaceful community setups with other nano fish like ember tetras, celestial pearl danios, and dwarf gouramis. Avoid large or aggressive fish.

Shrimp Safe:

Yes, generally safe with adult dwarf shrimp. As with most small fish, they may eat very young shrimp fry, so plenty of plant cover is helpful.

Breeding:

Egg scatterers that may spawn in heavily planted tanks, but eggs and fry are often eaten unless separated or protected. Spawning in fine-leaved plants or mosses is typical.

products.body_html:

We are unable to ship livestock from this listing to Maine. If you reside in Maine and try to checkout with this in your cart you will be unable to checkout and receive an error message until it is removed from the cart.

The Lollipop Prawn (Macrobrachium gracilirostre) is an incredibly unique, large freshwater prawn named for its elongated claws with vivid striped patterns that resemble candy or lollipops. This rare, wild-collected species is a showstopper for species-specific tanks and experienced keepers looking for something striking, interactive, and truly unusual.

Though peaceful toward fish too large to be considered prey, this prawn can show aggression toward other shrimp or smaller inverts and is best kept in a tank of its own or with select tankmates.

What We Love About This Species:

• Incredible Appearance: Long, banded claws give this prawn its “lollipop” name
• Rare and Wild-Collected: Uncommon in the hobby, and highly sought-after
• Personality-Packed: Curious and expressive, often visible and interactive
• Large and Impressive: Reaches a substantial size with a commanding presence

Natural Habitat:

Native to rivers, streams, and estuaries across Southeast Asia, Lollipop Prawns are typically found in mildly brackish to freshwater areas with sandy or muddy bottoms, submerged roots, and plenty of cover.

Aquascaping:

• Plants: Tough plants like Java Fern, Anubias, or floating plants recommended—avoid delicate species
• Hardscape: Driftwood, caves, and hiding spots are essential, especially during molting
• Substrate: Sand or fine gravel to mimic riverbeds and allow easy movement

Diet and Feeding:

Omnivorous and opportunistic—will accept sinking pellets, algae wafers, frozen foods (like bloodworms and brine shrimp), and fresh veggies like blanched zucchini or spinach. Provide a protein-rich diet to support growth and coloration.

Tank Mates and Compatibility:

Use caution. While generally not aggressive toward larger fish, they may catch and eat small fish or shrimp. Best kept alone, in a pair, or with robust fish that can’t be easily caught (like larger danios or rainbowfish).

Breeding:

Lollipop Prawns require brackish conditions for larval development, making them difficult to breed in captivity. All individuals offered are wild-collected.

Recommended Tank Parameters:

• Temperature: 72° - 80° F (22° - 27° C)
• pH: 7.5 - 8.0
• KH: 4 - 12
• Minimum Tank Size: 20 gallons for a single adult

Care Guidelines:

• Diet: Omnivorous—needs variety and protein
• Social Behavior: Semi-aggressive; best kept alone or with compatible tank mates
• Origin: Wild-collected
• Average Adult Size: 3 – 4.5 inches (7.6 – 11.4 cm), excluding claws
• Average Purchase Size: 2 – 3 inches (5 – 7.6 cm)

Add a striking conversation piece to your freshwater collection with the Lollipop Prawn (Macrobrachium gracilirostre)—a bold, brilliant species unlike any other in the hobby.

products.title: Burmese Marble Catfish (Akysis prashadi)
products.body_html:
For the safety of our animals, we now ship live fish exclusively via UPS Next Day Air. If your order contains this item, you will only see UPS Next Day Air as a shipping option during checkout.

The Burmese Marble Catfish (Akysis prashadi) is a rare and captivating nano catfish species collected from the fast-flowing streams of Myanmar. Known for its beautifully mottled black and tan body and its compact size, this species thrives in peaceful community or species-specific aquariums with plenty of cover. Despite their shy nature, they are highly sought after for their unique appearance and bottom-dwelling behavior.

What We Love About This Fish:

• Striking Pattern: Beautiful marbled contrast of black and tan
• Peaceful Bottom-Dweller: Perfect for nano and planted tanks
• Rare and Uncommon: A standout for collectors and species enthusiasts
• Low Bioload: Excellent option for small aquariums

Natural Habitat:

Akysis prashadi is native to cool, fast-moving streams in Myanmar, where it lives among leaf litter, rocks, and submerged roots. These environments are highly oxygenated and shaded, with a sandy or gravel substrate.

Aquascaping:

• Plants: Java Fern, Anubias, Cryptocoryne, and other low-light tolerant species
• Hardscape: Rounded stones, driftwood, and leaf litter to mimic streambeds
• Botanicals: Indian Almond Leaves and Catappa Bark to provide cover and tannins

Diet and Feeding:

This species is a micro-predator and scavenger that feeds on insect larvae, small crustaceans, and detritus in the wild. In the aquarium, offer a varied diet of sinking micro pellets, frozen bloodworms, daphnia, and tubifex. Feed at night or during low-light hours when they are most active.

Tank Mates and Compatibility:

Very peaceful and reclusive. Best kept with other small, non-aggressive tank mates such as rasboras, small tetras, dwarf shrimp, and snails. Avoid larger or boisterous species that may outcompete them at feeding time.

Shrimp Safe?:

Generally considered shrimp-safe, especially with adult Neocaridina or Caridina. May opportunistically consume very small shrimp or fry, so provide hiding spots if mixing.

Breeding:

Not commonly bred in captivity, and little is known about their reproduction. Observations suggest that they are egg layers, but successful spawning is rare.

Recommended Tank Parameters:

• Temperature: 70° - 77° F (21° - 25° C)
• pH: 6.0 - 7.5
• KH: 2 - 8
• Minimum Tank Size: 10 gallons (for a small group)

Care Guidelines:

• Diet: Carnivorous. Prefers protein-rich sinking foods.
• Social Behavior: Peaceful, shy bottom-dweller. Can be kept singly or in groups.
• Origin: Wild-collected from Myanmar
• Average Adult Size: 1.5 inches (3.8 cm)
• Average Purchase Size: 0.75 – 1.25 inches (1.9 – 3.2 cm)

The Burmese Marble Catfish (Akysis prashadi) is a hidden gem in the nano fish world—perfect for hobbyists who enjoy unique species and creating naturalistic aquascapes with subtle, beautiful detail.

Live Arrival Guarantee
We stand by the health and quality of every animal we ship. All Aquatic Arts animals come with a 100% Live Arrival Guarantee!

products.title: Zig Zag Tetra (Piabina argentea) – Tank-Bred
products.body_html:
For the safety of our animals, we now ship live fish exclusively via UPS Next Day Air. If your order contains this item, you will only see UPS Next Day Air as a shipping option during checkout.

The Zig Zag Tetra (Piabina argentea) is an active and sleek midwater swimmer known for its bold black lateral stripe that sharply zigzags through a shimmering silver body. Native to the fast-moving rivers and streams of South America, this lesser-known species is perfect for aquarists looking to add something truly unique to their freshwater community. These tank-bred specimens are hardy, peaceful, and ideal for planted setups with gentle flow.

What We Love About This Fish:

• Distinctive Pattern: Bold zigzag stripe adds visual interest to the aquarium
• Hardy and Peaceful: Ideal for peaceful community tanks and planted aquascapes
• Constant Motion: Active schooling fish that brings energy to the midwater column
• Underrated Gem: A unique alternative to more common tetras

Natural Habitat:

Native to the clearwater and whitewater streams of Brazil and neighboring regions, the Zig Zag Tetra is found in environments with moderate current, sandy or rocky bottoms, and abundant leaf litter or submerged vegetation.

Aquascaping:

• Plants: Fine-leaved species like Water Sprite, Ludwigia, and Rotala work well
• Hardscape: River stones, driftwood, and smooth gravel substrate mimic natural surroundings
• Botanicals: Indian Almond Leaves and seed pods provide tannins and cover

Diet and Feeding:

Zig Zag Tetras are micro-predators in the wild and do well on a diet of high-quality flakes, micro pellets, and frozen or live foods such as daphnia, brine shrimp, and bloodworms. Offer variety for best coloration and energy.

Tank Mates and Compatibility:

These tetras are peaceful and do best in groups of 6 or more. Great companions include other peaceful tetras, rasboras, dwarf cichlids, Corydoras, and smaller loaches. Avoid large or aggressive tank mates.

Shrimp Safe?:

Generally safe with adult shrimp, but like most tetras, they may snack on baby shrimp. Provide plenty of hiding spots and dense plant cover if keeping with shrimp.

Breeding:

Zig Zag Tetras are egg scatterers and can be bred in captivity with a separate spawning tank, dim lighting, and fine-leaved plants or spawning mops. Remove adults after spawning to protect eggs.

Recommended Tank Parameters:

• Temperature: 72° - 80° F (22° - 27° C)
• pH: 6.0 - 7.5
• KH: 2 - 10
• Minimum Tank Size: 15 gallons (for a group of 6 or more)

Care Guidelines:

• Diet: Omnivorous. Provide flakes, micro pellets, and live/frozen protein-rich foods.
• Social Behavior: Peaceful, active schoolers. Keep in groups for confidence and natural behavior.
• Origin: Tank-bred
• Average Adult Size: 2.5 inches (6.4 cm)
• Average Purchase Size: 1.25 - 1.75 inches (3.2 - 4.4 cm)

Add a flash of silver and a bold, zigzagging stripe to your aquarium with the energetic and elegant Zig Zag Tetra (Piabina argentea)—a perfect addition for any peaceful community tank looking for something different!

Live Arrival Guarantee
We stand by the health and quality of every animal we ship. All Aquatic Arts animals come with a 100% Live Arrival Guarantee!

products.title: Schuberti Barb (Barbodes semifasciolatus var. schuberti) – Tank-Bred
products.body_html:
For the safety of our animals, we now ship live fish exclusively via UPS Next Day Air. If your order contains this item, you will only see UPS Next Day Air as a shipping option during checkout.

The Schuberti Barb, also known as the Golden Barb, is a vibrant, active schooling fish known for its striking golden-yellow coloration with scattered orange and green iridescence. A cultivated variant of the native Chinese Barb (Barbodes semifasciolatus), this peaceful community fish brings energetic motion and a splash of sunshine to any freshwater aquarium. These are tank-bred specimens raised with care and consistency to thrive in a variety of setups.

What We Love About This Fish:

• Golden Coloration: Vibrant yellow and orange hues with subtle metallic green tones
• Community Friendly: Peaceful and active—perfect for lively planted tanks
• Hardy and Adaptable: Great for beginners and seasoned aquarists alike
• Schooling Behavior: Best kept in groups for a dynamic and natural display

Natural Habitat:

Although the wild form originates from rivers and streams in Southeast Asia, the Schuberti Barb is a selectively bred variant that has adapted well to life in aquariums. These fish naturally prefer moderate flow, dense vegetation, and clean, well-oxygenated water.

Aquascaping:

• Plants: Java Fern, Anubias, Vallisneria, Hornwort, and floating plants like Frogbit
• Hardscape: Smooth river stones, driftwood, and sandy or fine gravel substrate
• Botanicals: Indian Almond Leaves or Alder Cones to provide natural tannins and soft cover

Diet and Feeding:

Schuberti Barbs are omnivores and will thrive on a varied diet. Offer high-quality flakes or pellets as a staple, supplemented with live or frozen foods such as brine shrimp, bloodworms, or daphnia. They'll also nibble on algae wafers and blanched vegetables like spinach or zucchini.

Tank Mates and Compatibility:

These barbs are peaceful but active, so they’re best kept with other fast-moving community fish like danios, rasboras, and peaceful loaches. Avoid long-finned or slow-moving species that may be stressed by their energetic nature.

Shrimp Safe?:

Generally safe with adult shrimp, but may prey on very small shrimp or shrimp fry. Heavily planted tanks with lots of hiding places can reduce risk.

Breeding:

Schuberti Barbs are egg scatterers and will spawn readily in the right conditions. Provide fine-leaved plants or spawning mops, and separate the adults after spawning to prevent them from eating the eggs.

Recommended Tank Parameters:

• Temperature: 72° - 79° F (22° - 26° C)
• pH: 6.0 - 7.5
• KH: 4 - 10
• Minimum Tank Size: 20 gallons (for a group of 6 or more)

Care Guidelines:

• Diet: Omnivorous. Feed a variety of flakes, pellets, and live/frozen foods.
• Social Behavior: Peaceful, active schoolers. Best kept in groups of 6 or more.
• Origin: Tank-bred
• Average Adult Size: 2 - 2.5 inches (5 - 6.4 cm)
• Average Purchase Size: 1 - 1.5 inches (2.5 - 3.8 cm)

Add color, motion, and charm to your freshwater aquarium with the energetic and beautiful Schuberti Barb. Their golden shine and active nature make them an irresistible choice for any peaceful community setup!

products.title: Purple Bacopa (Bacopa salzamanii) - Bunched
products.body_html:
The Purple Bacopa (Bacopa salzamanii) is a striking stem plant known for its vibrant coloration and adaptability. This bunched version features multiple healthy stems bundled together and ready to plant. With hues that can range from deep green to rich violet under high lighting, this plant brings eye-catching contrast to any aquascape.

Ideal for midground to background placement, Purple Bacopa is an excellent choice for creating depth and texture. Its moderate growth rate and easy propagation make it perfect for aquarists of all experience levels.

Features We Love:

  • Unique Purple Tint: When grown under high light and with nutrient-rich water, leaves can develop a vibrant purple hue.

  • Versatile Placement: Works well as a midground or background plant in most freshwater tanks.

  • Beginner Friendly: Hardy and relatively undemanding, even in low-tech setups.

  • Shrimp-Safe Habitat: Offers great grazing and hiding spots for shrimp and other small inverts.

Care Guidelines:

  • Light: Medium to high

  • Temperature: 68° - 82° F (20° - 28° C)

  • pH Range: 6.0 - 7.5

  • Growth Rate: Moderate

  • Ideal Placement: Midground to background

Pro Tips:

  • Enhance Coloration: Use high-intensity lighting and iron-rich fertilizers to bring out the most vibrant purple tones.

  • Regular Trimming: Trim tops and replant cuttings to create a bushier, fuller appearance.

  • Root Tabs Help: Supplement with root tabs if planted in inert substrates.

  • CO₂ Optional: Grows well without CO₂, but will thrive even more with supplementation.

  • Acclimate Gently: Bunched plants may shed some lower leaves after planting—this is normal during transition.

Brighten your aquascape with Purple Bacopa (Bacopa salzamanii) - Bunched, a colorful and reliable stem plant that adds elegance and dimension to your freshwater setup!

products.title: Flame Moss (Taxiphyllum sp. “Flame”) Tissue Culture
products.body_html:
The fronds of flame moss grow upward, making this plant very unique and distinctive!
Flame Moss (Taxiphyllum sp. “Flame”), is a rare but increasingly popular and attractive species of creeping moss for the freshwater aquarium.  This is a great plant to keep in any freshwater aquarium due to its low lighting requirements and ease of care.  What makes this moss distinctive is the long, curly vertical growth of its stems, which resemble an underwater green “fire.”  Due to the nature of its stem growth, Flame Moss is an ideal moss for horizontal placement in the aquarium.  It also provides cultivation areas for tiny live food sources such as infusoria and biofilm.  Once established in the aquarium, Flame Moss can grow into a very impressive, large mat that will contribute to a stunningly beautiful aquascape.
 
This is a very easy plant to keep without much extra care, but like all plants, it can benefit from CO2 supplementation (although this is not required).  Like many mosses, it does not typically respond positively to most chemical plant supplements.  Many aquarists report success with this moss purely through keeping a well-maintained aquarium with no supplementation.
 

This listing is for the tissue culture form of Flame Moss. Tissue cultures are superior to traditional forms of aquarium plants in many ways. They are produced in a completely sterile environment which eliminates the possibility of them carrying pest snails or algae spores. They have a shelf life (before introduction to the aquarium) of several months if properly maintained and they are housed in a nutrient gel until introduced to the aquarium. To introduce a tissue culture plant to the aquarium, simply rinse off as much nutrient gel as possible, then plant as usual. The nutrient gel will not harm your aquarium.  

 

Packaging may vary. Tissue Culture plants may arrive in bag or cup form. 


What We Like About This Plant:
  • Tissue cultures contain no unwanted pest animals or algae
  • Extremely attractive alternative to Java Moss
  • Can be attached to almost any aquarium décor or left floating in loose form
  • Low light requirements
  • Little to no supplements needed
Care Guidelines:
  • Temperature: 75° - 84° F (23.9° - 28.9° C)
  • pH: 5.8 - 7.5
  • Lighting: Low to moderate
  • Origin: Lab-grown tissue culture, but indigenous to Southeast Asia
  • Aquarium Placement: Any
  • Care: Easy

products.title: Repashy Morning Wood Isopod
products.body_html:
Conditioning Gel Food for Breeding Fish & Growing Fry

Repashy Spawn & Grow (Freshwater) is a high-protein, nutrient-dense gel premix designed to support breeding fish and developing fry. Loaded with krill, eggs, and essential vitamins, this formula delivers the calories and nutrition needed for successful spawning and fast, healthy growth in juvenile fish.

At Aquatic Arts, we use Spawn & Grow to condition breeding groups and raise fry across a wide range of species. Whether you're prepping livebearers, corydoras, tetras, or dwarf cichlids for spawning—or growing out a new generation—this food helps ensure strong reproductive health and fast fry development.

Why We Love It:

  • Ideal for Breeding & Fry Growth: Supports reproductive health and juvenile development.

  • High-Protein & Calorie-Rich: Made with krill meal, egg, squid, and essential nutrients.

  • Versatile Feeding: Perfect for direct feeding, blending into other gels, or use as a fry paste.

  • Trusted by Aquatic Arts: Used in-house to prepare fish for spawning and raise healthy fry.

When Should You Use Spawn & Grow?

  • Before Spawning: Use to condition adult fish for breeding success.

  • During Fry Development: Provides optimal nutrition during early stages of growth.

  • Post-Spawning Recovery: Helps fish regain strength and body condition.

This formula is especially helpful for species like apistogrammas, livebearers, bettas, corydoras, rainbowfish, gouramis, and barbs—but is suitable for nearly all freshwater omnivores during high-nutrient phases.

How to Use:

  1. Boil water using stove, microwave, or kettle.

  2. Mix 1 part powder with 2–3 parts boiling water, stirring until smooth.

  3. Pour into a mold or container and let it set at room temperature.

  4. Once firm, cut into small cubes or slabs for feeding.

For Fry: Mix the powder with water into a slurry or paste for easier consumption.

Storage Tip: Store finished gel in the refrigerator for up to 2 weeks, or freeze for up to 6 months.

Full Ingredients:

Krill Meal, Whole Egg, Squid Meal, Pea Protein Isolate, Whey Protein Isolate, Alfalfa Leaf Meal, Fish Meal, Stabilized Rice Bran, Dried Seaweed Meal, Dried Brewer’s Yeast, Coconut Meal, Lecithin, Ground Flaxseed, Algae Meal, Locust Bean Gum, Citric Acid, Potassium Citrate, Taurine, Dried Kelp, Stinging Nettle, Garlic, Watermelon, Rose Hips, Hibiscus Flower, Calendula Flower, Marigold Flower, Paprika, Turmeric, Salt, Calcium Propionate and Potassium Sorbate (preservatives), Magnesium Amino Acid Chelate, Zinc Methionine Hydroxy Analogue Chelate, Manganese Methionine Hydroxy Analogue Chelate, Copper Methionine Hydroxy Analogue Chelate, Selenium Yeast.

Vitamins: Vitamin A Supplement, Vitamin D3 Supplement, Choline Chloride, Calcium L-Ascorbyl-2-Monophosphate, Vitamin E Supplement, Niacin, Beta Carotene, Pantothenic Acid, Riboflavin, Pyridoxine Hydrochloride, Thiamine Mononitrate, Folic Acid, Biotin, Vitamin B-12 Supplement, Menadione Sodium Bisulfite Complex.

Guaranteed Analysis:

  • Crude Protein: min. 45%

  • Crude Fat: min. 12%

  • Crude Fiber: max. 5%

  • Moisture: max. 8%

  • Ash: max. 10%

Please Note:

This product is not for human consumption. Feed as part of a complete diet tailored to the life stage of your fish.

Get your fish ready for breeding success or supercharge fry growth with Repashy Spawn & Grow—a trusted formula used by the Aquatic Arts team to raise the next generation.

products.title: Blue Amur Taiwanese Bitterling (Paracheilognathus himantegus)
products.body_html:
* For the safety of our animals, we now ship live fish exclusively via UPS Next Day Air.  If your order contains this item, you will only see UPS Next Day Air as a shipping option during checkout *

This colorful, subtropical bitterling is known to tolerate cold temperatures and can be a great fish for controlling pond algae and mosquitoes, or it can be a superb fish for the planted aquarium!

We are sorry, but we cannot ship this species to Michigan or Wisconsin. 
The Blue Amur Taiwanese Bitterling (Paracheilognathus himantegus), also known as the Tanakia Bitterling, is a very interesting, yet fairly uncommon, subtropical cyprinid species that is native to China and Taiwan. It schools and behaves similarly to many peaceful barb species and is a wonderful species for the subtropical planted aquarium, or even for outdoor ponds in some areas of the US, where it is beneficial as an algae and mosquito larvae eater and parasite-eating companion to koi. Both sexes of this fish will often develop bright red coloration on the fins, but males generally have the most intense coloration.
 
Care is simple for the Blue Amur Taiwanese Bitterling. It is tolerant of a wide range of water parameters, but it prefers fairly neutral pH and will thrive in an aquarium with ample filtration and moderate to high water flow. This active fish is known to jump occasionally, so a secure aquarium lid is recommended. It will readily eat most high-quality dry foods, but it should also be offered live, frozen, or freeze-dried foods.  
 
As stated above, the Blue Amur Taiwanese Bitterling is a peaceful fish, but it may prey on dwarf shrimp and other very small invertebrates. It does not seem to bother most snails, and we have found it to be generally peaceful with most larger shrimp (such as Amano, Flower, Green Lace, and Vampire Shrimp) as long as they are too large to be considered easy prey. The Blue Amur Taiwanese Bitterling is a schooling fish that should be kept in a group of at least 6 to 8 fish.  
 
If these requirements are met, the Blue Amur Taiwanese Bitterling will add a great deal of activity and attractive appearance to the aquarium or pond!
 
 
What We Like About This Fish:
  • Red fin coloration in mature specimens
  • Peaceful disposition with fish and peaceful larger invertebrates
  • Very active and noticeable fish in any aquarium
  • Hardy and undemanding
  • Excellent algae eater
 
RECOMMENDED TANK PARAMETERS:
  • Temperature:  59° - 82° F (15° - 28° C)
  • pH:  6.5 - 7.5
  • KH:  4 - 10 dKH
  • Minimum tank size:  40 gallons
 
CARE GUIDELINES:
  • Diet:  Omnivorous. Typically accepts dry foods, but also requires a variety of live and frozen foods with plenty of vegetable matter
  • Social behavior:  Peaceful, schooling/shoaling
  • Origin:  China, Taiwan
  • Average adult size: 2.5 - 3.5 inches (6 - 8 cm)
  • Average purchase size:  1 inch (2.5 cm) or larger

products.title: Rose Red Rotala (Rotala macrandra), bunch
products.body_html:
This plant has possibly the most potential of any aquatic plant for deep red coloration!

Rose Red Rotala (Rotala macrandra) is an exceptionally beautiful plant that is highly appreciated by aquarists. It is much more demanding than many other Rotala species, but it is extremely rewarding when given excellent to optimal care. With the correct water parameters, high lighting, and CO2 supplementation when possible, this plant will become almost unimaginably deep red from top to bottom! We do receive this plant in it's emersed growth form so it's leaves will look different than what is pictured until it starts to grow new submerged growth.

The stems of Rose Red Rotala should be planted far enough apart that the light will penetrate all the way to the bottom of the stem. High lighting is possibly the most important factor in keeping this plant in optimal condition. Fairly high phosphate and low nitrate levels are also preferred. Nutrient-rich substrate is also a necessity. CO2 is not absolutely necessary if these other factors are met, but it certainly is beneficial. The fastest growth will happen in soft water. If its needs are met, Rose Red Rotala will develop side shoots, which can be clipped and replanted as they reach enough length to not be overshadowed by other larger plants.

We are selling this plant in bunches of 4-6 stems each.

No aquarium plant distributor/nursery/wholesaler/retailer can or will guarantee their plants to be 100% snail free. We can not guarantee our plants will be snail free.

What We Like About This Plant:

  • Full, deep red coloration with proper care
  • Moderately prolific and easy to prune
  • Easily among the very most beautiful aquarium plants worldwide
  • Breathtaking focal point for the planted aquarium

Care Guidelines:

  • Temperature: 65° - 78° F (18° - 26° C)
  • pH: 6.0 - 7.0
  • Lighting: High, more red coloration is achieved with higher lighting, high phosphates, low nitrates, and CO2
  • Origin: Cultivated in nurseries, but indigenous to India
  • Aquarium placement: Middle
  • Care: Moderate to High
