products.title: Fancy Black Tiger Shrimp (Caridina sp. “Black Tiger”) – SS Grade
products.body_html:
For the safety of these shrimp and your investment, we only ship this species exclusively via UPS 1 Day Service. If your order contains this item, you will only see UPS 1 Day as a shipping option during checkout.

The Fancy Black Tiger Shrimp is a rare and visually striking freshwater Caridina shrimp known for its deep black body coloration, sharp leg banding, and unique tiger-style markings. The SS Grade variety features a bolder, more consistent black with excellent contrast, making it a premium choice for experienced shrimp keepers and aquascapers looking for a dramatic centerpiece shrimp.

What We Love About This Shrimp:

• Stunning Black Coloration: SS Grade shrimp show rich, velvety black bodies with defined markings.
• Tiger Patterning: Classic banding that gives these shrimp their “Tiger” name.
• Great for Advanced Hobbyists: A rare Caridina shrimp with show-quality appeal.
• Peaceful and Active: Always grazing and exploring their environment.

Natural Habitat:

While the Fancy Black Tiger Shrimp is a selectively bred variety, its wild ancestors originate from soft, acidic freshwater habitats in Southeast Asia. These areas are typically shaded with dense leaf litter and minimal flow, creating a calm, tannin-rich environment ideal for shrimp.

Aquascaping Recommendations:

• Plants: Java Moss, Bucephalandra, Subwassertang, Anubias Nana Petite
• Hardscape: Malaysian Driftwood, Cholla Wood, Dragon Stone
• Botanicals: Indian Almond Leaves, Alder Cones, Catappa Bark – all help provide biofilm and stabilize water chemistry

Diet and Feeding:

Fancy Black Tiger Shrimp are excellent scavengers that feed on biofilm, algae, and decaying plant matter in the aquarium. For a well-rounded and nutrient-rich diet, we recommend rotating the following foods:

  • Aquatic Arts Snowflake Shrimp Food
  • Aquatic Arts Pollen Granules
  • Aquatic Arts Algae Wafers
  • Blanched vegetables such as spinach or zucchini (offered sparingly)

A varied diet helps support healthy molting, reproduction, and color vibrancy.

Tank Mates and Compatibility:

These shrimp do best in species-only or shrimp-focused tanks. Suitable tank mates include small, peaceful nano fish like chili rasboras, as well as other non-aggressive Caridina. Avoid keeping them with large or predatory fish.

Shrimp Safe:

Yes, though mixing with other Caridina types may result in interbreeding. Keep this in mind if you're working on maintaining a pure colony.

Breeding:

Breeding Fancy Black Tiger Shrimp is possible in a stable, mature tank with soft, acidic water. Females carry fertilized eggs under their abdomen for 3–4 weeks until hatching. Ensure lots of moss, botanicals, and hiding spots for shrimplets to thrive.

Recommended Tank Parameters:

• Temperature: 68° - 74° F (20° - 23° C)
• pH: 5.5 - 6.5
• KH: 0 - 2
• GH: 4 - 6
• TDS: 100 - 160 ppm
• Minimum Tank Size: 5 gallons (10+ gallons preferred for colonies)

Care Guidelines:

• Diet: Biofilm, algae, and high-quality shrimp foods like Aquatic Arts Snowflake, Pollen Granules, and Algae Wafers
• Social Behavior: Peaceful; best in groups of 5 or more
• Origin: Tank-bred (SS Grade)
• Average Adult Size: 1 – 1.25 inches (2.5 – 3.2 cm)
• Average Purchase Size: 0.5 – 0.75 inches (1.3 – 1.9 cm)

Bring mystery and elegance to your shrimp tank with SS Grade Fancy Black Tiger Shrimp—a must-have for advanced aquarists and serious Caridina collectors.

products.title: Snow Zebra Sulawesi Shrimp (Caridina sp) - Bred by Aquatic Arts
products.body_html:
An Enchanting Sulawesi Beauty with Delicate Stripes and Snowy Elegance!

 

For the safety of our animals, we now ship our live Sulawesi shrimp exclusively via UPS 1 Day Service. If your order contains this item, you will only see UPS 1 Day as a shipping option during checkout.

At Aquatic Arts, we are dedicated to the world of freshwater invertebrates, and our passion for shrimp breeding has led us to offer some of the most unique and stunning species available. One of our latest achievements is the successful in-house breeding of the Snow Zebra Sulawesi Shrimp (Caridina sp.). This strikingly beautiful shrimp is now available tank-raised and thriving under our expert care.

Why We Love the Snow Zebra Sulawesi Shrimp

  • Unique Coloration: With its icy blue and white tones, this shrimp resembles a miniature work of art, standing out in any aquarium setup.

  • Bred for Success: Our tank-bred Snow Zebra Sulawesi Shrimp are adapted to aquarium life, making them more resilient and easier to care for compared to wild-caught specimens.

  • Active and Fascinating: These shrimp are constantly foraging, grazing on biofilm, and displaying intriguing social behaviors. Unlike many other Sulawesi shrimp, they are not shy and will actively swim around the tank.

Natural Habitat and Ideal Tank Conditions

Sulawesi shrimp in general originate from Lake Towuti and other ancient lakes of Sulawesi, known for their crystal-clear waters, unique biodiversity, and mineral-rich substrates. To mimic their natural habitat, we recommend:

  • Temperature: 78° - 86°F (25° - 30°C)

  • pH: 7.5 - 8.5

  • KH: 3 - 8

  • TDS: 150 - 250

  • Minimum Tank Size: 10 gallons (recommended colony setup for best results)

  • Filtration & Flow: A sponge filter or gentle flow to maintain pristine water conditions

  • Lighting: Moderate to high, as Sulawesi shrimp are accustomed to well-lit environments

  • Substrate & Hardscape: Fine sand, smooth stones, and mineral-rich materials to encourage biofilm growth

To ensure the most stable water parameters, we condition our breeding tanks with Salty Shrimp Sulawesi 7.5 remineralizer, which creates the optimal environment not only for Snow Zebra Shrimp but also for other Sulawesi shrimp species in our program.

Feeding and Diet

Snow Zebra Sulawesi Shrimp are omnivorous and require a well-balanced diet to maintain their vibrant coloration and health. Their diet should include:

  • Aquatic Arts Algae Wafers

  • Bacter AE – a micro-powdered food supplement rich in probiotics and beneficial bacteria. It supports biofilm development and improves digestion and survival rates in shrimp colonies.

  • Biofilm & Algae (an essential part of their natural diet)

  • Blanched Vegetables (such as cucumber and zucchini)

  • Protein Supplements (like freeze-dried or frozen brine shrimp and daphnia, fed occasionally)

  • Indian Almond Leaves or Botanicals (to enhance their environment and provide supplemental nutrition)

Tank Mates and Compatibility

The Snow Zebra Sulawesi Shrimp thrives in a species-only or Sulawesi-themed setup. These shrimp are best kept in a dedicated tank with:

  • Other Sulawesi Shrimp Species (e.g., Cardinal Shrimp, White Orchid Shrimp)

  • Sulawesi Snails (Rabbit Snails, Tylomelania species)

We’ve observed a surprising synergy between Snow Zebra Shrimp and Rabbit Snails. While we can't fully explain why, tanks with these peaceful snails consistently yield healthier, more active shrimp behavior and better breeding success.

  • No Aggressive Fish (Most traditional aquarium fish, even nano species, are not ideal tank mates)

Breeding Snow Zebra Sulawesi Shrimp

One of the biggest advantages of our in-house breeding program is that our Snow Zebra Sulawesi Shrimp are already acclimated to aquarium conditions, making them more likely to breed successfully for hobbyists. Key factors for breeding success include:

  • Keeping a healthy colony with a good male-to-female ratio

  • Providing stable, high-quality water conditions with minimal fluctuations

  • Using Salty Shrimp Sulawesi 7.5 remineralizer to maintain ideal parameters

  • Feeding a varied and mineral-rich diet, including Bacter AE and fresh vegetables

  • Offering hiding spots and fine substrates for egg-laying and molting security

  • Maintaining a consistent temperature is crucial—temperature fluctuations can significantly slow the breeding process

  • Including piles of rocks and natural hardscape to promote biofilm growth and provide safe spaces for molting shrimp

Why Choose Bred by Aquatic Arts?

Unlike wild-caught shrimp, which can be delicate and difficult to transition into aquariums, our Bred by Aquatic Arts Snow Zebra Sulawesi Shrimp are already adjusted to tank life. This means:

  • They have a higher survival rate and adaptability

  • They are more likely to breed in home aquariums

  • They are ethically and sustainably produced here in Indianapolis IN, reducing pressure on wild populations

Care Guidelines:

  • Diet: Omnivorous grazer; supplement with shrimp-safe foods.

  • Social Behavior: Peaceful and social; thrives in groups.

  • Origin: Bred by Aquatic Arts.

  • Average Adult Size: 1 - 1.2 inches (2.5 - 3 cm)

  • Average Purchase Size: 0.5 - 0.75 inches (1.2 - 1.9 cm)

Live Arrival Guarantee:

All Aquatic Arts brand shrimp come with a 100% live arrival guarantee, plus free email support!

products.title: Assorted Veil Angelfish ( Pterophyllum scalare ) - Tank Bred
products.body_html:
Graceful and Stunning Centerpieces

For the safety of our animals, we now ship live fish exclusively via UPS Next Day Air. If your order contains this item, you will only see UPS Next Day Air as a shipping option during checkout.

Elegant, unique, and mesmerizing, the Assorted Veil Angelfish is a stunning addition to any freshwater aquarium! These fish feature elongated, flowing fins that add a touch of grace and beauty to any aquascape. With their variety of colors and patterns, no two are exactly alike, ensuring each fish becomes a distinctive centerpiece in your tank. Whether you are a beginner or a seasoned aquarist, Veil Angelfish are a joy to observe and care for.


What We Love About This Fish:

  • Flowing Fins: Their long, veil-like fins create a captivating display as they glide through the water.

  • Varied Patterns: A mix of marbled, striped, and solid colors ensures each fish is one of a kind.

  • Peaceful Presence: Known for their calm and inquisitive nature.

  • Community Compatibility: Excellent for peaceful aquariums with similarly sized tank mates.

Natural Habitat:

The Assorted Veil Angelfish originates from the slow-moving rivers and floodplains of the Amazon Basin in South America. These waters are often shaded by dense vegetation, providing a natural habitat rich in driftwood, submerged roots, and soft, sandy substrates.

Aquascaping:

  • Plants: Amazon Swords, Vallisneria, and Java Fern create a natural, lush environment.

  • Hardscape: Incorporate driftwood and smooth stones to mimic their native habitat.

  • Botanicals: Add Indian Almond Leaves or alder cones to release beneficial tannins and enrich their surroundings.

Diet and Feeding:

Veil Angelfish are omnivorous and thrive on a varied diet. Feed them high-quality flakes, pellets, and occasional treats like bloodworms, brine shrimp, and daphnia to enhance their health and coloration.

Tank Mates and Compatibility:

Veil Angelfish do well with peaceful tank mates of similar size, such as:

  • Corydoras Catfish

  • Larger Tetras (e.g., Cardinal Tetras, Black Skirt Tetras)

  • Dwarf Gouramis

  • Bristlenose Plecos Avoid housing them with aggressive or fin-nipping species.

Not Shrimp Safe:

Angelfish may prey on shrimp, especially juveniles or smaller species. Consider larger, more robust shrimp or provide plenty of hiding spaces.

Breeding:

Veil Angelfish are relatively easy to breed in a well-maintained aquarium. They prefer vertical surfaces for spawning, such as broad plant leaves or flat decorations. Ensure the pair is well-fed and in a stress-free environment for successful breeding.

Recommended Tank Parameters:

  • Temperature: 75° - 82°F (24° - 28°C)

  • pH: 6.5 - 7.5

  • KH: 3 - 10 dKH

  • Minimum Tank Size: 29 gallons for a pair; larger for groups.

Care Guidelines:

  • Diet: Omnivorous; feed a varied diet of flakes, pellets, and frozen or live foods.

  • Social Behavior: Semi-social; best kept in pairs or small groups.

  • Origin: Tank-bred.

  • Average Adult Size: 6 inches (15 cm) body length, with fins adding additional height.

  • Average Purchase Size: 2 - 3 inches (5 - 7.5 cm).

Live Arrival Guarantee:

All Aquatic Arts brand plants and animals come with a 100% live arrival guarantee, plus free email support!

Transform your aquarium with the grace and elegance of Assorted Veil Angelfish. Each fish adds a unique beauty and serenity to your aquascape!

products.title: Red Shiner (Cyprinella lutrensis), Tank-Bred!
products.body_html:
* For the safety of our animals, we now ship live fish exclusively via UPS Next Day Air.  If your order contains this item, you will only see UPS Next Day Air as a shipping option during checkout * 

 

This US native fish is great for the aquarium and it often develops unbelievable coloration during breeding!

The Red Shiner (Cyprinella lutrensis) is a small cyprinid species that is native to the Southeasten United States. It is known for its incredible coloration (particularly in males) during breeding season. It can be kept in a room temperature or subtropical aquarium and it is great for planted aquariums. 

The Red Shiner typically occupies the top and middle level of the water column, although it can often be seen swimming and feeding in the middle and bottom levels as well. It is safe with other peaceful, small fish and is neither bashful nor brash. Due to its extra small size and peaceful demeanor, it can be safely kept in the community aquarium, though as with most fish, it may eat some shrimp fry. Larger, peaceful invertebrates can also make good tankmates. The Rainbow Shiner must be kept in schools of 6-8 or more, although much larger schools of 10+ are more ideal. Its natural habitat typically consists of shallow, clear water and lots of vegetation. While it can tolerate tropical water conditions as long as water quality is good, the Red Shiner is naturally a subtropical species that will thrive in cooler water, which makes it an ideal tankmate for many ornamental shrimp, Stiphodon and other smaller gobies, and hillstream loaches, among many other species. 

While not a picky eater, the Red Shiner will thrive and remain very colorful on a varied diet of mostly carnivorous foods. High-quality flake food, pellets, and live, frozen, or freeze-dried Artemia, microworms, Tubifex, Calanus, and cyclops will all be readily accepted. Some vegetable matter (such as spirulina) is ideal as well. 

 

What We Like About This Fish:

  • Vibrant coloration, especially in breeding males
  • Very outgoing and hardy
  • Peaceful disposition with fish and peaceful invertebrates
  • Very active and noticeable fish in any aquarium
  • Ideal for planted aquariums

 

RECOMMENDED TANK PARAMETERS:

  • Temperature: 59 - 77° F (15° - 25° C)
  • pH: 6.5 - 8.0
  • KH: 2-15 dKH
  • Minimum Tank Size: 20 gallons for a small school

 

CARE INFORMATION:

  • Diet: Micropredator. Will easily accept high quality dry and frozen foods of appropriate size. Requires a varied diet for optimal health and coloration.
  • Social Behavior: Peaceful, schooling/shoaling.
  • Origin: Tank-Bred, but indigenous to Southeastern United States
  • Average Adult Size: 3.6 inches (9 cm)
  • Average Purchase Size for smalls: 1 - 2 inches
  • Average Purchase Size for larges: 2+ inches

 

All Aquatic Arts brand plants and animals come with a 100% live arrival guarantee, plus free email support!

products.title: Monte Carlo (Micranthemum tweediei), 3 x 3 inch mat
products.body_html:
Micranthemum “Monte Carlo” is an ideal carpeting plant that is similar to Dwarf Baby’s Tears, but is much easier to keep!

 

Monte Carlo (Micranthemum tweediei) is an increasingly popular plant for carpeting the serious freshwater planted aquarium. With proper care, this hardy, fast-growing plant can flourish and cover the entire foreground of the aquascape with beautiful lime green coloration. Due to its comparatively easy care requirements and similar appearance, Monte Carlo is becoming a highly-desired alternative to the related species Dwarf Baby’s Tears (Hemianthus callitrichoides var. “Cuba”). Unlike many similar carpeting plants, this plant can thrive and proliferate even in low-tech setups!

 

This variety of Monte Carlo requires moderate to high lighting for regular growth, although some suggest that it will grow slowly even in low lighting as long as sufficient nutrients are present. Its trimmings can be replanted to increase the mat. It does not require CO2 supplementation, but CO2 supplementation will contribute to more optimal growth and health. While it is a relatively adaptable plant, it of course will flourish most in a higher tech setup.

 

This listing is for a 3 x 3-inch coco fiber mat of this high-quality plant. These mats are the ticket to full carpeting of the aquarium if the keeper so desires! These mats are not often available, so be sure to get yours now!  

 

Like most plants, Monte Carlo will benefit from supplementation such as Seachem Flourish, Flourish Excel, Flourish Nitrogen, Flourish Iron, and other plant supplements.  

No aquarium plant distributor/nursery/wholesaler/retailer can or will guarantee their plants to be 100% snail free. We can not guarantee our plants will be snail free. 

What We Like About This Plant:

  • Prolific carpeting plant
  • Easy care
  • Delicate, attractive appearance
  • Provides protective cover for fry and can attach to or cover many surfaces in the aquarium

 

Care Guidelines:

  • Temperature: 70° - 90° F (21° - 32° C)
  • pH: 6.0 – 7.0
  • Lighting: Moderate to High.
  • Potential Height: 1.2 - 2 inches (3-5 cm), sometimes taller.
  • Origin: Indigenous to Argentina, cultivated in US nurseries
  • Aquarium Placement: Foreground
  • Care: Moderate
  • Growth Rate: Medium to Fast

     

    All Aquatic Arts brand plants and animals come with a 100% live arrival guarantee, plus free email support!

products.title: Tiburon Limia (Limia tridens) – USA Bred
products.body_html:
For the safety of our animals, we now ship live fish exclusively via UPS Next Day Air. If your order contains this item, you will only see UPS Next Day Air as a shipping option during checkout.

The Tiburon Limia (Limia tridens) is a rare and fascinating livebearer endemic to Hispaniola. Known for its striking metallic sheen and subtle tiger-like barring, this species is a perfect choice for enthusiasts looking to add an uncommon yet hardy fish to their aquarium. Unlike many other Limia species, the Tiburon Limia is known for its active schooling behavior and social interactions, making it a lively addition to planted and community tanks.

This species thrives in hard, alkaline water conditions, similar to other Limia and livebearers. As an omnivorous fish, it requires a balanced diet of plant-based foods and protein sources to maintain its vibrant coloration and energy levels.

What We Love About This Fish:

✔ Rare & Unique Livebearer: A lesser-known species that adds diversity to community tanks.
✔ Subtle Yet Stunning Patterning: Metallic body with faint barring and an elegant sheen.
✔ Hardy & Active: A great choice for both beginner and experienced aquarists.
✔ Peaceful & Social: Best kept in groups for natural schooling behavior.

Natural Habitat:

The Tiburon Limia originates from freshwater streams and brackish coastal habitats in Hispaniola, where they inhabit areas with dense vegetation, rocky substrates, and moderate water flow. These environments provide plenty of hiding spots while allowing them to forage naturally.

Aquascaping:

  • Plants: Thrives in heavily planted tanks with floating or rooted vegetation.
  • Hardscape: Rock formations, driftwood, and sandy or fine gravel substrate.
  • Water Flow: Moderate, mimicking its natural habitat.

Diet and Feeding:

Tiburon Limia are omnivorous, requiring a balanced diet of both plant-based foods and protein sources. They will eagerly accept:

  • High-quality spirulina flakes and vegetable-based pellets.
  • Live or frozen foods such as brine shrimp, daphnia, and bloodworms.
  • Occasional treats of blanched vegetables like zucchini or spinach.

Tank Mates and Compatibility:

✔ Peaceful community fish such as guppies, mollies, swordtails, and other livebearers.
✔ Corydoras catfish, small tetras, and peaceful barbs.
✔ Avoid large, aggressive cichlids or fin-nipping species.

Shrimp Safe?

✔ Yes, mostly! While adults typically ignore shrimp, they may eat small shrimp fry. Providing dense plant cover can help protect them.

Breeding:

Tiburon Limia are livebearers, meaning females give birth to fully formed fry rather than laying eggs. To encourage successful breeding:

  • Maintain a male-to-female ratio of 1:3 to reduce stress on females.
  • Provide plenty of plant cover for fry to hide from adult fish.
  • Feed a high-protein diet to encourage healthy reproduction.

Recommended Tank Parameters:

  • Temperature: 72° - 82° F (22° - 28° C)
  • pH: 7.5 - 8.5
  • KH: 10 - 20 dKH
  • Minimum Tank Size: 20 gallons (for a small group)

Care Guidelines:

  • Diet: Omnivorous; requires a mix of plant and protein-based foods.
  • Social Behavior: Best kept in groups of 5 or more.
  • Origin: Tank bred.
  • Average Adult Size: 2 - 3 inches (5 - 7.5 cm).
  • Average Purchase Size: 1 - 1.5 inches (2.5 - 3.8 cm).

Live Arrival Guarantee:

All Aquatic Arts brand fish come with a 100% live arrival guarantee, plus free email support to ensure your success in keeping this incredible species!

Add the Tiburon Limia to your aquarium today and enjoy the beauty and activity of this rare and lively livebearer!

products.title: Red Widow Frontosa (Cyphotilapia frontosa 'Red Widow') – Tank Bred
products.body_html:
For the safety of our animals, we now ship live fish exclusively via UPS Next Day Air. If your order contains this item, you will only see UPS Next Day Air as a shipping option during checkout.

The Red Widow Frontosa is an exceptionally rare and selectively bred color morph of Cyphotilapia frontosa, showcasing a bold red variation of the Black Widow Frontosa’s pattern. Instead of traditional black and white banding, this stunning variant displays deep red-orange hues with dark widow-like markings, making it a truly unique addition to any large cichlid aquarium.

Frontosas are known for their slow growth rate, social nature, and long lifespan, often living for 15 years or more in well-maintained aquariums. They thrive in spacious tanks with ample rockwork, mimicking their deep-water habitat from Lake Tanganyika. As a colony-forming species, they do best when kept in groups with a structured hierarchy.

What We Love About This Fish:

✔ Rare Red Widow Morph: Features the striking widow-like pattern of the Black Widow Frontosa but in a rich red-orange hue.
✔ Regal Appearance: Long, flowing fins and a pronounced forehead hump (nuchal hump).
✔ Social & Colony-Oriented: Thrives in groups with an established hierarchy.
✔ Hardy & Long-Lived: Can live 15+ years with proper care.

Natural Habitat:

Cyphotilapia frontosa is native to Lake Tanganyika, where they inhabit deep rocky waters ranging from 35 to 165 feet (10 to 50 meters). In the wild, they form colonies in caves and crevices, which provide security and natural breeding areas.

Aquascaping:

  • Plants: Not recommended—Frontosas may uproot them.
  • Hardscape: Large rock structures and caves for shelter and hierarchy formation.
  • Substrate: Fine sand or smooth gravel to mimic their natural environment.
  • Water Flow: Low to moderate, as they prefer calmer deep-water conditions.

Diet and Feeding:

Frontosas are carnivorous predators, feeding on small fish and invertebrates in the wild. In captivity, they thrive on:

  • High-quality sinking cichlid pellets & sticks.
  • Frozen or live foods such as krill, shrimp, and earthworms.
  • Occasional small feeder fish or shellfish (avoid fatty foods like goldfish).
  • Varied diet to maintain optimal health and coloration.

Tank Mates and Compatibility:

Frontosas are relatively peaceful for large cichlids, but they can be territorial. Best suited with:
✔ Other Tanganyikan cichlids (Altolamprologus, Synodontis catfish, etc.).
✔ Large, slow-moving African cichlids.
✔ Avoid aggressive Mbuna cichlids and small, fast-moving species.

Shrimp Safe?

❌ No. Frontosas are predators and will eat shrimp and small invertebrates.

Breeding:

Frontosas are maternal mouthbrooders, meaning the female incubates eggs in her mouth for approximately 4-6 weeks. Successful breeding requires:

  • A harem setup (1 male with multiple females).
  • Cave-like structures for spawning.
  • Stable, warm water conditions (78-80°F).

Recommended Tank Parameters:

  • Temperature: 74° - 80° F (23° - 27° C)
  • pH: 7.8 - 8.3
  • KH: 10 - 20 dKH
  • Minimum Tank Size: 125 gallons (for a group)

Care Guidelines:

  • Diet: Carnivorous; requires high-protein foods.
  • Social Behavior: Best kept in groups with a dominant male.
  • Origin: Tank bred.
  • Average Adult Size: 12 - 14 inches (30 - 35 cm).
  • Average Purchase Size: 1.5 - 2 inches.

Live Arrival Guarantee:

All Aquatic Arts brand fish come with a 100% live arrival guarantee, plus free email support to ensure your success in keeping this incredible species!

Bring home the rare and stunning Red Widow Frontosa and add a majestic, one-of-a-kind presence to your aquarium!

products.title: Epipremnum aureum ‘Marble Queen’ Pothos – Node/Leaf Cutting
products.body_html:
The Epipremnum aureum ‘Marble Queen’ Node Cutting, also known as Marble Queen Pothos, is a stunning variegated plant with creamy-white and green marbled leaves. This variety of Pothos is highly sought after for its low-maintenance care, air-purifying qualities, and ability to thrive in multiple environments.

While not a fully aquatic plant, Epipremnum aureum can grow with its node submerged in water while the leaves remain above the surface, making it a great choice for paludariums, ripariums, and natural filtration setups. It also thrives as a trailing or climbing houseplant, bringing a touch of elegance to any space.

Features We Love:

  • Stunning Variegation: Beautiful creamy-white and green marbled foliage.
  • Natural Nitrate Absorber: Helps improve water quality when grown with roots submerged.
  • Versatile & Easy to Grow: Thrives in aquariums (emersed growth), paludariums, terrariums, or as a houseplant.
  • Hardy & Fast-Growing: Adapts well to a variety of conditions and requires minimal maintenance.

Care Guidelines:

  • Light: Moderate to bright indirect light; lower light slows variegation.
  • Temperature: 65° - 85° F (18° - 29° C).
  • pH Range: 6.0 - 7.5.
  • Growth Rate: Fast with proper care.
  • Ideal Placement: Emersed in aquariums with the node fully submerged, or grown in a terrarium, paludarium, or as a potted houseplant.

Pro Tips:

  • Encouraging Emersed Growth: Keep the node submerged while allowing the leaves to remain above the waterline.
  • Rooting Success: Place in clean water with good circulation before transferring to soil for best results.
  • Provide Climbing Support: Use a moss pole or trellis to encourage vertical growth.
  • Boost Humidity: If growing in a terrarium, maintain humidity above 60% for lush foliage.
  • Prune to Shape: Trim regularly to encourage fuller growth and more propagation opportunities.

Enhance your plant collection with a healthy Epipremnum aureum ‘Marble Queen’ node cutting—a stunning and functional addition to aquariums, terrariums, and indoor spaces!

products.title: Monstera adansonii Plant – Node/Leaf Cutting
products.body_html:
The Monstera adansonii Node Cutting, also known as the Swiss Cheese Vine, is an excellent way to start growing this sought-after vining plant. With its iconic fenestrated leaves, this species is a favorite for tropical plant collectors and aquascaping enthusiasts.

Though not a fully aquatic plant, Monstera adansonii can grow with its node submerged in water while the leaves remain above the surface. This makes it a fantastic option for paludariums and aquariums where it can thrive emersed, absorbing excess nutrients while developing strong roots. Over time, it produces long, trailing vines, making it a beautiful and functional addition to any setup.

Features We Love:

  • Excellent Nitrate Absorber: Helps remove excess nutrients when grown with roots submerged.
  • Thrives in Multiple Setups: Ideal for aquariums (emersed growth), paludariums, terrariums, or as a houseplant.
  • Unique Fenestrated Leaves: Develops characteristic holes as it matures.
  • Fast-Growing & Hardy: Easy to propagate and establish in a variety of environments.

Care Guidelines:

  • Light: Moderate to bright indirect light.
  • Temperature: 65° - 85° F (18° - 29° C).
  • pH Range: 6.0 - 7.5.
  • Growth Rate: Fast with proper care.
  • Ideal Placement: Emersed in aquariums with the node fully submerged, or grown in a terrarium, paludarium, or as a potted houseplant.

Pro Tips:

  • Emersed Growth in Aquariums: Keep the node submerged while allowing the leaves to grow above water for the best results.
  • Rooting Success: Place the node in clean water with good circulation to encourage root growth before planting.
  • Provide Climbing Support: Use a moss pole, trellis, or driftwood to encourage upward growth.
  • Maintain High Humidity: If growing in a terrarium, keep humidity above 60% for optimal development.
  • Regular Pruning: Trim vines to promote bushier growth and create new propagations.

Grow your own Monstera adansonii with this healthy node cutting—a stunning and functional addition to aquariums, terrariums, and plant collections!

products.title: Epipremnum Pinnatum Plant – Node/Leaf Cutting
products.body_html:
The Epipremnum pinnatum Node/Leaf Cutting is an excellent choice for aquarists and plant enthusiasts looking to incorporate natural greenery into their setup. While not a fully aquatic plant, this species thrives when grown emersed, meaning its roots can be submerged in water while the leaves and vines grow above the surface. Over time, it develops long, trailing vines with striking leaves that can fenestrate as they mature.

This cutting includes a healthy node and leaf, making it easy to root in water or transition into a terrarium or houseplant collection. Whether used as a natural nitrate absorber in an aquarium or as a unique addition to a paludarium, this plant is a fantastic low-maintenance option.

Features We Love:

  • Excellent Nitrate Absorber: Helps reduce excess nutrients when grown with roots submerged.
  • Thrives in Multiple Setups: Ideal for aquariums (emersed growth), paludariums, terrariums, or as a houseplant.
  • Fast-Growing & Hardy: Easy to propagate and can quickly establish itself.
  • Unique Growth Habit: Vines beautifully when given support and can develop fenestrated leaves over time.

  • Light: Moderate to bright indirect light.
  • Temperature: 65° - 85° F (18° - 29° C).

  • Ideal Placement: Emersed in aquariums with the node fully submerged, or grown in a terrarium, paludarium, or as a potted houseplant.

Pro Tips:

products.title: Irianto Red Crayfish (Cherax snowden)
products.body_html:
⚠️ Important Legal Note: Cherax species of crayfish are considered an invasive species in several U.S. states and is illegal to possess in any form or sell in: California, Florida, Maine, Texas, Washington, and Wisconsin. Please check your local regulations before purchasing. We are unable to ship this species to these states. If you reside in one of the above states and try to checkout with this crayfish in your cart you will be unable to checkout and receive an error message until the crayfish is removed from the cart.

For the safety of our animals, we now ship live cherax species of crayfish exclusively via UPS Next Day Air. If your order contains this item, you will only see UPS Next Day Air as a shipping option during checkout.

The Irianto Red Crayfish (Cherax snowden) is a stunning and rare species of freshwater crayfish native to the rivers of Papua New Guinea. This species is highly sought after for its striking appearance, featuring deep red to orange coloration with vivid claw tips. Unlike many commonly available crayfish, the Cherax snowden displays a more docile temperament, making it an excellent choice for experienced aquarists looking for a unique and eye-catching invertebrate.

What We Love About This Crayfish:

• Brilliant coloration featuring striking red claw tips against a deep red or orange body
• Larger size than dwarf crayfish, making it a centerpiece for freshwater tanks
• Intelligent and engaging behavior, often seen exploring its environment
• Hardy species that adapts well to properly maintained aquariums

Natural Habitat:

The Irianto Red Crayfish originates from slow-moving, freshwater rivers and streams in Papua New Guinea. These habitats are rich in driftwood, leaf litter, and submerged rocks, providing ample hiding spots and foraging opportunities. The water in these regions is clean, slightly acidic to neutral, and well-oxygenated.


Aquascaping Recommendations:

• Plants: Hardy species like Anubias, Java Fern, and floating plants such as Frogbit or Water Lettuce
• Hardscape: Driftwood, river rocks, and caves for shelter
• Botanicals: Indian Almond Leaves, Alder Cones, and other tannin-rich additions to simulate a natural environment

Diet and Feeding:

The Irianto Red Crayfish is an omnivore and will accept a variety of foods, including:
• High-quality sinking pellets and wafers
• Fresh vegetables like zucchini, spinach, and carrots
• Frozen or live protein sources such as bloodworms and shrimp
• Occasional calcium-rich foods to support healthy molting

Tank Mates and Compatibility:

This species can coexist with certain fast-moving fish but should not be housed with slow or bottom-dwelling tank mates.
✅ Compatible Tank Mates:
• Fast-moving fish like Danios and Barbs
• Large snails like Mystery Snails
• Some shrimp species (risk depends on individual crayfish)

❌ Avoid:
• Small, slow-moving fish like Bettas
• Other crayfish species (territorial behavior may lead to aggression)
• Delicate shrimp species (may be viewed as prey)

Shrimp Safe?

Not recommended. While some aquarists have successfully housed them with shrimp, the Irianto Red Crayfish may opportunistically prey on smaller or slow-moving shrimp. Providing ample hiding spaces can help reduce predation risk.

Breeding:

Breeding is possible in captivity but requires careful management. These crayfish are egg-layers, with females carrying fertilized eggs under their tails until hatching. Successful breeding depends on providing clean water, proper nutrition, and plenty of hiding spots.

Recommended Tank Parameters:

• Temperature: 72° - 78° F (22° - 26° C)
• pH: 6.5 - 7.5
• KH: 4 - 10 dKH
• Minimum Tank Size: 20 gallons for a single crayfish; larger for multiple individuals

Care Guidelines:

• Diet: Omnivorous, requires a mix of protein and plant matter
• Social Behavior: Territorial; best kept singly or with extreme caution in large tanks
• Origin: Wild-collected from Papua New Guinea
• Average Adult Size: 4 - 6 inches (10 - 15 cm)
• Average Purchase Size: 2 - 3 inches (5 - 7.5 cm)

Live Arrival Guarantee:

All Aquatic Arts invertebrates come with a 100% live arrival guarantee, plus free email support to assist with your crayfish care.

Bring home the Irianto Red Crayfish today and add a truly remarkable invertebrate to your aquarium!

products.title: Beckett's Water Trumpet (Cryptocoryne becketti) Tissue Culture
products.body_html:
Beckett's Water Trumpet (Cryptocoryne becketti) is a superb low-light plant known for producing tube-shaped flowers when it grows above the water line!

 

Beckett's Water Trumpet (Cryptocoryne becketti) is a fairly large plant that occurs throughout much of Asia. It can grow to a substantial size, so it is an excellent plant for the medium to large aquarium. It requires stable conditions, especially when newly established, but is not demanding otherwise.

 

Beckett's Water Trumpet is a slow-growing plant that requires low to moderate lighting. It should be placed in an area of the aquarium where it can be allowed to establish over time. Due to its wide natural range, it can adapt to a relatively wide variety of water parameters as long as it does not have to endure sudden changes. Like most Cryptocoryne species, it should be allowed plenty of time to “settle in” and should not be moved constantly. Once established, it will reproduce by sending runners that will grow into new plants.

 

CO2 supplementation is beneficial, but not necessary for this plant. It will also benefit from supplemental fertilizers and nutrient-rich substrate

 

This listing is for the tissue culture form of Beckett's Water Trumpet. Tissue cultures are superior to traditional forms of aquarium plants in many ways. They are produced in a completely sterile environment which eliminates the possibility of them carrying pest snails or algae spores. They have a shelf life (before introduction to the aquarium) of several months if properly maintained and they are housed in a nutrient gel until introduced to the aquarium. To introduce a tissue culture plant to the aquarium, simply rinse off as much nutrient gel as possible, then plant as usual. The nutrient gel will not harm your aquarium.  

 

What We Like About This Plant:

  • Tissue cultures contain no unwanted pest animals or algae
  • Large, long leaves
  • Very versatile when kept in stable conditions
  • Unique green and brown coloration
  • Can thrive in low-tech setups

 

Care Guidelines:

  • Temperature: 73 – 83° F (23 - 28° C)
  • pH: 5.5 - 8.0
  • Lighting: Low to Medium
  • Origin: Lab-grown tissue culture, but indigenous to India, Sri Lanka, and New Guinea
  • Aquarium Placement: Foreground to Midground, Background in smaller tanks
  • Care: Low to Moderate

     

    All Aquatic Arts brand plants and animals come with a 100% live arrival guarantee, plus free email support!

products.title: Downoi (Pogostemon helferi) Tissue Culture
products.body_html:
This rosette-shaped plant has a one-of-a-kind appearance and it is perfect for the high-light aquarium foreground!

 

Downoi (Pogostemon helferi) is a relatively newly cultivated stem plant that has recently developed some notice in the aquarium hobby. Native to Thailand, it is distinctive due to its rosette shape and wavy green leaves. Under high lighting, it will maintain this attractive shape and will be a superb foreground plant. It can even be attached to driftwood and rocks in the aquarium!

 

Care for Downoi is fairly simple. It grows quickly with good maintenance and care. Due to its fast growth, it requires a great deal of nutrients. Like many plants, this plant will respond significantly to CO2. It can be planted in the substrate and stems of excessive length can be trimmed, then the cuttings can be replanted to propagate the plant further. Mature plants will also propagate through runners. It can also be planted emersed in terrariums, paludariums, and viquariums.  

 

This listing is for the tissue culture form of Downoi. Tissue cultures are superior to traditional forms of aquarium plants in many ways. They are produced in a completely sterile environment which eliminates the possibility of them carrying pest snails or algae spores. They have a shelf life (before introduction to the aquarium) of several months if properly maintained and they are housed in a nutrient gel until introduced to the aquarium. To introduce a tissue culture plant to the aquarium, simply rinse off as much nutrient gel as possible, then plant as usual. The nutrient gel will not harm your aquarium.  

Packaging may vary. Tissue Culture plants may arrive in bag or cup form.

What We Like About This Plant:

  • Tissue cultures contain no unwanted pest animals or algae
  • Incredible bright green coloration
  • Moderately prolific and easy to prune
  • Distinctive foreground plant
  • Relatively easy to maintain

 

Care Guidelines:

  • Temperature: 68° - 86° F (20° - 30° C)
  • pH: 6.0 - 7.5
  • Lighting: Moderate to High (High is most preferred)
  • Origin: Lab-grown tissue culture, but indigenous to Thailand
  • Aquarium placement: Foreground
  • Care: Moderate

 

All Aquatic Arts brand plants and animals come with a 100% live arrival guarantee, plus free email support!

products.body_html:
Anubias barteri 'Mini Coin' is a compact and uniquely shaped variant of the popular Anubias family. Known for its small, rounded leaves and slow-growing nature, this plant is an excellent choice for aquascapers looking to add detail and texture to their aquarium. Cultivated through sterile tissue culture, this plant is free from pests, algae, and disease, ensuring a healthy start in your aquarium setup.

Features We Love:

  • Compact Growth: Small, rounded leaves create a dense, lush appearance.

  • Low Maintenance: Thrives in a variety of tank conditions and requires minimal care.

  • Pest-Free Guarantee: Tissue-cultured for a clean, disease-free introduction to your tank.

  • Versatile Placement: Perfect for foreground or midground placement in aquascapes.

Care Guidelines:

  • Light: Low to moderate.

  • Temperature: 68° - 82°F (20° - 28°C).

  • pH Range: 6.0 - 7.5.

  • Growth Rate: Slow.

  • Ideal Placement: Foreground to midground, best attached to rocks or driftwood.

Pro Tips:

  • Attach with Care: Avoid burying the rhizome; instead, attach to rocks or wood for best growth.

  • Algae Prevention: Keep in moderate lighting and maintain water circulation to prevent algae buildup.

  • Minimal Fertilization: Does well with occasional root tabs or liquid fertilizers.

  • Excellent for Shrimp Tanks: Provides grazing surfaces and natural shelter for shrimp and small fish.

Enhance your aquascape with the charming Anubias barteri 'Mini Coin', the perfect blend of beauty, durability, and ease of care!

products.title: Amazon Whiptail Catfish (Farlowella amazonum) - Tank Bred
products.body_html:
Also Known as Twig Catfish

For the safety of our animals, we now ship live fish exclusively via UPS Next Day Air. If your order contains this item, you will only see UPS Next Day Air as a shipping option during checkout.

The Amazon Whiptail Catfish (Farlowella amazonum), also known as the Twig Catfish, is a fascinating and uniquely shaped species with an elongated body that mimics the appearance of a twig. Native to slow-moving streams and tributaries of the Amazon River Basin, this peaceful and gentle fish is an excellent addition to well-maintained community aquariums. Its slender form and natural ability to blend in with driftwood and plants make it a standout species for those who appreciate unusual and naturalistic fish.

This species thrives in mature, well-oxygenated aquariums with plenty of plant cover, driftwood, and smooth rocks. As a natural algae grazer, it requires a well-balanced diet supplemented with high-quality plant-based foods.

What We Love About This Fish:

• Incredible Camouflage – Resembles a twig, blending seamlessly with driftwood and plants.
• Peaceful and Docile – An excellent choice for calm community tanks.
• Natural Algae Grazer – Helps keep biofilm and algae in check.
• Unique and Uncommon – A standout addition to any aquarium.

Natural Habitat:

The Amazon Whiptail Catfish is found in slow-moving, soft-water tributaries of the Amazon River. These waters are typically rich in tannins from decaying leaf litter and have a sandy or muddy substrate with an abundance of submerged driftwood and aquatic plants.

Aquascaping:

• Plants: Thrives in aquariums with soft-leaved plants such as Anubias, Java Fern, and Amazon Sword.
• Hardscape: Prefers driftwood and smooth rocks for perching and hiding.
• Botanicals: Indian Almond Leaves and Alder Cones help create a tannin-rich environment that mimics their natural habitat.

Diet and Feeding:

The Amazon Whiptail Catfish is an herbivore and requires a diet high in plant matter. A well-rounded feeding routine includes:
✔ High-quality algae wafers and sinking vegetable-based pellets
✔ Fresh vegetables such as zucchini, cucumber, and blanched spinach
✔ Occasional protein-rich treats like bloodworms or daphnia (in moderation)

Providing a varied diet rich in fiber ensures their long-term health and helps maintain their natural grazing behavior.

Tank Mates and Compatibility:

This species is extremely peaceful and should be kept with similarly calm fish. Best suited for a community tank with other non-aggressive species, such as:
✔ Tetras (Rummynose, Cardinal, Ember)
✔ Rasboras and peaceful barbs
✔ Corydoras catfish
✔ Small, peaceful cichlids like Apistogramma and Bolivian Rams
✔ Other gentle bottom-dwellers like Otocinclus and small plecos

🚫 Avoid housing with aggressive or overly active fish, as the Amazon Whiptail Catfish is easily outcompeted for food.

Shrimp Safe?

Yes, this species is completely shrimp-safe and will not harm adult shrimp or their fry.

Breeding:

Breeding Amazon Whiptail Catfish in captivity is possible but requires a well-established tank with stable water parameters. Males tend to have broader heads and develop bristles along their snouts. They typically lay eggs on flat surfaces, and the male guards the eggs until they hatch. Fry require a diet of finely crushed plant-based foods and algae.

Recommended Tank Parameters:

• Temperature: 74° - 82° F (23° - 28° C)
• pH: 6.0 - 7.5
• KH: 2 - 8 dKH
• Minimum Tank Size: 20 gallons (larger preferred for groups)

Care Guidelines:

• Diet: Herbivorous; requires a steady supply of plant-based foods and algae.
• Social Behavior: Extremely peaceful; best kept in small groups or with calm tank mates.
• Origin: Tank-bred; not wild-caught.
• Average Adult Size: 6 - 7 inches (15 - 18 cm).
• Average Purchase Size: 3 - 4 inches (7.5 - 10 cm).

Live Arrival Guarantee:

All Aquatic Arts animals come with a 100% Live Arrival Guarantee, plus free email support to help you care for your new fish!

Add the Amazon Whiptail Catfish to your aquarium today and enjoy the unique elegance of this fascinating twig-like fish!

products.title: C005 Pantanalensis Cory (Corydoras pantanalensis)
products.body_html:
For the safety of our animals, we now ship live fish exclusively via UPS Next Day Air. If your order contains this item, you will only see UPS Next Day Air as a shipping option during checkout.

The C005 Pantanalensis Cory (Corydoras pantanalensis) is a rare and highly sought-after Corydoras species, known for its striking coloration and slightly larger size compared to many other Corydoras. Native to the Pantanal wetlands of South America, this species features a shimmering silver-gray body with subtle dark markings and an iridescent sheen along its flanks. Unlike many Corydoras, the C005 develops a slightly more elongated body shape and robust build, making it a standout addition to any aquarium.

These peaceful bottom dwellers are highly social and thrive in groups, making them an excellent choice for community tanks with soft substrates and plenty of hiding spots.

What We Love About This Fish:

• Rare and Unique Corydoras – Not commonly available in the hobby.
• Shimmering, Iridescent Body – Subtle hues shift under different lighting.
• Peaceful and Social – Best kept in groups of 5 or more.
• Great for Community Tanks – Non-aggressive and gets along with a variety of tank mates.

Natural Habitat:

The C005 Pantanalensis Cory originates from the slow-moving, soft-water streams and floodplains of the Pantanal wetlands in South America. These waters are typically tannin-rich, with sandy or muddy substrates and dense aquatic vegetation providing shade and shelter.

Aquascaping:

• Plants: Best suited for planted aquariums with broad-leafed plants like Amazon Sword, Anubias, and Cryptocoryne.
• Hardscape: Smooth driftwood and rounded river stones provide natural shelter.
• Botanicals: Indian Almond Leaves and Alder Cones help create a soft, tannin-rich environment.

Diet and Feeding:

The C005 Pantanalensis Cory is an omnivore and will readily accept a variety of sinking foods. A balanced diet includes:
✔ High-quality sinking pellets and wafers
✔ Live or frozen foods such as bloodworms, brine shrimp, and blackworms
✔ Occasional blanched vegetables like zucchini and cucumber

Providing a varied diet ensures optimal health and longevity.

Tank Mates and Compatibility:

The C005 Pantanalensis Cory is a peaceful species that thrives in a well-maintained community aquarium. Suitable tank mates include:
✔ Tetras (Rummynose, Cardinal, Ember)
✔ Rasboras and peaceful barbs
✔ Dwarf cichlids (Apistogramma, Bolivian Rams)
✔ Other Corydoras species and peaceful catfish

🚫 Avoid housing with aggressive cichlids, large predatory fish, or fin-nipping species.

Shrimp Safe?

Yes, this species is safe with adult shrimp but may consume baby shrimp.

Breeding:

The C005 Pantanalensis Cory follows typical Corydoras breeding behavior. Spawning is often triggered by cooler water changes and an increase in oxygenation. Eggs are laid on smooth surfaces like plant leaves, glass, or decor, and hatch within a few days. Fry require fine foods such as microworms or crushed flakes.

Recommended Tank Parameters:

• Temperature: 72° - 79° F (22° - 26° C)
• pH: 6.0 - 7.2
• KH: 2 - 6 dKH
• Minimum Tank Size: 20 gallons for a group

Care Guidelines:

• Diet: Omnivorous; thrives on sinking pellets, live/frozen foods, and occasional vegetable matter.
• Social Behavior: Peaceful; best kept in groups of 5 or more.
• Origin: Tank-bred; not wild-caught.
• Average Adult Size: 2.5 - 3 inches (6.3 - 7.6 cm).
• Average Purchase Size: 1.5 - 2 inches (3.8 - 5 cm).

Live Arrival Guarantee:

products.body_html:
For the safety of our animals, we now ship live fish exclusively via UPS Next Day Air. If your order contains this item, you will only see UPS Next Day Air as a shipping option during checkout.

The Smokey Chocolate Veil Angelfish is an exquisite variant of Pterophyllum scalare, featuring a rich, velvety brown coloration that deepens into a smoky gradient along its body. Its striking veil tail extends gracefully, giving it an elegant, flowing appearance as it moves through the water. This unique angelfish is a standout in any freshwater aquarium, particularly in planted or soft-lit setups that highlight its dramatic hues.

What We Love About This Fish:

• Unique Smokey Brown Tones – A rich chocolate coloration with a soft gradient effect.
• Elegant Veil Fins – Longer, flowing fins add to its graceful movement.
• Active and Intelligent – Recognizes owners and interacts with its environment.
• Perfect for Planted Aquariums – Looks stunning against lush greenery and dark substrates.

Natural Habitat:

Angelfish are native to the slow-moving waters of the Amazon River basin, where they inhabit densely planted areas with submerged roots, driftwood, and gentle water flow. They thrive in soft, slightly acidic water with plenty of shaded areas for security.

Aquascaping:

• Plants: Best suited for aquariums with tall and broad-leafed plants like Amazon Sword, Anubias, and Java Fern.
• Hardscape: Smooth driftwood, vertical rock structures, and shaded areas mimic their natural habitat.
• Botanicals: Indian Almond Leaves or Alder Cones help provide tannins for a more naturalistic blackwater effect.

Diet and Feeding:

The Smokey Chocolate Veil Angelfish is omnivorous and thrives on a well-balanced diet, including:
✔ High-quality flakes and protein-rich pellets
✔ Live or frozen treats such as brine shrimp, daphnia, and bloodworms
✔ Occasional vegetable matter like spirulina or blanched spinach

Providing a varied diet enhances their vibrant coloration and fin development.

Tank Mates and Compatibility:

This angelfish is generally peaceful but can display mild territorial behavior, especially during breeding. Best suited for community tanks with other non-aggressive fish. Recommended tank mates include:
✔ Peaceful tetras (Rummynose Tetras, Cardinal Tetras)
✔ Corydoras catfish and plecos
✔ Dwarf cichlids like Apistogramma and Bolivian Rams
✔ Peaceful loaches and rainbowfish

🚫 Avoid housing with aggressive cichlids, fin-nipping species, or very small nano fish.

Shrimp Safe?

Not recommended for shrimp tanks. Angelfish may prey on dwarf shrimp or their fry.

Breeding:

The Smokey Chocolate Veil Angelfish is relatively easy to breed in a well-maintained tank. Pairs form strong bonds and lay eggs on vertical surfaces like broad leaves or slate. The eggs hatch within 48-72 hours, with fry becoming free-swimming shortly after. A separate breeding tank is recommended for optimal success.

Recommended Tank Parameters:

• Temperature: 76° - 84° F (24° - 29° C)
• pH: 6.0 - 7.5
• KH: 1 - 5 dKH
• Minimum Tank Size: 30 gallons for a pair, 55+ gallons for a group

Care Guidelines:

• Diet: Omnivorous; requires a balanced mix of proteins and plant matter.
• Social Behavior: Semi-peaceful; best kept in small groups or pairs.
• Origin: Tank-bred; not wild-caught.
• Average Adult Size: 6 inches (15 cm) body height, up to 10 inches (25 cm) fin span.
• Average Purchase Size: 1.5 - 2.5 inches (3.8 - 6.3 cm)

Live Arrival Guarantee:

products.title: Blue Turquoise Marble Angelfish (Pterophyllum scalare) – Tank Bred
products.body_html:
For the safety of our animals, we now ship live fish exclusively via UPS Next Day Air. If your order contains this item, you will only see UPS Next Day Air as a shipping option during checkout.

The Blue Turquoise Marble Angelfish is a breathtaking variant of the classic angelfish, featuring an elegant mix of blue and turquoise hues accentuated by intricate marbled black and silver markings. This distinctive coloration shifts depending on lighting and water conditions, making it a stunning centerpiece for any freshwater aquarium. Like all Pterophyllum scalare, this variety has a graceful, tall-bodied shape and long flowing fins that give it a majestic presence in planted and community tanks.

What We Love About This Fish:

• Striking Coloration – A mesmerizing blend of blue, turquoise, and black marbling.
• Graceful and Elegant – Tall-bodied angelfish with long, flowing fins.
• Active and Engaging – Recognizes its owners and exhibits social behaviors.
• Excellent for Planted Tanks – Looks stunning among lush greenery.

Natural Habitat:

Angelfish are native to the slow-moving waters of the Amazon River basin, where they are commonly found in areas with dense vegetation, submerged roots, and driftwood. Their natural habitat consists of soft, slightly acidic waters with gentle flow and plenty of shaded hiding spots.

Aquascaping:

• Plants: Best kept in aquariums with live plants like Amazon Sword, Vallisneria, Anubias, and Java Fern.
• Hardscape: Utilize driftwood and smooth river stones to replicate their natural environment.
• Botanicals: Indian Almond Leaves and Alder Cones can help maintain optimal water conditions.

Diet and Feeding:

The Blue Turquoise Marble Angelfish is an omnivore and thrives on a varied diet that includes:
✔ High-quality flakes and pellets (protein-rich formulas preferred)
✔ Frozen or live foods such as bloodworms, brine shrimp, and daphnia
✔ Occasional vegetable matter like blanched spinach or spirulina-based foods

A well-balanced diet will enhance their vibrant coloration and overall health.

Tank Mates and Compatibility:

This angelfish is generally peaceful but can become territorial, especially during breeding. Best suited for community tanks with other semi-peaceful fish of similar size. Recommended tank mates include:
✔ Peaceful tetras (Congo Tetras, Lemon Tetras)
✔ Dwarf cichlids (Apistogramma, Bolivian Rams)
✔ Corydoras catfish
✔ Larger-bodied rasboras and peaceful loaches

🚫 Avoid fin-nipping species like tiger barbs or overly aggressive cichlids.

Shrimp Safe?

Not recommended for shrimp tanks. Adult angelfish may prey on dwarf shrimp or their fry.

Breeding:

Blue Turquoise Marble Angelfish are relatively easy to breed in the right conditions. They prefer vertical surfaces for egg-laying, such as broad-leaf plants or slate. Pairs will form strong bonds and defend their eggs, with fry hatching in 48-72 hours.

Recommended Tank Parameters:

• Temperature: 76° - 84° F (24° - 29° C)
• pH: 6.0 - 7.5
• KH: 1 - 5 dKH
• Minimum Tank Size: 30 gallons for a pair, 55+ gallons for a group

Care Guidelines:

• Diet: Omnivorous; thrives on a variety of high-quality foods.
• Social Behavior: Semi-peaceful; can be territorial, especially during breeding.
• Origin: Tank-bred; not wild-caught.
• Average Adult Size: 6 inches (15 cm) body height, up to 10 inches (25 cm) fin span.
• Average Purchase Size: 1.5 - 2.5 inches (3.8 - 6.3 cm)

Live Arrival Guarantee:

All Aquatic Arts animals come with a 100% Live Arrival Guarantee, plus free email support to help you care for your new fish!

Bring home the mesmerizing beauty of the Blue Turquoise Marble Angelfish and watch it become the centerpiece of your aquarium!

products.title: Pitcher Plant (Sarracenia) – Potted Carnivorous Plant
products.body_html:
The Pitcher Plant (Sarracenia) is a striking carnivorous plant known for its tall, tubular leaves that act as natural insect traps. Native to North America, these fascinating plants lure insects into their pitchers with nectar, bright colors, and downward-facing hairs, making escape nearly impossible. Once inside, the trapped insects are slowly digested, providing the plant with essential nutrients.

With their elegant, sculptural shape and vibrant hues ranging from green to deep red, Sarracenia species are perfect for terrariums, bog gardens, and windowsill displays. Pre-potted in a 2" Plastic Pots, this hardy carnivorous plant is easy to care for and thrives with the right conditions.

Features We Love:

  • Natural Pest Control: Effortlessly traps and digests insects, reducing unwanted pests in your space.
  • Unique Tubular Shape: The striking, upright pitchers add an architectural element to any plant collection.
  • Hardy and Low Maintenance: Adaptable to various conditions and requires minimal feeding.
  • Vibrant Colors: Many varieties develop bold red, purple, or yellow hues under the right lighting.

Care Guidelines:

  • Light: Full sun (at least 6+ hours of direct sunlight or strong artificial grow lights).
  • Water: Use only distilled water, rainwater, or reverse osmosis water—never tap water. Keep soil consistently moist but not waterlogged.
  • Temperature: 55° - 85° F (13° - 29° C) during the growing season; can tolerate brief cold periods in dormancy.
  • Humidity: Prefers moderate to high humidity but adapts well to normal indoor conditions with proper watering.
  • Growth Rate: Slow to moderate; forms new pitchers seasonally.
  • Dormancy: Requires a winter dormancy period with cooler temperatures (around 35° - 50° F) for 2-3 months.

Pro Tips:

  • Feeding: If grown indoors, provide occasional small insects like fruit flies—do not feed human food.
  • Soil: Thrives in a nutrient-poor mix of sphagnum moss and perlite; never use standard potting soil.
  • Repotting: Repot every 1-2 years in early spring with a carnivorous plant soil mix.
  • Watering Method: Keep the pot in a shallow tray of distilled water to maintain proper moisture levels.
  • Winter Dormancy: Allow the plant to rest in cooler temperatures with reduced watering during the winter months.

100% Live Arrival Guarantee

products.title: Tropical Pitcher Plant (Nepenthes) – Potted Carnivorous Plant
products.body_html:
The Nepenthes Pitcher Plant, also known as the Monkey Cup Plant, is an exotic, tropical carnivorous plant that develops unique hanging pitcher traps to capture and digest insects. These elegant and colorful pitchers produce sweet nectar that lures in prey, making it a natural pest controller while adding a stunning visual appeal to any indoor or greenhouse setup.

Nepenthes thrive in high-humidity environments and make excellent additions to terrariums, paludariums, and greenhouse setups. Their ability to absorb moisture from the air makes them a fantastic choice for enclosed tropical displays. Pre-potted in a 2" Plastic Pot, this low-maintenance carnivorous plant is a fascinating addition to any plant collection.

Features We Love:

  • Exotic, Hanging Pitchers: Beautiful, vase-shaped traps dangle from tendrils, perfect for unique displays.
  • Natural Pest Control: Attracts and digests insects, reducing flies, gnats, and other pests naturally.
  • Terrarium & Paludarium Friendly: Thrives in humid, enclosed environments with proper air circulation.
  • Perfect for Indoor Growing: Can thrive in warm, humid conditions with bright, indirect light.
  • Diverse and Unique Species: Different varieties produce pitchers in shades of red, green, or even speckled patterns.

Care Guidelines:

  • Light: Bright, indirect light or filtered sunlight; avoid direct afternoon sun.
  • Water: Use only distilled water, rainwater, or reverse osmosis water—never tap water. Keep soil moist but not waterlogged.
  • Temperature: 65° - 85° F (18° - 29° C); prefers warm, humid environments.
  • Humidity: Requires high humidity (60-80%); mist regularly or grow in a terrarium/paludarium/greenhouse setup.
  • Growth Rate: Moderate to fast; pitchers can grow for months before needing replacement.
  • Feeding: If grown indoors, occasionally provide small insects like fruit flies or freeze-dried bugs—never feed meat or human food.

Perfect for Terrariums & Paludariums!

The Nepenthes Pitcher Plant is an excellent choice for rainforest-style terrariums and tropical paludariums. Its ability to absorb moisture from the air and thrive in warm, humid conditions makes it a natural fit for bioactive enclosures. Pair it with mosses, ferns, and creeping vines for a lush, exotic look, and provide perches or hanging spots to allow the pitchers to develop freely.

Best Tank Mates for a Bioactive Setup:
✅ Dart Frogs & Tree Frogs – Safe in a tropical, misted terrarium.
✅ Geckos – Great for humid enclosures like Crested Gecko setups.
✅ Small Invertebrates – Thrives with springtails and isopods in bioactive setups.
❌ Not Recommended for Tanks with Herbivorous Reptiles – May get damaged by heavy foraging animals.

Pro Tips:

  • Watering Method: Keep soil consistently moist but avoid standing water in the pot.
  • Soil Mix: Prefers a well-draining, nutrient-poor mix like sphagnum moss and perlite.
  • Air Circulation: Good airflow prevents mold and helps maintain pitcher production.
  • Encourage Pitchers: Regular misting and stable humidity help maintain healthy pitcher growth.
  • Placement: Ideal for hanging baskets, terrarium perches, or attached to driftwood in a paludarium.

products.title: Vallisneria spiralis - Tissue Culture

Featuring long, ribbon-like leaves that sway beautifully with the current, this hardy plant is perfect for creating lush, jungle-like aquascapes while also providing shelter for fish and invertebrates.

Grown in sterile tissue culture, this plant is 100% pest-free, disease-free, and algae-free, ensuring a clean and healthy start for your aquarium. Unlike traditionally grown plants, tissue culture specimens arrive with strong, young growth, ready to establish in your tank.

Care Guidelines:

Pro Tips:

products.title: Bucephalandra (Sp. 'Sekadau Bukit Betung') - Tissue Culture

Like all Bucephalandra species, it thrives when attached to rocks or driftwood, making it a fantastic choice for aquascapers looking to create intricate, natural layouts.

This tissue-cultured specimen is 100% pest-free, disease-free, and algae-free, ensuring a pristine and healthy addition to your aquarium. Its slow-growing nature makes it an excellent plant for low-tech and high-tech setups alike, requiring minimal maintenance.

products.title: Albino Pygmy Corydoras (Corydoras pygmaeus 'Albino') – Tank Bred
products.body_html:
For the safety of our animals, we now ship live fish exclusively via UPS Next Day Air. If your order contains this item, you will only see UPS Next Day Air as a shipping option during checkout.

The Albino Pygmy Corydoras (Corydoras pygmaeus 'Albino') is a rare and fascinating nano catfish with the same peaceful and social nature as its standard Pygmy Cory relatives. This unique albino variant features a soft pinkish-white body with red eyes, making it a striking addition to any small freshwater aquarium. Like all Corydoras pygmaeus, this species prefers to swim midwater in active shoals, creating an engaging display of synchronized movement.

This tank-bred variety is perfect for planted aquariums and nano setups and thrives in groups of six or more. Their gentle nature and algae-grazing habits make them an excellent choice for peaceful community tanks.

What We Love About This Fish:

  • Rare Albino Variant – A unique take on the beloved Pygmy Cory with a soft pinkish-white body and red eyes.
  • Active Schooling Behavior – Unlike most Corydoras, this species prefers midwater swimming in tight groups.
  • Peaceful Nano Community Fish – Safe with small tank mates, including shrimp and snails.
  • Great for Planted Tanks – Their small size and gentle nature make them ideal for aquascapes.

Natural Habitat:

The Pygmy Corydoras species originates from slow-moving, heavily planted streams and tributaries in South America, particularly in the Madeira and Nanay River basins. They naturally inhabit areas with fine sand, leaf litter, and plenty of aquatic plants, which provide shelter and foraging opportunities.

While the Albino variant is captive-bred, it retains the same natural behaviors and habitat preferences.

Aquascaping:

  • Plants: Thrives in lush, planted aquariums with species like Anubias, Java Moss, Cryptocorynes, and Dwarf Hairgrass.
  • Hardscape: Prefers smooth river stones, driftwood, and fine sand substrates for safe foraging.
  • Botanicals: Benefits from Indian Almond Leaves and alder cones, which provide tannins and biofilm.

Diet and Feeding:

The Albino Pygmy Corydoras is an omnivore and thrives on a balanced diet:

  • Staple Diet: High-quality sinking micro pellets and small granules.
  • Protein Treats: Frozen or live foods like baby brine shrimp, daphnia, and microworms.
  • Vegetable Matter: Blanched zucchini, spinach, and spirulina-based foods.

To support optimal health and breeding, a varied diet with regular small feedings is recommended.

Tank Mates and Compatibility:

  • Best Tank Mates:
    ✅ Other peaceful nano fish such as Ember Tetras, Celestial Pearl Danios, Chili Rasboras, and Dwarf Rasboras.
    ✅ Shrimp-friendly and safe with Neocaridina and Caridina shrimp.
    ✅ Snails and other bottom-dwellers like Otocinclus and small Loaches.

  • Species to Avoid:
    ❌ Aggressive or large fish such as Cichlids or large Barbs.
    ❌ Fast-moving species that may outcompete them for food.

Shrimp Safe?

✅ Yes! The Albino Pygmy Corydoras is shrimp-safe and coexists well with dwarf shrimp species. Baby shrimp (shrimplets) may be eaten if small enough, but adults are safe.

Breeding:

Breeding Albino Pygmy Corydoras follows the same process as their wild-type relatives.

  • Temperature Triggers: Raising the temperature to 75-78°F and performing cool water changes simulates rainy season conditions.
  • Spawning Behavior: Females lay small adhesive eggs on tank surfaces, which hatch in 3-5 days.
  • Fry Care: Newly hatched fry can be fed infusoria and powdered fry food, followed by baby brine shrimp.

Breeding is easiest in a species-only setup with plenty of live plants and fine substrate.

Recommended Tank Parameters:

  • Temperature: 72° - 80° F (22° - 27° C)
  • pH: 6.2 - 7.5
  • KH: 2 - 10 dKH
  • Minimum Tank Size: 10 gallons (ideal for a school of 6+)

Care Guidelines:

  • Diet: Omnivorous; requires a varied diet of small sinking foods.
  • Social Behavior: Highly social, best kept in groups of 6 or more.
  • Origin: Tank-Bred.
  • Average Adult Size: 0.75 - 1 inch (2 - 2.5 cm).
  • Average Purchase Size: 0.5 - 0.75 inch (1.3 - 2 cm).

Live Arrival Guarantee:

products.title: Sunset Thicklip Gourami (Trichogaster labiosa) - Tank Bred
products.body_html:
For the safety of our animals, we now ship live fish exclusively via UPS Next Day Air. If your order contains this item, you will only see UPS Next Day Air as a shipping option during checkout.

The Sunset Thicklip Gourami (Trichogaster labiosa) is a peaceful, beautifully colored labyrinth fish that makes a fantastic centerpiece for a well-planted community aquarium. This tank-bred variety features warm golden-orange hues with subtle iridescence, making it a standout addition to any freshwater setup. Known for their gentle nature and fascinating behaviors, these gouramis are easy to care for and thrive in a variety of water conditions.

What We Love About This Fish:

  • Vibrant Sunset Colors – Stunning golden-orange body with subtle metallic sheen.
  • Peaceful and Hardy – Excellent choice for community tanks with compatible tank mates.
  • Intelligent and Curious – Interactive personality, often recognizing their keepers.
  • Labyrinth Fish – Can breathe atmospheric air, allowing them to thrive in various conditions.

Natural Habitat:

The Sunset Thicklip Gourami originates from slow-moving rivers, ponds, and floodplains in India and Bangladesh. In the wild, they inhabit dense vegetation, shallow waters, and areas with abundant floating plants, which provide shelter and a sense of security. Their ability to breathe atmospheric oxygen allows them to survive in low-oxygen environments.

Aquascaping:

  • Plants: Ideal for heavily planted tanks with species like Java Fern, Amazon Swords, Cryptocorynes, and Floating Plants (e.g., Frogbit, Water Lettuce).
  • Hardscape: Enjoys a mix of driftwood, smooth river stones, and leaf litter for natural aesthetics.
  • Botanicals: Indian Almond Leaves or Alder Cones provide tannins for a more natural blackwater effect.

Diet and Feeding:

The Sunset Thicklip Gourami is an omnivore that thrives on a varied diet:

  • Staple Diet: High-quality flakes or micro pellets.
  • Protein Treats: Frozen or live foods such as bloodworms, brine shrimp, and daphnia.
  • Vegetable Matter: Spirulina-based flakes, blanched zucchini, and peas.

Feeding a mix of plant-based and protein-rich foods enhances their coloration and supports optimal health.

Tank Mates and Compatibility:

  • Best Tank Mates:
    ✅ Peaceful community fish like Tetras, Rasboras, Corydoras, and small Loaches.
    ✅ Compatible with Dwarf Cichlids (such as Apistogramma) and other labyrinth fish with caution.
    ✅ Snails and larger shrimp like Amano Shrimp may coexist safely.

  • Species to Avoid:
    ❌ Fin-nipping species like Tiger Barbs.
    ❌ Aggressive or overly active fish that may stress them out.
    ❌ Very small shrimp (like Neocaridina) may be at risk of being eaten.

Shrimp Safe?

🔸 Caution Advised – Larger shrimp like Amano Shrimp are usually safe, but dwarf shrimp (Neocaridina) may be at risk, especially juveniles. If keeping with shrimp, provide dense plants and hiding spaces.

Breeding:

The Sunset Thicklip Gourami is a bubble nest builder. Males construct floating nests using saliva and plant materials, where the female will deposit eggs. After spawning, males guard the eggs until they hatch. Breeding requires warmer temperatures (78-82°F) and a well-structured tank with floating plants to encourage nest-building.

Recommended Tank Parameters:

  • Temperature: 72° - 82° F (22° - 28° C)
  • pH: 6.0 - 7.5
  • KH: 2 - 10 dKH
  • Minimum Tank Size: 20 gallons

Care Guidelines:

  • Diet: Omnivorous; benefits from a varied diet of flakes, pellets, and live/frozen foods.
  • Social Behavior: Peaceful but can be territorial with other gouramis; best kept singly or in compatible groups.
  • Origin: Tank-Bred.
  • Average Adult Size: 3.5 - 4 inches (8.9 - 10.2 cm).
  • Average Purchase Size: 1.5 - 2.5 inches (3.8 - 6.4 cm).

Live Arrival Guarantee:

products.title: Venus Fly Trap (Dionaea muscipula) - Potted Carnivorous Plant
products.body_html:
The Venus Fly Trap (Dionaea muscipula) is one of the most iconic and fascinating carnivorous plants in the world. Known for its rapid snap-trap mechanism, this unique plant captures and digests insects, making it a natural and effective way to control pests. With its striking green and red-hued traps, the Venus Fly Trap is perfect for terrariums, windowsills, or dedicated carnivorous plant setups.

Pre-potted in a 3" in plastic pot, this plant is easy to place in your home or greenhouse with minimal disturbance. Whether grown indoors with proper lighting or outdoors in the right climate, the Venus Fly Trap offers an exciting and interactive experience for plant enthusiasts of all levels.

Features We Love:

  • Natural Pest Control: Captures and digests flies, gnats, and other small insects.
  • Fascinating Motion Traps: Unique snap-trap leaves close within seconds when triggered.
  • Easy to Grow: Low-maintenance with the right conditions—requires distilled water and bright light.
  • Striking Appearance: Beautiful green foliage with red-tinged traps adds a dramatic touch to any plant collection.

Care Guidelines:

  • Light: Full sun (at least 12 hours of direct light or strong artificial grow lights).
  • Water: Use only distilled water, rainwater, or reverse osmosis water—never tap water. Keep soil consistently moist but not waterlogged.
  • Temperature: 70° - 85° F (21° - 29° C) during the growing season; can tolerate brief cold periods in dormancy.
  • Humidity: Prefers moderate to high humidity but adapts to normal indoor conditions with proper watering.
  • Growth Rate: Slow to moderate; forms clumping rosettes over time.
  • Dormancy: Requires a winter dormancy period with cooler temperatures (around 35° - 50° F) for 2-3 months.

Pro Tips:

  • Feeding: If grown indoors, feed it live or freeze-dried insects occasionally—avoid feeding human food.
  • Soil: Thrives in a nutrient-poor mix of sphagnum moss and perlite; never use regular potting soil.
  • Repotting: Repot every 1-2 years in spring using a carnivorous plant soil mix.
  • Avoid Overhandling: Excessively triggering traps can exhaust the plant, so avoid touching them unless necessary.
  • Winter Dormancy: If grown outdoors, allow natural seasonal changes; if indoors, gradually reduce watering and keep in a cooler location.

100% Live Arrival Guarantee

products.title: Green Wavy Buce Plant (Bucephalandra sp. "Green Wavy") Tissue Culture
products.body_html:
This lovely buce plant has very unique, wavy leaves and is a perfect plant for the nano aquarium!

 

Green Wavy Buce Plant (Bucephalandra sp. “Green Wavy”) is a variant of the extremely popular Bucephalandra group. This particular Buce plant is known for its ruffled, bright green leaves. It is a very hardy plant that can be grown emersed or submerged in the aquarium, terrarium, and paludarium.  It requires only low to moderate lighting and can thrive in a variety of water parameters. With proper care, Green Wavy Buce Plant will grow at a slow to moderate rate. The care of Buce plants is similar to Anubias and Cryptocoryne species. Also, like Anubias, Buce plants are rhizome plants that attach to and grow on hard surfaces. Ther attach particularly well to rocks, but can also grow very well on driftwood and other surfaces. If planted on the substrate, Buce plants should be only partially embedded in the substrate with the rhizome exposed. If the rhizome is buried, it is prone to rot.

 

In addition to its attractive appearance, Green Wavy Buce Plant provides a valuable refuge for aquarium animals, especially dwarf shrimp, nano fish, and fry.  With proper care, Green Wavy Buce Plant can easily flourish and grow to become a major centerpiece of the planted aquarium!

 

Like most plants, Green Wavy Buce Plant will benefit from supplementation such as Seachem Flourish, Flourish Excel, Nitrogen and other plant supplements. However, supplementation is not required for this plant to grow and stay healthy. Buce plants generally show their most intense coloration and optimal growth when submerged with high lighting and stable, consistent fertilization. Drastic changes in fertilization can result in gradual leaf loss, although the plant rhizome will typically recover in time.

 

This listing is for the tissue culture form of Green Wavy Buce Plant. Tissue cultures are superior to traditional forms of aquarium plants in many ways. They are produced in a completely sterile environment which eliminates the possibility of them carrying pest snails or algae spores. They have a shelf life (before introduction to the aquarium) of several months if properly maintained and they are housed in a nutrient gel until introduced to the aquarium. To introduce a tissue culture plant to the aquarium, simply rinse off as much nutrient gel as possible, then plant as usual. The nutrient gel will not harm your aquarium. 

 

What We Like About This Plant:

  • Tissue cultures contain no unwanted pest animals or algae
  • Exceptional coloration
  • Thrives in low-tech and high-tech setups
  • Can be attached to almost any aquarium décor
  • Perfect for shrimp and nano tanks

 

Care Guidelines:

  • Temperature: 71.6° - 82.4° F (22° - 28° C)
  • pH: 5.0 - 8.0, although 6.0 - 7.5 is more ideal.
  • Lighting: Low to High
  • Origin: Lab-grown tissue culture, but indigenous to Borneo, Indonesia
  • Aquarium placement: Middle and foreground. Rhizome should not be completely buried.
  • Care: Easy to Moderate

The Vienna Guppy is a vibrant and highly sought-after guppy variety, known for its stunning multi-colored tail and iridescent body. This dazzling strain features a mesmerizing mix of blues, purples, and reds, making it a true showpiece in any aquarium. As an active, peaceful fish, the Vienna Guppy thrives in community tanks and is easy to care for, making it an excellent choice for both beginners and experienced aquarists. These guppies are tank-bred, ensuring they are well-adapted to aquarium life.

What We Love About This Fish:

• Brilliant Multi-Colored Tail – A dazzling mix of blues, purples, and reds.
• Hardy and Adaptable – Thrives in a variety of water conditions.
• Active and Social – Adds vibrant movement to the aquarium.
• Easy to Breed – A great choice for hobbyists interested in guppy genetics.

Natural Habitat:

Wild guppies originate from freshwater streams and ponds in South America, where they thrive in warm, slow-moving waters with abundant vegetation.

Aquascaping:

• Plants: Java Moss, Hornwort, Anubias, Amazon Sword
• Floating Plants: Frogbit, Duckweed, Water Lettuce
• Hardscape: Driftwood and smooth stones for natural aesthetics

Diet and Feeding:

Vienna Guppies are omnivores that thrive on a diet of high-quality flake food, micro pellets, and occasional live or frozen foods like brine shrimp, daphnia, and bloodworms. A varied diet enhances their color and health.

Tank Mates and Compatibility:

Peaceful and social, Vienna Guppies do well with other small community fish, such as tetras, rasboras, corydoras, and peaceful shrimp. Avoid housing them with aggressive or fin-nipping species.

Shrimp Safe?

Generally safe with adult shrimp but may eat shrimp fry if given the opportunity. Providing dense plant cover can help shrimp colonies thrive.

Breeding:

Vienna Guppies are livebearers and reproduce readily in well-maintained aquariums. Fry should be provided with hiding spots, such as dense plants or breeding mops, to increase survival rates.

Recommended Tank Parameters:
